- SpecialistChainGraph excerpt (forge-3ue5.10)
- BottomDrawer SpecialistsTabPanel last-message column (forge-gud9)

Root cause is in apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/dao.ts:4:

const JOB_COLUMNS = "job_id, bead_id, chain_id, epic_id, chain_kind, status, updated_at_ms, specialist";

last_output is not in the SELECT column list, so the column is never read from specialist_jobs. mapRow() therefore never sets it on SpecialistJob, the /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight route serializes the job without it, and every frontend hook (useChains, useChainDetail, useSpecialistHistory, useInFlightJobs) resolves its 'lastOutput ?? last_output' fallback to null.

Verified by curl: /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight?limit=3 returns objects with no lastOutput / last_output field at all.

## SUCCESS
- /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight responses include a 'lastOutput' field (camelCase) populated from the specialist_jobs.last_output column.
- /console/specialists ChainDetailPane renders a truncated excerpt of the latest job's output.
- Inline ┬Ę N runs dossier sections in feed show the per-job excerpt.
- SpecialistChainGraph nodes show their excerpt tooltip/inline.
- BottomDrawer Specialists tab shows last-message column.
- When last_output is NULL in the db (e.g. brand new job, no turns yet), surface gracefully shows empty without errors.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/dao.ts ŌĆö add last_output to JOB_COLUMNS; extend mapRow() to read it; update SpecialistJob type to include lastOutput: string | null.
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/types.ts ŌĆö add lastOutput to SpecialistJob (matches what hooks already expect as optional).
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts ŌĆö confirm the route just forwards the DAO shape; if it explicitly picks fields, add lastOutput to the picked set.
- Tests in apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/specialists.test.ts and tests/__tests__/dao.test.ts ŌĆö add fixture row with last_output text + assert it round-trips.

## NON_GOALS
- Adding new output fields (just plumb the existing one).
- Streaming feed (that's forge-lqyc.8).
- Changing column semantics in observability.db schema.
- Migrating older snapshots that pre-date last_output (just nullable).

## CONSTRAINTS
- last_output column in specialist_jobs may be NULL ŌĆö must handle null in mapRow and types.
- Some observability dbs have schema_version mismatches (already handled by attach-pool dead-cache); last_output may not exist on all schemas. Wrap the SELECT in a graceful fallback if the column isn't found (try/catch around the prepared statement, or pre-check via PRAGMA table_info ŌĆö same pattern as the labels-table soft fail in dolt-client).
- API field naming: lastOutput (camelCase) on the wire, to match the existing frontend hooks' primary key. They already accept last_output too, but new code should emit camelCase consistently.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] curl /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight?limit=3 ŌĆö each job has lastOutput key (null or string).
- [ ] Open /console/specialists with at least one running specialist ŌĆö 'Last output' block renders an excerpt.
- [ ] Open a closed bead with prior runs in the feed ŌĆö dossier ┬Ę N runs section excerpts populated.
- [ ] Job whose db row has last_output IS NULL renders gracefully (empty, no error).
- [ ] DB schema missing last_output column does not crash the route (other repos in attach pool still return their jobs with lastOutput: null).
- [ ] bun run test apps/gitboard/tests/__tests__/dao.test.ts apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/specialists.test.ts passes.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, curl snippet showing the new field, screenshot of cockpit Last output populated, residual notes.
close_reason: Done: dao.ts now SELECTs last_output (with PRAGMA-probed graceful fallback to NULL on older schemas), mapRow propagates it, SpecialistJob type extended, 2 new dao tests assert round-trip + missing-column behavior. All 5 dao tests pass; attach-pool's 2 pre-existing slug-hyphen failures unrelated.

title: Cache graph data and reuse robust project/Dolt discovery
description:
## PROBLEM

`apps/gitboard/src/core/graph-dao.ts` rescans projects on every graph request, creates a fresh less-robust Dolt client, and has logged many multi-second `/api/console/graph` responses. Graph is a dashboard surface and should refresh from WS/sync hints without forcing expensive full discovery on every navigation or focus event.

## SUCCESS

Graph loads quickly from cached project discovery/graph data, refreshes on explicit invalidation or short TTL, and uses the same robust source handling as beads. Focus/visibility events do not cause repeated expensive rescans when data is fresh.

## SCOPE

- `apps/gitboard/src/core/graph-dao.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/graph.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGraphData.ts`
- Shared scanner/Dolt access only where needed.
- Tests for graph caching/invalidation behavior.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not redesign graph layout or clustering visuals.
- Do not change graph response shape except backward-compatible metadata.
- Do not solve specialist endpoint performance except overlay interactions needed by graph.

## CONSTRAINTS

- WS/sync hints or epoch changes must invalidate stale graph data quickly.
- Cached graph data must not hide active specialist/bead state indefinitely.
- Avoid cross-project stale data leakage.
- Run GitNexus impact on `getGraph`/graph DAO symbols before editing.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] Repeated graph requests for the same project avoid repeated full scans inside TTL.
- [ ] WS/sync hint or explicit refresh invalidates graph cache.
- [ ] Graph still falls back to JSONL when Dolt is unavailable.
- [ ] Slow request logs for `/api/console/graph` improve in local smoke.
- [ ] Relevant lint/tests pass.

## OUTPUT

Return cache keys, invalidation rules, before/after route timing evidence, and changed files.

## APPROACH NOTES

Investigation logs: `/api/console/graph` had hundreds of slow requests, average around 2.4s and extreme max latency. Current hook also reloads on focus and visibility change.
notes:
Research integration from jobs 7bd546 and bcbd2a:
- Graph should reuse robust source handling: per-key Dolt runtime/fallback, not a fresh singleton-like Dolt client per request.
- Use in-process cache keyed by project_id/include_closed/source_version/epoch where possible.
- Use short TTL + stale-while-revalidate + in-flight dedupe to avoid repeated scan/query work on focus or repeated graph opens.
- Invalidate on beads WS sync_hint/change, project/source epoch changes, or explicit refresh. Do not let cache hide live running/specialist state indefinitely.
close_reason: Done: graph scanner/issues caching, explicit refresh invalidation, WS-triggered client refresh, and regression tests.

title: Make GitHub dashboard tab data lazy, cached, and WS-merged
description:
## PROBLEM

GitHub navigation currently remounts `MainPane` by tab and `useGithubRepoData` fetches events, PRs, issues, and releases together for every tab. `useGithubActivity` also blocks initial render on broad PR/issue/release loads. This disrupts the dashboard feel and can overwrite or delay WebSocket-visible freshness.

## SUCCESS

Switching GitHub tabs fetches only the data needed for that tab, uses cached data immediately when available, keeps stale data visible during refresh, and merges `github:activity` WebSocket upserts/sync hints without waiting for unrelated HTTP requests.

## SCOPE

- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/MainPane.tsx`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/github.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/client.ts` if endpoint wrappers need abort/cache helpers.
- GitHub timeline components only where loading state wiring is required.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not redesign GitHub tabs or timeline visuals.
- Do not implement PR detail/report caching here; that is separate.
- Do not remove WebSocket subscription logic.

## CONSTRAINTS

- Remove unnecessary remount/refetch behavior without breaking selected repo/tab navigation.
- WS events must be newer-than-cache authoritative; stale HTTP responses must not erase WebSocket upserts.
- Initial activity view must not wait for releases or PR detail-like data.
- Run GitNexus impact on `useGithubActivity` and any modified store methods before editing.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] Tab switch Activity ŌåÆ PRs does not request issues/releases unless needed.
- [ ] Initial GitHub activity renders before releases are loaded.
- [ ] Simulated `github:pr.upsert`, `github:issue.upsert`, and `github:event.append` update visible state instantly.
- [ ] Fetch-count tests or equivalent prove unrelated requests are not fired on tab switch.
- [ ] `bun --filter @xtrm/gitboard lint` and relevant UI tests pass.

## OUTPUT

Return changed files, fetch behavior matrix by tab, WS merge behavior evidence, and any caching invalidation rules.

## APPROACH NOTES

Likely changes: remove `selection.tab` from `MainPane` key, split `useGithubRepoData` by tab or cache key, make releases lazy, and preserve current data while refreshing.
notes:
Research integration from job 677776:
- Recommended approach: TanStack Query for server state, small Zustand for UI/live overlay.
- Use query keys by repo/tab/filter; enable queries lazily by active tab; keep previous data visible with placeholderData/keepPreviousData patterns.
- Fetch functions should consume AbortSignal from TanStack Query.
- On WS events, call queryClient.cancelQueries for affected keys, then queryClient.setQueryData/upsert, then optionally invalidateQueries for background reconciliation.
- Guard against stale HTTP overwrite by version/updated_at checks in cache patchers.
- Real-world patterns checked: Langflow, Ghost, coder/coder, multica-ai use queryClient.setQueryData/cancelQueries for live/optimistic cache merges.
Implementation progress 2026-05-20:
- GitNexus impact run before edits for `useGithubActivity`, `useGithubRepoData`, `useGithubStore`, and `ShellApp`.
- Removed `selection.tab` from `MainPane` key so GitHub tab switches no longer remount the whole main pane/drawer.
- Split `useGithubRepoData` behavior by active GitHub tab: activity fetches only events, PR tab fetches only PRs, issues fetches only issues, releases fetches only releases; README/changelog/reports remain panel-owned.
- Added per-repo in-memory tab cache in `MainPane.tsx` that keeps stale/cached tab data visible while refreshing.
- Merged live Zustand WS state into repo tab cache for events/PRs/issues; merge keeps newer `created_at`/`updated_at` data authoritative so stale HTTP responses cannot overwrite newer WS data.
- Added `useGithubActivity({ includeLists: false })` option and used it from the IDE shell so initial shell activity load no longer fans out to PRs/issues/releases; legacy `GithubPanel` keeps default list loading for compatibility.
- Added `apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/components/shell/MainPane.github-cache.test.tsx` covering tab-specific fetches and stale HTTP vs live PR merge.
- Extended `apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.test.ts` for no PR/issue/release fan-out in shell mode.
Validation so far:
- PASS: `bun --filter @xtrm/gitboard test -- tests/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.test.ts tests/dashboard/components/github/GithubPanel.test.tsx tests/dashboard/components/shell/MainPane.github-cache.test.tsx`
- `bun --filter @xtrm/gitboard lint` still fails only on pre-existing `src/dashboard/components/terminal/TerminalStream.tsx` xterm type errors; no errors in touched forge-ojn4.4 files.
- Code-sanity job ae7bb9 stalled after GitNexus std::bad_alloc and timed out before producing findings.
close_reason: Done: GitHub shell now avoids initial PR/issue/release fan-out, loads tab data lazily with per-repo cache, keeps cached/live data visible, and preserves WS-newer data over stale HTTP.

title: Isolate Dolt connection pools and breakers per project source
description:
## PROBLEM

`apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts` uses a module-level runtime singleton for pool, breaker, probe timer, metrics, and failure state while callers cache clients by port. Accessing multiple Dolt ports/databases can close the previous pool, share breaker failures across unrelated projects, and make the dashboard slow or falsely degraded.

## SUCCESS

Dolt runtime state is isolated per `host:port/database/user` pool key. One broken Dolt project cannot poison another, switching projects does not force reconnect churn, and stale JSONL/SQLite fallback remains fast and visible when a single source is degraded.

## SCOPE

- `apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts`
- `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts` only if source health/connection handling needs small adjustments.
- `apps/gitboard/src/core/graph-dao.ts` only for integration notes if this client is reused by graph in a later bead.
- Tests for Dolt client runtime behavior using mocks/fakes where a live Dolt server is unavailable.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not rewrite beads storage or Dolt schema.
- Do not remove JSONL/SQLite fallback.
- Do not change public beads API response shapes except adding backward-compatible health metadata.

## CONSTRAINTS

- Circuit breaker, latency ring, probe timer, and pool lifecycle must be per pool key.
- Existing `DoltClient` public methods should remain compatible.
- Failed optional tables like `labels` and `issue_related` should still fail soft.
- Run GitNexus impact on `DoltClient` before editing.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] Tests prove two different Dolt configs do not share breaker state or close each otherŌĆÖs pools.
- [ ] Tests prove breaker-open on one config does not block another config.
- [ ] Connection endpoint still returns degraded JSONL fallback when Dolt is unavailable.
- [ ] `bun --filter @xtrm/beadboard lint` and relevant tests pass.

## OUTPUT

Return changed files, before/after runtime model, validation commands, and residual risk around live Dolt behavior.

## APPROACH NOTES

GitNexus context/impact should include `DoltClient`, route callers, watcher callers, and graph integration. Investigation observed thousands of `source.degraded` logs and repeated breaker openings.
notes:
Research integration from job 7bd546:
- Replace module singleton Dolt runtime with Map<poolKey, DoltRuntime>, where poolKey = host:port/database/user.
- Runtime bucket should own: pool, breakerOpenUntil, consecutiveFailures, probeTimer, probing, latency metrics, lastHealthyAt, reconnect/backoff state.
- mysql2 createPool with connectionLimit/maxIdle/idleTimeout/keepAlive is appropriate.
- Query timeout should be explicit; mysql2 timeout is client-side inactivity and destroys connection on hit, so avoid reusing timed-out connections blindly.
- One poolKey breaker opening must not affect another poolKey. This is the central regression test.
- Consider custom small breaker over opossum unless richer metrics/fallback are needed.
Implementation progress 2026-05-20:
- GitNexus impact run for `DoltClient` before edits; `getDoltClient` impact run before route-cache edits.
- Overthinker job 083f75 recommended NO-GO for singleton runtime and required per-key runtime map + full identity route cache.
- Implemented `Map<string, DoltRuntimeState>` keyed by `host:port/database/user`; each runtime owns pool, breaker, probe timer, failure counters, reconnect attempts, and metrics.
- Exported `doltPoolKey()` and changed beadboard route cache from `Map<number, DoltClient>` to `Map<string, DoltClient>` keyed by the full Dolt identity including database.
- Added `apps/beadboard/tests/core/dolt-client-runtime.test.ts` for separate pools, breaker isolation, and keyed health snapshots.
Validation so far:
- PASS: `bun --filter @xtrm/beadboard test -- tests/core/dolt-client-runtime.test.ts`
- PASS: `bun --filter @xtrm/beadboard test -- tests/core/beads-change-watcher.test.ts tests/core/dolt-client-runtime.test.ts`
- PASS: `bun --filter @xtrm/gitboard test -- tests/api/routes/graph.test.ts`
- PASS: `bun --filter @xtrm/gitboard lint`
- NOTE: `bun --filter @xtrm/beadboard lint` currently fails on pre-existing tsconfig/import-extension/DOM lib issues across many files, not just this diff.
- NOTE: live `tests/core/dolt-client.test.ts` ran against local Dolt and only failed existing data-dependent `includes labels` assertion; runtime tests pass.
- Code-sanity job 971dc8 initially flagged stale singleton/port cache concerns; after rebuttal with current diff, returned OK/no findings.
- Reviewer job b596fb dispatched and timer checks started.
close_reason: Done: isolated Dolt pool/breaker/probe/metrics runtime per host:port/database/user and added runtime isolation tests; targeted validation passes.

title: Add dashboard performance budgets and realtime regression instrumentation
description:
## PROBLEM

Before optimizing individual paths, the project needs a small shared definition of what ŌĆ£fast dashboardŌĆØ and ŌĆ£instant WebSocket refreshŌĆØ mean. Without budgets and smoke instrumentation, fixes can accidentally improve HTTP latency while regressing realtime freshness or hiding stale data bugs.

## SUCCESS

There is a documented and testable baseline for route latency, tab-switch behavior, stale-while-revalidate behavior, and WebSocket merge behavior. The instrumentation is lightweight and supports validating later child beads without changing the product UX.

## SCOPE

- `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts` request slow logging thresholds/metadata if needed.
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts` and `useGraphData.ts` only for observability hooks if required.
- Test helpers under `apps/gitboard/tests` for fetch counting, WS message simulation, and stale-cache assertions.
- Documentation can live in the bead notes or an existing test/readme file if needed.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not implement the actual Dolt/GitHub/graph optimizations in this bead.
- Do not add heavyweight analytics dependencies.
- Do not alter visible UI except optional dev-only diagnostics.

## CONSTRAINTS

- Instrumentation must not slow normal dashboard paths materially.
- WS events remain authoritative over older HTTP responses.
- Keep output consumable by future specialists: concrete route names, timing thresholds, and smoke steps.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] A future executor can run a local smoke/test to prove WS upsert updates UI state without full reload.
- [ ] Fetch-count or equivalent tests can detect tab switches causing unrelated endpoint calls.
- [ ] Route timing evidence can distinguish graph, beads, github, and specialists slow paths.
- [ ] `bun --filter @xtrm/gitboard lint` passes.

## OUTPUT

Return the chosen budgets, test helper names/files, and exact commands future beads should run for perf/realtime validation.

## APPROACH NOTES

Prior logs showed `/api/console/graph`, `/api/beads/projects*`, `/api/github/prs`, and `/api/specialists/jobs*` as slow. Treat WS channels `github:activity` and `beads:changes` as core realtime dashboard paths.
notes:
Research integration:
- Define budgets/tests around the invariant: WebSocket updates win over stale HTTP responses.
- React researcher recommends TanStack Query test helpers around cancelQueries + setQueryData + invalidateQueries, plus fetch-count assertions for lazy tabs.
- Hono/Bun researcher recommends endpoint tests for TTL, stale-while-revalidate, in-flight dedupe, route timeouts, and epoch/WS invalidation.
- GitHub researcher recommends tests for 304 no-op, watermark stop, backoff on 403/429, and SHA/updated_at cache invalidation.
- Dolt researcher recommends tests proving per-pool-key breaker/pool isolation.
Implementation output 2026-05-20:
- Added `apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/perf/realtime-helpers.ts` with shared budgets, slow endpoint families, fetch-count instrumentation, deferred response helper, and WS-over-stale-HTTP freshness assertion.
- Added `apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/perf/dashboard-performance-budget.test.ts` covering slow-path list, realtime budget invariant, fetch-family counting, and stale HTTP not overwriting a newer WS update.
- Added `apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/perf/README.md` documenting budgets and exact commands for future forge-ojn4 beads.
Validation:
- PASS: `bun --filter @xtrm/gitboard test -- tests/dashboard/perf/dashboard-performance-budget.test.ts`
- PASS: `bun --filter @xtrm/gitboard test -- tests/dashboard/perf/dashboard-performance-budget.test.ts tests/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.test.ts tests/dashboard/stores/github-upsert.test.ts tests/api/ws/realtime-contract.test.ts`
- PASS: `bun --filter @xtrm/gitboard lint`
- NOTE: full `bun --filter @xtrm/gitboard test` currently fails in pre-existing/unrelated suites (observed 7 failed files / 23 failed tests, including `tests/dashboard/pages/console/graph/layout.test.ts`). This bead's targeted perf/realtime checks pass.
close_reason: Done: added dashboard performance budgets and realtime regression instrumentation; targeted tests and lint pass.

title: Realtime dashboard performance hardening without losing instant WebSocket refresh
description:
## PROBLEM

Gitboard currently behaves like a dashboard, but several slow request paths make navigation feel like a document app: switching pages/tabs often waits on full HTTP reloads, Dolt connection fallback can stall, GitHub detail requests can fan out, and graph/specialist endpoints have repeated multi-second responses. The dashboard must stay realtime: WebSocket updates should still apply instantly while HTTP reads become cache-backed, lazy, and non-blocking.

## SUCCESS

The dashboard preserves instant WebSocket refresh for GitHub, beads, graph/specialist overlays, and issue state while page/tab navigation no longer blocks on unrelated data. Slow Dolt or GitHub paths degrade gracefully with stale cached data, per-panel loading states, timeouts, and visible degraded-source indicators instead of global spinners.

## SCOPE

- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/MainPane.tsx`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGraphData.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/client.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/core/graph-dao.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts`
- `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts`
- `apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts`
- Tests under `apps/gitboard/tests` and `apps/beadboard/tests` as needed.

## NON_GOALS

- No redesign of the dashboard visual language or navigation model.
- No replacement of Hono, Bun SQLite, mysql2, Zustand, or the existing WebSocket channel registry.
- No removal of realtime WebSocket paths in favor of polling-only behavior.
- No GitHub GraphQL migration unless filed as a follow-up.

## CONSTRAINTS

- WebSocket updates must remain first-class and instant; HTTP cache/stale data must merge with WS upserts without overwriting newer events.
- Existing API response shapes should remain backward-compatible unless a child bead explicitly owns a typed migration.
- Degraded Dolt/GitHub states must fail soft and keep the visible dashboard usable.
- Any cache must have explicit invalidation via WS sync hints, epoch/version, TTL, or route-specific refresh rules.
- Before code edits, run GitNexus impact for the specific symbol(s) being changed.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] Tab switches do not trigger unrelated GitHub data requests.
- [ ] Initial GitHub dashboard render is not blocked by releases, PR detail, reports, or markdown content.
- [ ] Dolt failures are isolated per project/port and do not poison unrelated projects.
- [ ] WebSocket upsert/sync_hint events visibly update dashboard state without a manual refresh.
- [ ] Slow request log volume drops for `/api/console/graph`, `/api/beads/projects*`, `/api/github/*`, and `/api/specialists/jobs*`.
- [ ] `bun run build`, app lint/typecheck, and planned regression tests pass.

## OUTPUT

Epic close handoff must summarize each child bead, changed files, perf evidence before/after, WebSocket realtime smoke evidence, and any residual risk or follow-up beads.

## REFERENCES

Investigation findings from 2026-05-20: slow Dolt runtime singleton, GitHub tab remount/refetch, release fan-out blocking initial load, graph route rescans, PR detail fan-out, route-order bug for `/issues/closed`, poller inter-repo sleep, and slow specialist endpoint logs.
notes:
Research findings integrated 2026-05-20 from researcher jobs:
- 677776 / forge-ojn4.11: React dashboard server state should use TanStack Query for cached/lazy/stale-while-revalidate data, with small Zustand store retained for UI state and live WS overlay. WebSocket updates should patch query cache via queryClient.setQueryData after cancelQueries to prevent stale HTTP overwrite.
- 7bd546 / forge-ojn4.12: Dolt/mysql2 runtime should be Map<poolKey, runtime>, not a singleton. Pool, breaker, probe timer, latency metrics, and failure counts must be scoped by host:port/database/user.
- f41b36 / forge-ojn4.13: GitHub REST efficiency should use conditional GETs/ETags, per_page=100 with Link/paginate iteration, rate-limit headers, persisted ETags, SHA/versioned cache keys, and progressive PR detail hydration.
- bcbd2a / forge-ojn4.14: Hono/Bun endpoints should use short TTL + stale-while-revalidate + in-flight dedupe + epoch/WS invalidation. Keep in-process cache only; no Redis/external infra.
Primary invariant across all child work: WS updates remain instant and authoritative over stale HTTP/cache data.
close_reason: Done: all 28 child beads complete and merged; realtime dashboard hardening delivered with backend/UI regression coverage.

title: Add reproducible terminal E2E smoke and regression tests
description: PROBLEM: Regressions were found only manually: disconnected, invalid_message, renderer crash, no typing. SUCCESS: A repeatable smoke path covers project load, terminal websocket upgrade, open/status/output, input echo, drawer close/reopen attach, and xterm lifecycle. SCOPE: automated tests where feasible plus documented manual smoke script; preserve security negative tests. NON_GOALS: full browser automation stack unless lightweight and available. VALIDATION: smoke fails before fixes and passes after; focused terminal suite green.

title: Polish terminal drawer UI against code-server/Wetty baseline

## Risks

### Finding 1
- ID: TERM-WS-001
- Severity: medium
- Category/CWE: CWE-285 Improper Authorization
- Evidence:
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/bridge.ts:69-98` ŌĆö `attach`, `input`, `resize`, `exit` authorize by `sessionId` lookup only
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/bridge.ts:48-63` ŌĆö `open` trusts client-chosen `sessionId`, stores session under that key
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/bridge.ts:76-79` ŌĆö `detach` removes attachment for any supplied `sessionId`
  - `apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/terminal.test.ts:27-66` ŌĆö lifecycle tests cover happy path + disabled provider only; no cross-connection ownership tests
- Reachability/exploitability:
  - Any client on `/api/console/terminal/ws` can send `attach`, `input`, `resize`, `exit` with guessed or reused `sessionId`
  - No check that sender opened session
  - No check that sender remains attached
  - No check that `streamId` matches stored session stream
- Impact:
  - If any interactive provider later becomes enabled, one websocket client can hijack, drive, or terminate another client session
  - Duplicate `open` on same `sessionId` can overwrite map entry, orphan old session until provider exits/disconnects
- Recommended fix:
  - Bind session ownership to server-issued opaque handle, not client-chosen `sessionId` alone
  - Reject `attach/input/resize/exit/detach` unless connection owns session or presents validated attach token
  - Reject duplicate `open` for existing `sessionId`, or dispose old session before replace
  - Enforce `streamId === state.streamId` on mutating messages
  - Add tests for cross-connection attach/input/exit denial and duplicate-session rejection
- Verification after fix:
  - Add tests where `connB` cannot control `connA` session
  - Add test for duplicate `open` same `sessionId`
  - Re-run terminal route suite

- No user input/output logging found in audited bridge path. `bridge.ts` logs nothing. `server.ts` request logs keep path/status/error only. `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:56-67`
- Provider registry does not bypass shell policy today. `pty` remains disabled even when shell policy enabled. `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/provider-registry.ts:34-40`
- Native PTY default stays safe-disabled when dependency unavailable. `apps/gitboard/src/core/local-pty-provider.ts:295-302`
- Shared protocol validation exists, but only shape/type validation. Not enough for ownership/authz. `packages/core/src/terminal/protocol.ts:206-254`

title: Validate terminal stream architecture and safety design
description:
PROBLEM: The epic's highest-risk design choices need critique before implementation starts: reusable TerminalStream boundaries, generic stream protocol shape, provider/session ownership, local PTY safety, optional tmux persistence, future SSH/Tailscale scope, and specialist-feed reuse.
SUCCESS: Overthinker either validates the current architecture or proposes sharper boundaries/sequence changes with clear tradeoffs and updated implementation guidance.
SCOPE: Beads forge-lqyc, forge-lqyc.1 through forge-lqyc.11; current Gitboard architecture only as needed for design fit. Focus on contracts and design, not code edits.
NON_GOALS: No implementation; no broad codebase audit; no decision to make tmux mandatory; no SSH implementation plan beyond future-provider boundaries.
CONSTRAINTS: READ_ONLY. Treat raw shell exposure as high-risk. Keep terminal rendering separate from process ownership. Prefer MVP that ships local/dev PTY safely while preserving future provider extensibility.
VALIDATION: Findings should answer: are child bead scopes sequenced correctly; is provider abstraction too broad/too narrow; are safety gates sufficient; should specialist-feed be in MVP or follow-up; what should change before executor dispatch.
OUTPUT: PASS/ADJUST recommendation, revised design notes, dependency/order changes if needed, and explicit next beads safe to start.
acceptance_criteria: Overthinker reviews the epic and critical child beads; validates or revises provider abstraction; validates or revises stream protocol boundaries; validates or revises shell safety model; evaluates whether specialist-feed belongs in MVP path; confirms tmux is optional; identifies any missing child beads/dependencies; returns actionable changes before implementation begins.
notes:
### Specialist Output ŌĆö overthinker (job 9696a7) [WAITING ŌĆö more output may follow]

## Summary

Recommendation: **ADJUST**.

Architecture direction good. Boundaries need sharper before executor dispatch. Biggest risk: provider/session layer can become remote shell API too early. MVP must ship local/dev PTY with strict safety gates, renderer/process split, future providers behind narrow contract.

## Status

Phase work complete. No files changed. GitNexus used before code evidence. `bd` claim/close not run: no shell tool available in harness.

## Phase 1 - Initial Analysis

Goal: validate terminal stream design before implementation.

Known repo fit:
- Existing realtime stack has `WsClient`, shared subscriptions, resume, sequence tracking, boot id.
- Server side has `ChannelRegistry` with per-channel sequence, ring buffer, replay gap detection.
- Specialist routes expose job/chains/in-flight state; no process ownership there.
- No terminal implementation found in visible evidence.

Core design should split into three layers:

1. **TerminalStream UI**
   - Renders bytes.
   - Sends input/resize intents.
   - Knows `sessionId`, `streamId`, protocol version.
   - Does not know shell, cwd, spawn, tmux, SSH, specialist ownership.

2. **Transport/protocol**
   - WebSocket channel or session-scoped stream.
   - Discriminated event union.
   - Supports output, input, resize, exit, error, snapshot, heartbeat, backpressure.

3. **Provider/session owner**
   - Owns PTY/tmux/future SSH lifecycle.
   - Applies safety policy.
   - Enforces cwd/session limits/kill behavior.
   - Emits stream events.

## Phase 2 - Devil's Advocate

Main failure modes:

- **Generic stream protocol too broad**
  - If protocol tries to cover terminal, logs, specialist feed, SSH, tmux all at once, MVP stalls.
  - Fix: generic envelope, terminal-specific payloads. No generic RPC.

- **Provider abstraction too early**
  - If provider interface includes SSH/Tailscale now, security model blurs.
  - Fix: `TerminalSessionProvider` narrow. Local provider only in MVP. Remote provider capability flags later.

- **Raw shell exposure high-risk**
  - Browser-to-shell path becomes critical attack surface.
  - Fix: opt-in dev gate, repo allowlist, command/session policy, audit, rate limits, timeouts.

- **Terminal rendering coupled to process ownership**
  - Reuse dies if component imports PTY/session APIs.
  - Fix: UI receives stream client props only.

- **tmux persistence can distort MVP**
  - Mandatory tmux adds install/runtime assumptions.
  - Fix: optional provider capability. PTY first. tmux follow-up.

- **Specialist-feed reuse may overfit**
  - Specialist output likely log/job stream, not interactive PTY.
  - Fix: reuse viewer shell/render frame later, not MVP process protocol.

## Phase 3 - Synthesis

ADJUST, not reject.

Use existing websocket patterns only for transport inspiration:
- Keep sequence/version/boot concepts.
- Do not force terminal onto existing `ChannelRegistry` replay semantics without byte-volume/backpressure review.
- Terminal output needs stronger flow control than system logs.

Revised MVP boundary:
- MVP = local provider + TerminalStream UI + terminal protocol + safety policy.
- Non-MVP = tmux persistence, SSH/Tailscale provider, specialist-feed integration.

Provider contract should be narrow:

```ts
type TerminalProviderCapability =
  | "interactive"
  | "resize"
  | "snapshot"
  | "persistence";

interface TerminalSessionProvider {
  kind: "local-pty" | "tmux" | "ssh";
  capabilities: TerminalProviderCapability[];

  create(request: CreateSessionRequest): Promise<TerminalSessionHandle>;
  attach(sessionId: string): AsyncIterable<TerminalStreamEvent>;
  input(sessionId: string, data: string): Promise<void>;
  resize(sessionId: string, cols: number, rows: number): Promise<void>;
  terminate(sessionId: string, reason: string): Promise<void>;
}
```

Safety gates required before shell launch:
- Explicit dev-mode config.
- Localhost/admin-only access.
- CWD constrained to assigned repo/worktree.
- Shell allowlist.
- Env scrub.
- Session count cap.
- Idle timeout and hard TTL.
- Input/output byte limits.
- Audit log for create/input/terminate.
- Orphan cleanup.
- No arbitrary command launch API in MVP unless allowlisted.

## Phase 4 - Final Refined Output

## Recommendation

**ADJUST**.

Current architecture can work if executor starts with contracts and safety, not terminal feature breadth.

## Revised Design Notes

- `TerminalStream` owns rendering only.
- `TerminalSessionProvider` owns PTY/tmux/SSH lifecycle.
- Protocol uses generic envelope + terminal event payloads.
- Local PTY provider only in MVP.
- tmux optional follow-up capability.
- SSH/Tailscale future provider only. No MVP route/code path.
- Specialist-feed reuse follow-up. Avoid blocking terminal MVP.

## Dependency / Order Changes

Recommended order:

1. **Protocol contract bead**
   - Event types, versioning, stream/session ids, error model, resize/input/output semantics.

2. **Safety policy bead**
   - Dev gate, cwd allowlist, shell allowlist, env scrub, caps, audit, cleanup.

3. **Renderer-only TerminalStream bead**
   - No provider/process imports.

4. **Local PTY provider bead**
   - Depends on protocol + safety.

5. **API/WebSocket integration bead**
   - Depends on provider and protocol.

6. **Optional tmux bead**
   - After PTY stable.

7. **Specialist-feed reuse bead**
   - After terminal renderer proves stable.

8. **SSH/Tailscale boundary bead**
   - Design-only later. No implementation now.

## Next Beads Safe To Start

Safe now:
- Bead scoped to terminal protocol/types.
- Bead scoped to safety policy/spec/tests.
- Bead scoped to renderer-only `TerminalStream`.

Not safe yet:
- Local PTY implementation before safety gates.
- tmux persistence before PTY contract stable.
- specialist-feed reuse before terminal MVP.
- SSH/Tailscale implementation.

## Verification

- GitNexus query run for terminal stream architecture.
- GitNexus context inspected for `useSpecialistOwnership`, `handleMessage`, `ChannelRegistry`, `createSpecialistsRouter`.
- GitNexus change detection run: no changed symbols; low risk.
- No file edits performed.

## Risks

- Exact child bead text missing from accessible evidence; sequencing mapped by scope, not verified per child description.
- Existing websocket ring buffer may be insufficient for terminal byte volume.
- Raw shell exposure remains highest risk; safety bead must precede PTY bead.

## Follow-ups

- Orchestrator should provide child bead descriptions if exact forge-lqyc.1 through forge-lqyc.11 reorder needed.
- Security-auditor review should run before PTY executor starts.
- Reviewer should verify protocol/safety contracts before integration.

## Final Answer

**ADJUST.** Ship local/dev PTY MVP only after protocol and safety contracts land. Keep `TerminalStream` rendering separate from provider/session ownership. Defer tmux, SSH/Tailscale, and specialist-feed reuse. Start protocol, safety, and renderer-only beads first; block PTY implementation until safety gates exist.

title: Add shell-provider safety gates
description:
PROBLEM: A browser-accessible raw shell is dangerous in remote/production deployments. Shell-capable providers need explicit safety gates and clear disabled behavior before any PTY provider can be implemented.
SUCCESS: Local PTY, tmux, and SSH-capable providers are disabled by default in production/remote contexts and require explicit config/env enablement. Disabled state is enforced server-side and reflected safely in UI.
SCOPE: Terminal provider config/env parsing; server-side provider enablement checks; frontend disabled/status messaging; docs/comments explaining risk boundary; tests. Include dev gate, localhost/admin-only intent, cwd allowlist, shell allowlist, env scrub, session caps, idle/hard TTL, byte/rate limits, create/terminate audit events, and orphan cleanup policy.
NON_GOALS: No auth system redesign; no SSH/tmux implementation; no arbitrary command-launch API; no logging of commands for audit.
CONSTRAINTS: Server refusal is authoritative; frontend messaging is convenience only. Never log terminal input, command lines, secrets, or output by default. Separate readonly specialist-feed permission from shell-capable provider permission.
VALIDATION: Tests prove production-disabled behavior, explicit enablement behavior, disabled websocket/session creation refusal, and safe frontend message.
OUTPUT: Config surface, safety docs/comments, tests run, residual deployment risks.
acceptance_criteria: Local PTY is disabled by default in production; tmux and SSH providers are disabled unless explicitly enabled; config/env flag enables shell-capable providers; readonly feeds can be allowed separately from shell sessions; disabled state renders a safe UI message; server refuses disabled provider creation; no command input or secrets are logged; safety model is documented in code/docs.

title: Wire local terminal end-to-end
description:
PROBLEM: The completed substrate must be wired so a user can open Gitboard's drawer, start or attach to a local PTY session, type commands, see output, resize, and recover across drawer close/reopen.
SUCCESS: Terminal tab uses TerminalStream plus websocket transport to create/attach to the local PTY provider and display clear connected/disconnected/error/exit states.
SCOPE: Frontend Terminal tab transport wiring; backend websocket/provider integration glue; session attach/reuse behavior; build fixes directly required for the end-to-end path.
NON_GOALS: No tmux; no SSH/Tailscale; no specialist-feed UI; no broad drawer redesign.
CONSTRAINTS: Respect safety gates. Close/open drawer must not immediately destroy the active session; hard browser/server disconnect should detach and schedule cleanup. Resize messages must flow after fit calculations.
VALIDATION: Manual or automated smoke: open Terminal, type a harmless command, observe output, resize drawer, close/reopen, confirm same session, exit, and build passes.
OUTPUT: Changed files, smoke/build evidence, remaining known limitations.
acceptance_criteria: Opening Terminal creates or attaches to local PTY session; typing in xterm sends input to PTY; PTY output appears in terminal; resizing drawer updates PTY size; close/reopen preserves active terminal session for configured grace period; disconnected/error/exit states are visible; build passes.

title: Add terminal WebSocket bridge
description:
PROBLEM: Browser terminal clients need a websocket bridge that maps generic terminal-stream messages to backend providers without hardcoding local PTY behavior into the frontend.
SUCCESS: Backend websocket/channel supports opening, attaching, detaching, input, output streaming, resize, exit, and error for terminal stream sessions.
SCOPE: Server websocket routing/channel; provider registry integration; session ID validation; disconnect cleanup; frontend transport client only if needed to exercise protocol.
NON_GOALS: No TerminalStream UI implementation; no provider-specific business logic beyond registry calls; no SSH/tmux implementation.
CONSTRAINTS: Reject invalid session IDs and disabled provider kinds safely. Detach on websocket disconnect and schedule disposal rather than leaking processes. Avoid logging user input. Surface provider errors as protocol error/status messages.
VALIDATION: Protocol tests cover open/attach/input/output/resize/exit/error/invalid session/disabled provider/disconnect cleanup using mocked providers.
OUTPUT: Websocket bridge files, protocol test evidence, cleanup behavior summary.
acceptance_criteria: Browser can open a terminal session; browser can attach to existing session; input messages write to provider; provider output streams back to frontend; resize messages resize provider session; exit/error messages are forwarded; disconnects detach or schedule cleanup; invalid session IDs are rejected safely; no orphaned processes after disconnect/timeout.

title: Implement local PTY provider
description:
PROBLEM: The drawer terminal needs a concrete server-side provider that owns a real local PTY process scoped to the current repo/workspace while exposing only the generic terminal-stream interface.
SUCCESS: Backend can create, resize, write to, emit output from, and dispose local PTY sessions, with lifecycle/idle tracking and tests using mocks where native PTY is hard to run in CI.
SCOPE: Backend terminal provider/session manager; repo-root cwd resolution; shell selection; lifecycle cleanup; unit tests/mocks. Prefer node-pty unless install/build constraints make that impractical.
NON_GOALS: No websocket protocol handling beyond provider interface; no tmux; no SSH; no production enablement bypass.
CONSTRAINTS: Spawn in repo/workspace root only. Default shell to user shell or bash. Never log command input or secrets. Provider must be disabled by safety config when not allowed. Clean up child processes on dispose, idle timeout, and server/session shutdown.
VALIDATION: Unit tests cover create, write, resize, output event, idle timeout, explicit dispose, and shell/cwd configuration using mocks where needed.
OUTPUT: Provider implementation, tests run, native dependency notes, residual platform risks.
acceptance_criteria: Uses node-pty or vetted equivalent; creates a terminal session with cwd set to repo/workspace root; spawns configured shell defaulting to user shell or bash; supports input writes; streams output chunks; supports resize rows/cols; tracks lifecycle and last activity; kills idle sessions after configured timeout; kills PTY on explicit close; lifecycle tests cover create/write/resize/dispose.

title: Define terminal stream protocol
description:
PROBLEM: Frontend and backend need a shared terminal stream contract before adding providers, otherwise drawer terminal, specialist feeds, tmux, and future SSH support will drift into incompatible one-off protocols.
SUCCESS: Shared protocol/types define a versioned terminal stream envelope, terminal-specific payloads, provider kinds, capabilities, lifecycle states, and websocket message shapes.
SCOPE: Shared TypeScript types/schema/validation utilities used by frontend and backend terminal code. Include protocol documentation close to the types.
NON_GOALS: No provider implementation; no websocket server wiring beyond types; no SSH/tmux implementation; no broad generic RPC framework.
CONSTRAINTS: Use a generic envelope plus terminal-specific payloads, not an over-broad stream RPC. Model provider kinds pty, tmux, ssh, command, specialist-feed. Capabilities must distinguish readonly vs interactive, resizable, snapshot, and persistent. Include stream/session ids, protocol version, error model, resize/input/output semantics, heartbeat or status as needed, and a backpressure/byte-volume note.
VALIDATION: Protocol validation tests cover open/attach/detach/input/output/resize/exit/error/status and reject invalid state transitions.
OUTPUT: Shared protocol files, documented message examples, validation evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Shared protocol types live in shared code; protocol supports provider capabilities readonly/interactive/resizable/persistent; message types include open, attach, detach, input, output, resize, exit, error, and status; invalid state transitions are rejected; frontend can render disabled/errored/exited states consistently; protocol is documented enough for future providers.

title: Create reusable TerminalStream xterm component
description:
PROBLEM: Gitboard needs a reusable terminal renderer that can display interactive shell output now and readonly/non-shell streams later without embedding provider-specific assumptions in UI code.
SUCCESS: A TerminalStream frontend component wraps xterm.js and exposes a provider-agnostic API for bytes in, optional input out, resize events, attach/detach lifecycle, status rendering, and styling.
SCOPE: Frontend terminal component and any minimal styles/tests needed for static rendering. Likely areas: drawer/component tree, shared UI components, test mocks for xterm and @xterm/addon-fit.
NON_GOALS: No websocket implementation; no PTY/tmux/SSH/session ownership logic; no Specialists tab redesign.
CONSTRAINTS: Component must support readonly mode for specialist feeds and read/write mode for shells. Keep API generic: callbacks/props should not mention PTY, tmux, SSH, or specialists. Cleanly dispose xterm and fit addon on unmount.
VALIDATION: Static render and resize smoke tests with xterm mocked; manual or test evidence that readonly mode blocks keyboard input while still rendering ANSI output.
OUTPUT: Component API, styling notes, tests run, and any residual integration risks.
acceptance_criteria: Uses xterm and @xterm/addon-fit; supports readonly and read/write modes; supports ANSI output and scrollback; fills parent container; refits on drawer resize, window resize, and tab activation; accepts provider-agnostic onInput/onResize/onAttach/onDetach callbacks; does not know about PTY, tmux, SSH, or specialists; matches Gitboard neutral dark UI; cleans up xterm/addons/listeners on unmount.

title: Observability tokens read wrong JSON path (token_usage nested)
description:
## PROBLEM
metrics-dao.ts:240 reads token fields directly from each trajectory entry (input_tokens, output_tokens, etc.) but the actual JSON structure nests them under .token_usage:
  {"t":..., "source":"turn_end", "token_usage":{"input_tokens":11895, "output_tokens":114, "cache_creation_tokens":0, "cache_read_tokens":0, "total_tokens":12009}}
  {"turn_index":1, "t":..., "token_usage":{...}}
Result: every token table renders 0 across all specialists.

## FIX
In parseTokenTrajectory (line 236-247), for each item extract the nested token_usage object first:
  const item_record = firstRecord(item);
  const usage = firstRecord(item_record.token_usage);
Then read input_tokens / output_tokens / cache_creation_tokens / cache_read_tokens / total_tokens from `usage`. Keep the fallback math when total_tokens is missing.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/metrics-dao.ts (parseTokenTrajectory only). ~3-line patch.

## CONSTRAINTS
- No gitnexus_* calls.

## VALIDATION
- bunx tsc clean.
- Manual orchestrator hits /api/console/observability/summary?range=all and verifies tokens.totals.total > 0.
notes:
Fixed token usage path in parseTokenTrajectory; now reads nested token_usage before summing fields.

title: Observability metrics-dao must skip dbs without specialist_job_metrics
description:
## PROBLEM
forge-che shipped a metrics-dao.ts that queries every attached repo's specialist_job_metrics table. Some attached dbs (e.g. repo_barchart_scraper_1) don't have that table ŌĆö the per-alias query throws SQLiteError 'no such table: <alias>.specialist_job_metrics' and 500s the whole /api/console/observability/summary endpoint.

Existing observability/dao.ts handles this safely because it queries specialist_jobs which all dbs have.

## SUCCESS
- /api/console/observability/summary returns 200 even when some attached dbs lack specialist_job_metrics.
- Aliases lacking the table are silently skipped (NOT logged at error level ŌĆö debug level OK).
- Aliases with the table are still queried normally; the response merges across the valid ones.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/metrics-dao.ts ONLY.

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: do NOT call gitnexus_*.
- For each alias in the attached list: try { db.prepare("SELECT 1 FROM " + alias + ".specialist_job_metrics LIMIT 0").run(); } catch { skip; }
- OR a single sqlite_master probe: SELECT name FROM <alias>.sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name IN ('specialist_job_metrics','specialist_jobs','specialist_events','specialist_results').
- Skip an alias if specialist_job_metrics is missing. If only events/results exist, still skip ŌĆö the v1 dao doesn't have a fallback path.

## VALIDATION
- bunx tsc clean
- /api/console/observability/summary?range=all returns 200 with non-empty roles/tokens fields (orchestrator will verify post-merge).

## OUTPUT
- 1-file diff (metrics-dao.ts)
notes:
Skipped attached dbs missing specialist_job_metrics via per-alias probe in metrics-dao.ts; kept valid aliases queried.

title: Firefox-safe drawer resize: simple onMouseDown + document listeners + diagnostic log
description:
## PROBLEM
Operator is on Firefox (lsof confirmed firefox connected to localhost:5173). All Playwright tests have run on headless Chromium ŌĆö which is why every fix passed in tests but failed in the operator's real browser. Firefox has quirks with setPointerCapture and onPointerDown that Chromium doesn't share.

## SUCCESS
- Drag works in Firefox.
- Maximize works in Firefox.

title: Defensive fix: drawer resize fails for some real mice (StrictMode + preventDefault order + touch-action)
description:
## PROBLEM
After forge-wtkk switched to onPointerDown + setPointerCapture, real-pointer Playwright drag passes BUT the operator's real browser still does nothing on drag or maximize click.

Observed browser console error from a synthetic-PointerEvent probe:
  NotFoundError: Failed to execute 'setPointerCapture' on 'Element': No active pointer with the given id is found.
  at startResize (...:83:10)

Three compounding root causes:
1. apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/main.tsx wraps the app in `<StrictMode>`. In dev, JSX handlers are bound twice per render; under some pointer-capture race conditions setPointerCapture is called on a pointer that has already been released, throwing and aborting the rest of startResize so no pointermove listeners are attached.
2. BottomDrawer.tsx calls `event.preventDefault()` BEFORE `target.setPointerCapture(event.pointerId)`. preventDefault on pointerdown in some browser builds invalidates the implicit pointer activation, then setPointerCapture throws NotFoundError.
3. No `touch-action: none` on `.bottom-drawer-resizer`. Trackpad / touchscreen / Logitech Options gesture layers can convert the pointer drag into a scroll/pan, cancelling the pointer before pointermove fires.

The maximize button never fires for the operator because the same React StrictMode double-mount may also be giving them a stale toggleMaximize closure ŌĆö OR (more likely) they are clicking the X icon-btn (which is right next to maximize) accidentally. Verify by adding distinct titles / icons / spacing.

## SUCCESS
1. Drag resize works on a real mouse drag in the operator's browser (operator confirms by feedback after deploy + hard reload).
2. Maximize button reliably toggles even after StrictMode double-mount.
3. Console shows NO `NotFoundError` from setPointerCapture during real or synthetic drag.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawer.tsx
  - Reorder startResize: call `target.setPointerCapture(event.pointerId)` BEFORE `event.preventDefault()`.
  - Wrap the setPointerCapture call in try/catch. If it throws, log a one-line console.warn and CONTINUE ŌĆö fall back to document-level pointermove/pointerup listeners (universal, less precise but works).
  - When setPointerCapture succeeds, keep the element-scoped listeners as today. When it fails, attach the pointermove/pointerup/pointercancel listeners to `document` instead so we still capture moves outside the 8px handle.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css
  - Add `touch-action: none` to `.bottom-drawer-resizer`.
  - Optional: also `user-select: none` on `.bottom-drawer` while drag is in progress ŌĆö set/clear via a data attribute or a runtime class toggle if simple. (If complicated, skip ŌĆö `touch-action: none` alone should fix the operator.)

## NON_GOALS
- No new features. No store changes. No test churn beyond what's needed.
- Do not remove StrictMode ŌĆö it surfaces real bugs in other code.

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: do NOT call gitnexus_*.
- Keep onPointerDown / pointer-event approach. Just make it robust.
- Console.warn must be one line, not a stack trace.

## VALIDATION
- bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit clean
- Real Playwright pointer-drag passes (page.mouse.down/move/up or browser_drag).
- Real Playwright maximize-click toggles height twice and aria-label flips.
- No NotFoundError in browser console messages.

## OUTPUT
- 2-file diff (BottomDrawer.tsx + globals.css)
- Confirmation: setPointerCapture wrapped in try/catch with document-fallback
- Confirmation: touch-action: none on the resizer
notes:
Added pointer-capture try/catch fallback in BottomDrawer resize; touch-action:none on resizer; tsc command blocked by repo-wide missing react/zustand/JSX deps, unrelated to this diff.

BottomDrawer resize hardened: capture first, preventDefault after, doc fallback on `NotFoundError`; resizer gets `touch-action: none`.

## Status

## Changes

  - `setPointerCapture()` now before `preventDefault()`

title: Debug: drawer drag + maximize fail for real mouse despite passing playwright synthetic tests
description:
## PROBLEM
Operator reports the bottom drawer's drag-to-resize and maximize button do nothing when used with a real mouse on their browser. Three sessions of fixes (forge-75qx, forge-rl31, forge-todz) all left Playwright synthetic-event tests passing while the real-mouse behavior remains broken. Something between "JS dispatch works" and "real browser pointer events work" is being missed.

## EVIDENCE
- Current main: commit b653336.
- BottomDrawer.tsx:9 ŌåÆ MAXIMIZED_OFFSET=24; shell.ts setDrawerHeight clamp is (window.innerHeight - 24). No local MAX_HEIGHT in BottomDrawer anymore.
- BottomDrawer.tsx:31 ŌåÆ `<div className="bottom-drawer-resizer" ... onMouseDown={(event) => startResize(event, setDrawerHeight)} />`
- startResize: event.preventDefault() then window.addEventListener('mousemove'/'mouseup', onMove/onUp). onMove calls setDrawerHeight(window.innerHeight - moveEvent.clientY).
- Playwright synthetic-dispatch test PASSES (mousedown on resizer + window mousemove ŌåÆ height changes 600ŌåÆ707).
- Playwright real-pointer dragTo test was NEVER run ŌĆö we only verified via `dispatchEvent` which bypasses React's actual event system.
- The maximize button click was last verified via Playwright `.click()` AFTER manual store-state was already in a moved range ŌĆö verdict 707ŌåÆ783 was observed in that synthetic check. The user reports clicking it does nothing in their real browser.

## SUCCESS
- Reproduce the failure with a REAL pointer interaction (use Playwright's `browser_drag` or pointer.move/down/up API, NOT dispatchEvent of JS MouseEvent).
- Identify exactly why real pointer events don't drive setDrawerHeight while synthetic events do.
- Land a minimal fix (probably switching from onMouseDown/window-mousemove to native PointerEvents with pointer capture, OR adding the `style.userSelect = 'none' / pointerEvents` toggles during drag, OR investigating a stale-closure bug in startResize).
- Also confirm the maximize button click works in a real Playwright run (button click is a basic click ŌĆö if it doesn't fire, focus or pointer-events on the parent is wrong).
- After fix: orchestrator will retest live and operator must confirm.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawer.tsx (resize handler, possibly switch to onPointerDown + pointer capture)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawerTabBar.tsx (maximize button wiring ŌĆö confirm onClick prop chain reaches toggleMaximize)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css (look for any z-index/pointer-events/user-select interactions affecting .bottom-drawer-resizer or .bottom-drawer-icon-btn)
- DO NOT touch store.ts beyond reading.

## NON_GOALS
- No new features. No styling changes beyond what the fix requires.
- Do not retest with `dispatchEvent` for verification ŌĆö that's the trap that hid the bug before. Use Playwright real pointer / browser_drag tool, OR run the dev server and have the orchestrator manually drag.

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: do NOT call gitnexus_* (MCP stall pattern).
- Inspect the actual served HTML/JS in browser via Playwright if possible. The dev server is running at http://localhost:5173/gitboard/. If Vite HMR is showing stale code (cached old build), do a hard reload via `window.location.reload()` after `localStorage.clear()` and confirm the resizer JS matches main.
- Reproduction MUST use real pointer events (page.mouse.move/down/up via Playwright). Synthetic dispatchEvent is forbidden as proof.
- Investigate React 18+ strict-mode double-effect issue if applicable.

## HYPOTHESES to test in order
1. Vite is serving STALE code ŌĆö check the bundled JS in browser for the string `MAXIMIZED_OFFSET = 24` and `setDrawerHeight(window.innerHeight - moveEvent.clientY)` directly. If stale, restart Vite or hard-reload.
2. React onMouseDown synthetic event swallows the native event in such a way that window.addEventListener('mousemove') registered inside the handler fires too late / not at all. Fix: switch to onPointerDown with element.setPointerCapture + onPointerMove/Up on the same element.
3. Drawer body content has user-select or some CSS that swallows the mouse drag. Check `*::selection`, `user-select`, `-webkit-user-drag` on body/main.
4. Maximize button: verify the click handler actually calls toggleMaximize. The button might be receiving the click but the React closure captures stale `height`/`restoredHeight` from a previous render.

## VALIDATION
- Real Playwright pointer-drag from the resizer's center DOWN 100px ŌåÆ drawer.style.height shrinks.
- Real Playwright pointer-drag UP 200px ŌåÆ drawer.style.height grows toward viewport-24.
- Real Playwright click on the maximize button ŌåÆ drawer.style.height = viewport - 24 (within 2px); click again ŌåÆ drawer restores to pre-max height.
- `bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit` clean.

## OUTPUT
- Root cause statement (which hypothesis was correct, with evidence)
- Diff with one-line per change
- Real-pointer Playwright test output proving fix
notes:
Switched BottomDrawer resize from mouse events on window to pointer events with pointer capture on resizer. Could not run real Playwright pointer repro in this worktree: playwright package missing. tsc run fails due existing dependency/type environment issues unrelated to this file.

Bug hunt done. Root cause likely event model gap: synthetic `dispatchEvent` hit window mouse listeners, real pointer path unreliable without pointer capture. Resize handler used `onMouseDown` + `window.mousemove/mouseup` only.

## Status

## Changes
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawer.tsx`

title: Fix realtime stack production bugs (Codex review on PR #10)
description:
PROBLEM
Codex left 4 substantive review findings on PR #10 (merged 2026-05-18) ŌĆö three P1 + one P2 ŌĆö pointing at production-correctness bugs in the realtime stack shipped via forge-igg + forge-f0g + forge-dgd. Tests passed locally because none exercise these specific runtime paths. All four are real and high-impact.

REFERENCES
- PR #10 https://github.com/Jaggerxtrm/gitboard/pull/10
- Codex review id 4313755986; 4 inline comments
- Existing memories: realtime-envelope-uses-boot-id, realtime-envelope-version-is-string

FOUR FIXES ŌĆö ALL REQUIRED FOR CLOSE

FIX 1 (P1, useBeadsLive envelope unwrapping)
File: apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useBeadsLive.ts:~44
Bug: Handler does `if (msg.projectId !== selectedProjectId) return;` BUT the WS envelope shape is `{ type, channel, event, seq, ts, version, boot_id, data }` ŌĆö projectId lives inside msg.data, not at top level. msg.projectId is always undefined ŌåÆ `undefined !== selectedProjectId` is always true ŌåÆ handler returns early for every event ŌåÆ live Beads sync IS SILENTLY DEAD in production.
Fix: change the discriminator and downstream type narrowing. Either:
  (a) const payload = (msg.data as { projectId?: string }) ?? {}; if (payload.projectId !== selectedProjectId) return;
  (b) Add proper RealtimeEnvelope typing import from apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts and narrow with msg.event tag.
  Preferred: (b) ŌĆö surfaces the same drift if any future event union changes.
Validation: add a test that subscribes, publishes a beads:issue.upsert envelope through ChannelRegistry, and asserts useBeadsStore.getState().issues contains the upserted issue.

FIX 2 (P1, github-poller.ts pollIssues pagination)
File: apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts around line 376
Bug: Single `per_page=100` request, no pagination follow. Initial backfill (watermark = 1970-01-01) drops all issues past page 1. Heavy poll cycles with >100 updated items truncate silently.
Fix: restore page loop. While response.length === 100, fetch page+1 (preserve since=watermark + per_page=100). Stop when shorter than 100 OR rate-limit budget enters backoff (<500 remaining).
Validation: test with a mocked apiGet that returns two pages (100 items page 1, 5 items page 2); assert both pages ingested.

FIX 3 (P1, github-poller.ts pollPullRequests pagination)
File: apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts around line 421
Bug: Same as Fix 2 but for PRs. Single page; no follow. Sort=updated desc + watermark mitigates steady-state but breaks initial backfill.
Fix: same page-loop pattern as Fix 2; same stop conditions.
Validation: parallel test for PRs.

FIX 4 (P2, useGithubActivity sync_hint reload path)
File: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts around line 84
Bug: `if (msg.event === 'github:sync_hint' && msg.data && typeof msg.data === 'object' && 'repos' in msg.data)` ŌĆö server emits sync_hint with `{ reason, channel, since_seq }` per the buffer-miss replay path (forge-dgd), NOT with a repos field. So the reload branch never runs ŌåÆ after a replay gap or boot_id mismatch the client silently stops getting updates.
Fix: drop the 'repos' key check. Trigger refetch on any github:sync_hint envelope; optionally inspect msg.data.scope (if present) to scope the refetch.
Validation: test that emits a github:sync_hint envelope with {reason: 'buffer_miss', since_seq: 12, boot_id: 'x'} and asserts the refetch path fires.

SCOPE
- MODIFY: apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useBeadsLive.ts (Fix 1)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts (Fix 2 + Fix 3)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts (Fix 4)
- NEW or MODIFY tests covering each fix path: apps/beadboard/tests/dashboard/hooks/useBeadsLive.test.ts (new), apps/gitboard/tests/core/github-poller-loop.test.ts (extend), apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.test.ts (new or extend)

NON_GOALS
- No envelope-schema redesign ŌĆö the schema is correct; consumers drifted.
- No new pagination strategy beyond the proven page-loop pattern that existed pre-forge-f0g.
- No re-architecture of sync_hint semantics.
- Do not touch forge-3mdw watcher fast-path code.

CONSTRAINTS
- TypeScript strict.
- Import RealtimeEnvelope + per-event payload aliases from apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts instead of writing local discriminated unions in the hook (Fix 1).
- Pagination loop MUST respect the rate-limit backoff already implemented in apiGet (do not bypass).
- Each test MUST exercise the envelope-shape path, not a flattened payload.

VALIDATION
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test ŌåÆ 219+ pass with new test cases.
- bun run --filter @xtrm/beadboard test -- --run tests/dashboard/hooks/useBeadsLive.test.ts ŌåÆ green.
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard build ŌåÆ clean.
- Live smoke (manual, on running server):
  1. Open dashboard with bd active project selected; run `bd update <id> --notes 'live test'`; the change should appear in the UI within ~3s without page refresh.
  2. Pick a repo with >100 issues; on cold start, verify all issues are ingested across multiple pages (check github_events table count vs gh CLI count).
  3. Force a WS reconnect; server emits sync_hint with buffer_miss; client refetches.

OUTPUT
- File diff list (3 source + 2-3 test files).
- Test counts (expect ~225+ gitboard).

title: forge-7xu hotfix: per-repo github fetch + feed shows all issues
description:
PROBLEM: Operator reports (1) Beads > Feed only shows open issues, should show all from Dolt (open + closed); (2) GitHub Activity/PRs/Issues all show 0 for most selected repos because the global API limit (50 events / 100 prs) returns only most-recent across ALL repos.

SUCCESS:
- Beads Feed tab includes closed issues alongside open
- GitHub tabs fetch per-repo data on (repo, tab) change instead of relying on globally-paginated store
- Tabs render real per-repo content for any selected repo

SCOPE: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/MainPane.tsx, components/beads/BeadsRepoView.tsx

NON_GOALS: No CSS changes. No backend changes.

CONSTRAINTS: Use existing apiClient.getEvents({repos:[..]}), getPrs({repo}), getIssues({repo}), getReleases({repo}). Don't regress beads tabs.

VALIDATION: Playwright ŌĆö select gitboard repo on GitHub side, Activity tab shows events; Beads > Feed shows open + closed mixed.
notes:

- BeadsRepoView: switched to single listIssues({limit:1000}) call (backend /closed endpoint 404s on Dolt-backed projects). Split client-side by status. Verified Feed for /gitboard now shows 182 rows (172 closed + 9 open + 1 in-progress + 11 epics).
- GithubTabView: replaced global useGithubStore filtering with per-repo useGithubRepoData hook that fetches apiClient.getEvents({repos:[fullName],limit:200}) + getPrs({repo,limit:200}) + getIssues({repo,limit:200}) + getReleases({repo,limit:50}) on (repo, tab) change. Verified Activity for Jaggerxtrm/gitboard now shows 74 events.

### Specialist Output ŌĆö explorer (job 6f3c75) [WAITING ŌĆö more output may follow]

Writing the full inventory report.

## Summary

Legacy beadboard has 6 primary tabs/surfaces with 18 components, 5 stores/data sources, and 3 modal/overlay surfaces. forge-5w9 ported only KanbanBoard + IssueOverlay ŌĆö missing ProjectRail, IssueFeed (inline), TriagePanel, ClosedIssuesPanel, MemoriesPanel, and all header controls.

## Architecture Overview

**Top-level layout**: `App.tsx` controls tab routing, stores project selection state, orchestrates data loading. Three vertical regions: header (tabs + search + filters), ProjectRail sidebar, main content area.

**Component hierarchy**:
```
App.tsx
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ Header (tabs: issues/board/triage/closed/memories)
Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ ModuleSegments (5 tabs with counts)
Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ ModuleActions (search + filter toggle)
Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ ModuleQuickFilters (all/ready/blocked/stale)
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ ProjectRail (sidebar)
Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ ProjectRow (per-project stats + health)
ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ Main content (tab-switched)
    Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ IssueFeed (tab: issues)
    Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ IssueRow + IssueDossier (inline expansion)
    Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ KanbanBoard (tab: board)
    Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ StatusColumn (4 lanes)
    Ōöé   Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ BeadCard
    Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ IssueOverlay (portal)
    Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ TriagePanel (tab: triage)
    Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ IssueRow + TriageStatCard
    Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ ClosedIssuesPanel (tab: closed)
    ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ MemoriesPanel (tab: memories)
```

**Data flow**: Zustand store (`stores/beads.ts`) ŌåÆ API client (`lib/api.ts`) ŌåÆ REST endpoints (`/api/beads/projects/:id/*`).

notes:
### Specialist Output ŌĆö explorer (job e5b0df) [WAITING ŌĆö more output may follow]

Have all component files analyzed. Now compiling the legacy map.

## Summary

Legacy gitboard frontend (pre-forge-5w9) has **1 top-level tabbed shell** (`GithubPanel`) with **7 sub-tabs**, **1 resizable repo sidebar**, **4 timeline views**, **3 repo content views**, and **1 dossier modal**.

## Architecture overview

**Top-level surface**: `DashboardShell` in App.tsx wraps `GithubPanel` + `StatsHeader` + `RepoSidebar` in 3-column layout.

**Data flow**: Zustand store (`useGithubStore`) holds events/PRs/issues/releases/repos/summary. `useGithubActivity` hook fetches via `apiClient` on mount and subscribes to WebSocket for real-time updates.

**Virtualization**: All timeline views use `@tanstack/react-virtual` for performance.

**Key components**:
- `GithubPanel` ŌĆö orchestrates sidebar + tabs + content
- `RepoSidebar` ŌĆö filterable repo list with inline EventDetail
- `EventDetail` ŌĆö collapsible commit accordion used in sidebar
- `ActivityTimeline` ŌĆö virtualized event list with day headers + keyboard nav
- `PrTimeline` ŌĆö PR rows with expand ŌåÆ conversation + files + linked beads
- `IssueTimeline` ŌĆö issue rows with expand ŌåÆ body + labels
- `ReleaseTimeline` ŌĆö release rows with expand ŌåÆ markdown body
- `RepoContentPanels` ŌĆö README/CHANGELOG/Reports async render
- `RepoDossier` ŌĆö modal overlay (portal to body) with repo dossier tabs

## Execution flows

App.tsx ŌåÆ DashboardShell ŌåÆ GithubPanel ŌåÆ { RepoSidebar + StatsHeader + TabBar + {ActivityTimeline|PrTimeline|IssueTimeline|ReleaseTimeline|ReadmeView|ChangelogView|ReportsView} }
