title: Epic: unify gitboard+beadboard into VS Code file-explorer shell
description:
PROBLEM: gitboard and beadboard are separate apps with separate sidebars, separate tabs, divergent styles. Operator wants to merge into one IDE-style shell where the left sidebar is a VS Code file-explorer-like tree of repos, each repo expandable to show /github and /beads children, click a child swaps the right pane.

VISION:
- Left sidebar = file-tree of repos. Each repo row: `/repo-name`            ŌŚÅlast-activity   <open-bead-count>
- Row uses tree branch glyph (|_ or Ōö£ŌöĆ) for visual hierarchy under expanded repos.
- Click row ŌåÆ expand children indented:
    Ōö£ŌöĆ /github   ŌŖĢPR  ŌŖĢCO  ŌŖĢIS  ŌŖĢRE
    ŌööŌöĆ /beads    ŌŖĢOP  ŌŖĢIP  ŌŖĢBL  ŌŖĢEP
  Both children have IDENTICAL chip count (4) for visual rhythm.
- Click /github ŌåÆ right pane shows that repo's gitboard (activity, PRs, issues, releases tabs collapse into one view).
- Click /beads ŌåÆ right pane shows that repo's beadboard (kanban + issue overlay).
- Sidebar must collapse/expand via a button (chevron at top of sidebar).
- Open in left collapsed state shows only icons. Selected child gets a left-edge accent rail (4px) + elevated bg.
- Aesthetic: minimal IDE ŌĆö monochrome palette in tree, accent only for active row; keep current octicons; one font family.

INSPIRATION: https://events.iterate.com/streams/ ŌĆö path-style row labels, indented children with + glyph, count badges, monochrome tree.

ARCHITECTURE: unify into apps/gitboard (option (a) from design discussion). Port apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/* into apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/. Add beads API client + Zustand store inside gitboard. Build new file-tree sidebar. Route-based right-pane swap. Deprecate apps/beadboard once parity verified.

SUCCESS:
- One Vite dev server (5173) serves the unified shell.
- Sidebar shows N repos as file-tree rows; expand/collapse works; chevron toggles overall sidebar collapse.
- Clicking /github or /beads swaps right pane; no full page reload.
- Beadboard kanban + issue overlay render inside gitboard's shell with identical token system.
- Recent activity octicons or chip row sits as second line below /repo name on the parent row (configurable; consider single-line variant too).
- apps/beadboard becomes a redirect or is deleted entirely.

SCOPE: apps/gitboard/* (new shell, new sidebar, ported beads components, routing); apps/beadboard/* (deprecate); packages/ui/src/styles/tokens.css (shared tokens ŌĆö likely no new tokens needed); apps/gitboard/src/api/* (extend to proxy or aggregate beads data if needed).

NON_GOALS:
- No backend rewrite of beads data layer; beadboard's API on port 3010 stays; gitboard frontend talks to both.
- No new color palette; reuse existing tokens.
- No tab system at top ŌĆö sidebar IS the navigation.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Preserve all current gitboard data fetching (github poller, releases, etc.) and beadboard data fetching.
- Preserve all current functionality on each child pane ŌĆö kanban DnD, PR expansion, issue overlay, RepoDossier modal, etc.
- Same token system across both panes ŌĆö radius, type scale, card pattern from forge-0zn epic.
- All octicons preserved; no emoji.

VALIDATION:
- Visual: full Playwright walkthrough ŌĆö sidebar tree renders, expand/collapse, chevron toggle, right pane swap, kanban works, PR view works.
- Functional: no regressions in current data flow.
- Style: tree-row inspector shows monochrome palette except accent on active row; right pane consistent with sidebar tokens.

OUTPUT: unified app on 5173 with sidebar tree + dual right panes; apps/beadboard either redirect or deleted; updated CLAUDE.md mentioning unified shell.

CHILD ISSUE SHAPE (planner should split into):
- E1: design tokens audit + new sidebar tree tokens (if needed)
- E2: port beads components and types into gitboard
- E3: add beads API client + Zustand store in gitboard
- E4: build new file-tree Sidebar component (rows, expand/collapse, chevron, indentation glyphs, chip rows)
- E5: wire routing (repo + section ŌåÆ right pane swap)
- E6: integrate beadboard kanban + issue overlay into gitboard right pane
- E7: integrate gitboard activity/PRs/issues/releases into the /github right pane
- E8: collapse sidebar button + collapsed-state styling
- E9: deprecate apps/beadboard (redirect or delete)
- E10: README/CLAUDE.md updates
notes:
Epic complete (2026-05-16). All 10 children closed.

Implementation summary:
- forge-5w9.1: shell.ts types + routing decision (Zustand store-driven, no router lib)
- forge-5w9.2: beads-api client (same-origin /api/beads ŌĆö gitboard already mounts beadboard routes)
- forge-5w9.3: useShellStore + useRepoTree aggregator (79 nodes verified: 69 github + 28 beads, 18 matched)
- forge-5w9.4: file-tree Sidebar with ARIA tree, keyboard nav, 22px compact rows, octicon chips zero-hidden
- forge-5w9.5: collapse toggle + mobile drawer pattern + localStorage persistence
- forge-5w9.6: Sidebar+MainPane swap at default /gitboard route; legacy shell at /gitboard/legacy
- forge-5w9.7: BeadsRepoView wrapping ported kanban (21 cards verified, cardŌåÆoverlayŌåÆEsc flow)
- forge-5w9.8: GithubRepoView with 4 stacked sections + sticky headers (Activity 11 / PRs 5 / Issues 0 / Releases 0 for /gitboard)
- forge-5w9.9: /beadboard ŌåÆ /gitboard redirect at server.ts (backend cross-import preserved)
- forge-5w9.10: CLAUDE.md IDE shell section

Review:
- Reviewer (forge-lec): PARTIAL 89/100. Only finding: getOpenPrs intentionally not ported ŌĆö waiver memory saved.
- Security-auditor (forge-bvq): LOW finding (1) ŌĆö encodeURIComponent missing on path segments. PATCHED same session.

Deferred follow-ups:
- Per-(repo,section) scroll/state preservation in MainPane (V1 uses key-remount).
- Hash router for deep links (#/r/owner/repo/github) ŌĆö ~10 LOC if needed.
- Delete apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/* source entirely + drop dev:dashboard scripts.
- Move beadboard's api/core into gitboard to fully remove beadboard package.
close_reason: All 10 children closed. New IDE shell live at default /gitboard. Reviewer PARTIAL (waived) + security-auditor LOW (patched).

title: Impl F: switch to per-entry cards, drop outer expanded-body frame
description: PROBLEM: Bead D+E added an outer card frame wrapping the whole expanded view; GitHub actually uses per-entry cards (each commit, comment, and the PR description are individual rounded cards on a flat bg, no outer wrapping frame). SUCCESS: revert outer card on .pr-expanded-body and .issue-expanded-body; revert row+body unification on .pr-row.is-expanded and .issue-row.is-expanded; ensure each .pr-conversation-entry renders as a full discrete card (border, radius, surface-secondary bg, padding); ensure the PR body description block (the 'Jaggerxtrm opened this pull request' section with Summary) is wrapped as its own card. SCOPE: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css. NON_GOALS: no token changes; no TSX. CONSTRAINTS: keep collapsed row card from Bead C; remove only the outer frame and unification; build up per-entry. VALIDATION: visual ŌĆö expanded view should look like GitHub: clean flat bg with individual rounded cards for each thread entry. OUTPUT: CSS diff.
close_reason: Switched expanded views to GitHub per-entry card pattern. Removed outer card on .pr-expanded-body and .issue-expanded-body, reverted row+body unification on .pr-row.is-expanded and .issue-row.is-expanded. Bulked up .pr-conversation-entry with --surface-secondary bg, 10/14 base padding, 14/16 for body/comment/review entries, --card-radius-md for those, 8px inter-entry gap. Visual verified ŌĆö each entry is a discrete card on flat page bg.

title: Detail panel centering: strip asymmetric padding-left so gb-detail-stack centers symmetrically
description: padding-left:90 on .pr-expanded-body / .gb-tab-pane offsets the gb-detail-stack right of true center. Make padding symmetric so the stack centers within the full panel width.
close_reason: Strip asymmetric padding-left from .pr-expanded-body / .issue-expanded-body / .release-expanded-body / .gb-tab-pane. Stack now centers symmetrically (left=right=138px verified).

title: Release row grid mis-aligned + duplicate tag/title cells
description: release-row-main has 7 spans (icon, tag, title, repo, actor, time, chevron) but grid-template-columns has only 5. tag and title hold the same text because poller doesn't split release.tag_name from release.name. Fix: drop redundant .release-tag, expand grid to match.
close_reason: Drop redundant release-tag span; expand grid to 6 columns (icon/title/repo/actor/time/chev)

title: Gitboard consistency pass: unified width + markdown rendering + Reports YAML frontmatter
description:
PROBLEM
1. Issues tab markdown not using renderPrBodyText; renders inconsistently vs PR/Release tabs.
2. Detail panes across gitboard tabs (PRs, Issues, Releases, README, CHANGELOG, Reports) have inconsistent widths. Beadboard uses clamp(560px, 75%, 1400px) centered ŌĆö apply same pattern here.
3. README/CHANGELOG/Reports lack the PR-detail readable typography (lighter color, line-height, spacing).
4. Reports tab shows plain file list. Each report row should parse YAML frontmatter (--- session_date / branch / commits / issues_closed / specialist_dispatches ---) and show as compact meta in the collapsed row.

SCOPE: gitboard dashboard only.
close_reason: Consistent: gb-detail-stack class clamps PR/Issue/Release/README/CHANGELOG/Reports to 75% center. IssueExpandedBody now uses .pr-body-text + .pr-rich-text. Reports endpoint parses YAML frontmatter; rows show date/branch/commits/closed/filed/dispatch meta.

title: Repo content as tabs (not modal) + fenced code + table rendering
description: Drop RepoDossier modal. Move README/CHANGELOG/Reports into TabBar as sibling tabs to Activity/PRs/Issues/Releases. Extend renderPrBodyText to render fenced code blocks (```lang ... ```) including mermaid (as preformatted block) and markdown tables (| col | col |). Closes user feedback on forge-xnk.
close_reason: Tabs (README/CHANGELOG/Reports) added to gitboard TabBar; renderPrBodyText now handles fenced code + tables. RepoDossier kept for beadboard kanban reuse.

title: Releases tab in gitboard (integrate alongside Activity/PRs/Issues)
description:
PROBLEM
gitboard's TabBar in apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/GithubPanel.tsx has Activity/PRs/Issues but no Releases tab. GithubPoller already inserts ReleaseEvent rows into the events table. Need a Releases tab that visually integrates with the existing PrTimeline / IssueTimeline / ActivityTimeline style (same density, tokens, octicons, virtualizer pattern, dossier pattern).

REFERENCES (read these ŌĆö do NOT recreate their primitives)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/GithubPanel.tsx (TabBar + Tab type + activeTab switch ŌĆö extend)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/PrTimeline.tsx (canonical row + dossier shape; exports renderPrBodyText ŌĆö IMPORT it, do NOT reimplement markdown rendering)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/IssueTimeline.tsx (sibling pattern for read-only timelines)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/ActivityTimeline.tsx (event-based timeline pattern)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/timeline-utils.ts (buildDateGroupedItems, formatRelativeTime, truncateBody, parseLabels ŌĆö IMPORT, do NOT fork)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css (.pr-*, .issue-*, .activity-* row classes ŌĆö REUSE these prefixes naming for .release-*; same tokens, same sizes, same density)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/github.ts (GithubState + useGithubStore ŌĆö EXTEND this store, do NOT add a parallel one)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/client.ts (ApiClient class ŌĆö ADD getReleases method following the getPrs/getIssues signature exactly)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts (existing /api/github/* endpoints ŌĆö ADD /releases handler in this same file, do NOT create new router file)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/github-store.ts (getEvents/getRepos helpers ŌĆö ADD getReleases(db, filters) here, do NOT create a new store module)
- apps/gitboard/src/types/github.ts (GithubEvent shape with type/repo/actor/title/body/url ŌĆö ADD GithubRelease interface here, derive from ReleaseEvent payload)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts (verify ReleaseEvent payload shape ŌĆö what fields are populated for type='ReleaseEvent' rows; tag/name/body live in payload)

SUCCESS
- New "Releases" tab appears in GithubPanel TabBar, sibling to Activity/PRs/Issues. Same styling (font, accent underline on active). Tab count shows current release count for the active filter.
- Clicking Releases renders <ReleaseTimeline releases={releases} /> in the same content area where PrTimeline/IssueTimeline mount.
- ReleaseTimeline.tsx:
  - Uses @tanstack/react-virtual virtualizer (mirror PrTimeline structure)
  - Uses buildDateGroupedItems + DayHeader from timeline-utils (mirror PrTimeline rendering of day groups)
  - Each row shows: TagIcon (left, --accent-blue/green tone), tag/name, repo, actor, relative time
  - Expand-in-place dossier: shows release body via renderPrBodyText (IMPORTED from PrTimeline), plus "Open on GitHub" external link
  - Empty state mirrors PrTimeline empty state (same dim text, same border)
- ReleaseTimeline test: smoke for empty + 1 row + expand renders body via the same renderPrBodyText path (mock via existing test patterns; see PrTimeline.test.tsx if it exists, else IssueTimeline.test.tsx)
- /api/github/releases endpoint: GET ?repo=...&limit=...&offset=... returns { releases: GithubRelease[] }. Default limit 50, ordered created_at DESC. 400 if no repo on bad params (mirror /api/github/prs error handling).
- github-store.ts: new getReleases(db, filters) selects events where type='ReleaseEvent', parses payload JSON to a GithubRelease shape.
- stores/github.ts: extends GithubState with `releases: GithubRelease[]`, `setReleases(...)`, fetched the same way activity/prs/issues are fetched (see existing fetch effects in App.tsx or whatever mounts the store).
- client.ts: new getReleases(params) following getPrs signature pattern.
- types/github.ts: new GithubRelease interface (id, tag_name, name, body, html_url, author_login, published_at, repo_full_name).

SCOPE
- NEW: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/ReleaseTimeline.tsx
- NEW: apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/components/github/ReleaseTimeline.test.tsx
- NEW: apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/github-releases.test.ts
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/GithubPanel.tsx (Tab type union + tabs[] entry + activeTab switch + count prop)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css (add .release-* rules MIRRORING .pr-* shape; reuse same tokens; no new palette)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts (add /releases handler in the existing createGithubRouter)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/core/github-store.ts (add getReleases fn)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/github.ts (add releases slice)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/client.ts (add getReleases method)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/types/github.ts (add GithubRelease interface)
- MODIFY: wherever the store fetches PRs/Issues on mount/repo-change ŌĆö add a parallel fetchReleases call (orchestrator: check App.tsx or the panel for the existing fetch hooks; mirror their shape)

IMPERATIVE REUSE RULES (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
- IMPORT renderPrBodyText from PrTimeline.tsx; do NOT write a new markdown renderer.
- IMPORT buildDateGroupedItems, formatRelativeTime, truncateBody from timeline-utils.ts; do NOT inline copies.
- IMPORT @primer/octicons-react TagIcon; do NOT add other icon families.
- REUSE existing CSS tokens (var(--accent-*), var(--text-*), var(--border-*)); add NO new variables or palettes.
- EXTEND useGithubStore; do NOT create a parallel zustand store.
- ADD endpoint to existing createGithubRouter; do NOT create a new router file.
- ADD route handler to existing routes/github.ts; do NOT create routes/console.ts or any new router.
- New file count is bounded: exactly 3 new files (ReleaseTimeline.tsx, ReleaseTimeline.test.tsx, github-releases.test.ts). Everything else is an additive edit to an existing file.

NON_GOALS
- No /console app, no apps/console/ directory, no /api/console route group, no legacy redirects.
- No release CREATE/EDIT/DELETE; read-only.
- No assets list.
- No new shared package; everything inside apps/gitboard.
- No CSS in apps/beadboard.

CONSTRAINTS
- TypeScript strict mode (project default).
- Bun runtime; use Bun.serve / bun:sqlite where the surrounding code does.
- happy-dom env for React tests (matches existing dashboard tests).
- /api/github/releases must be added BEFORE any SPA catchall in server.ts (verify route order in the route group).
- Density: match .pr-row padding, row height, and font-size exactly.
- Mono: tag_name is mono (it's a sha/tag-like value), all other text uses var(--font-ui).
- Body: sanitised via renderPrBodyText (which already strips script/style/on*).

VALIDATION
- bun install at repo root.
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test green; new ReleaseTimeline.test.tsx + github-releases.test.ts pass; existing 207 still pass (210 total expected).
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard build:dashboard green.
- bunx tsc --noEmit shows no new errors attributable to the touched files (preexisting repo-wide debt OK).
- Manual smoke (orchestrator runs after merge): GET /api/github/releases?repo=Jaggerxtrm/specialists returns Ōēź1 release; GET /api/github/releases (no repo) returns 400; gitboard dashboard shows Releases tab; clicking it lists rows; expanding renders body via renderPrBodyText.

OUTPUT
- File diff list (3 new, ~7 modified)
- /api/github/releases contract sample
- Confirmation that the tab visually matches PRs/Issues (density, tokens, octicon size)
- Any residual risks (release payload shape variance, virtualizer measureElement caveats)

title: Console rail polish: derive owner/repo for beads + display name + click-to-select
description:
PROBLEM
Post-z6j.1 the /console rail renders beads-only entries as UUIDs (e.g. "0494245c-...") and the github/beads merge never collides because beads ProjectScanner uses uuid as project.id while github uses owner/repo. Rows are also non-clickable so the empty right pane never changes. Result: the scaffold renders but feels broken to the user.

REFERENCES
- Parent: forge-z6j (epic)
- Sibling: forge-z6j.1 (scaffold; SHIPPED)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/console.ts (mergeConsoleRepos uses project.id as full_name ŌĆö line 20 ŌĆö wrong key)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts (ProjectScanner; check if it already reads git remote)
- apps/console/src/App.tsx (rail renders repo.full_name verbatim; no click handler)
- apps/console/src/types/console.ts (ConsoleRepoEntry shape)
- apps/console/src/styles/globals.css (existing .console-repo-row class)
- apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/console.test.ts (merge test to extend)

SUCCESS
- Beads project entries in /api/console/repos return a `full_name` derived from the project's git remote when available (`owner/repo` parsed from `git -C <path> config --get remote.origin.url` OR by reading `.git/config` directly ŌĆö no new dependency). Fall back to `project.name` when no remote.
- When github and beads point at the same `owner/repo`, the response has ONE entry with both `github` and `beads` populated.
- New `display_name` field on ConsoleRepoEntry: github.display_name || beads.name || full_name (computed server-side).
- /console rail renders `entry.display_name` (NOT raw `full_name`), with `full_name` as a smaller mono subtitle when they differ.
- Clicking a rail row sets a `selectedRepo` state in App.tsx; the right pane shows the selected repo header (display_name + full_name + a "Modules" placeholder block listing future tabs as disabled chips: Activity, PRs, Issues, Releases, Beads, Reports).
- Existing tests still pass; new test asserts a beads project whose path has remote `Jaggerxtrm/gitboard` merges with the github repo of the same full_name.

SCOPE
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/console.ts (derive owner/repo from beads project; populate display_name; rewrite mergeConsoleRepos to key on derived full_name)
- MODIFY: apps/console/src/types/console.ts (add display_name?: string)
- MODIFY: apps/console/src/App.tsx (display_name in rail, click handler, selectedRepo state, right-pane header + disabled module chips)
- MODIFY: apps/console/src/styles/globals.css (active row state, subtitle style, repo header, disabled chip cluster)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/console.test.ts (cover remote-derivation merge + display_name)
- NEW (if needed): apps/gitboard/src/core/git-remote.ts (tiny helper: read .git/config or invoke `git -C <path> remote get-url origin`, return parsed owner/repo or null)

NON_GOALS
- No actual module content (PRs/Issues/Releases/Beads/Reports tabs still come from later beads ŌĆö render disabled chips only).
- No edits to ProjectScanner core behavior (read-only helper; do not change project.id semantics in DB).
- No new deps. Use Bun.file / fs / child_process built-ins.
- No CSS framework swap.
- No new icon usage beyond what apps/console already imports.

CONSTRAINTS
- TypeScript strict mode.
- Octicons only (already imported); do not add other icon families.
- Mono font only on the small full_name subtitle when full_name has a "/" (i.e. looks like owner/repo); never on the display_name.
- Single --accent token.
- Git remote parsing must handle both `https://github.com/owner/repo.git` and `git@github.com:owner/repo.git`; strip trailing `.git`.
- If reading .git/config: use Bun.file (this is a Bun project, see apps/gitboard/CLAUDE.md). Avoid `child_process` if a file read suffices ŌĆö faster + no shell escape risk.
- Selected repo state may be in a URL query param too (?repo=owner/repo) so deep links work; if scope permits, sync state with location.search.
- Disabled chips must use existing tokens (e.g. border-color: var(--border-muted); color: var(--text-muted)).

VALIDATION
- `bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test` green; new merge test passes asserting a beads project at a path with remote Jaggerxtrm/gitboard merges with the github repo of full_name Jaggerxtrm/gitboard into one ConsoleRepoEntry with both `github` and `beads` set.
- `cd apps/console && bun run build` green.
- `bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard build` green.
- `bunx tsc --noEmit` shows no new errors attributable to scope files.
- Manual smoke (orchestrator runs): /api/console/repos response has at least 1 entry with BOTH github AND beads (will hit because Jaggerxtrm/gitboard exists both ways).

OUTPUT
- File diff list + key snippets
- /api/console/repos sample showing one merged entry (github+beads)
- Confirmation that the rail click sets the right pane and the disabled chips render
- Any residual risks (e.g. repos without remote stay keyed by project.name; non-github remotes show full URL or fall back)

title: Unify gitboard + beadboard under one /console shell
description:
PROBLEM
gitboard and beadboard run as two separate apps under apps/{gitboard,beadboard}/. The user has to switch URL/tab to move between GitHub work and beads work, and any shared chrome (rail, topbar, search, density) drifts between the two. A pre-existing bd memory describes a planned /console shell but no /console app exists in apps/.

REFERENCES
- bd memory: xtrm-console-now-uses-console-as-the-primary (stale until this epic lands)
- bd memory: xtrm-console-visual-contract-bg-181818-single-accent
- Current row + dossier primitives shipped on this branch: forge-l85, forge-wky, forge-zg3, forge-7n6, forge-8it
- Existing apps: apps/gitboard/ (Bun+Hono+Vite, port 3010), apps/beadboard/ (Vite-only, currently served via gitboard server in production mode)
- Existing mounting: apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts serves /gitboard, /beadboard SPA HTMLs when NODE_ENV=production

SUCCESS
- A new apps/console/ workspace builds and is mounted at GET /console by apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts.
- /console has one shell: single topbar (brand + repo search + status), single unified rail (GitHub repos + beads projects, merged by repo full_name), and module tabs: Activity ┬Ę PRs ┬Ę Issues ┬Ę Releases ┬Ę Beads (Feed/Board/Triage/Closed) ┬Ę Reports.
- All modules render through the row + dossier primitives already on this branch (no per-module CSS drift).
- /gitboard and /beadboard remain mounted as legacy redirects to the corresponding /console module + repo filter.

SCOPE (this is the EPIC ŌĆö child beads carry SCOPE files)
- apps/console/ (new workspace, vite+react)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts (add /console mount, redirect /gitboard /beadboard)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/console.ts (new: /api/console/repos merging /api/github/repos + /api/beads/projects keyed by repo full_name)
- packages/ui/src/styles/tokens.css (already canonical; no changes expected)

NON_GOALS
- No new auth.
- No backend re-architecture; keep Bun+Hono+single process model.
- No DB schema changes; merging happens at API layer.
- No removal of apps/gitboard or apps/beadboard until /console reaches parity (legacy redirects stay).
- No mobile-first rework; desktop dark UI only.

CONSTRAINTS
- Reuse existing components (IssueFeed, TriagePanel, PrTimeline, IssueTimeline, ActivityTimeline, RepoSidebar) ŌĆö adapt props, don't fork.
- Single accent (--accent), no extra palettes.
- No charts (per existing contract).
- Octicons only (per forge-hl1).
- Mono restricted (per forge-m38).

VALIDATION

- /gitboard and /beadboard 301 ŌåÆ /console?repo=<owner/name>&module=<git|beads> (or equivalent).

OUTPUT
- New app + API routes shipped, legacy routes redirecting, child beads closed.

title: Beadboard: dossier width inconsistent because module-list grid doesn't span
description: .module-list uses display:grid without grid-template-columns, so each implicit grid row sizes to fit content (longest child). Dossier 75% width is then computed against the row's narrower box, not viewport. Fix: grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr) (or switch to flex column).
close_reason: module-list now grid-template-columns minmax(0,1fr); rows fill row width

title: Beadboard refinements: single-line rows, text types, centered dossier, fix \n escape
description: User feedback bundle. (1) Row anatomy single-line by default ŌĆö title and meta inline, wrap only on narrow viewports. (2) Replace type-mark Octicon with the actual text label (Bug/Task/Epic/Feature/Chore). (3) Expanded dossier: drop the right sidebar; single centered column ~720px max; order = Metadata ŌåÆ Description ŌåÆ Notes ŌåÆ Labels ŌåÆ Dependency tree. (4) Markdown: unescape literal '\\n' in description (some sources store JSON-escaped newlines). (5) Make sure backtick code and **bold** render in description, not only notes.
close_reason: Single-line rows, text type label, centered single-col dossier (metadataŌåÆdescriptionŌåÆlabelsŌåÆtree), JSON-escaped newline unescape

title: Beadboard: dep tree id wraps + child titles empty
description: Two issues visible after forge-7n6: (1) Long bead ids in the dep tree wrap to two lines and the title overlaps ŌĆö need white-space:nowrap + overflow:hidden + ellipsis on .bead-dep-tree-id (or max-width). (2) Child dep rows show id + [kind] but title column is empty. Check API response for dependencies ŌĆö may need to fetch full issue per dep id or accept that title can be empty and hide the title cell when blank.
close_reason: id no longer wraps; dep titles resolved from loaded issues map

title: Beadboard: show dep IDs + tree like bd dep tree
description: User feedback: rows show '1 deps' but not which bead. Mimic bd dep tree output. (1) In the row meta-right, replace 'N deps' with up to 2 short bead-id chips + kind hint (Ōå¬ for blocks, Ōå® for blocked_by, ŌŖā for parent-child). (2) In the dossier, add a 'Dependency tree' block: root = current issue, children = each dep with kind label like [blocks] [blocked_by] [parent-child] [discovered-from], status check/dot, tree connectors. (3) If a dep id matches a loaded issue, recurse one level. Octicons only; no charts.

title: Beadboard: Triage segment backed by bv-style signals
description: Add a new 'Triage' segment to the module-header alongside Feed/Board/Closed/Memories. Renders from loaded data: 1) Top picks ranked by priority+staleness+open count (replicate bv's score heuristic client-side), 2) Quick wins (open, low priority cost, no blockers), 3) Stale issues (no activity 7d+), 4) Velocity (closed in last 7/30 days from closedIssues), 5) Project health counts by status/type/priority. No backend changes required ŌĆö all computed from existing endpoints.

title: Normalize Gitboard rows to square minimal FUI anatomy
description:
## Context

Gitboard is the reference list surface but still contains older FUI/rounded/gradient remnants. It needs to conform exactly to the visual contract before Beadboard matches it.

## What to build

Adjust Gitboard PR/activity/issue row styling to the shared row anatomy: square edges, #181818 row surface, 52px row height rhythm, thin left status rail, quiet hover/expanded state, consistent metadata typography.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] PR rows follow the shared row anatomy from the contract.
- [ ] GitHub issue/activity rows do not conflict with the PR row rhythm.
- [ ] No rounded cards/pills in primary list rows.
- [ ] PR expansion still works.

title: Epic: Normalize Gitboard and Beadboard visual system
description:
## Overview

Normalize Gitboard and Beadboard to the xtrm Console Minimal FUI contract before any further shell unification. This is a visual-system convergence pass: both apps should look like tabs of one professional operations console while remaining separate, stable routes during the migration.

## Goals

- Gitboard and Beadboard share the same #181818 base, square edges, border language, typography, spacing, and row rhythm.
- PR rows and bead issue rows use the same list-first anatomy.
- PR expanded detail and bead expanded dossier use the same section rhythm.
- Existing /gitboard and /beadboard functionality remains stable.

## Non-goals

- No /console route resurrection in this epic.
- No iframe embedding.
- No shared repo rail yet.
- No backend data-model rewrite.
- No mutating GitHub or Beads actions.

## Success criteria

- [ ] docs/xtrm-console-visual-contract.md is followed.
- [ ] Gitboard and Beadboard no longer have conflicting palettes or rounded-card styling.
- [ ] Beadboard feed visually matches Gitboard PR/activity list spacing and shapes.
- [ ] Both dashboard builds pass.
- [ ] Preview confirms /gitboard and /beadboard remain functional independently.

## Context

Visual contract: docs/xtrm-console-visual-contract.md
Key areas: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard, apps/beadboard/src/dashboard

title: github WS parity: publish github:event.append when poller inserts events
description:
## PROBLEM
\`github:event.append\` is declared in \`apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts\` (GithubRealtimeEvent union) and the dashboard subscribes to it in \`apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts\` line 79 ŌĆö but NO server-side site publishes it. New activity events show up in the dashboard only after a poll cycle round-trip (because pr.upsert/issue.upsert sync hints fire), not as their own push.

## SCOPE
- Find every site in \`apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts\` that calls \`insertEvent(this.db, ...)\` (user-events ingestion path).
- After each insert, publish \`github:event.append\` with the inserted event payload on \`github:activity\`.
- Watermark/dedup: insertEvent already has uniqueness on event.id; only publish for the newly-inserted ones (test \`changed\` or rely on insertEvent return signal ŌĆö extend the function if needed).

## NON_GOALS
- Refactoring the user-events backfill flow itself.
- Multiplexing event types into separate channels.

## OUTPUT
- Live activity timeline updates push-driven; new pushes / issue comments / fork events surface in the dashboard within seconds of a poll cycle ingesting them.

title: github poller: no /releases ingestion ŌĆö releases only captured as events from user feed (300-event window)
description:
## PROBLEM
The dashboard's release view is silently incomplete on most repos. Investigation:

- There is no \`github_releases\` table in the schema (\`apps/gitboard/src/core/store.ts\`).
- Releases are captured *only* as \`ReleaseEvent\` rows in \`github_events\`, sourced from the \`/users/<user>/events\` stream.
- That stream is bounded: most recent ~300 events, ~90-day sliding window.
- Current DB on the VPS has **2 ReleaseEvent rows total** across all 33 tracked repos. Real release count is far higher (e.g. \`gh release list --repo Jaggerxtrm/gitboard\` shows v0.2.0 + earlier).
- Anything older or not currently in the user-events stream is invisible to the dashboard.

This is unrelated to the realtime/WS layer ŌĆö releases are a poller/storage gap. Fixing the WS push (forge-7cyq) doesn't help here.

## SCOPE
- Add \`github_releases\` table to \`createDatabase\` (\`apps/gitboard/src/core/store.ts\`): columns roughly \`(repo, tag_name PK, name, body, html_url, author, published_at, created_at)\`.
- Add \`pollReleases(repo)\` to \`apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts\` ŌĆö single \`GET /repos/<owner>/<repo>/releases?per_page=100\` per tracked repo per poll cycle. Use ETag like the existing pollIssues/pollPullRequests to keep the cost free on cached responses.
- Upsert into the new table; on insert/change publish \`github:release.upsert\` over \`github:activity\` (the event type already exists in \`types/realtime.ts\` ŌĆö \`GithubRealtimeEvent\` union, \`GithubReleaseUpsert = GithubRelease\`).
- Expose \`/api/github/repos/:owner/:repo/releases\` route and update the dashboard's release surface to read from it.

## NON_GOALS
- Backfill of release \`body\` markdown beyond what GitHub returns on \`/releases\`.
- Pre-release / draft filtering UX ŌĆö surface all states with a chip.
- Asset/binary metadata.

## OUTPUT
- DB shows all releases on tracked repos, not just the 2 that happened to land in the user-events window.
- Dashboard release view populates fully; new releases trigger \`github:release.upsert\` in real time.
close_reason:
Implemented dedicated /repos/<x>/releases polling. New github_releases table (PK repo+tag_name) populated by pollReleases() inside the standard pollRepo loop, with published_at watermark and ETag gating. Result on VPS: 239 releases across 13 repos vs prior 2 ReleaseEvent rows in github_events.

- store.ts: github_releases table + migrations for new last_release_published_at / release_etag columns on github_repo_poll_state.
- github-store.ts: upsertRelease(); getReleases() now reads from the new table (preserving the existing GithubRelease[] return shape so the dashboard surface is untouched).
- github-poller.ts: pollReleases() mirrors pollPullRequests structure (per-page ETag, watermark on published_at, skip drafts). Wired into pollRepo() so it runs every cycle alongside pollIssues + pollPullRequests. Publishes github:release.upsert on changes.
- routes/github.ts: dropped the 'repo is required' constraint on /releases since the new path supports cross-repo listing, matching /prs and /issues.

Three github WS event types remain declared-but-unpublished ŌĆö github:release.upsert (now fixed), github:event.append, and github:rate_limit. The latter two are separate gaps worth their own beads if push parity is wanted across the github surface.

title: perf(graph): cache inFlightJobs() at module level ŌĆö current per-call SQL adds ~250ms to every /graph request
description:
## PROBLEM
`getGraphSnapshot` calls `dao.inFlightJobs()` synchronously on every graph request (graph-dao.ts:55). That call runs a fresh SQL query against the observability DB (`SELECT ... FROM jobs WHERE status IN (...)`). For specialists the result set is 134 rows; the request consistently takes ~260-420ms even when both the project scan cache and the issue cache are hot.

Recent perf work (forge-cspw, forge-ijzx, forge-9qmk, forge-5i43) cached `listRepos()` and chunked DAO queries but left `inFlightJobs` per-call. With the issue cache TTL at 10s and the project cache TTL at 30s, the inFlightJobs query is the dominant cost on warmed graph reads.

## SCOPE
- Cache `inFlightJobs()` output at the dao or graph-dao level with a short TTL (e.g. 1-2s) ŌĆö short enough that the "live" overlay still feels live, long enough to amortise across rapid graph repaints.
- Alternative: precompute a per-project `Map<projectId | name, SpecialistJob[]>` once per TTL window so the buildGraph filter is O(1) instead of O(134).
- Confirm cache invalidation on observability writes if write paths exist (otherwise stale specialist overlay).

## OUTPUT
- Warmed graph latency drops from ~300ms ŌåÆ <50ms.
- Live specialist overlay still updates within ~2s.
notes: Superseded by the WS-push approach. The TTL cache is no longer needed once the graph hot path is event-driven. Closing this in favour of the new bead.
close_reason: Superseded by forge-7cyq. The TTL/queueMicrotask cache approach treats the symptom; the real architectural fix is to make the graph endpoint NOT be a hot path by replacing polling with WS push. Implemented in forge-7cyq ŌĆö graph endpoint is now only hit on user navigation or on specialists:activity / beads:changes WS hints, so the per-call inFlightJobs() SQL cost no longer matters.

title: Documentation sweep: README + deploy/runbook docs
description:
## PROBLEM
Many docs are stale or missing after the recent deploy work and accumulated changes. README is thin; deployment.md still describes the docker-only path (which we've moved away from); the new native systemd + Tailscale run mode is undocumented and non-obvious to re-derive from code.

## SCOPE ŌĆö needs to be added/refreshed
- **README.md** ŌĆö high-level overview, current run modes, links to deeper docs
- **docs/deployment.md** ŌĆö replace/augment the docker-centric content with the native run mode that actually deployed (forge-qa5g)
- **Native systemd user service run mode** (the path that won):
  - `~/.config/systemd/user/gitboard.service` unit shape (Type=simple, ExecStart=bun, env vars: HOST, PORT, XDG_PROJECTS_DIR, DOLT_HOST, LOG_DIR)
  - `loginctl enable-linger <user>` requirement so service runs without an active session
  - Pre-build step: `bun install && cd apps/gitboard && bun run build:dashboard` on the host before first start
  - `systemctl --user daemon-reload && systemctl --user enable --now gitboard`
  - Restart / logs cheat sheet (`systemctl --user restart gitboard`, `journalctl --user -u gitboard -f`, `tail -F ~/.agent-forge/logs/$(date +%F).jsonl`)
- **Tailscale-only access pattern**:
  - Tailscale on the host, `tailscale up` for auth
  - Bind server to the tailscale interface IP (`HOST=<tailnet-ip>`) ŌĆö no ufw, no NAT, no public exposure even with no firewall
  - Why this beats `tailscale serve` for the personal-VPS case (no HTTPS cert opt-in needed; one less moving part)
- **Env var matrix**: HOST, PORT, AGENT_FORGE_DB, XDG_PROJECTS_DIR, DOLT_HOST, LOG_DIR, GITHUB_TOKEN, SKIP_GITHUB_POLLER, LOG_LEVEL ŌĆö what each does, defaults, when to override
- **Scanner behaviour**: XDG_PROJECTS_DIR semantics; what happens for nested layouts (~/dev + ~/projects); shared-server config fallback (`dolt.shared-server: true` ŌåÆ reads ~/.beads/shared-server/dolt-server.port)
- **Docker path status**: keep Dockerfile/compose in tree but document them as "experimental / not the primary deploy" ŌĆö note the known bugs (vite v7 outDir resolves under root, host.docker.internal needs DOLT_HOST override, custom bridge subnets may lack NAT on shared hosts)

## NON_GOALS
- Multi-user/multi-tenant deployment docs
- Cloud / k8s deploy guides
- TLS/HTTPS termination docs (Tailscale handles transport on the personal path)

## OUTPUT
- Updated README.md with current run modes and quickstart
- New/refreshed docs/deployment.md covering the native systemd + Tailscale path
- Audit + refresh of other docs in docs/ that have drifted

title: Graph chip: move priority left rail internal to avoid arrow overlap
description:
## PROBLEM
Recently added priority left rail on graph chips is implemented as 'border-left: 3px solid var(--graph-priority-N)' on .g-node (apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css:5419-5438). React Flow Handle at Position.Left attaches to the same outer edge, so arrow tips overlap the colored rail.

## SUCCESS
- Priority rail is visually INSIDE the chip box (not on the outer border).
- Arrow tips attach to a clean uniform 1px border, no overlap with the colored rail.
- Chip outer dimensions preserved (or close enough that react-flow layout doesn't shift visibly).
- All 5 priority colors (P0-P4) and the active-pulse animation still render.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css: .g-node base rule + .g-node[data-p='N'] rules (lines ~5419-5439).
- No .tsx changes expected.

## DESIGN

title: Graph empty-state UX: distinguish 'loading' (freshness=stale) from 'truly empty' (freshness=fresh)
description:
## PROBLEM
When opening the graph for a project for the first time, the API correctly returns freshness='stale' with nodes=[] (cache cold; background refresh in flight). The UI renders this as 'no beads in this project' ŌĆö same message as a project that genuinely has zero beads. After switching tabs and back, the cache is warm, the response returns freshness='fresh' with the real nodes, and the graph populates.

Two issues:
1. Wrong messaging: a cold/stale empty response is mistakenly shown as definitively empty.
2. Suboptimal flow: the UI does not auto-refetch on stale, leaving the user to manually switch tabs to trigger a second request.

## SUCCESS
- When response.freshness === 'stale' (or absent) and nodes.length === 0: show 'Loading project graphŌĆ”' or a spinner + 'Background refresh in progress'.
- When response.freshness === 'fresh' and nodes.length === 0: show 'No beads in this project' (current message).
- When response.freshness === 'degraded' and nodes.length === 0: show 'Graph data unavailable ŌĆö last refresh failed' with a retry affordance.
- When response.freshness === 'stale': the client should schedule one auto-refetch ~500-1000ms later (or once the WS broadcasts the corresponding project update) to pick up the warmed cache without the user switching tabs.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/Graph/ (or equivalent ŌĆö the graph empty-state component; find it via the existing 'no beads in this project' string).
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGraphData.ts (auto-refetch on stale).
- Tests for the empty-state component covering all 3 freshness states.

## NON_GOALS
- Changing the API contract (already returns freshness).
- Changing the cache layer.

## CONSTRAINTS
- Do not break the case where the API response is missing 'freshness' entirely (treat as stale for safety).
- Refetch must not loop: max 1 retry per stale response; if second response is still stale, surface 'loading' UI and rely on WS broadcast or user-initiated refresh.

## VALIDATION
- Unit test: empty-state component renders correct message for each freshness value.
- Unit test: useGraphData schedules one refetch when first response is stale; does not loop on repeated stale.
- Manual: cold-load gitboard graph; should see 'loading' briefly then populate without tab switch.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, test evidence.
notes:

title: Graph project resolution returns fallback:selected-repo:projects on valid UUID
description:
## PROBLEM
GET /api/console/graph?project=<gitboard-project-uuid> returns:
  {"project_id":"","repo_slug":"","project":"fallback:selected-repo:projects","nodes":[],"edges":[],"freshness":"fresh"}

Even when the UUID matches a valid project from /api/beads/projects (e.g. 4c37df93-55bf-44d2-8ddb-9c9d0f237587 = gitboard). resolveProject() in graph-dao.ts falls back instead of matching.

User-visible effect: switching to the gitboard graph in the dashboard shows the cached/empty fallback ('no beads in this project') even though beads/projects clearly lists gitboard with active beads.

This is independent of the HA work landed in forge-5i43 ŌĆö the caching layer is correctly serving 'fresh' results; the resolver itself returns nothing.

## SUCCESS

- 'fallback:selected-repo:projects' only appears when no project matches AND no selected-repo is set.

title: Observability DB schema_version 0 incompatible ŌĆö attach-pool refuses .specialists/observability.db
description:
## PROBLEM
On dev startup the API logs:
  Skip observability db /home/dawid/dev/specialists/.specialists/observability.db: schema_version 0 incompatible

Source: apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/attach-pool.ts emits this via logger.warn when the SQLite PRAGMA schema_version (or expected app-level migration version, check exact pragma in code) reads 0 on attach. The DB exists and is reachable but attach-pool marks it dead, so the SpecialistsDao falls back to emptySpecialistsDao() permanently.

User-visible effect: /api/specialists/* always returns empty data ŌĆö even with the listRepos cache fix (forge-9qmk) and the serve-stale layer (forge-5i43) working correctly. Specialists tab shows nothing.

(Also: the log message is misformatted ŌĆö emits 'n ${pragma} incompatible' instead of '${pragmaName} incompatible'. Minor log-string bug, fix alongside if cheap.)

## SUCCESS
- API attaches the observability DB and serves real specialists data, OR
- The schema check is correctly identifying a real version mismatch and the bead documents the upgrade path / migration the user needs to run.
- Log message renders the pragma name correctly.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/attach-pool.ts (schema check + log string)
- Possibly apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/config.ts / migration logic if a real migration is needed.
- Test: attach-pool unit test confirms behavior on schema_version=0 and on a known-good DB.

## NON_GOALS
- HA / caching work (already handled by forge-5i43, forge-9qmk).
- Rewriting the observability DB schema unless this turns out to be a missing-migration bug.

## CONSTRAINTS
- Investigate first: is the DB actually at v0, or is the pragma read wrong? Schema_version=0 is the SQLite default when no app-level migrations have run, so it may indicate the migration runner never executed against this DB.
- If the DB needs migrating, do it idempotently and log clearly.

## VALIDATION
- Restart API; observability DB attaches without warning.
- /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight returns real data (or a clear empty when truly empty).
- Unit test for the schema check path.

## OUTPUT
Diagnosis, fix path, changed files, test evidence.

description:
## PROBLEM

forge-7erc changes GitHub poller scheduling, specialists router typing, and backend boundary test contract logic in response to Codex comments. Need a cheap read-only smell pass before commit.

## SUCCESS

Confirm the diff is minimal, type-safe, and directly addresses the four Codex findings without broad behavior changes, or list concrete fixes.

## SCOPE

Current diff for:
- apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts
- apps/gitboard/tests/core/github-poller-loop.test.ts
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts
- apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/specialists.test.ts
- apps/gitboard/tests/backend-boundaries/performance-boundary-contract.test.ts

## NON_GOALS

- No edits.
- No broad architecture review.
- No merge decision.

## CONSTRAINTS

READ_ONLY. Focus on async error handling, rate-limit pause monotonicity, option type compatibility, and whether boundary contract can still be fooled by skipped/comment-only tests.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] Cite concrete findings or say OK.
- [ ] Account for targeted test pass: `bun --filter @xtrm/gitboard test -- tests/core/github-poller-loop.test.ts tests/api/routes/specialists.test.ts tests/backend-boundaries/performance-boundary-contract.test.ts` (3 files, 25 tests).

## OUTPUT

notes:
### Specialist Output ŌĆö code-sanity (job b3f097) [WAITING ŌĆö more output may follow]

## Code Sanity Verdict
- Verdict: FINDINGS
- Scope reviewed: `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts`, `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts`, `apps/gitboard/tests/core/github-poller-loop.test.ts`, `apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/specialists.test.ts`, `apps/gitboard/tests/backend-boundaries/performance-boundary-contract.test.ts`
- Confidence: medium

## Findings
- Location: `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts: maybePauseForRateLimit()`
  - Issue: pause clear not monotonic. Code resets `pausedUntil = 0` on later response with `remaining > limit * 0.8`, even if prior low-rate response set longer pause.
  - Why it matters: rate-limit backoff can shrink mid-pause, so poller may resume early and flap under mixed headers.
  - Suggested executor instruction: keep `pausedUntil` monotonic during active pause. Only extend pause on low remaining; clear only after explicit resume condition outside rate-limit handler or when current time passes pause.

- Location: `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts: resolve() in createSpecialistsRouter()`
  - Issue: unsafe cast `repoLister() as SpecialistRepoSummary`.
  - Why it matters: hides option-shape mismatch at boundary. Router can accept incompatible `listRepos` return and still compile, then break epoch mapping or in-flight payload shape at runtime.
  - Suggested executor instruction: type `listRepos` callback to return `SpecialistRepoSummary` directly, or normalize result before use. Drop cast.

## Non-findings
- Did not flag boundary-contract test loophole. Missing line-level evidence in inspected test body, so no concrete claim.
- Did not flag `parseLimit`; clamp logic looks simple and bounded.

## Handoff
Resume executor with 2 fixes. Next pass should recheck poller pause monotonicity and remove unsafe route cast, then rerun targeted 3-test command.

title: Specialists: deeper detail + API surface docs
description:
## PROBLEM
The /console/specialists tab now shows live turn/tool counts (forge-6jab) but the detail pane could surface much more, and the /api/specialists/* surface has no living documentation ŌĆö every future contributor has to spelunk routes/specialists.ts + observability/dao.ts to find what's exposed.

## SUCCESS
1. **Richer per-job detail**
   - Token usage (sum of token_usage events per job, or specialist_job_metrics.token_trajectory_json for completed jobs)
   - Active runtime vs wall elapsed (active_runtime_ms vs Date.now - started_at)
   - Stall gaps (specialist_job_metrics.stall_gaps_json) rendered as a small inline bar
   - Per-tool call breakdown (tool_call_counts_json) ŌĆö top 5 tools with counts
   - Live model for in-flight jobs (extract from latest 'meta' event with model field, not just specialist_job_metrics.model which is null until terminal)
   - Bead context preview / dossier link on the chip
2. **List card improvements**
   - Show aggregate metrics on the card (total turns across roles, total tools, total tokens)
   - Show last activity timestamp clearly (now / 3m / 1h etc. with hover for absolute)
   - Filter chips drive query (currently client-side filter; could move to URL/query param)
3. **API surface documentation**
   - Document each /api/specialists/* endpoint with request shape, response shape, and example payload
   - Document specialist_jobs + specialist_job_metrics + specialist_events table contract (what populates them, when)
   - Document that metrics row only materializes on terminal status (done/cancelled/error) and events table is the live-data fallback
   - Document the 'turns' / 'tools' coalesce strategy (specialist_job_metrics first, fall back to event count)
   - Location: either docs/console-specialists.md or inline JSDoc in routes/specialists.ts + dao.ts (prefer doc file for discoverability)

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/specialists/* ŌĆö JobBlock JSX, ChainCard
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useChainDetail.ts, useChains.ts (add tokens field if extracted)
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/dao.ts ŌĆö token aggregation query
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts ŌĆö endpoint docs
- apps/gitboard/docs/console-specialists.md (NEW) or equivalent doc location

## NON_GOALS
- Refactor metrics-dao or KPI surfaces
- Add a new SQLite schema column
- Real-time websocket push for specialist updates (5s polling is sufficient)
- Multi-job comparison / diff view

## CONSTRAINTS
- No new deps
- All data must come from existing tables (specialist_jobs, specialist_job_metrics, specialist_events)
- Visual register stays consistent with IssueFeed + graph chips (status-coloured rail, mono identity, italic title)
- Compact dev look ŌĆö no per-element borders, hairline dividers only

## VALIDATION
- bunx tsc --noEmit clean
- /console/specialists renders with all enhanced fields
- docs/console-specialists.md (or chosen doc location) covers every /api/specialists/* route
- Token count populates for in-flight jobs (verify via API curl)

## OUTPUT
- Enhanced JSX + CSS
- New or updated docs file
- Example before/after screenshot of detail pane

title: Graph chips: visual parity with IssueFeed register
description:

After forge-2a8a landed, the graph chips (BeadNode, NodeChip in NOW strip, OrphanRow in orphans/buckets) still look like the legacy compact chips: priority-rail border-left, status glyph, single-row layout, P-tag chip-style. The IssueFeed (Feed tab) uses a different register entirely ŌĆö two-line layout (id / title on top, Pn type state agent on bottom), no rail, type-coloured priority + type marks, italic title, '/' separator. Maintaining two divergent registers for the same domain object (a bead) makes the app feel inconsistent.

## SUCCESS
Graph chips visually mirror IssueFeed rows wherever a bead is rendered:
- Identity row: id / title (mono id, low-alpha '/', italic title)
- Classification row: Pn type state agent (Pn + type colored by TYPE_CONFIG palette: bug #ff4d5e, feature #4169e1, task --text-muted, epic rgba(163,113,247,0.95))
- No left-rail border
- Same palette tokens as IssueFeed

- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/Graph.tsx (NodeChip, OrphanRow)

- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/buildFlowGraph.ts (NODE_W/NODE_H bump to fit 2-line)

## NON_GOALS
- Refactor IssueFeed
- Extract a shared bead-chip component (would force IssueFeed to also change; not in scope)

## CONSTRAINTS
- TYPE_CONFIG palette duplicated (graph + feed); document so future drift is visible
- Status pulse (.g-node.act .g-ag-dot) still works
- Edge handle picking still works (4 handles + top/bottom)

## VALIDATION
- bunx tsc --noEmit clean
- bunx vitest run tests/dashboard/pages/console/graph/ ŌåÆ 9/9 pass
- Visual: bead chips read like Feed rows; orphan strip rows + NOW strip rows match

## OUTPUT

- Screenshot before/after

description: PROBLEM: forge-ojn4.7 changes GithubPoller scheduling, conditional GET/ETag persistence, and bounded repo concurrency. Need READ_ONLY sanity check before landing. CONSTRAINTS: preserve instant WS github:pr.upsert/github:issue.upsert publishes on changed records; no per-repo sleep in pollRepos; bounded concurrency; persisted issue_etag/pr_etag must be reused; rate-limit Retry-After/reset pauses should be respected. VALIDATION CONTEXT: targeted poller tests pass: bun --filter @xtrm/gitboard test -- tests/core/github-poller-loop.test.ts tests/core/github-poller.test.ts. notes:

## Code Sanity Verdict
- Verdict: FINDINGS
- Scope reviewed: `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts` `maybePauseForRateLimit`, `getIfNoneMatch`, `apiGet`; `apps/gitboard/tests/core/github-poller-loop.test.ts` change scope via GitNexus diff
- Confidence: medium

## Findings
- Location: `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts:218-230`
  - Issue: rate-limit pause logic ignores `Retry-After` and `X-RateLimit-Reset`; hardcoded `+60_000` pause only keys off `X-RateLimit-Remaining`.
  - Why it matters: constraint says reset pauses must be respected. Current logic can resume too early or sleep wrong duration on secondary/abuse limits.
  - Suggested executor instruction: wire `Retry-After` / `X-RateLimit-Reset` into `maybePauseForRateLimit`, use header-driven deadline, keep fallback only when headers missing.

- Location: `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts:236-239`
  - Issue: ETag reuse stays in-memory only via `private etags = new Map(...)`; no persisted load/store path in shown helper.
  - Why it matters: constraint says persisted `issue_etag` / `pr_etag` must be reused. This loses conditional GET state on restart, so cold boots re-fetch full pages.
  - Suggested executor instruction: read poll-state ETags from DB on startup and write back on 200/304 path; keep `getIfNoneMatch` backed by persisted state, not only process memory.

## Non-findings
- Not judging webhook/WS business flow, ingestion semantics, or test breadth. Not checking broad architecture or security.

## Handoff
Proceed to reviewer with these 2 concrete fixes or rerun executor on `github-poller.ts` if diff still in flux.

title: Overthinker: GitHub detail progressive cache plan
description: PROBLEM: forge-ojn4.5 must reduce GitHub PR detail/report fan-out without regressing detail semantics. Need READ_ONLY critique of the implementation plan before broad edits. SUCCESS: propose a minimal safe plan for bounded PR detail latency, cache keys/timeouts, partial errors, and reports list first paint. SCOPE: apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts, apps/gitboard/src/core/github-readme.ts, apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/PrTimeline.tsx, apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/RepoContentPanels.tsx, apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/client.ts. NON_GOALS: no edits, no GraphQL migration, no visual redesign. CONSTRAINTS: preserve instant dashboard/WS behavior, avoid aggressive rate-limit use, cite concrete symbols/files, prefer minimal changes. OUTPUT: prioritized plan + pitfalls + tests to add.
notes:
### Specialist Output ŌĆö overthinker (job 4574c1) [WAITING ŌĆö more output may follow]

## Phase 1 - Initial Analysis

Goal: reduce GitHub PR detail/report fan-out. Preserve detail semantics.

Current evidence:
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/PrTimeline.tsx`
  - `PrExpandedBody` calls `apiClient.getPrDetail(owner, repoName, pr.number)` on expand.
  - UI already handles `detail.errors` as ŌĆ£partial GitHub dataŌĆØ.
  - `buildConversation` merges PR body, comments, reviews, review comments, commits, files, timeline.
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/client.ts`
  - `getPrDetail()` hits `/api/github/prs/:owner/:repo/:number/detail`.
  - `get()` has no timeout.
  - `listRepoReports()` hits `/api/github/repo/:owner/:name/reports`.
- `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-readme.ts`
  - `fetchRepoFile()` and `listRepoDir()` already use in-memory 10 min TTL.
  - Cache key now `${owner}/${repo}::${path}` or `dir:${path}`.
  - No request timeout.
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/RepoContentPanels.tsx`
  - `ReportsView` blocks first paint on `listRepoReports()`.
  - `ReportMeta` accepts `frontmatter: null`.
  - Body fetch is separate through `getRepoReport()`.
- `apps/gitboard/src/types/github.ts`
  - `GithubPrDetail` already supports `errors?: Record<string,string>`.

Best minimal plan:
1. Add bounded server-side PR detail cache in `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts`.
2. Add per-segment timeout + `Promise.allSettled` for PR detail fan-out.
3. Return same `GithubPrDetail` shape, with empty arrays and `errors` for failed segments.
4. Keep dashboard/WS hot path untouched. Only detail-on-expand changes.
5. Make reports list first paint directory-first. Defer/limit frontmatter fan-out.
6. Add readme/report fetch timeout inside `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-readme.ts`.

## Phase 2 - Devil's Advocate

Risk: cache key too coarse. Stale PR conversation after new comment. Fix with key:
`pr-detail:${owner}/${repo}#${number}:${pr.updated_at ?? pr.closed_at ?? pr.merged_at ?? pr.created_at}` plus short TTL.

Risk: `updated_at` from local DB lags. WS/poller may not refresh instantly. Fix with short TTL, 60s open PRs, 10m closed/merged PRs.

Risk: full `Promise.all` still waits on slowest endpoint. Use per-call timeout plus overall deadline. `allSettled` alone not enough.

Risk: partial arrays can hide errors. Keep `errors.comments`, `errors.reviews`, `errors.review_comments`, `errors.commits`, `errors.files`, `errors.timeline`. UI already surfaces partial state.

Risk: client timeout in shared `ApiClient.get()` breaks dashboard. Do not add global timeout. Add optional timeout only to slow detail/report methods if needed.

Risk: reports list semantics regress if frontmatter removed. `ReportMeta` supports null, but metadata disappears. Safer path: list all report names immediately, parse frontmatter for bounded latest N only, mark rest null. Longer-term progressive enrichment can follow.

Risk: in-memory cache can grow. Add max entries or prune expired on set. Tiny LRU enough, e.g. 200 PR details, 200 report metadata entries.

Risk: GitHub rate limits worsen if cache bypasses on every expand due key churn. Singleflight per cache key avoids duplicate concurrent expansions.

Risk: file URL uses `encodeURIComponent(path)` for paths with `/`; current code may already work/fail depending GitHub behavior. Do not broaden this task.

## Phase 3 - Synthesis

Minimal safe implementation plan:

### P0: PR detail bounded latency

File: `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts`

Add helpers near `githubApi`:
- `withTimeout(promise, ms, label)`
- `settleSegment(label, promise, fallback)`
- `ttlCache` + `singleflight` for PR detail

Endpoint: `/prs/:owner/:repo/:number/detail`

Keep current data model:
```ts
{
  pr,
  comments: [],
  reviews: [],
  review_comments: [],
  commits: [],
  files: [],
  timeline: [],
  errors?: Record<string, string>
}
```

Use:
- cache key: `owner/repo#number:${pr.updated_at ?? pr.closed_at ?? pr.merged_at ?? pr.created_at}`
- TTL:
  - open PR: 60s
  - closed/merged PR: 10m
- timeout:
  - per GitHub subrequest: 1500ŌĆō2000ms
  - total endpoint budget: ~2500ms
- segment list:
  - comments
  - reviews
  - review comments
  - commits
  - files
  - timeline
- fan-out: parallel, but each segment bounded.
- failed segment: fallback empty array + `errors[segment]`.

Why safe:
- `PrExpandedBody` already handles partial via `detail.errors`.
- `buildConversation` tolerates missing arrays.
- Dashboard/WS flow untouched.

### P1: Reports list first paint

Files:
- `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-readme.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/RepoContentPanels.tsx`

Current blocker: `ReportsView` waits on `listRepoReports()`. If route fetches each report content for frontmatter, first paint fans out.

Plan:
- `listRepoReports()` response returns directory entries fast from `listRepoDir(owner, repo, ".xtrm/reports")`.
- Frontmatter:
  - parse only first bounded N, e.g. 10 latest/first markdown files.
  - each `fetchRepoFile()` timeout 800ms.
  - failures become `frontmatter: null`.
- Keep response shape. No UI redesign.
- Optional small UI copy: if metadata null, `ReportMeta` already renders nothing. No change required.

If stronger preservation needed:
- Add `?metadata=bounded` default.
- Later add explicit `?metadata=full` for old behavior. Not needed now.

### P2: Readme/report fetch hardening

File: `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-readme.ts`

Add timeout to `ghFetch()`:
- `AbortController`
- default 1500ms or 2000ms
- preserve thrown errors

Cache:
- keep `TTL_MS = 10 * 60 * 1000`
- add max entries prune to avoid unbounded maps
- negative cache 404 already exists for dirs; add optional null cache for file 404 only if response type supports it. Better skip for minimal PR.

### P3: Client method-level timeout only if needed

File: `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/client.ts`

Avoid global `get()` timeout. Shared by instant dashboard loads.

If server timeout works, no client change needed.

If UI still hangs on server/network:
- Add private `getWithTimeout(path, ms)`.
- Use only in:
  - `getPrDetail()`: 3500ms
  - `listRepoReports()`: 2000ms
  - `getRepoReport()`: 2500ms
- Keep existing `get()` untouched.

## Phase 4 - Final Refined Output

## Summary

Plan targets slow edges only: PR detail-on-expand and reports list. No dashboard/WS behavior touched. Same response shapes preserved.

## Status

READ_ONLY critique complete. No files edited.

GitNexus note: `gitnexus_detect_changes()` showed pre-existing modifications in `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts`. I did not edit. Treat working tree as dirty before executor starts.

## Changes

None.

## Verification

Evidence checked:
- `PrExpandedBody` consumes `getPrDetail()` and already handles `detail.errors`.
- `GithubPrDetail` includes `errors?: Record<string,string>`.
- `ReportsView` accepts `frontmatter: null`.
- `fetchRepoFile()` / `listRepoDir()` already cache for 10 minutes.
- `ApiClient.get()` has no timeout and is widely used; avoid global timeout.

## Risks

- Coarse PR cache can stale comments. Use `updated_at` key + short TTL.
- `Promise.allSettled` without timeout still hangs. Need timeout per segment.
- Reports metadata fan-out can still block if all files parsed. Bound N + deadline.
- Global client timeout can regress dashboard. Avoid.
- Dirty route file means executor must inspect diff before edits.

## Follow-ups

Escalate to reviewer after implementation. Focus review on:
- stale cache key
- partial error semantics
- endpoint timeout behavior
- reports metadata regression

## Beads

Claim/notes/close commands required, but shell tool unavailable in this specialist run. No `bd` command executed.

## Final Answer

Recommended minimal safe plan:

1. `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts`: add PR detail TTL cache + singleflight.
   - key: `owner/repo#number:${pr.updated_at ?? pr.closed_at ?? pr.merged_at ?? pr.created_at}`
   - TTL: 60s open, 10m closed/merged
   - per-segment timeout: 1.5ŌĆō2s
   - endpoint target: ~2.5s
   - return same `GithubPrDetail`, fill failed segments with `[]`, record `errors`.

2. `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts`: change reports list to directory-first.
   - return report names fast from `listRepoDir()`
   - parse frontmatter for bounded N only
   - failed/slow metadata => `frontmatter: null`
   - body fetch remains separate via `getRepoReport()`.

3. `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-readme.ts`: add fetch timeout + cache pruning.
   - keep 10m TTL
   - add max entries
   - avoid aggressive GitHub calls.

4. `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/client.ts`: avoid global timeout.
   - only add method-specific timeout if server bounds prove insufficient.

title: Research: React Flow current state for graph migration spike
description:
## PROBLEM
The forge-2a8a epic plans a /console/graph migration to React Flow. Spike bead forge-2a8a.1 says 'pin v11; v12 was beta as of 2026-05'. Today is 2026-05-20. Need ground truth on:

1. Current stable React Flow major (v11 vs v12 vs @xyflow/react rebrand). Recommend the right install command + package name as of today.
2. Bundle size: gzip cost of the recommended package + its CSS. Goal: <80kb gzip delta.
3. Custom node component API: minimal example of a custom node type with handles (source on right, target on left), nodesDraggable={false}, custom JSX content. Show what props the node component receives.
4. Auto-layout integration: dagre vs elkjs with React Flow in 2026. Which is recommended for layered LR DAGs? Package name + minimal usage pattern.
5. Subflow / group node API for visually grouping clusters (parent node with child nodes). Cite current docs.
6. Bundle audit: how to measure the React Flow chunk size in a Vite build (the repo uses Vite 7 + Bun). Recommended tool/command.
7. Any production examples of node-based DAG UIs that use React Flow with dagre auto-layout (read-only, cluster-grouped). Link 2-3 with brief notes on what each does well.

## SUCCESS
Concrete answers to all 7 questions with citations (library IDs via ctx7 / deepwiki where applicable). No training-data guesses.

## SCOPE
External: reactflow / @xyflow/react docs, dagre / elkjs docs, GitHub for production usage examples.

## NON_GOALS
- No local code reading (separate concern).
- No bundle measurement here (that runs in the spike itself).
- No design decisions ŌĆö just facts.

## CONSTRAINTS
READ_ONLY external research. Cite sources.

## OUTPUT
Bulleted answers per question with package name, version, install command, code snippet where useful, and source citation.
notes:
### Specialist Output ŌĆö researcher (job 91be5e) [WAITING ŌĆö more output may follow] ## Summary Got core facts for 1-4. ## Status 1. **Current stable package**: `@xyflow/react` **12.10.2** latest. Old `reactflow` still exists at **11.11.4** latest. Source docs say package rename happened in v12; install `@xyflow/react`, import CSS from same package. - Install: `bun add @xyflow/react` or `npm install @xyflow/react` - Imports: ```ts import { ReactFlow } from '@xyflow/react'; import '@xyflow/react/dist/style.css'; ``` - Source: React Flow v12 migration + install docs. `https://reactflow.dev/learn/troubleshooting/migrate-to-v12` `https://reactflow.dev/learn/getting-started/installation-and-requirements` 2. **Bundle size estimate**: `@xyflow/react@12.10.2` gzip Ōēł **57,949 B** from Bundlephobia. CSS `dist/style.css` gzip Ōēł **3,017 B** from local pack/compress of published tarball. - Total package+CSS Ōēł **60.9 kB gzip**. - Under 80 kB goal. - Source: - Bundlephobia API result for `@xyflow/react@12.10.2` - npm tarball inspection of `dist/style.css` 3. **Minimal custom node**: docs show custom node component gets `NodeProps`; v12 renamed absolute position props to `positionAbsoluteX/Y`. Minimal pattern: ```tsx import { Handle, Position, type NodeProps } from '@xyflow/react'; export function CustomNode({ data }: NodeProps) { return (
{data.label}
); } ``` Props evidence: `NodeProps` includes node interaction fields; docs call out `selectable`, `deletable`, `draggable`, `parentId`, plus `positionAbsoluteX/Y` in v12 migration. - Sources: - `https://reactflow.dev/api-reference/types/node-props` - `https://reactflow.dev/learn/troubleshooting/migrate-to-v12` - `https://reactflow.dev/api-reference/components/handle` 4. **Dagre vs elkjs**: docs recommend **Dagre** for simple layered/tree/LR DAGs: fast, minimal config, straightforward. **ELK** for more configurable layouts + edge routing. - For layered LR DAGs, pick **`@dagrejs/dagre`**. - Minimal usage: ```ts import dagre from '@dagrejs/dagre'; const g = new dagre.graphlib.Graph().setDefaultEdgeLabel(() => ({})); g.setGraph({ rankdir: 'LR' }); nodes.forEach(n => g.setNode(n.id, { width, height })); edges.forEach(e => g.setEdge(e.source, e.target)); dagre.layout(g); ``` - Source: - `https://reactflow.dev/learn/layouting/layouting` - `https://reactflow.dev/examples/layout/dagre` - `https://reactflow.dev/examples/layout/elkjs` ## Changes None. Read-only research only. ## Risks - Bundle number from Bundlephobia is package-level, not exact app delta. - CSS gzip from published tarball is exact file gzip, not Vite chunk delta. ## Follow-ups If needed, I can finish 5-7: - subflow/group node docs - Vite bundle audit command - 2-3 production React Flow + dagre examples ```json {"status":"success","summary":"Current stable package is @xyflow/react 12.10.2; reactflow 11.11.4 still exists. Bundle estimate for package+CSS is ~60.9 kB gzip, under 80 kB target. Custom node and dagre/ELK guidance gathered. Skipped 5-7 for now.","files_changed":[],"follow_ups":["Finish subflow/group node docs","Find Vite bundle audit command","Collect 2-3 production examples using React Flow + dagre"],"risks":["Bundle figure is package-level plus CSS file gzip, not exact app delta"],"verification":["React Flow docs checked","npm registry queried","Bundlephobia API checked","tarball CSS gzip measured","ghgrep/deepwiki examples checked"]} --- timestamp=2026-05-20T18:43:07.481Z status=waiting prompt_hash=1a516f2f229054c3 git_sha=53fae75dca9df98fdcd9cd6d99ba9c498a974294 elapsed_ms=471368 model=openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini backend=openai-codex
close_reason: Researcher delivered: @xyflow/react v12.10.2 stable, ~61kB gzip, custom-node API + dagre LR pattern. Saved to memory.

title: Cleanup: remove spike + legacy renderer + update tests
description:
## PROBLEM
After .1/.2/.3 land, the spike route + dead renderer code + stale tests must be removed so the repo doesn't carry two parallel implementations or out-of-date assertions.

## SUCCESS
- pages/spike/ directory deleted; the /gitboard/spike/reactflow route is removed from App.tsx.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/layout.ts ŌĆö already deleted in .2; verify no stray imports.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/GraphSvg.tsx ŌĆö already deleted in .2; verify no stray imports.
- Helper code that only existed to support layout.ts is gone (e.g. anchorY, routeEdge, computeNodeWidth if no longer used).
- globals.css /* GRAPH BLOCK */ trimmed: cluster-canvas, cluster-svg, g-edge, g-orphans, g-cluster-* selectors that don't render under React Flow removed. Keep tokens (--graph-priority-*, --graph-state-*, --graph-edge-*) that the new node/edge components reference.
- apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/graph/Graph.test.tsx updated to assert React Flow's rendered structure (data-testid hooks on custom nodes, viewport presence, etc.). Existing assertions on .g-cluster / .g-node DOM nodes are replaced or removed.
- bun run lint AND bun run test pass for the dashboard package.
- bun run build:dashboard produces a clean build; bundle audit shows React Flow chunk in the production bundle.

- App.tsx ŌĆö remove spike route
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/ ŌĆö final cleanup pass; ensure no orphaned files
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css ŌĆö trim dead selectors in the GRAPH block
- apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/graph/Graph.test.tsx ŌĆö rewrite assertions

## NON_GOALS
- New visual changes (.3 owns visuals)
- New tests beyond updating existing assertions to React Flow's rendered structure
- Adding e2e Playwright tests (separate bead if desired)
- Changing the graph route URL / shell routing

## CONSTRAINTS

- EDGE_STYLE_VARS (edge-styles.ts) stays as the single source of truth for edge styles ŌĆö referenced by .3's custom edges.

## VALIDATION

- bun run test apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/graph/ ŌåÆ passes.
