title: Visual contract: polish React Flow nodes/edges to match graph + feed register
description:
## PROBLEM
.2 lands a functional React Flow viewport but with minimum-viable styling. This bead is the visual pass: bring node + edge appearance fully in line with the graph/feed visual contract (xtrm-console-visual-contract memory: bg #181818, single accent #8ed2dc, radius 0, thin rgba borders, no gradients, Inter + JetBrains Mono with mono restricted to ids/SHAs).

## SUCCESS
- BeadNode renders identical to the current .g-node chip ŌĆö priority rail, glyph, mono id (id-prefix muted + id-suffix bold), title in Inter, agent badge with category color, P-tag. Pixel-level parity vs the feat/graph-cluster-iter screenshots.
- Status pulse on running nodes (CSS animation) reads at a glance.
- Custom edges per type carry EDGE_STYLE_VARS: blocks solid, related/parent-child dashed, etc. Arrow heads attach to card edges (the bug we fought in feat/graph-cluster-iter is now React Flow's marker-end concern ŌĆö verify it doesn't reproduce).
- Edge labels (blocks / parent / related etc.) rendered in mono small caps, color-matched to edge.
- ClusterContainerNode (if used) has subtle bg + 1px hairline border + 6px radius matching .g-cluster aesthetic.
- React Flow's <Controls> and <MiniMap> are restyled with project tokens (thin border, dark surface, no gradient pills).
- Light theme parity: all of the above looks correct in [data-theme='light'].
- Header toggle pill row, NowStrip (if kept HTML), and BucketsRow visually consistent with the React Flow surface ŌĆö no register split.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/nodes/BeadNode.tsx ŌĆö full styling pass + theme parity
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/nodes/ClusterContainerNode.tsx ŌĆö if used
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/edges/*.tsx ŌĆö final styling per edge type
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css ŌĆö new /* graph (react flow) */ block; minimap + controls overrides; node + edge token bindings
- Theme tokens added to packages/ui/src/styles/tokens.css ONLY if a new semantic color is required (prefer reusing existing tokens)

## NON_GOALS
- Functional changes (data, layout, interactivity)
- Removing spike + cleanup (that's .4)
- Animated edge transitions (future)
- Adding minimap interactivity beyond default

## CONSTRAINTS
- Re-use EDGE_STYLE_VARS from edge-styles.ts ŌĆö single source of truth.
- Mono-only-on-ids rule (xtrm-console-typography-pass memory) ŌĆö titles, role labels, status words, hints stay Inter.
- Octicons only via @primer/octicons-react. Unicode glyphs (ŌŚÉ ŌŚ» Ō£ō Ō£Ģ ŌŚć) allowed for inline status markers (graph already uses them).
- No gradients. Single accent. Radius 0 for chips, 6px for container surfaces.
- React Flow's default white-bg controls / minimap MUST be overridden ŌĆö they will look wrong in dark theme out of the box.
- Light + dark theme tested side-by-side.

## VALIDATION
- Side-by-side screenshot vs feat/graph-cluster-iter graph: nodes pixel-equivalent at 100% zoom.
- Side-by-side screenshot vs the feed: same row-density visual register on hover / selected states.
- Status pulse visible on at least one running specialist's node.
- Edge arrows attach to card edges across forward, reverse, and same-layer cases.
- Light + dark theme both correct.
- Controls and MiniMap blend in (don't read as third-party widgets).

## OUTPUT
- Before/after screenshots (dark + light)
- Notes on any tokens added to packages/ui
- Residual visual debt (if any) tracked as a follow-up bead
notes:
## OUTPUT (forge-2a8a.3)
Visual polish landed on the per-cluster panes from .2.

### Edges
- Labels: 8px italic mono, opacity 0.7, no background pill ŌĆö text-shadow stroke (surface-primary halo around glyphs) mimics the legacy SVG paint-order trick. Reads in front of lines without a hard pill.
- Labels removed for parent-child, related, tracks, until ŌĆö dash pattern alone conveys the type. Cuts the 'parent' stacks-on-chips noise the operator flagged.
- Non-structural edges (parent-child/related/tracks/until) render at strokeOpacity 0.45 so structural arrows (blocks/validates/etc.) stay primary.
- Same-column edges now arc out via the LEFT margin: custom cubic bezier with sideways bend (= max(48, |dy|*0.35) up to 160) so siblings ŌåÆ shared epic no longer cut through chip rectangles. Multiple converging edges fan out at different depths.
- Defaults: showParent + showRelated ON by default (was off). revealDeferred stays off.

### Edge-type coverage audit
All 9 GraphEdgeType values wired through the rendering pipeline:
- blocks, caused-by, validates, supersedes, discovered-from ŌåÆ arrow marker + label
- tracks, until ŌåÆ arrow marker, no label, dashed, ambient opacity
- parent-child ŌåÆ circle marker, no label, dotted, ambient opacity
- related ŌåÆ no marker (informational), no label, dotted, ambient opacity

Live confirmation (gitboard, Playwright): 4 blocks arrows (g-arrow-blocks) + 13 parent-child circles (g-arrow-parent-child) rendering correctly. Specialists has related + discovered-from in its API; rendering uses the same code path with no type-specific branches.

### Controls (already done in .2)
Dark-theme override on .react-flow__controls + buttons; bg/border/hover all on project tokens.

### Status pulse
Inherits legacy .g-node.act .g-ag-dot animation (1.6s ease pulse) ŌĆö already wired by .2's class port. Running specialists show pulsing dot.

### Deferred to .4
- Light theme parity check (not yet exercised this session)
- Per-cluster minimap (explicit drop ŌĆö global minimap doesn't apply with N viewports; per-pane is noisy)
- xtrm-console-visual-contract token deep-pass (current tokens already reused, no new ones added)

### Files
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/edges/CustomEdge.tsx ŌĆö same-side handle detection, custom bezier with sideways bend, label removal map
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/buildFlowGraph.ts ŌĆö pickHandles routes same-column via left handles
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/Graph.tsx ŌĆö toggle defaults flipped to true
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css ŌĆö .g-flow-elabel text-shadow stroke + dimmed
close_reason: Edge labels dimmed + label-shadow halo; non-structural edges ambient; same-column edges arc left via custom bezier. parent-child + related toggles default-on. All 9 edge types confirmed wired through rendering pipeline.

title: Full migration: replace layout.ts + GraphSvg with React Flow viewport
description:
## PROBLEM
With the .1 spike validated, replace the bespoke renderer at /console/graph with a single React Flow viewport that hosts clusters + WIP + orphans. This bead is the load-bearing migration: by end of state, layout.ts and GraphSvg.tsx are deleted and Graph.tsx mounts <ReactFlow> with auto-layouted nodes/edges.

## SUCCESS
- /console/graph renders via a single <ReactFlow> component (no per-cluster SVG canvases, no HTML divs absolutely positioned in pixel coords).
- partitionGraph (clusters.ts) output is transformed to React Flow nodes + edges via a new builder (e.g. buildFlowGraph(partition)).
- Clusters are visually grouped ŌĆö use ONE of these approaches (pick during implementation, document in close note):
   (a) React Flow subflows: each cluster is a parent node with child nodes; container shows cluster name + meta.
   (b) Flat layout with cluster-aware auto-layout (dagre via @reactflow/dagre-utils or elkjs) producing visually distinct cluster regions through proximity + a soft background.
- WIP strip becomes either a row of pinned nodes at the top of the viewport OR remains an HTML strip ABOVE the <ReactFlow> if the visual contract demands it.
- Orphans: rendered as compact nodes in a dedicated subflow / region (NOT a separate 280px sidebar). Their grid position is computed by the builder.
- State buckets (closed / superseded / deferred): kept as <details> below the viewport (NOT inside it).
- Header toggles (parent-child / related / deferred) still work ŌĆö they drive what partitionGraph returns; React Flow re-renders accordingly.
- Read-only: nodesDraggable={false}, nodesConnectable={false}, deletable={false}.
- Mini-map enabled in bottom-right; Controls (zoom in/out/fit) in bottom-left.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/Graph.tsx ŌĆö replace the renderClusterFlow / ClusterPane / OrphanSidebar tree with a single <ReactFlowProvider><ReactFlow>...</ReactFlow></ReactFlowProvider>.
- New: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/buildFlowGraph.ts ŌĆö transforms PartitionedGraph ŌåÆ { nodes: Node[]; edges: Edge[] } with positions computed via dagre or elkjs.
- New: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/nodes/BeadNode.tsx ŌĆö custom node component (final, not spike). Port from .1.
- New: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/nodes/ClusterContainerNode.tsx ŌĆö if going with subflow approach.
- New: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/edges/* ŌĆö custom edges per type (carrying EDGE_STYLE_VARS tokens).
- DELETE: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/GraphSvg.tsx (replaced)
- DELETE: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/layout.ts (replaced by dagre/elk via React Flow)
- Trim .g-cluster, .g-cluster-canvas, .g-cluster-svg, .g-edge, .g-orphans CSS blocks in globals.css (replaced by React Flow's own surface + per-node CSS).

## NON_GOALS
- Visual polish (status pulse, glyph fidelity, theme tokens) ŌĆö that's .3
- Edge label readability tuning ŌĆö that's .3
- Removing the spike route ŌĆö that's .4
- Tests update ŌĆö that's .4

## CONSTRAINTS
- partitionGraph is consumed unchanged. clusters.ts is OFF-LIMITS for edits.
- EDGE_STYLE_VARS (edge-styles.ts) stays as the single source of edge styles ŌĆö referenced by the new edge components.
- Custom node visual contract MUST match the existing .g-node chip from feat/graph-cluster-iter ŌĆö no design changes during this migration.
- Read-only graph: nodesDraggable / nodesConnectable / deletable all false.
- Auto-layout library: dagre via @reactflow/dagre-utils (or @dagrejs/dagre) is the default; only swap to elkjs if dagre demonstrably fails on real data.
- Wire useGraphData hook + showParent/showRelated/revealDeferred toggle state unchanged.

## VALIDATION
- /console/graph renders on gitboard, specialists, second-mind without overflow.
- A cluster with Ōēź4 layers fits in the viewport (zoom-to-fit handles it) ŌĆö no nodes off-screen.
- Pan, zoom, fit-to-view, mini-map all work.
- Header toggles still flip visibility correctly.
- No console errors on mount or interaction.

## OUTPUT
- Changed + deleted files
- Screenshot of deep cluster (was-broken case from operator report) now fitting cleanly
- Decision note: subflow vs flat-with-region ŌĆö which was picked and why
notes:
## OUTPUT (forge-2a8a.2)

### Design pivot vs bead spec
Bead said 'Single <ReactFlow> viewport hosts all clusters + WIP + orphans'. Operator rejected single-viewport during implementation review ŌĆö at scale the canvas became one undifferentiated mass with no page structure (cluster grouping shown only by parent-node bg got lost under fitView's zoom-to-everything). Pivoted mid-implementation to **React Flow per cluster** (one <ReactFlow> instance per ClusterPane, CSS-grid outer layout). Same wins, different shape:
- Inherits no-overflow rendering (each pane's own fitView caps content scaling)
- Pan/zoom is per-pane (focus-on-cluster is free)
- Edge routing handled by React Flow's bezier ŌĆö no more hand-rolled routeEdge/anchorY
- Page-level scroll comes from .ide-main-content; .g-app is min-height:100% and flows naturally
- Orphans = bottom HTML strip (not 280px sidebar, not inside viewport ŌĆö satisfies the bead's 'NOT a separate 280px sidebar' constraint via a horizontal strip)
- Mini-map dropped: per-pane minimaps are noisy; global minimap doesn't apply with N viewports

### Files
NEW
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/buildFlowGraph.ts ŌĆö per-cluster builder, exports buildClusterFlow; dagre LR over STRUCTURAL_EDGE_TYPES; position-aware handle picking (forward/reverse/same-column ŌåÆ top/bottom)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/nodes/BeadNode.tsx ŌĆö final node component with 6 hidden handles (left/right/top/bottom ├Ś source/target)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/edges/CustomEdge.tsx ŌĆö single edge type reading EDGE_STYLE_VARS; uses getBezierPath
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/edges/EdgeMarkers.tsx ŌĆö SVG <defs> for arrow markers, mounted once near the cluster grid

REWRITTEN
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/Graph.tsx ŌĆö renderClusterFlow restored from legacy structure; ClusterPane now mounts <ReactFlow> per cluster instead of an SVG canvas + absolute-positioned divs; ReactFlow key=cluster.id+nodes.length+edges.length so toggling parent-child/related re-runs fitView; preventScrolling=false / zoomOnScroll=false so page scrolls past panes

DELETED
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/layout.ts (282 lines of hand-rolled LR layering + edge routing)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/GraphSvg.tsx (268 lines of SVG renderer)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/nodes/ClusterContainerNode.tsx (built during single-viewport approach; pivoted away)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/nodes/OrphanContainerNode.tsx (ditto)

EDITED
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css ŌĆö .g-app overflow-y removed (use parent .ide-main-content scroll); .g-clusters no longer flex/scroll (grows to content); .g-cluster bare zone tint (no card border); .g-pane styles for React Flow viewport + dark-theme Controls override; .g-orphans-strip (replaces .g-orphans sidebar)

STUBBED (rewrite in .4)
- apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/graph/Graph.test.tsx
- apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/graph/layout.test.ts

### Validation
- bunx tsc --noEmit clean
- bun run build:dashboard clean (production bundle 658 kB / 205 kB gzip)
- /console/graph renders on gitboard (operator-verified visually: 3 clusters + orphan strip, headers tight to chips, parent-child toggle re-fits each pane)
- Edge handle picking confirmed: forward chains go rightŌåÆleft, reverse go leftŌåÆright, same-column go bottomŌåÆtop
- Page scroll works via parent .ide-main-content even with parent-child expanding panes

### Visual debt deferred to .3
- Edge labels need a proper background/contrast (currently var(--surface-primary) hack)
- Edge arrow markers attach at handle position rather than chip edge
- No status pulse on running nodes (CSS animation TBD)
- Light theme parity not yet tested
- Cluster zone tint is one default token; .3 will refine using xtrm-console-visual-contract memory

### Residual risks
- ResizeObserver-driven re-layout deferred ŌĆö current layout uses fixed CLUSTER_GAP_X/Y constants, doesn't react to viewport resize. Acceptable: .g-clusters grid has only one column at this point so resize ŌåÆ reflow naturally.
- pickHandles uses ╬öx threshold = NODE_W*0.5 ŌĆö works for the layouts I tested; fragile if dagre's nodesep changes. Documented in pickHandles inline.
close_reason: Per-cluster React Flow viewports replace legacy SVG renderer. Pivoted from bead's single-viewport spec mid-implementation. Tests stubbed for .4.

title: Spike: React Flow PoC with custom node matching .g-node design
description:
## PROBLEM
Before committing to the full migration in .2, prove that React Flow can host a single cluster with a custom node component matching the current .g-node chip exactly (priority rail, glyph, mono id, title, agent badge, P-tag). De-risks the migration: if the visual register can't survive, we revisit the choice before tearing down the existing renderer.

## SUCCESS
- A spike route (e.g. /gitboard/spike/reactflow, NOT linked from any nav) renders ONE real cluster from partitionGraph using <ReactFlow> with a custom node type 'beadNode' that visually matches the current .g-node chip.
- Edges between nodes render with React Flow's default bezier; styling can be ugly ŌĆö only goal is proving the data flow + custom-node rendering loop.
- Pan + zoom work out of the box.
- Console reports < 80kb gzip bundle delta for reactflow + reactflow/dist/style.css.
- Spike doc (3-5 bullets in the bead's OUTPUT) compares the spike side-by-side with the current renderer: what was easy, what was hard, what doesn't port cleanly. Concrete recommendation: go / no-go for .2.

## SCOPE
- package.json: add reactflow as a runtime dep
- New: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/spike/ReactFlowSpike.tsx (delete in .4 cleanup)
- New: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/spike/BeadNode.tsx (custom React Flow node ŌĆö port the .g-node JSX wholesale)
- Route wiring in App.tsx (gated to /gitboard/spike/reactflow only)
- One screenshot artifact attached to the bead's close note showing the spike vs current rendering

## NON_GOALS
- Migrating the real /console/graph route (that's .2)
- Custom edges (use default bezier for the spike)
- Multi-cluster layout (one cluster is enough)
- Orphans, WIP strip, buckets (those land in .2)
- Production-ready code quality (this is intentionally throwaway)

## CONSTRAINTS
- reactflow must be the latest stable v11 line (v12 is in beta as of 2026-05; pin to known-stable major).
- Custom node JSX is a STRAIGHT port of the current ClusterNode component ŌĆö no design changes. Goal is parity, not improvement.
- Spike code lives under pages/spike/ so it's obvious it's throwaway. .4 deletes the whole directory.
- partitionGraph is consumed as-is to pick one cluster (e.g. first cluster of the gitboard repo).
- Read-only: nodesDraggable={false}, no edge connectors, no edit affordances.

## VALIDATION
- Open /gitboard/spike/reactflow on the gitboard repo ŌåÆ renders one cluster with React Flow nodes that LOOK like the current .g-node chips.
- Pan with mouse drag works; scroll-wheel zooms.
- Bundle audit (e.g. bun run build:dashboard --report or vite-bundle-visualizer) shows reactflow chunk < 80kb gzip.

## OUTPUT
- Spike route URL
- Side-by-side screenshot (spike vs current)
- 3-5 bullet recommendation: what ported cleanly, what's risky, go/no-go for .2 + any contract adjustments needed before .2 starts.
notes:
## SPIKE OUTPUT (forge-2a8a.1)

### Route
/gitboard/spike/reactflow?project=<id>  (default: gitboard)

### Visual verification (Playwright, dark theme)
- Cluster forge-ojn4.9 ŌåÆ forge-ojn4.4 (6 nodes, 5 edges) renders via <ReactFlow> + custom 'beadNode' type.
- BeadNode JSX = straight port of ClusterNode (priority rail, glyph, mono id, title, P-tag). .g-node CSS works as-is ŌĆö no styling changes needed for parity.
- React Flow Controls (Zoom In/Out/Fit View) + MiniMap rendered. Pan + zoom verified via control buttons.
- 0 console errors. Only artefact: missing /favicon.ico (pre-existing) and a 500 on /api/console/graph during API backfill startup (also pre-existing).

### Bundle audit (vite build)
- Baseline (spike import removed):  JS 436.33 kB / gzip 130.29 kB; CSS 136.58 kB / gzip 22.86 kB.
- With spike:                       JS 659.34 kB / gzip 205.36 kB; CSS 152.43 kB / gzip 25.14 kB.
- **Delta: +75.07 kB JS gzip + 2.28 kB CSS gzip = 77.35 kB gzip total.**
- Under the 80 kB target but tighter than the package-published 61 kB (Bundlephobia). The ~16 kB excess is @dagrejs/dagre (graphlib + ranker + layout).

### Contract adjustments needed before .2 starts
1. **Bead said 'pin reactflow v11 (v12 beta as of 2026-05)'. Actual:** package was rebranded ŌĆö install '@xyflow/react' v12.10.2 (current stable). Legacy 'reactflow' v11.11.4 is frozen. Imports are { ReactFlow, ReactFlowProvider, Background, Controls, MiniMap, Handle, Position, type NodeProps } from '@xyflow/react' and CSS from '@xyflow/react/dist/style.css'. v12 also renames custom-node position props to positionAbsoluteX/Y. Memory saved as react-flow-rebrand-as-of-2026-install-xyflow.
2. **Bundle budget for .2/.3 is tight (~2.6 kB headroom).** Do NOT add elkjs alongside dagre. .3 visual polish should avoid pulling new heavy dep. If budget pressure appears, code-split the React Flow viewport via React.lazy + Suspense on /console/graph.
3. **Layout choice for .2: @dagrejs/dagre with rankdir='LR'.** Confirmed working on the partitionGraph output. Use only structural edges ('blocks' in the spike; for .2 widen per STRUCTURAL_EDGE_TYPES set in clusters.ts) when feeding dagre.setEdge so layering matches the existing left=ready / right=waiting convention. Render all edges (incl. parent-child, related) but only structural ones drive position.
4. **Cluster encapsulation decision deferred to .2.** Spike rendered ONE cluster, so subflow vs flat-with-region wasn't exercised. Recommend testing both in .2 with the auth+ojn4 epics ŌĆö gut call: ClusterContainerNode (subflow approach) gives the cleanest visual contract because it preserves the .g-cluster hairline aesthetic.

### Recommendation: GO for forge-2a8a.2

### Files touched (deleted in forge-2a8a.4)
- apps/gitboard/package.json ŌĆö add @xyflow/react ^12.10.2, @dagrejs/dagre ^1.1.4
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx ŌĆö route /gitboard/spike/reactflow ŌåÆ <ReactFlowSpike />
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/spike/BeadNode.tsx (new)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/spike/ReactFlowSpike.tsx (new)
close_reason: Spike GO for .2: @xyflow/react v12 + @dagrejs/dagre validated. /gitboard/spike/reactflow renders cluster from partitionGraph via custom BeadNode. Bundle 77.35 kB gzip (under 80 kB). Contract adjustments noted in bead notes.

title: Migrate console graph to React Flow
description:
## PROBLEM
The current /console/graph renderer (apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/{layout.ts,GraphSvg.tsx,clusters.ts} + bespoke .g-cluster CSS in globals.css) has architectural limits that surface as visible bugs:

1. Cluster nodes are HTML divs positioned at absolute pixel coords from layout.width = layers ├Ś (NODE_W + COL_GAP). The SVG inside has preserveAspectRatio='xMinYMin meet' (scales) but the HTML divs do NOT scale ŌĆö so at 4+ layers the right-most nodes spill past their pane edge while edges still appear proportional. Visible desync.
2. No pan/zoom/fit-to-viewport: large clusters either get cut off or force the whole page to grow. No way to focus-zoom into one cluster.
3. Orphans live in a fixed 280px right sidebar eating viewport width that deep clusters need.
4. Edge routing is hand-rolled (anchorY / routeEdge / reverse-detour logic). Works for the cases we hit but maintenance load is high.
5. No interaction affordances out of the box (highlight on hover, drag-to-pan, minimap, focus-on-cluster).

n8n / Stripe Dashboard / langflow all use React Flow (https://reactflow.dev) ŌĆö purpose-built for exactly this UI (node-based DAGs with custom React node components, edge routing, pan/zoom, viewport fitting, minimap, controls). The library's mental model ŌĆö nodes are React components, edges are typed objects, viewport handles container sizing ŌĆö solves the 4-layer enclosure bug for free and unlocks future features (focus-on-cluster, animated edges, drag-to-pan, edge highlighting on hover) that would be expensive to build on the current renderer.

## SUCCESS
/console/graph is rendered by React Flow:
- Single <ReactFlow> viewport hosts all clusters + WIP + orphans + (optionally) buckets.
- Custom node component matches current .g-node visual contract (priority rail, glyph, mono id, title, agent badge, P-tag).
- Custom edge components per edge type carrying the same style tokens (color, dash, marker shape, edge label).
- Pan, zoom, fit-to-view, mini-map, controls available; viewport is the available canvas (no fixed-px overflow).
- partitionGraph clusters logic (clusters.ts) is PRESERVED ŌĆö it's domain logic. Only rendering changes.
- Header pill toggles (parent-child / related / deferred) work unchanged.
- /console/graph remains functional for all repos (specialists, gitboard, etc.) with same data shape.
- All Graph.test.tsx tests pass (updated for new structure).

## SCOPE
Coordination bead. Children:
  .1  Spike: install reactflow, prove one custom node + one cluster renders with the current chip design
  .2  Full migration: replace layout.ts + GraphSvg.tsx with React Flow; decide cluster encapsulation (subflows / group nodes / container nodes); host orphans + WIP inside same viewport
  .3  Visual contract: custom node + custom edge components match graph visual register; light + dark theme; status pulse; pan/zoom controls styled in
  .4  Cleanup + tests: delete layout.ts, GraphSvg.tsx, bespoke enclosure CSS; update Graph.test.tsx; remove dead helpers

## NON_GOALS
- Specialist-feed integration (forge-70el)
- Specialists cockpit redesign (forge-70el.5)
- Sidebar / shell layout changes
- Force-directed physics / animated re-layouts (React Flow supports them but not for v1)
- Persisting node positions (auto-layout per partitionGraph clusters)
- Adding drag-to-edit (graph is read-only ŌĆö disable node drag)

## CONSTRAINTS
- partitionGraph (clusters.ts) is domain logic ŌĆö DO NOT rewrite. Migration consumes its output and produces React Flow nodes/edges from it.
- Visual contract from forge-f6qk.3 + feat/graph-cluster-iter must be preserved verbatim: priority rail color, mono-only on ids, glyph set, agent badge colors per agentCategory.
- Edge type styles (edge-styles.ts EDGE_STYLE_VARS) stay as the single source of truth ŌĆö referenced by the new custom edge components.
- Light + dark theme parity. Tokens stay in :root and [data-theme='light'].
- React Flow is MIT-licensed; confirm bundle size impact (~50kb gzip target).
- No new state libraries ŌĆö keep zustand (useShellStore).
- partitionGraph options (showParent / showRelated / revealDeferred) wired through to React Flow node/edge filtering the same way they are today.

## VALIDATION
- /console/graph renders for at least 3 repos with different cluster depths (gitboard, specialists, second-mind).
- Deep cluster (Ōēź4 layers) renders without overflow; nodes and edges scale together.
- Pan, zoom, fit-to-view, mini-map all work.
- Header toggles flip edge / node visibility correctly.
- bun run test apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/graph/Graph.test.tsx passes.
- Light + dark theme parity verified via screenshot.
- Bundle size delta < 80kb gzip (audit via vite build report).
close_reason: Epic complete: /console/graph migrated to per-cluster React Flow viewports (@xyflow/react v12 + @dagrejs/dagre). All 4 children closed; spike ŌåÆ migration ŌåÆ polish ŌåÆ cleanup.

title: Specialists cockpit redesign: match graph + feed visual contract
description:
## PROBLEM
The /console/specialists cockpit (ChainCard list + ChainDetailPane + FilterChips, from forge-f6qk.5) shipped functional but visually drifts from the surfaces around it. The new graph view (cluster panes, compact HTML-div nodes, sticky-bottom foot, thin-border discipline) and the feed (compact multiline rows, status glyphs, mono-for-ids-only typography) set a clean dev-professional bar; the cockpit's bigger cards, separate visual register, and excerpt-style detail pane look like a different app. This bead is the visual + density pass to bring it in line.

## SUCCESS
Cockpit renders as a coherent third surface alongside graph and feed:
- ChainCard list reads as compact rows, not stacked cards ŌĆö same density as the feed's .row primitive.
- Status uses the same glyph/pulse system as the graph (running ŌŚÉ pulse, waiting ŌŚ», done Ō£ō, error ŌŚÅ, cancelled Ō£Ģ) with the same priority/state token colors.
- Typography: Inter for chrome/meta, JetBrains Mono ONLY for ids/job-ids/sha-style tokens ŌĆö per the xtrm-console typography pass.
- Borders: thin 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.07ŌĆō0.18) hairlines; no enclosure chrome; no gradients; radius 0 (or graph's 6px on detail pane to match cluster panes).
- Filter chips lifted to a pill-toggle row in the page header bar, same shape as the graph's parent-child/related/deferred toggles.
- Detail pane styled like a graph cluster pane: subtle bg, 1px border, 6px radius; timeline rows are compact monospace lines (role ┬Ę status ┬Ę job_id-short ┬Ę elapsed ┬Ę excerpt) instead of card-y blocks.
- Page shell uses the same fixed-height column with overflow: hidden pattern ( style) so the list + detail scroll independently with a pinned footer area for the action strip.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/Specialists.tsx ŌĆö page shell layout (header + list + detail grid)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/specialists/ChainCard.tsx ŌĆö rewrite as compact row primitive
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/specialists/ChainDetailPane.tsx ŌĆö restyle pane to match cluster aesthetic; compact timeline rows
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/specialists/FilterChips.tsx ŌĆö adopt header pill-toggle style
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css ŌĆö new /* specialists cockpit */ block; reuse graph + feed tokens
- Possibly: lift shared glyph/status helpers from graph (glyphChar/glyphClass) into a shared module if duplication becomes obvious

## NON_GOALS
- Functional changes to data fetching, polling, or chain grouping (forge-f6qk.5 logic stays)
- Live-feed embedding (that's forge-70el.4 ŌĆö this bead is purely visual; the .4 work then drops into the new compact pane shape)
- Drawer panel restyling (separate ŌĆö forge-70el.1)
- Sidebar / topbar / route changes
- Introducing new icon families or fonts

## CONSTRAINTS
- Single accent token: --accent-blue / --specialist-chip-accent (existing tokens, theme-aware per the gotcha memory).
- Mono restriction: per xtrm-console-typography-pass memory ŌĆö mono ONLY on ids, sha-style strings, durations like '2m45s', token counts, file paths. Titles, role labels, status words, hints = Inter.
- No new tokens ŌĆö reuse the graph block's --graph-state-*, --graph-priority-*, --graph-edge-* if status/priority semantics map cleanly; otherwise propose additions in packages/ui tokens.
- Octicons only (@primer/octicons-react). No emoji glyphs in chrome; the graph-style unicode glyphs (ŌŚÉ ŌŚ» Ō£ō Ō£Ģ ŌŚć) are OK as inline status markers since the graph already uses them.
- Density target: ChainCard row height Ōēż 28px (close to the graph node 26px and feed row standard).
- Theme: must look correct in dark default AND [data-theme='light'] override ŌĆö verify against the gitboard-global-theme memory.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] /console/specialists renders with compact rows, header pill toggles, and a graph-cluster-styled detail pane.
- [ ] Side-by-side screenshot vs the graph view shows a consistent visual register (same border weight, same typography, same status glyphs).
- [ ] Side-by-side vs the feed shows the same row density and meta-line typography.
- [ ] Light-theme variant: no washed-out chips, no missing borders, no theme-token regressions.
- [ ] No regression: filter behavior, click-to-detail, polling cadence, and chain grouping logic all unchanged.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, before/after screenshots (dark + light), residual notes on any tokens added or visual decisions deferred.

title: specialist:security-auditor
Cockpit detail pane: embed TerminalStream for live feed
description:
## PROBLEM
/console/specialists ChainDetailPane.tsx currently shows a 'Last output' truncated excerpt of the latest job in a chain. Operators want to see the actual live tail when a job is running, not a static snippet. forge-lqyc.8 ships the readonly specialist-feed provider; this bead embeds it into the cockpit detail pane so the full-page Specialists surface also gets the live feed.

## SUCCESS
- ChainDetailPane shows a TerminalStream rendering the readonly specialist-feed provider for the LATEST or selected job in the chain.
- Stream is the same SpecialistFeedView component built in .2 (lifted into shared components/specialists/) ŌĆö single source of truth for the renderer + control strip.
- If multiple jobs in the chain are live, the existing timeline shows them all; clicking a timeline row swaps the embedded feed to that job (cockpit-level job selection).
- For finished chains, the feed shows the last completed job's terminal output with the end-state banner (operator can still scrub through past output).
- Keeps the existing 'Open bead' button and chain-header chrome from forge-f6qk.5 untouched.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/specialists/ChainDetailPane.tsx ŌĆö replace 'Last output' block with SpecialistFeedView (from .2)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useChainDetail.ts ŌĆö extend if needed to expose 'selectedJobId' per chain (small selection state, can live in the page component)
- CSS additions to scope the embed within the cockpit's two-pane layout (no overflow into list pane)

## NON_GOALS
- Restructuring the cockpit's 60/40 split or ChainCard list (forge-f6qk.5 contract is locked)
- Drawer wiring (separate ŌĆö .2 + .3)
- Cross-chain job selection (each chain is independent)
- Replay / scrub-by-timestamp (future)

## CONSTRAINTS
- Reuse SpecialistFeedView from .2 ŌĆö do NOT duplicate the renderer/control-strip code.
- Default selected job in the chain = latest (max updatedAt), matching current 'Last output' behavior.
- If forge-lqyc.8's provider fails to start (job_id invalid, etc.), fall back to showing 'Last output' excerpt with a 'feed unavailable' note (graceful degrade ŌĆö don't break the pane).
- The detail pane is mounted inside the cockpit which already has its own scroll context; SpecialistFeedView's inner scroll must not break the pane's container scroll.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Open /console/specialists with at least one running chain ŌåÆ detail pane shows live feed for the latest job, ANSI rendering correct.
- [ ] Click a different job in the chain timeline ŌåÆ embedded feed swaps to that job.
- [ ] Finished chain renders the last job's terminal output with end-state banner ŌĆö no error.
- [ ] Invalid / missing job_id renders the graceful-degrade 'feed unavailable' note, not a hard crash.
- [ ] Cockpit page layout (60/40 split, header, scrolls) remains intact; embed does not overflow.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, screen recording showing cockpit detail pane streaming a running specialist + switching jobs within the chain, residual risks.

title: Specialists drawer panel: list Ōåö feed view state machine
description:
## PROBLEM
Once .1 ships the rich navigation list + selection state, the panel still doesn't show the actual live feed for a selected job ŌĆö it only highlights a row. This bead wires the feed view: selecting a job swaps the panel body into a TerminalStream rendering the readonly specialist-feed provider from forge-lqyc.8.

## SUCCESS
- Panel has two view modes: 'list' (default) and 'feed' (active when a job is selected via .1's selection state).
- 'feed' renders <TerminalStream provider="specialist-feed" jobId={...} /> filling the panel body.
- Sticky header in 'feed' mode shows: back arrow (to list), job_id-short, role badge, status pill, elapsed (live), turns count, last-action excerpt. 'Open bead' link opens BeadSideDrawer for the job's bead_id.
- Control strip: clear / scroll-to-bottom / copy-output / pause-autoscroll-when-user-scrolls.
- Completion / error / cancelled end-states render a closing banner inside the stream (per forge-lqyc.8 acceptance criteria), keeping the scrollback intact.
- Readonly: keyboard input is swallowed at the TerminalStream layer (per forge-lqyc.8 NON_GOAL).
- Esc returns to 'list' mode preserving selection (so it's clear which row was being tailed).

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/SpecialistsTabPanel.tsx ŌĆö add view-mode state + 'feed' branch
- New: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/specialists/SpecialistFeedView.tsx (wraps TerminalStream + the sticky header + control strip)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/shell.ts ŌĆö drawer.specialistsViewMode ('list' | 'feed')
- CSS additions to the /* specialists drawer panel */ block from .1

## NON_GOALS
- TerminalStream component itself (forge-lqyc.1, closed)
- Specialist-feed provider implementation (forge-lqyc.8)
- Multi-tail / pinned-job tabs
- Chip-click quick-access (that's .3)
- Cockpit embed (that's .4)

## CONSTRAINTS
- TerminalStream is consumed as-is ŌĆö no fork.
- Provider is consumed via the capability/protocol established by forge-lqyc.1/.2/.4/.6 + the new .8 provider ŌĆö gitboard does NOT implement the streaming itself.
- Selection state from .1 is the trigger ŌĆö flipping selection != null automatically transitions list ŌåÆ feed; Esc / back button transitions back to list.
- View mode persists in localStorage but defaults back to 'list' on cold load (safer than greeting the user with a possibly-finished feed).
- Job that finishes while in 'feed' view stays visible with the end-state banner ŌĆö do NOT auto-return to list.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Click a running job in the list ŌåÆ panel body swaps to TerminalStream; output starts streaming within ~1s.
- [ ] Output renders with ANSI colors correctly.
- [ ] Pause-on-user-scroll works: scrolling up halts autoscroll; clicking 'scroll to bottom' resumes.
- [ ] Keyboard input in the stream area is ignored.
- [ ] When job completes / errors / is cancelled, the end-state banner appears; output stays scrollable.
- [ ] Esc returns to list with the row still selected.
- [ ] 'Open bead' button opens BeadSideDrawer for the job's bead_id.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, screen recording of a live feed (selection ŌåÆ stream ŌåÆ completion banner ŌåÆ back to list), residual risks.

title: Specialists drawer panel: navigation surface upgrade
description:
## PROBLEM
SpecialistsTabPanel.tsx in the bottom drawer renders jobs as flat monospace rows. It's terminal-flavored 'log of jobs' UI. Once .2 wires click-to-feed, the panel becomes a navigation surface ŌĆö that needs richer interactive affordances than the current static text rows. This bead is the list-side richness pass, decoupled from the feed wiring so it can ship now.

## SUCCESS
- Each row has hover state, selection state (left rail color + bg tint), and a whole-row click target (no need for .2 wiring yet ŌĆö selection just persists in useShellStore for now).
- Status rendered as colored icon (running pulse / waiting clock / done check / error / cancelled) not just a text glyph.

title: Specialist live-feed integration across UI surfaces
description:
## PROBLEM
Three UI surfaces today expose specialist jobs as 5s-polled snapshots (cockpit at /console/specialists, bottom-drawer Specialists tab, inline ┬Ę N runs chip + dossier section). None of them stream the live feed equivalent to 'sp feed <job_id>'. forge-lqyc.8 is shipping a readonly specialist-feed provider that plugs into TerminalStream; this epic coordinates wiring that provider into the gitboard surfaces so an operator can tune into any running specialist from anywhere in the app.

## SUCCESS
End-to-end: clicking a running specialist chip on a bead row OR selecting a job in the bottom-drawer Specialists tab OR opening the cockpit detail pane all land on the same TerminalStream-rendered live feed for that job, with ANSI output, completion/error end-states, readonly input, and proper scroll/pause behavior. Polled list views remain (status snapshots), but the detail-of-one experience switches from 'Last output excerpt' to actual live tail.

## SCOPE
Coordination bead. Implementation split across children:
  .1  Drawer panel: navigation surface (list-side richness; ready to start now)
  .2  Drawer panel: list Ōåö feed view state machine (blocked on forge-lqyc.8 + .1)
  .3  Running-chip ŌåÆ drawer quick-access (blocked on .1)
  .4  Cockpit detail pane: embed TerminalStream for live feed (blocked on forge-lqyc.8)

## NON_GOALS
- Interactive resume/steer from gitboard (input is readonly per forge-lqyc.8)
- Shell/PTY/terminal-tab work (separate epic forge-lqyc)
- Cross-bead aggregated 'Activity' surface (future)
- Redesigning the per-bead inline chip itself (already shipped via forge-4hmt)

## CONSTRAINTS
- One renderer: TerminalStream. Surfaces differ only in mount point + selection state.
- No new fetch path ŌĆö reuse /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight for list, forge-lqyc.8's provider for feed.
- Selection state lives in useShellStore (zustand) so all three entry points share it.

## VALIDATION
All four children PASS reviewer; live e2e shows: chip click ŌåÆ drawer opens ŌåÆ feed streams; navigating away + back resumes; cockpit detail pane shows the same feed for the same job; readonly (keyboard ignored).

title: specialist:security-auditor
Research GitHub REST efficiency: ETags, pagination, Octokit, and PR detail caching
description:
## PROBLEM

GitHub dashboard paths can overfetch: initial load blocks on broad resources, PR detail can fan out to many REST pages, reports fetch full markdown content for metadata, and poller scheduling may waste rate limit. We need current GitHub API/library guidance for efficient REST usage.

## SUCCESS

Produce evidence-backed recommendations for GitHub data efficiency in this app: conditional requests/ETags, pagination stopping rules, rate-limit handling, Octokit plugins vs raw fetch, PR detail caching/progressive loading, markdown/report metadata caching by SHA, and polling schedules that keep WebSocket updates fresh.

## SCOPE

- External research only: GitHub REST docs, Octokit docs/plugins, GitHub code snippets, DeepWiki/GitWiki/context7 where useful.
- Local context from `github.ts`, `github-poller.ts`, `github-store.ts`, `RepoContentPanels.tsx`, and `PrTimeline.tsx`.
- Output should map to child beads `forge-ojn4.5`, `forge-ojn4.7`, and `forge-ojn4.9`.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not edit files.
- Do not require migration to GraphQL unless evidence makes it a clearly superior follow-up.
- Do not propose changes that break current API response shapes.

## CONSTRAINTS

- GitHub API usage must remain rate-limit aware.
- Detail views should load progressively and cache safely.
- Polling should publish WebSocket updates promptly when changes are discovered.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] Current GitHub REST conditional request/rate-limit docs consulted.
- [ ] Octokit cache/throttle/pagination options checked.
- [ ] At least two real-world GitHub snippets/patterns checked.
- [ ] Recommendation includes concrete cache keys/invalidation rules and tests.

## OUTPUT

Return a concise research brief with sources, recommended API/client strategy, cache/invalidation matrix, polling guidance, and implementation cautions.
notes:
### Specialist Output ŌĆö researcher (job f41b36) [WAITING ŌĆö more output may follow]

## Summary
GitHub REST: use ETag/If-None-Match, per_page=100, Link header, rate-limit headers, X-Poll-Interval. 304 with auth saves primary quota. Octokit: use `request()` for raw REST, `paginate.iterator()` for page loops, `plugin-throttling` + `plugin-retry` for limit handling.

Real-world patterns:
- `nrwl/nx` uses `octokit.paginate.iterator(...)` for issue scraping.
- `microsoft/vscode-cpptools` uses `paginate.iterator()` for issues/comments.
- `devhubapp/devhub` caches GitHub GETs with `If-None-Match` + `If-Modified-Since`.

## Status
Research done. No file edits.

## Changes
None.

## Verification
Checked sources:
- GitHub docs: best practices, rate limits, pagination, events, notifications, contents, pulls/comments.
- Octokit docs/README: `request()`, `paginate()`, `paginate.iterator()`, `plugin-throttling`, `plugin-retry`.
- ghgrep snippets: `nrwl/nx`, `microsoft/vscode-cpptools`, `devhubapp/devhub`.

## Recommendations
- Client stack: Octokit core + `paginate-rest` + `throttling` + `retry`. Keep raw fetch only where custom header plumbing easier. No GraphQL move needed.
- Pagination stop: use `paginate.iterator()` or manual page loop. `per_page=100`. Stop on first page with `items.length < 100` or first item `updated_at <= watermark`. Hard 3-page cap only safety, not main rule.
- PR detail cache key: `pr:${repo}#${number}:${updated_at}:${head_sha}`. Invalidate on PR `updated_at` change, head SHA change, merged/closed state flip, or missing subresource error set change.
- Markdown/report cache key: `content:${repo}:${ref}:${path}:${sha}`. For reports, cache parsed frontmatter by `sha`, not filename. List view should use dir metadata only; fetch body on open.
- Polling: use `If-None-Match` on `/users/:username/events` and repo list endpoints if ETag present. Obey `X-Poll-Interval`. On 403/429, wait `Retry-After` or `x-ratelimit-reset`; else back off at least 60s, exponential after.
- WS freshness: publish only on DB delta. 304/no-change poll should not emit.

## Cache/invalidations matrix
| Artifact | Cache key | Invalidate |
|---|---|---|
| User events | `events:${username}` | ETag change / 304 miss |
| Repo issue list | `issues:${repo}:${state}` | ETag change, watermark advance |
| Repo PR list | `prs:${repo}:${state}` | ETag change, watermark advance |
| PR detail | `pr:${repo}#${number}:${updated_at}:${head_sha}` | `updated_at` or `head_sha` change |
| Markdown file | `md:${repo}:${ref}:${path}:${sha}` | `sha` or `ref` change |
| Report meta | `report:${repo}:${ref}:${path}:${sha}` | `sha` or `ref` change |

## Polling guidance
| Path | Rule |
|---|---|
| `/users/:username/events` | Honor `X-Poll-Interval`; ETag first; 304 = no WS emit |
| `/repos/:repo/issues` | Page until old watermark or short page |
| `/repos/:repo/pulls` | Same; sort updated desc |
| PR detail fan-out | Progressive hydrate; cache each subresource by PR key |
| Rate-limit low | Pause until reset / backoff; do not hammer |

## Risks
- PR detail route can overfetch 6 endpoints ├Ś many pages.
- Fixed 3-page cap can truncate big PRs.
- Path-only markdown cache can stale on branch move.
- In-memory ETags vanish on restart; `github_repo_poll_state` ETag columns unused.

## Follow-ups
- Tests: 304 no-op, watermark stop, sha invalidation, backoff on 403/429, X-Poll-Interval obey.
- Warm detail cache from poller before UI expand.
- Persist ETags in repo poll state; use them after restart.

## Beads
- `forge-ojn4.5`: cache/progressive PR detail + markdown/report metadata.
- `forge-ojn4.7`: Octokit/raw fetch, pagination stop rules, rate-limit handling.
- `forge-ojn4.9`: poll schedule, ETag use, WS freshness.

title: Research Hono/Bun dashboard endpoint caching and SQLite/observability performance patterns
description:
## PROBLEM

Graph and specialist endpoints are repeatedly slow, likely due to rescans, attach-pool/DAO rebuilds, and insufficient short-lived caching. We need current practical patterns for Hono/Bun server-side caching, route timeouts, SQLite query/statement practices, and invalidation from WebSocket/epoch signals.

## SUCCESS

Produce evidence-backed recommendations for cheap, correct server-side caching and invalidation in this Hono/Bun dashboard. The guidance should cover TTL caches, stale-while-revalidate, in-flight request dedupe, route-level timeout handling, SQLite prepared statement/DAO reuse, and websocket/epoch-based invalidation.

## SCOPE

- External research only: Hono docs, Bun runtime/server docs, Bun SQLite docs, common TTL/SWR cache snippets, GitHub implementation examples.
- Local context from `api/server.ts`, `graph-dao.ts`, `routes/specialists.ts`, observability DAO/attach pool, and `useGraphData`.
- Output should map to child beads `forge-ojn4.1`, `forge-ojn4.6`, `forge-ojn4.8`, and `forge-ojn4.10`.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not edit files.
- Do not introduce Redis or external infrastructure.
- Do not redesign graph/specialist response schemas.

## CONSTRAINTS

- Cached data must not hide live WS updates or running specialist status beyond a short explicit freshness window.
- Prefer in-process strategies suitable for a local developer dashboard.
- Keep testability high: injectable clocks, fake DAOs, deterministic invalidation.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] Current Hono/Bun/Bun SQLite docs consulted.
- [ ] At least two GitHub snippets/patterns for in-process SWR/TTL/in-flight dedupe checked.
- [ ] Recommendation includes cache keys, TTLs, invalidation triggers, and tests.
- [ ] Recommendation calls out where caching is unsafe.

## OUTPUT

Return a concise research brief with sources, recommended cache/invalidation design, endpoint-specific advice, and test criteria.

### Specialist Output ŌĆö researcher (job bcbd2a) [WAITING ŌĆö more output may follow]

## Summary
Hono/Bun dashboard cache plan: short TTL + SWR + in-flight dedupe + epoch invalidation. Keep live overlay fresh, stop rescans and attach churn.

## Sources
- Hono timeout middleware: https://hono.dev/docs/middleware/builtin/timeout
- Hono cache middleware: https://hono.dev/docs/middleware/builtin/cache
- Bun `server.timeout(req, seconds)` / idle timeout / SSE: https://bun.com/docs/runtime/http/server
- Bun WebSocket upgrade: https://bun.com/docs/runtime/http/websockets
- Bun SQLite prepared statements + WAL: https://bun.com/docs/runtime/sqlite
- GitHub TTL + inflight patterns:
  - MetaMask perps cache: https://github.com/MetaMask/metamask-extension/blob/main/ui/providers/perps/perps-cache.ts
  - OpenClaw cache helper: https://github.com/TianyiDataScience/openclaw-control-center/blob/main/src/runtime/usage-cost.ts
  - OpenClaw insights cache: https://github.com/TianyiDataScience/openclaw-control-center/blob/main/src/runtime/openclaw-cli-insights.ts

## Recommendations
- Graph endpoint:
  - key: `projectId + includeClosed + repoEpoch`
  - TTL: 250-500ms
  - SWR: 1-2s
  - invalidate on epoch bump, WS live-status event, repo change
- Specialists in-flight:
  - key: `repoSlug + limit + repoEpoch`
  - TTL: 250ms
  - SWR: 750ms
  - invalidate on job write, watcher bump, WS event
- Project scan list:
  - key: roots hash
  - TTL: 1-2s
  - invalidate on filesystem watcher, roots config change
- Attach pool:
  - keep current dead-cache
  - reuse prepared statements per attach alias
  - do not re-probe dead DBs on mtime tick
- Client `useGraphData`:
  - kill forever `Map`
  - use same TTL/epoch rule
  - refresh on focus + visibility + WS epoch

## Unsafe caching
- live specialist status
- graph response with live overlay
- `generated_at`
- cache keyed only by `projectId`
- long cache on attach-pool failures

## Route/timeouts
- Hono `timeout` middleware on slow JSON routes
- Bun `AbortSignal.timeout()` for outbound fetch
- Bun `server.timeout(req, 0)` only for streaming/SSE

## Test criteria
- cache hit within TTL
- stale serve during SWR, refresh in background
- single upstream call under concurrent miss
- epoch bump forces refresh
- WS update invalidates client cache
- dead DB stays skipped across mtime churn
- live overlay never hidden past explicit TTL

## Final
Brief done. No edits. No infra change.

- Hono cache middleware: https://hono.dev/docs/middleware/builtin/cache
- Bun `server.timeout(req, seconds)` / idle timeout / SSE: https://bun.com/docs/runtime/http/server
- Bun WebSocket upgrade: https://bun.com/docs/runtime/http/websockets
- Bun SQLite prepared statements + WAL: https://bun.com/docs/runtime/sqlite
- GitHub TTL + inflight patterns:
  - MetaMask perps cache: https://github.com/MetaMask/metamask-extension/blob/main/ui/providers/perps/perps-cache.ts
  - OpenClaw cache helper: https://github.com/TianyiDataScience/openclaw-control-center/blob/main/src/runtime/usage-cost.ts
  - OpenClaw insights cache: https://github.com/TianyiDataScience/openclaw-control-center/blob/main/src/runtime/openclaw-cli-insights.ts

## Recommendations
- Graph endpoint:
  - key: `projectId + includeClosed + repoEpoch`
  - TTL: 250-500ms
  - SWR: 1-2s
  - invalidate on epoch bump, WS live-status event, repo change
- Specialists in-flight:
  - key: `repoSlug + limit + repoEpoch`
  - TTL: 250ms
  - SWR: 750ms
  - invalidate on job write, watcher bump, WS event
- Project scan list:
  - key: roots hash
  - TTL: 1-2s
  - invalidate on filesystem watcher, roots config change
- Attach pool:
  - keep current dead-cache
  - reuse prepared statements per attach alias
  - do not re-probe dead DBs on mtime tick
- Client `useGraphData`:
  - kill forever `Map`
  - use same TTL/epoch rule
  - refresh on focus + visibility + WS epoch

## Unsafe caching
- live specialist status
- graph response with live overlay
- `generated_at`
- cache keyed only by `projectId`
- long cache on attach-pool failures

## Route/timeouts
- Hono `timeout` middleware on slow JSON routes
- Bun `AbortSignal.timeout()` for outbound fetch
- Bun `server.timeout(req, 0)` only for streaming/SSE

## Test criteria
- cache hit within TTL
- stale serve during SWR, refresh in background
- single upstream call under concurrent miss
- epoch bump forces refresh
- WS update invalidates client cache
- dead DB stays skipped across mtime churn
- live overlay never hidden past explicit TTL

## Final
Brief done. No edits. No infra change.


title: Research Dolt/mysql2 pool, timeout, and circuit-breaker best practices
description:
## PROBLEM

The epic identified a likely Dolt performance bug: a singleton mysql2 runtime shares pool, breaker, probe, and metrics across project ports/databases. We need current best practices for efficient MySQL/Dolt access from Bun/Node, especially per-key pools, query timeouts, health probes, circuit breakers, and fallback behavior.

## SUCCESS

Produce evidence-backed guidance for implementing per-project/per-pool-key Dolt connections safely and efficiently. Include whether mysql2 pool patterns are correct, how to implement timeout/cancellation without leaking work, how to structure health probes, and whether to use a circuit-breaker library or keep a small custom breaker.

## SCOPE

- External research only: mysql2 docs/source examples, Dolt SQL server docs if available, Node/Bun timeout patterns, circuit-breaker examples.
- Local context from `apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts`, beads route fallback, and graph DAO use.
- Output should map to child beads `forge-ojn4.2`, `forge-ojn4.6`, and `forge-ojn4.9`.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not edit files.
- Do not require a live Dolt server.
- Do not propose broad storage migrations away from Dolt.

## CONSTRAINTS

- One broken project source must not poison other project sources.
- Fallback to JSONL/SQLite must remain fast and visible.
- Prefer solutions that are testable with fakes/mocks.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] Current mysql2 pool docs/examples consulted.
- [ ] Dolt SQL server behavior/docs checked where possible.
- [ ] At least two GitHub snippets/patterns for pool maps or breakers checked.
- [ ] Recommendation includes concrete runtime-state shape and failure-mode tests.

## OUTPUT

Return a concise research brief with sources, recommended runtime design, timeout/breaker details, testing strategy, and red flags to avoid.
notes:
### Specialist Output ŌĆö researcher (job 7bd546) [WAITING ŌĆö more output may follow]

## Summary
Singleton `runtime` bad shape. Need `Map<poolKey, runtime>` so one broken project no poison other project.

## Status
External evidence checked:
- mysql2 pool + timeout docs
- Node AbortSignal / timers cancel docs
- Dolt MySQL-server docs
- breaker library docs
- GitHub patterns for keyed caches, pool setup, breaker state

## Changes
None. Research only.

## Evidence
- **mysql2 pool**
  - `mysql.createPool()` canonical.
  - `connectionLimit`, `maxIdle`, `idleTimeout`, `enableKeepAlive`, `keepAliveInitialDelay` supported.
  - query `timeout` is **client-side inactivity timeout** and **destroys connection** on hit. Good for fail-fast, no stale conn reuse.
  - `connection.release()` and `pool.end()` required.
  - Source: `ctx7 /websites/sidorares_github_io_node-mysql2`
- **Node cancellation**
  - `AbortSignal.timeout(ms)` exists.
  - `AbortSignal.any([...])` exists.
  - `timers/promises.setTimeout(..., { signal })` aborts with `AbortError`.
  - Source: `ctx7 /nodejs/node`
- **Dolt**
  - Dolt SQL server speaks MySQL-compatible client protocol.
  - Connect via host/port/user like MySQL.
  - Source: `ctx7 /dolthub/dolt`
- **Breaker library**
  - `opossum` gives `timeout`, `errorThresholdPercentage`, `resetTimeout`, `volumeThreshold`, `fallback`, stats.
  - Good if want canned metrics/fallback.
  - Source: `ctx7 /nodeshift/opossum`
- **GitHub patterns**
  - `n8n-io/n8n/packages/nodes-base/nodes/MySql/v2/transport/index.ts` uses `mysql2/promise.createPool(...)`.
  - `n8n-io/n8n/packages/cli/src/utils/circuit-breaker.ts` uses `CLOSED | OPEN | HALF_OPEN`.
  - `n8n-io/n8n/packages/@n8n/instance-ai/src/mcp/mcp-client-manager.ts` caches keyed clients in `Map<string, ...>` and cleans up by key.
  - `HeyPuter/puter/src/backend/clients/database/MySQLDatabaseClient.ts` keeps distinct primary/replica pools.
- **Local code**
  - `apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts` has module singleton `runtime` shared across all `DoltClient` instances.
  - `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts` already routes fallback `dolt -> sqlite -> jsonl`, but Dolt state still global.

## Recommended runtime design
Use one runtime bucket per pool key:

```ts
type PoolKey = string; // host:port/database/user

type DoltRuntime = {
  pool: Pool | null;
  breakerOpenUntil: number;
  consecutiveFailures: number;
  probeTimer: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null;
  probing: boolean;
  metrics: { ok: boolean; latencyMs: number; at: string }[];
  lastHealthyAt: number;
};

const runtimes = new Map<PoolKey, DoltRuntime>();
```

Key = `host:port/database/user`.
Rules:
- one broken key stays local
- pool + breaker + probe + metrics all bucketed by key
- `disconnect()` closes only that key
- stale key rotation ends old pool before new pool lives
- probe timer `unref()`

## Timeout / cancellation
Best path:
- use mysql2 query-level `timeout` first
- wrap user request with `AbortSignal.timeout()` or `AbortSignal.any([reqSignal, AbortSignal.timeout(...)])`
- on abort, destroy in-flight connection, not only reject wrapper promise

Why:
- pure `Promise.race` hides latency but lets query keep running
- mysql2 `timeout` kills connection, so work cannot leak into reused conn
- for pooled connection, timeout should poison only that conn, not whole project bucket

## Health probe
Keep probe small:
- `SELECT 1`
- no heavy commit hash or join
- single probe in flight per key
- skip probe if breaker open
- probe interval `unref()`
- success resets failure count + opens breaker closed
- failure increments counter and records latency

## Breaker choice
- **Need simple, testable, tiny state machine?** keep custom breaker.
- **Need built-in stats, fallback hooks, richer knobs?** use `opossum`.
- For this app, custom breaker wins: fewer deps, easier per-key map, easier fake tests.

## Testing strategy
Must add tests for:
- key A fail does not trip key B
- key rotation closes old pool, opens new pool
- query timeout destroys conn / marks failure
- breaker open skips DB and returns fast fallback
- probe success closes breaker
- probe failure opens breaker after threshold
- JSONL/SQLite fallback still visible and fast

## Red flags
- module-global singleton runtime
- `Promise.race` timeout with no destroy path
- one breaker for all projects
- probe interval without `unref()`
- fallback that hides degraded state

## Child bead map
- **forge-ojn4.2**: per-key pool map + lifecycle
- **forge-ojn4.6**: timeout/cancel path + no leaked work
- **forge-ojn4.9**: probe/breaker/fallback behavior + tests

## Verification
- mysql2 docs consulted
- Dolt docs consulted
- Node timeout docs consulted
- GitHub snippets checked
- Local runtime shape reviewed

## Follow-ups
Need if want sharper answer:
- current `dolt-client.test.ts`
- any prod load numbers
- any Dolt server config file for timeout knobs

## Beads
Claimed then closed `forge-ojn412`.

```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "summary": "Research done. Singleton Dolt runtime bad shape; per-key map with mysql2 query timeout, abort-aware cancel, small probe, local breaker best.",
  "files_changed": [],
  "follow_ups": [
    "Add tests for per-key isolation, timeout destroy, probe recovery, breaker-open fallback."
  ],
  "risks": [
    "Promise.race timeout alone leaks DB work.",
    "Global breaker/metrics still poison unrelated project ports."
  ],
  "verification": [
    "mysql2 pool and query-timeout docs checked.",
    "Node AbortSignal.timeout / AbortSignal.any docs checked.",
    "Dolt MySQL-compatible server docs checked.",
    "GitHub patterns checked for pool usage and breaker state."
  ]
}
```

title: Research React dashboard stale-while-revalidate + WebSocket merge patterns
description:
## PROBLEM

The realtime dashboard performance epic needs current external guidance for React state/data-loading patterns that keep cached data visible, refresh in the background, and merge WebSocket updates without stale HTTP responses overwriting newer events. The current app uses React, Zustand, manual fetch calls, and `@tanstack/react-virtual`, but not a dedicated query/cache library.

## SUCCESS

Produce evidence-backed recommendations on whether to keep a small custom Zustand cache or introduce a library such as TanStack Query/SWR for this dashboard. Recommendations must specifically address instant WebSocket upserts, stale-while-revalidate, tab-level lazy queries, abort/cancellation, request dedupe, and avoiding unrelated refetches on tab navigation.

## SCOPE

- External research only: current docs, GitHub snippets, GitWiki/DeepWiki/context7 where useful.
- Local context from epic `forge-ojn4`, especially `useGithubActivity`, `MainPane`, `stores/github`, and `useGraphData`.
- Output should map recommendations to child beads `forge-ojn4.1`, `forge-ojn4.4`, and `forge-ojn4.10`.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not edit files.
- Do not implement the recommended library.
- Do not review unrelated UI aesthetics.

## CONSTRAINTS

- WebSocket updates must stay instant and authoritative over older HTTP responses.
- Prefer minimal dependency changes unless evidence strongly supports a library.
- Cite current docs/snippets and distinguish docs from opinion.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] Current React/Zustand/TanStack Query/SWR docs consulted.
- [ ] At least two real-world GitHub implementation snippets or repo patterns checked.
- [ ] Recommendation includes pros/cons and migration cost for this repo.
- [ ] Recommendation includes test strategy for WS-vs-HTTP freshness.

## OUTPUT

Return a concise research brief with sources, recommended approach, rejected alternatives, code patterns to copy/adapt, and specific acceptance criteria to add to the implementation/test beads.
notes:
### Specialist Output ŌĆö researcher (job 677776) [WAITING ŌĆö more output may follow]

## Summary
Recommendation: **TanStack Query for server state, small Zustand for UI/live overlay**.  
Why: current dashboard needs cached data visible, background refresh, dedupe, abort/cancel, and WS wins over stale HTTP. TanStack Query docs cover all of that directly; SWR covers some, but Query fits multi-tab, multi-source merge better.

## Status
- React docs checked
- TanStack Query docs checked
- SWR docs checked
- Zustand docs checked
- Real-world GitHub snippets checked: `langflow-ai/langflow`, `TryGhost/Ghost`, `coder/coder`, `multica-ai/multica`

## Evidence
### Docs
- **React**: `useEffect` cleanup/ignore flag stops stale async result overwrite.  
  Source: https://github.com/reactjs/react.dev/blob/main/src/content/learn/synchronizing-with-effects.md
- **TanStack Query**:
  - `staleTime` marks fresh vs stale
  - `gcTime` keeps inactive cache 5 min by default
  - `enabled` gates lazy/dependent queries
  - `placeholderData: keepPreviousData` keeps old data on key change
  - `queryFn` gets `AbortSignal`
  - `cancelQueries`, `setQueryData`, `invalidateQueries`
  - in-flight request dedupe via shared promise
  Sources:
  - https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/reference/QueryClient.md
  - https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/guides/caching.md
  - https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/guides/important-defaults.md
  - https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/guides/query-cancellation.md
  - https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/guides/dependent-queries.md
  - https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/guides/placeholder-query-data.md
  - https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/guides/updates-from-mutation-responses.md
- **SWR**:
  - `mutate` updates cache + revalidate
  - `dedupingInterval` dedupes
  - `keepPreviousData` keeps old data on key change
  - `revalidateOnFocus` default on
  Sources:
  - https://github.com/vercel/swr-site/blob/main/content/docs/mutation.es.mdx
  - https://github.com/vercel/swr-site/blob/main/content/docs/revalidation.mdx
  - https://github.com/vercel/swr-site/blob/main/content/blog/swr-v2.es.mdx
- **Zustand**:
  - selector subscriptions + shallow equality good for client state
  Sources:
  - https://github.com/pmndrs/zustand/blob/main/README.md
  - https://github.com/pmndrs/zustand/blob/main/docs/reference/middlewares/subscribe-with-selector.md

### Real-world snippets
- **Langflow**: live message stream merges into cache with `queryClient.setQueryData(cacheKey, (old: Message[] = []) => ...)`.  
  https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/blob/main/src/frontend/src/components/core/playgroundComponent/chat-view/utils/message-utils.ts
- **Ghost**: keyed cache patching with `setQueryData(queryKey, ...)` for live account/activity updates.  
  https://github.com/TryGhost/Ghost/blob/main/apps/activitypub/src/hooks/use-activity-pub-queries.ts
- **coder/coder**: `cancelQueries` before optimistic writes to stop old snapshot overwrite.  
  https://github.com/coder/coder/blob/main/site/src/api/queries/chats.ts
- **multica-ai/multica**: same anti-overwrite pattern with `cancelQueries` + `setQueryData`.  
  https://github.com/multica-ai/multica/blob/main/packages/core/issues/mutations.ts

## Recommendation
### Pick
- **Use TanStack Query**
- **Keep Zustand only for**:
  - selected tab / UI state
  - unread flags / instant WS overlay if needed
  - small local graph cache if single-consumer

### Reject
- **SWR-only**: good for simple fetch cache, but Query gives cleaner cancel/refetch/invalidate toolbox for this dashboard shape.
- **Zustand-only**: doable, but means rebuilding stale flags, request dedupe, cancellation, background refetch policy, and cache key semantics by hand.

## Copy/adapt patterns
- **WS upsert**: `queryClient.setQueryData(key, old => mergeById(old, incoming))`
- **Stale HTTP guard**: carry `updated_at`, `version`, or `seq`; drop older payloads
- **Background refresh**: `staleTime` > 0 + `isFetching` indicator
- **Lazy tab loads**: `enabled: activeTab === 'prs'`
- **No sibling tab refetch**: separate query keys per tab/repo
- **Abort stale fetch**: pass `signal` into `fetch`
- **Avoid flicker on tab switch**: `placeholderData: keepPreviousData`

## Bead mapping
- **forge-ojn4.1**: activity feed / WS merge
  - WS event wins over older HTTP
  - merge by id
  - sync hint = targeted invalidate, not blind full reload
- **forge-ojn4.4**: repo tab data loading
  - lazy query per tab
  - keep old data visible on switch
  - no unrelated refetch on hidden tabs
- **forge-ojn4.10**: graph / secondary console data
  - keep small cache if single consumer
  - if reused across panes, move to Query
  - focus refetch only for active view

## Acceptance criteria
- Late HTTP response cannot overwrite newer WS event
- Switching tabs does not refetch inactive tab data
- Same query key across components makes one network request
- In-flight request aborts on tab change/unmount
- Cached data stays visible during background refresh
- Tests cover WS-before-HTTP and HTTP-before-WS order

## Verification
- Current docs consulted: yes
- Real-world snippets consulted: yes
- Migration cost understood: moderate for TanStack Query, high for custom Zustand-only

- If WS payload lacks version/seq, merge safety weaker

title: specialist:security-auditor
notes:
### Specialist Output ŌĆö security-auditor (job 6ed699) [DONE]

## Summary

Scoped audit run on uncommitted terminal changes. Result: **FAIL**.  
Main blocker: terminal WebSocket opens shell path with no request auth, no origin check, no per-user admin verification. If shell provider enabled, reachable user can open PTY. Reattach/session logic itself mostly okay, but access gate broken before token matters.

## Status

findings

## Changes

None. Read-only audit only.

## Verification

- Read source:
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/provider-registry.ts`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/node-pty-helper.cjs`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/bridge.ts`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/core/shell-provider-policy.ts`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/TerminalTabPanel.tsx`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/terminal/TerminalStream.tsx`
- Checked uncommitted diff with `git diff`, `git status --short`
- Used line-numbered review with `nl -ba`

## Risks

- Unauthenticated shell access when provider enabled
- Admin-only gate not tied to requester
- Secret-bearing env inherited into spawned shell beyond tiny scrub list

## Follow-ups

- Gate `/api/console/terminal/ws` with real requester authz before upgrade
- Bind verified-admin to request/session identity, not process env
- Expand env scrub or switch to explicit allowlist env for PTY child
- Add origin check for WebSocket upgrade if browser clients only

## Security Audit Summary

- Scope reviewed:
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/provider-registry.ts`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/node-pty-helper.cjs`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/core/shell-provider-policy.ts`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/TerminalTabPanel.tsx`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/terminal/TerminalStream.tsx`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/bridge.ts` for reachability/session checks
- Overall risk: high
- Mode(s) used:
  - static secure-code review
  - bounty-style exploitability triage
- Commands/sources used:
  - `git status --short`
  - `git diff --stat`
  - `git diff -- ...scoped files...`
  - `sed -n`
  - `nl -ba`
  - local source evidence only

## Findings

- ID: GB-TERM-001
- Severity: high
- Category/CWE when applicable: CWE-306 Missing Authentication for Critical Function; CWE-1385 Missing Origin Validation in WebSockets
- Evidence:
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:129-140` upgrades any WebSocket request with no auth/origin check
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:147-163` routes `/api/console/terminal/ws` straight into `terminalBridge`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/bridge.ts:77-107` `open()` creates PTY session for any connected socket
  - `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/TerminalTabPanel.tsx:58` client opens provider kind `"pty"`
- Reachability/exploitability:
  - Direct. Browser or script can connect to `/api/console/terminal/ws`.
  - CORS middleware does not protect WebSockets.
  - If operator enables shell provider and host reachable, attacker gets interactive shell.
  - Even localhost-only setup still exposed to cross-site WebSocket from malicious webpage opened by local user.
- Impact:
  - Full command execution in repo-root PTY.
  - Read repo, modify worktree, pivot via inherited env/secrets.
- Recommended fix:
  - Enforce authz before `server.upgrade()` for `/api/console/terminal/ws`.
  - Check `Origin` against trusted UI origin.
  - Reject unauthenticated or non-admin callers before socket creation.
- Verification after fix:
  - Unauthenticated WebSocket upgrade to `/api/console/terminal/ws` returns 401/403.
  - Cross-origin browser attempt rejected.
  - Authenticated admin path still opens PTY.

- ID: GB-TERM-002
- Severity: high
- Category/CWE when applicable: CWE-285 Improper Authorization
- Evidence:
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/provider-registry.ts:33-35` marks requester verified admin from process env: `env.GITBOARD_SHELL_PROVIDER_ADMIN_VERIFIED === "1"`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/core/shell-provider-policy.ts:70-89` admin-only decision depends on `context.isVerifiedAdmin`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:124` registry created once from process env, not per request/connection
- Reachability/exploitability:
  - Direct design flaw. Admin-only control not bound to socket owner/session/user.
  - If process env sets verified flag, every terminal client becomes verified admin.
  - If env not set, no one can use admin-only feature. Both cases break intended policy.
- Impact:
  - Admin-only shell restriction collapses into global server toggle.
  - Any connected client can inherit admin shell capability once env flag enabled.
- Recommended fix:
  - Move provider enable/authz decision to request-time.
  - Pass authenticated caller context into terminal open/attach flow.
  - Remove `GITBOARD_SHELL_PROVIDER_ADMIN_VERIFIED` process-wide trust for user authorization.
- Verification after fix:
  - Non-admin authenticated client gets 403 even when shell provider globally enabled.
  - Verified admin client can open session.
  - Two simultaneous users see different authz outcomes.

- ID: GB-TERM-003
- Severity: medium
- Category/CWE when applicable: CWE-200 Exposure of Sensitive Information
- Evidence:
  - `apps/gitboard/src/core/shell-provider-policy.ts:33` scrub list only removes `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`, `GITHUB_TOKEN`, `SSH_AUTH_SOCK`, `SSH_AGENT_PID`, `NPM_TOKEN`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/provider-registry.ts:192-200` child env clones almost all `process.env`, then re-adds `PATH`, `HOME`, `SHELL`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/node-pty-helper.cjs:10-17` helper passes config.env straight into PTY spawn
- Reachability/exploitability:
  - Reachable through GB-TERM-001. Interactive shell can read inherited env immediately.
  - Also risks leaking tokens with variant names: `GH_TOKEN`, `AWS_SESSION_TOKEN`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, cloud creds, internal service vars.
- Impact:
  - Secret exposure inside terminal session.
  - Turns shell access into credential exposure/pivot.
- Recommended fix:
  - Replace denylist scrub with explicit allowlist env for PTY child.
  - Keep only minimal vars: `HOME`, `PATH`, `SHELL`, `PWD`, `TERM`, maybe locale vars.
- Verification after fix:
  - `env` inside spawned shell shows only approved keys.
  - Known secret vars absent.

## Dependency Advisory Triage

- Package: `node-pty`
- Installed version: `1.1.0`
- Advisory/source: none reviewed in this scoped audit
- Vulnerable range: unknown
- Fixed version: unknown
- Reachability: direct
- Recommendation:
  - Run package advisory check in separate dependency-audit bead if merge depends on supply-chain signoff.

## Non-findings / Dropped Noise

- `cwd` allowlist check present. `provider-registry.ts:174-181`. Fixed cwd resolves to repo root only. No user-controlled cwd path seen.
- Shell allowlist present. `provider-registry.ts:184-189`, `shell-provider-policy.ts:32`. zsh support intentional. No direct injection sink.
- Node-pty helper IPC uses JSON lines + base64 payloads. `node-pty-helper.cjs:22-43`, `provider-registry.ts:130-166`. No shell interpolation or command-string injection found in helper channel.
- Reattach token exists and required for attach. `bridge.ts:113-124`. Token stored in memory only, not localStorage. Good.
- Session ownership enforced for `input`, `resize`, `detach`, `exit`. `bridge.ts:127-171`. No ownership bypass found after attach.
- Dev gate logic exists. `shell-provider-policy.ts:75-87`. But auth blockers above still fail task because gate/auth not enough when feature enabled.

## Residual Risk and Follow-ups

- Block merge until GB-TERM-001 and GB-TERM-002 fixed.
- After fix, run narrow re-audit on:
  - WebSocket upgrade auth/origin checks
  - request-bound admin verification
  - PTY env allowlist contents
- Optional next bead:
  - dependency audit for new `node-pty` addition

## Beads

- No bead id provided in prompt. No `bd` command run.

## Machine-readable block
