title: Add UI realtime smoke tests for stale-while-revalidate dashboard behavior
description:
## PROBLEM

The central product constraint is that the dashboard stays realtime: HTTP caching and lazy loading must not make WS updates feel delayed or allow stale HTTP responses to overwrite fresh WS state. This needs explicit UI-level regression coverage.

## SUCCESS

UI tests or a reliable smoke harness prove that cached/stale data can render immediately, loading is panel-scoped, tab switches avoid unrelated fetches, and simulated WS messages update visible GitHub/beads state instantly.

## SCOPE

- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts` tests or component tests.
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/MainPane.tsx` / GitHub tab tests.
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/BeadsRepoView.tsx` tests for WS open/closed transitions if feasible.
- Test helpers for fake fetch and fake websocket messages.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not require a real browser if component/hook tests cover the behavior.
- Do not test every visual detail of timelines/cards.
- Do not replace backend boundary tests.

## CONSTRAINTS

- WS events must be simulated at the same abstraction used by existing hooks/components.
- Tests should assert visible/dashboard state, not just internal store calls.
- Fetch-count assertions should remain stable across harmless implementation refactors.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] GitHub tab switch test proves only tab-relevant endpoints are fetched.
- [ ] GitHub WS event test proves event/PR/issue upsert is visible without manual refresh.
- [ ] Stale HTTP response test proves older fetch results do not remove newer WS upserts.
- [ ] Beads close/upsert WS test proves issue moves between lists without full reload where feasible.
- [ ] `bun --filter @xtrm/gitboard test` or targeted tests pass.

## OUTPUT

Return test files, commands, and the realtime behaviors covered vs. left for manual smoke.

## APPROACH NOTES

This is the explicit guardrail for the user requirement: keep WS instant refresh and dashboard feel while optimizing slow HTTP/data paths.
notes:
Research integration:
UI realtime tests should assert the core invariant: WS updates are instant and authoritative over stale HTTP/cache.
- With TanStack Query: simulate an older HTTP response arriving after a WS upsert; visible state must retain the WS-updated record.
- Tab switch fetch-count tests should prove only active tab data is requested, with cached previous data visible during refresh.
- Beads close/upsert WS tests should move items between open/closed lists without full reload.
- Graph/specialist UI should show cached data immediately and refresh via WS/epoch invalidation rather than focus-triggered full reloads.
close_reason: Done: UI realtime smoke coverage added for GitHub and beads stale-while-revalidate behavior.

title: Add boundary regression tests for Dolt, beads, graph, and GitHub latency paths
description:
## PROBLEM

The optimization work touches API boundaries, source fallback, route matching, and remote GitHub fan-out. Without boundary tests, future changes can reintroduce slow full-list fetches, route-order 404s, global Dolt breaker coupling, or unbounded GitHub detail waits.

## SUCCESS

Boundary regression tests cover the key backend failure modes and can run in CI/local development without requiring live GitHub or live Dolt where mocks/fakes are sufficient.

## SCOPE

- `apps/beadboard/tests` or colocated route/client tests for beads route order and Dolt runtime isolation.
- `apps/gitboard/tests` for graph route cache/invalidation, GitHub detail timeout/partial response, and poller scheduling.
- Test fixtures/fakes only; no product code except minimal testability hooks if approved by implementation beads.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not create E2E browser tests here; UI realtime tests are separate.
- Do not depend on live external GitHub or Dolt servers for deterministic tests.
- Do not overfit tests to implementation-private details unless needed for the bug class.

## CONSTRAINTS

- Tests must be deterministic and reasonably fast.
- Prefer public route/client behavior assertions over deep internals where possible.
- If product code needs testability hooks, keep them small and non-invasive.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] Test fails against the old `/issues/closed` route-order bug.
- [ ] Test covers per-pool Dolt runtime isolation or equivalent behavior.
- [ ] Test covers graph cache avoiding repeated scan calls.
- [ ] Test covers GitHub detail timeout/partial results.
- [ ] Test covers poller no inter-repo sleep and prompt WS publication.

## OUTPUT

Return test files, command output, and any remaining untested risk that needs manual smoke coverage.

## APPROACH NOTES

This test bead should be wired after the related implementation beads but can be developed incrementally as each fix lands.
notes:
Research integration:
Boundary regression tests should cover:
- Dolt Map<poolKey,runtime>: one key timeout/breaker does not affect another.
- Beads `/issues/closed` route order and open/closed payload split.
- Graph cache: repeated request avoids repeated scan; WS/epoch invalidates.
- GitHub REST: 304 no-op, persisted ETag use, watermark stop, 403/429 backoff, PR detail partial timeout, SHA cache invalidation.
- Hono/Bun cache: TTL, SWR stale return, in-flight dedupe, timeout handling.
close_reason: Done: backend performance boundary coverage contract added and target suites pass.

title: Rework GitHub poller scheduling for bounded concurrency and persisted freshness
description:
## PROBLEM

`GithubPoller.pollRepos()` serializes repos and sleeps between repos based on a computed active window. With many repositories, a cycle can take a very long time, keeping dashboard GitHub state stale even though WebSocket updates are meant to feel instant. ETags are partly modeled in DB but runtime ETag use is not fully persisted.

## SUCCESS

GitHub polling checks due repositories with bounded concurrency, no inter-repo sleep inside the loop, persisted ETags/watermarks, rate-limit-aware backoff, and WebSocket publication of changed PRs/issues as soon as they are discovered.

## SCOPE

- `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-store.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/core/store.ts` only for backward-compatible poll-state schema additions if needed.
- Tests for scheduling/watermark/ETag behavior.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not replace the current GitHub REST ingestion pipeline wholesale.
- Do not alter dashboard rendering directly.
- Do not increase GitHub API usage beyond safe bounded limits.

## CONSTRAINTS

- Respect rate limits and pause states.
- Persist enough poll state that restarts do not force unnecessary backfill.
- WS `github:pr.upsert` and `github:issue.upsert` events must publish promptly when records change.
- Run GitNexus impact on `GithubPoller` before editing.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] Tests prove `pollRepos()` does not sleep once per repo in production scheduling path.
- [ ] Tests prove due repos can be processed with bounded concurrency.
- [ ] ETag/watermark state survives through `github_repo_poll_state` where applicable.
- [ ] Changed PR/issue records still publish WS events immediately.
- [ ] Relevant lint/tests pass.

## OUTPUT

Return scheduling model, concurrency/backoff values, DB state changes if any, and validation evidence.

## APPROACH NOTES

Current `pollRepos()` uses `getRepoActiveWindow()` then awaits a timeout inside the repo loop. That should become per-repo next-due scheduling, not a blocking delay between repos.
notes:
Research integration from job f41b36:
- GitHub polling should use conditional GET with persisted ETags/If-None-Match, obey X-Poll-Interval where present, and respect Retry-After/x-ratelimit-reset on 403/429.
- Use per_page=100 and stop pagination on old watermark or short page; do not rely only on fixed 3-page caps.
- Octokit core + paginate-rest + throttling + retry is recommended if adding a library; raw fetch is acceptable only if header/rate-limit behavior remains explicit.
- Poll due repos with bounded concurrency and no inter-repo sleep; publish WS only when DB delta occurs. 304/no-change must not emit.
close_reason: Done: bounded due-repo GitHub polling, persisted ETags, rate-limit backoff, and regression tests.

title: Reduce specialist jobs endpoint latency without breaking live overlays
description:
## PROBLEM

Logs show `/api/specialists/jobs` and `/api/specialists/jobs/in-flight` repeatedly taking multiple seconds. These endpoints feed graph/console overlays and can make the dashboard feel blocked when navigating, even though live specialist status should be lightweight.

## SUCCESS

Specialist job endpoints reuse expensive DAO/attach resources, cache summaries safely, and expose live status quickly enough for dashboard overlays. Realtime or epoch-based invalidation keeps running/waiting job state fresh.

## SCOPE

- `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/attach-pool.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/dao.ts` only where profiling identifies expensive repeated work.
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useAllSpecialistJobs.ts`, `useInFlightJobs.ts`, or graph overlay hooks only if client refresh behavior needs adjustment.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not redesign specialist data model.
- Do not remove live specialist overlays from graph/beads UI.
- Do not change specialist CLI behavior.

## CONSTRAINTS

- Running/waiting job status must not be stale beyond the chosen short TTL or epoch invalidation rule.
- Avoid repeated attach-pool construction per request when safe.
- Keep endpoint response shapes backward-compatible.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] Local timing evidence shows `/api/specialists/jobs/in-flight` avoids repeated multi-second work for cached/unchanged state.
- [ ] Live running/waiting jobs still update in graph/beads overlays.
- [ ] Tests or smoke cover cache invalidation via epoch/repo changes.
- [ ] Relevant lint/tests pass.

## OUTPUT

Return bottleneck source, cache/reuse strategy, timing evidence, changed files, and residual freshness risks.

## APPROACH NOTES

Current default DAO bundle TTL is 2s and may recreate attach resources often. Start by profiling before changing DAO internals.
notes:
Research integration from job bcbd2a:
- For specialist endpoints, use short in-process TTL, stale-while-revalidate, and in-flight dedupe around expensive attach-pool/DAO reads.
- Prefer epoch/file-watcher invalidation over polling-only freshness. Running/waiting jobs may need shorter TTL than historical job lists.
- Cache keys should include repo set / query params / epoch. Avoid response-shape changes.
- Do not add Redis or external infrastructure; this is a local dashboard cache.
close_reason: Done: specialist job endpoints use short epoch-aware caches and longer reusable DAO bundle with tests.

title: Cache and progressively load GitHub PR/repo detail fan-out
description:
## PROBLEM

GitHub detail endpoints can fan out heavily: PR detail fetches comments, reviews, review comments, commits, files, and timeline with up to three pages each; reports listing fetches full content for every report just to parse frontmatter; markdown/report tabs fetch on every open. These detail paths are secondary but currently can make navigation feel blocked.

## SUCCESS

PR details and repo content panels load progressively with timeouts and short-lived cache. Opening a detail view shows cached or partial data quickly, then fills slow sections without blocking the rest of the dashboard.

## SCOPE

- `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-readme.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/PrTimeline.tsx`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/RepoContentPanels.tsx`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/client.ts`
- Optional SQLite cache additions in `apps/gitboard/src/core/store.ts` only if needed.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not migrate GitHub detail fetching to GraphQL in this bead.
- Do not change timeline/comment rendering semantics.
- Do not block the main Activity/PR list on detail hydration.

## CONSTRAINTS

- Use request timeouts/AbortController or equivalent for remote GitHub calls.
- Preserve partial-result behavior with explicit per-section errors.
- Cache invalidation must account for PR `updated_at`, report SHA, README SHA, or TTL.
- Avoid consuming GitHub rate limit aggressively.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] PR detail route has bounded latency under a failing/slow GitHub subrequest.
- [ ] Detail UI can show partial/cached data while slow sections load.
- [ ] Reports list does not fetch every full report body before first paint.
- [ ] Tests cover timeout/partial error behavior where practical.
- [ ] Relevant lint/tests pass.

## OUTPUT

Return cache strategy, timeout values, partial response behavior, changed files, and validation evidence.

## APPROACH NOTES

One PR detail request can currently produce up to 18 GitHub REST calls. Consider section-level fetches or server-side cache keyed by repo/number/updated_at.
notes:
Checked local GitHub fetch/poll paths plus GitHub REST, Octokit, pagination, rate-limit, ETag, and contents docs. Found current PR detail route fans out 6 REST pages; markdown/report fetches eagerly parse file content; poller has in-memory ETag only and repo_poll_state ETag fields unused.
Research integration from job f41b36:
- PR detail should be progressive and cache-backed; avoid one UI action waiting on all subresources.
- Recommended cache key: pr:${repo}#${number}:${updated_at}:${head_sha}; invalidate on updated_at/head_sha/state/subresource-error change.
- Markdown/report cache key should include repo/ref/path/sha; report metadata should be cached by SHA and body fetched only when opened.
- Use per_page=100 and Link/paginate iteration where paging is needed, with hard caps as safety not primary logic.
- Use timeouts and partial section errors so one slow GitHub endpoint does not block the whole detail panel.
Reset to open: forge-ojn4.5 depends on forge-ojn4.4 and no implementation work is being done in this session yet.
close_reason: Done: bounded PR detail fan-out with partial errors/cache, directory-first reports list, readme/report fetch timeouts, and regression tests.

title: Fix beads closed-issue routing and reduce full-list payload work
description:
## PROBLEM

`apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts` defines `/projects/:id/issues/:issueId` before `/projects/:id/issues/closed`, so closed-list requests can be interpreted as an issue detail request. The gitboard frontend works around this by fetching up to 1000 issues and splitting closed/open client-side, which increases Dolt hydration and payload work.

## SUCCESS

Closed issue list requests reliably hit the closed route, clients can request only the data they need, and the dashboard no longer needs the ŌĆ£backend `/issues/closed` returns 404ŌĆØ workaround for Dolt-backed projects.

## SCOPE

- `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/BeadsRepoView.tsx`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/beads-api.ts` if client behavior needs adjustment.
- Route regression tests in beadboard/gitboard test surface.

## NON_GOALS

- Do not change issue card rendering or triage scoring.
- Do not redesign beads API filtering beyond closed/open payload partitioning.
- Do not remove the all-issues endpoint; keep compatibility.

## CONSTRAINTS

- Route order fix must be backward-compatible.
- WS `beads:issue.*` updates must continue to update open/closed collections instantly.
- If reintroducing separate closed fetches, do not block initial open issue render on closed history.

## VALIDATION

- [ ] `/api/beads/projects/:id/issues/closed` returns closed issues, not 404 detail lookup.
- [ ] Open feed can render without fetching all closed history synchronously.
- [ ] WS close/upsert events move issues between open and closed lists without refresh.
- [ ] Relevant lint/tests pass.

## OUTPUT

Return API behavior before/after, changed frontend fetch pattern, and regression test evidence.

## APPROACH NOTES

This is a small boundary fix but high leverage because it removes one cause of overfetching around Dolt hydration.
notes:
Research integration:
- Route-order and overfetch fix supports the wider SWR plan: clients should request exactly the visible slice and let WS events patch open/closed state.
- Avoid using the all-issues endpoint as a workaround for `/issues/closed`; closed history should be lazy/non-blocking.
- UI tests should prove `beads:issue.close` and `beads:issue.upsert` move items between visible lists without a full reload.
Implementation output 2026-05-20:
- Moved `/projects/:id/issues/closed` above `/projects/:id/issues/:issueId` in `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts`.
- `BeadsRepoView` now requests active statuses with limit 100 for initial render, then loads closed history separately via `/issues/closed` without blocking the open feed.
- Added `apps/beadboard/tests/api/routes/beads-routes.test.ts` route regression coverage.
- Extended `apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/components/beads/BeadsRepoView.test.tsx` for non-blocking closed load and realtime close movement.
Validation:
- PASS: `bun --filter @xtrm/beadboard test -- tests/api/routes/beads-routes.test.ts`
- PASS: `bun --filter @xtrm/gitboard test -- tests/dashboard/components/beads/BeadsRepoView.test.tsx`
- PASS: `bun --filter @xtrm/gitboard lint`
- Code-sanity 9617ae was stopped after stale/no findings; targeted tests cover the small route/UI diff.
close_reason: Done: fixed closed-issue route order, removed full-list overfetch workaround, and added route/UI realtime regression tests.

title: Sidebar repo tree: replace 10s poll with WS-driven updates
description: Currently useRepoTree (apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useRepoTree.ts) polls every 10s and fires ~2N backend calls per tick (getStats + getConnection for every beads project). With ~30 projects that's ~63 calls / 10s ŌĆö saturates the API and causes projects to flicker in/out mid-navigation. Short-term fix bumps interval to 60s and moves getConnection to mount-only; this issue tracks the proper end-state: WS-driven sidebar with zero steady-state polling load.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Initial mount: one-shot fetch of /api/github/repos + /api/github/repo-stats + /api/beads/projects + per-project getStats + getConnection. (2) Subscribe to WS channels: beads:changes, beads:source_health, github:activity. (3) On beads:changes envelope: debounce ~500ms then refetch getStats(projectId) only for that project. (4) On beads:source_health envelope: update beadsSource chip directly from the envelope payload (no refetch). (5) On github:activity envelope: refetch repo-stats for the affected full_name only (debounced). (6) Honor since_seq/boot_id reconcile on WS reconnect per realtime-envelope contract. (7) Remove setInterval / REFRESH_MS. (8) Sidebar counts and chips visibly update within ~1s of the underlying change (verified via manual e2e: claim a bead, watch sidebar count tick).
notes: Related memories: realtime-envelope-uses-boot-id-uuid-per-server, beads-watcher-forge-igg-diffs-4-entity-snapshots, gitboard-realtime-websocket-server-expects-client-messages-w, gitboard-sidebar-beads-source-chip-userepotree-polls-api. Predecessor: short-term fix on PR 12 / fix/forge-live-specialist-observability already lands attach-pool dead-cache + shared-server scanner routing. This bead is the polling-pressure follow-up.

title: Reviewer: f6qk.5 specialists tab

title: Specialists tab: live cockpit (chain cards + structured detail pane)
description:
## PROBLEM
The Console > Specialists tab is currently a placeholder. Operators have no full-page surface for "show me every chain across all repos right now, grouped by root bead, with status badges, click-to-expand turn logs." The existing BottomDrawer Specialists panel is the glance view (last 50 jobs, terminal-style rows); the Console tab needs the deep view: chain cards grouped by root bead, filter chips, click-card ŌåÆ structured detail pane on the right.

## SUCCESS
- Console > Specialists renders a two-pane layout:
  - LEFT (60% width): chain card list, one card per chain_id, grouped by root bead. Each card shows: root bead id (JBM) + title (Inter, truncated), chain length count, dominant status badge (running / waiting / done / error / cancelled ŌĆö pick the latest non-done state), list of role chips (executor, reviewer, etc.) with status dots, elapsed time of the chain, last-message excerpt (1 line).
  - RIGHT (40% width): structured detail pane. Empty state when no card is selected (small octicon + "Select a chain to see details"). When a card is selected: chain timeline (rows of jobs in chain order with status icon, role, job_id, elapsed, turns, tools, model), full last-output excerpt of the most recent job, "Open bead" button (calls useBeadSideDrawer.open(bead_id)).
- Filter chips above the card list: All ┬Ę Running ┬Ę Waiting ┬Ę Done ┬Ę Error ┬Ę Cancelled. Multi-select OR-combined.
- Auto-refresh: poll /api/specialists/chains every 5s while the tab is visible. Pause when hidden (visibilitychange listener).
- Per-bead detail: when a chain card is clicked, the detail pane fetches /api/specialists/chains/:chain_id for the full job list.
- Live pulse on currently-running cards (same animation as Graph node).
- Empty state: "No specialist chains for this project yet" with a small dim octicon when the API returns an empty array.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/Specialists.tsx (REPLACE the placeholder with real cockpit)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/specialists/ChainCard.tsx (NEW) ŌĆö single card primitive
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/specialists/ChainDetailPane.tsx (NEW) ŌĆö right pane structured view
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/specialists/FilterChips.tsx (NEW) ŌĆö top filter chip row
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useChains.ts (NEW) ŌĆö polls /api/specialists/chains (new endpoint if absent) or aggregates from /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight + /api/specialists/jobs/recent. PREFER aggregating from existing endpoints first ŌĆö do NOT add a new backend endpoint in this task unless aggregation is impossible.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useChainDetail.ts (NEW) ŌĆö fetches /api/specialists/chains/:chain_id on selection
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css (MOD ŌĆö .console-specialists-* classes, card layout, two-pane split)

## NON_GOALS
- No xterm specialist-feed pane in this task. The structured detail pane is the v1 deliverable. A future task tracks forge-lqyc.8 to add the xterm pane to the right side of the detail pane.
- No kill/stop/resume actions ŌĆö read-only.
- No editing of bead notes from this view.
- No new backend endpoints if existing endpoints suffice. PREFER aggregating /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight + /api/specialists/jobs/recent (already returns chain_id) over adding /api/specialists/chains.
- No drag-to-resize on the pane split (60/40 fixed for v1).
- No multi-host coordination ŌĆö single-host only.

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: do NOT call gitnexus_* tools.
- Visual contract: same flat surface as Feed. No card shadows, no rounded corners (radius 0). 1px var(--border-subtle) between cards. Hover background var(--row-hover).
- Each card height ~80px (header line + role chips line + footer line).
- Card selected state: 2px var(--accent) left border replacing the default 1px border-subtle.
- Status badges: small octicon + label. Use existing color tokens ŌĆö no new colors. Map: running=PlayIcon (--accent), waiting=ClockIcon (--text-secondary), done=CheckIcon (--text-muted), error=AlertIcon (--text-secondary, just text, no red flood ŌĆö operator's neutralized palette doesn't have a hot red), cancelled=XCircleIcon (--text-muted).
- Filter chips reuse the same `.drawer-log-chip` class pattern from LogsTabPanel.tsx (toggleable, active=is-active, monospace).
- Detail pane scrolls independently from the card list.
- Polling MUST pause when document.visibilityState !== 'visible'.
- All chain_id and bead_id values render in JBM. All titles in Inter.
- Touch-friendly: cards have 8px vertical padding so they're scannable but not cramped.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit clean.
- [ ] Manual: Console > Specialists renders the two-pane layout with at least 1 chain card visible.
- [ ] Manual: clicking a chain card populates the detail pane with chain timeline.
- [ ] Manual: filter chips work ŌĆö selecting "Running" hides done/error/cancelled chains.
- [ ] Manual: during a live specialist run, the chain card pulses; chain stops pulsing when status flips to done.
- [ ] Manual: "Open bead" button in detail pane opens the BeadSideDrawer (from forge-f6qk.4).
- [ ] Network tab: polling pauses when tab is hidden.
- [ ] Empty state renders when no chains exist (test with a fresh project).

## OUTPUT
- 7-file diff stat (1 replaced + 4 new + 1 CSS + 1 type)
- Playwright screenshot of Specialists tab with 1 chain selected and detail pane showing.
- Confirmation that no new backend endpoint was added (or, if absolutely required, the new endpoint's contract documented in OUTPUT).
- Confirmation that the existing BottomDrawer Specialists panel still works (no regression in drawer behavior).

## REFERENCES
- forge-gud9 (closed) ŌĆö drawer Specialists panel pattern (apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/SpecialistsTabPanel.tsx). REUSE patterns from there but DO NOT touch that file.
- forge-lqyc.8 ŌĆö future readonly specialist-feed xterm provider. After it lands, a follow-up task will mount it inside ChainDetailPane.tsx as a tab next to the structured view.

## APPROACH NOTES
- Aggregation strategy: /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight already returns `{in_flight, recent_history}`. Both have `chain_id`. Group by chain_id in the frontend to form chains. For chain detail, /api/specialists/chains/:chain_id already exists (per forge-3ue5.7).
- ChainCard's "dominant status" = the most recent job's status in that chain. If chain has any running/waiting job ŌåÆ use that. Otherwise ŌåÆ latest by updated_at.
- Roles in the chain card: derive from the union of `specialist` field across that chain's jobs.
- Detail pane chain timeline: render jobs sorted by chain_kind (executor first, then reviewer/sanity/etc., then any post-impl roles), then by updated_at ASC within each kind.
- For "last-message excerpt" ŌĆö use the latest job's `last_output` (already in /jobs response, truncate to 80 chars stripped of newlines).
- Performance: chain list re-renders only when /jobs response data hash changes. Use a single useMemo on `chains` derivation.

title: Bead side-drawer: reusable right-slide overlay for bead context
description:
## PROBLEM
Graph nodes and Feed rows both need a way to drill into a single bead's full context (description, notes, dossier sections, live + past specialists) without losing place. A modal kills the cross-tab pulse and scroll position; a full-page nav loses the graph; expanding inline breaks the graph layout. A right-slide side-drawer (separate from the existing bottom drawer) is the canonical "context pane" pattern ŌĆö bead lives on the right, the active tab stays underneath. Once it exists, Graph node-clicks and Feed row-clicks both target the same overlay.

## SUCCESS
- New component `BeadSideDrawer` renders a right-slide overlay (slide in from right, half-width, opaque) over the Console MainPane content.
- Opens with `useBeadSideDrawer().open(beadId)`; closes with `.close()` or ESC.
- Content layout: bead id (JBM), title (Inter), classification metadata strip (priority chip, type, status), specialist owner chip if live + run-count chip if any history; dossier sections (Description, Notes, Labels, Related, Audit log, Dependency tree, SPECIALIST ACTIVITY) reusing the existing `DossierSection` primitive from IssueFeed.tsx.
- Reuses existing hooks: `useSpecialistOwnership(beadId)`, `useSpecialistHistory(beadId)`. No new fetching layer.
- Closes on: ESC key, click outside the drawer, an explicit X button in the header.
- Persistent header with X button + bead id; body scrolls; footer hosts a "Open in Feed" button that switches Console tab to Feed and scrolls the row into view.
- Closing the drawer fires onClose callback; component manages its own open state via the shared hook.
- BottomDrawer (Ctrl+J) continues to coexist ŌĆö both can be open simultaneously without z-index fighting. SideDrawer is z-index 50; BottomDrawer is z-index 40.
- Mobile-friendly: viewport < 768px ŌåÆ drawer becomes full-screen overlay instead of half-width.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/BeadSideDrawer.tsx (NEW)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useBeadSideDrawer.ts (NEW ŌĆö Zustand or local-context-shaped store with .open / .close / .currentBeadId)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css (MOD ŌĆö .bead-side-drawer classes, slide animation, backdrop)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx (MOD ŌĆö mount <BeadSideDrawer /> at shell root inside ShellApp, sibling to MainPane)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/IssueFeed.tsx (MOD ŌĆö wire row-click to open the side-drawer instead of inline-expanding. KEEP the inline-expand toggle as an option ŌĆö the side-drawer is a NEW affordance, not a replacement.)

## NON_GOALS
- No bead editing inside the side-drawer. Click "Open in Feed" to mutate.
- No drag-to-resize on the side-drawer (fixed half-width on desktop, full-screen on mobile).
- No multi-bead navigation (back/forward arrows) in v1.
- No xterm pane inside the side-drawer in v1 ŌĆö same forge-lqyc.8 deferred polish.
- No replacement of the existing inline-expand dossier in IssueFeed ŌĆö both options coexist.

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: do NOT call gitnexus_* tools.
- HARD: do NOT modify the existing BottomDrawer. It stays independent.
- Slide animation: transform translateX, 200ms ease-out (faster than the bottom drawer's 80ms because side-drawer slides further).
- Backdrop: rgba(0,0,0,0.4) overlay on the rest of the screen, click ŌåÆ close.
- Visual contract: same as Feed ŌĆö var(--surface-primary) bg, JBM on IDs, Inter on title/sections, no new hex.
- Header height: 40px, X icon button (XIcon from @primer/octicons-react), aria-label "close bead drawer".
- ESC key handler must NOT conflict with the BottomDrawer's Ctrl+J (different key).
- Drawer state lives in module-scope (Zustand or singleton) so any tab can call `open(beadId)` without prop drilling.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit clean.
- [ ] Manual: clicking a row in Feed opens the side-drawer with that bead's dossier.
- [ ] Manual: pressing ESC closes the side-drawer.
- [ ] Manual: clicking the backdrop closes the side-drawer.
- [ ] Manual: opening the side-drawer + Ctrl+J (bottom drawer) ŌåÆ both visible, no z-index fight.
- [ ] Manual: Open-in-Feed button switches Console tab to Feed if not already there and scrolls the row into view.
- [ ] Mobile viewport (< 768px): drawer goes full-screen.
- [ ] Playwright integration: useBeadSideDrawer.open is callable from outside React tree.

## OUTPUT
- 5-file diff stat.
- Playwright screenshot of side-drawer open over Feed tab.
- Playwright screenshot of side-drawer + bottom drawer co-open.
- Confirmation that IssueFeed.tsx's inline-expand still works (no regression in run-count chip or SPECIALIST ACTIVITY section).

## APPROACH NOTES
- Mount point: <ShellApp> in App.tsx, sibling of <MainPane>. The drawer needs to live above MainPane to receive backdrop clicks.
- Hook shape: `useBeadSideDrawer(): { beadId: string | null; open(id: string): void; close(): void }`. Backed by Zustand for cross-tree communication.
- Reuse `<IssueDossier />` exported from IssueFeed.tsx ŌĆö same component that renders the inline-expand body. Pass the same props (issue, detail, projectId, issueById).
- For Open-in-Feed: use existing `useShellStore` setSurface/setTab + a DOM scroll-into-view by bead-id selector on the .row element.
- Don't reinvent dossier rendering inside the drawer ŌĆö DossierSection + SafeMarkdown are already exported from IssueFeed.tsx.

title: Graph frontend: layered SVG renderer with full-relationship layout + node pulse + interactions
description:
## PROBLEM
The Graph backend (forge-f6qk.2) ships a payload of nodes + edges + live specialist overlay. The frontend needs to turn that into a single SVG canvas that lays out 250+ nodes legibly, encodes 9 distinct edge types visually, pulses the currently-running agent on its bead, and supports hover-highlight + click-to-side-drawer. The reference design (docs/graph/reference.html) shows the visual contract for 8 nodes; this bead scales it to the operator's real bead set and rewires the palette/fonts to the app's existing token system.

## SUCCESS
- New page mounted at Console > Graph (replaces the placeholder from forge-f6qk.1).
- Fetches /api/console/graph?project_id=<current-repo> on mount + on focus.
- Computes node positions via a layered LR layout that uses ALL 9 edge types as inputs (not blocks-only). `blocks` and `supersedes` are weighted heavier as scheduling primitives; `parent-child` and `related` are weighted lighter so they don't force layer changes. Specific algorithm: weighted dag-layered layout (e.g. dagre or a lightweight in-app port), with edge weights {blocks: 5, supersedes: 3, parent-child: 0.5, discovered-from: 2, validates: 2, caused-by: 2, tracks: 1, until: 1, related: 0.2}. Multi-edges between the same pair get arc routing (top + bottom) and are NEVER merged into a single line.
- Each node rendered per the reference HTML's anatomy: 3px priority band (left edge, color per priority: P0 red, P1 amber, P2-P4 dim), state glyph (ŌŚ» ready, ŌŚÉ in_progress, ŌŚć blocked, ŌŚł epic, Ō£ō closed, Ō£Ģ superseded), JBM bead-id (suffix bright, prefix dim), Inter title (truncate with ellipsis), small live-pulse dot on the left of the id when the bead has a running specialist.
- Pulse animation is 1.6s ease-in-out, applied to the dot AND a small ring around the agent badge ŌĆö ONLY animation in the graph (per detail.md rule).
- Edges encoded per the reference HTML's palette but using app tokens for color (operators see the same neutral grays as Feed; the 9 edge colors are local SVG `stroke` values derived from the reference HTML's hex set, namespaced as CSS custom properties --graph-edge-blocks / --graph-edge-tracks / etc. so future theme work can override).
- Arrowhead direction is sourceŌåÆeffect for blocks, supersedes, caused-by, validates, discovered-from, parent-child, tracks, until. `related` has no arrowhead.
- Hover a node ŌåÆ all incident edges get full opacity, all non-incident edges + nodes drop to 30% opacity. Hover off ŌåÆ restore.
- Click a node ŌåÆ fires `onNodeClick(beadId)` callback. The Graph component itself does not mount the side-drawer; it just emits the event. forge-f6qk.4 wires the side-drawer at the Console-tab level.
- Closed beads render at 45% opacity with dashed node border; superseded adds strikethrough on the title and shows the supersedes arrow toward the new bead.
- Pan/zoom via mouse drag + scroll wheel. Reset-view button (octicon SyncIcon) in the top-right corner of the panel.
- Performance target: 250 nodes / 400 edges render < 200ms after data arrival; hover-highlight is instant (no re-layout, only opacity).

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/Graph.tsx (REPLACE the placeholder with the real component)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/layout.ts (NEW) ŌĆö pure layout algorithm; takes nodes + edges + weights, returns positioned nodes + routed edges
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/GraphSvg.tsx (NEW) ŌĆö pure SVG renderer; takes positioned data + interaction callbacks, no fetching
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/edge-styles.ts (NEW) ŌĆö exports color/stroke-width/dash-pattern per edge type
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGraphData.ts (NEW) ŌĆö fetch + cache + on-focus refetch for /api/console/graph
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css (MOD ŌĆö add .graph-* classes + --graph-edge-* tokens + pulse keyframes; tiny additions, keep flat with Feed style)
- apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/graph/layout.test.ts (NEW) ŌĆö pure unit tests for the layout function with fixture data

## NON_GOALS
- No xterm pane on node click ŌĆö that's a future polish tracking forge-lqyc.8. Click opens the structured side-drawer only.
- No graph editing / bead mutations from this view.
- No real-time WS subscription. Refetch on focus + on visibility change is sufficient for v1.
- No filtering chips (per edge type / per priority / per agent) ŌĆö those are explicit follow-ups for the v2 polish bead, not this one.
- No mini-map / overview pane.
- No node grouping by parent-child collapse / expand.
- No new dependency on a graph library beyond what's already in deps. If dagre is needed, prefer porting a 200-line layered-layout function inline.

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: do NOT call gitnexus_* tools.
- HARD visual contract:
  - Background: var(--surface-primary) (matches Feed bg)
  - Node card background: var(--surface-primary) ŌĆö same as bg
  - Node border: 1px solid var(--border-subtle); priority band on the left is the only accent
  - Title font: Inter (var(--font-ui))
  - ID font: JBM (var(--font-mono))
  - Active/hover state border: var(--accent)
  - NO new colors outside --graph-edge-* CSS custom properties and the existing app tokens
- HARD layout rule: layered LR, all 9 edge types weighted. Document the weights in a comment at the top of layout.ts.
- HARD multi-edge rule: same-pair edges get top-arc + bottom-arc routing; reference HTML lines 633-652 show the pattern.
- HARD: arrowhead direction is invariant sourceŌåÆeffect for all 8 directed types; related has no arrowhead.
- Pulse animation must be the only animation in the graph component. No hover transitions on nodes beyond opacity. No edge animations.
- Closed beads in graph: same node, 45% opacity, dashed border. Do not remove them; they're context.
- Component must be SSR-safe ŌĆö guard window/document access.
- For repos with no beads: render an empty-state with "No beads in this project" + a small octicon, NOT a blank canvas.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit clean.
- [ ] bun --bun vitest run apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/graph/layout.test.ts passes (covers: layered ordering, multi-edge top/bottom arc, closed/superseded preservation, weight-tuned column assignment).
- [ ] Manual: Console > Graph for the gitboard project renders all current beads with at least 3 distinct edge types visible.
- [ ] Manual: hover any node ŌåÆ incident edges highlight; non-incident dim.
- [ ] Manual: click any node ŌåÆ console logs (or stubs) the bead id (side-drawer wiring in forge-f6qk.4).
- [ ] Manual: a bead with a known running specialist (during a test dispatch) pulses live.
- [ ] Playwright: graph canvas DOM contains >= 100 SVG nodes for the gitboard project after first fetch.
- [ ] No new hex colors outside --graph-edge-* (grep sweep: `rg -n '#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}' apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/` matches only inside edge-styles.ts and the CSS).

## OUTPUT

title: Graph backend: /api/console/graph endpoint with 9 edge types + specialist join
description:
## PROBLEM
The Graph tab needs a single GraphQL-shape endpoint that returns the full topology for the selected repo: every bead, every relationship, plus a live specialist overlay so the frontend can paint the agent-pulse on running beads without a second round-trip. Today the dashboard would have to call bd via subprocess or hit beads-api per bead ŌĆö neither scales to 250+ bead graphs. Need one endpoint, one fetch, payload < 250kb for typical repos.

## SUCCESS
- New endpoint: GET /api/console/graph?project_id=<id>&include_closed=true|false (default false).
- Returns JSON:
  ```
  {
    project_id: string,
    repo_slug: string,
    generated_at: string (ISO),
    nodes: Array<{
      id: string,              // bead id, e.g. "forge-gud9"
      title: string,
      type: "task"|"bug"|"feature"|"epic"|"chore"|"decision"|"molecule",
      priority: 0|1|2|3|4,
      status: "open"|"in_progress"|"blocked"|"closed"|"deferred",
      assignee: string|null,
      closed_at: string|null,
      superseded_by: string|null,   // resolved from supersedes edge if exists
    }>,
    edges: Array<{
      from: string,            // source bead id
      to: string,              // target bead id
      type: "blocks"|"tracks"|"related"|"parent-child"|"discovered-from"|"validates"|"caused-by"|"until"|"supersedes",
    }>,
    specialists: Array<{
      bead_id: string,
      job_id: string,
      role: string,            // executor / reviewer / etc.
      status: "starting"|"running"|"waiting"|"done"|"error"|"cancelled",
      updated_at: string (ISO),
    }>
  }
  ```
- All 9 bd edge types preserved verbatim in the `edges` array.
- The `specialists` overlay only includes jobs with status IN {starting, running, waiting} (live set). Closed runs come from a separate per-bead query, not this endpoint.
- Endpoint scoped to a single project (`?project_id=...`). Default project resolution falls back to the selected repo's beads project name.
- Endpoint reads bd via the existing beads-api bridge (same path /api/beads/* uses), NOT via subprocess.
- New endpoint mounted under /api/console/* (consistent with /api/console/observability already in place).

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/graph.ts (NEW)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts (MOD ŌĆö mount the new router at /api/console/graph)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/graph-dao.ts (NEW) ŌĆö pure query layer; reads from beads-api + observability attach-pool
- apps/gitboard/src/types/graph.ts (NEW) ŌĆö shared type definitions for the response shape above
- apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/graph.test.ts (NEW) ŌĆö endpoint integration test with fixture project

## NON_GOALS
- No frontend changes ŌĆö forge-f6qk.3 consumes this endpoint.
- No graph layout computation (positions, edge routing) ŌĆö also forge-f6qk.3 responsibility.
- No write endpoint ŌĆö read-only.
- No real-time WS feed of graph changes ŌĆö the frontend will refetch on tab focus + on bead state change events.
- No mass-edit endpoint ŌĆö Graph cannot mutate beads in this epic.
- No payload pagination ŌĆö assume single-project graphs fit in one response.

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: do NOT call gitnexus_* tools.
- Use the beads-api bridge already in apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts to query beads + edges. Do NOT shell out to `bd`.
- For the specialist overlay, reuse the existing observability dao (apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/dao.ts) ŌĆö its inFlightJobs() method returns exactly the shape needed. Filter to the current project's repo_slug.
- Endpoint must return 200 with an empty graph (nodes=[], edges=[], specialists=[]) when the project has no beads ŌĆö never 404.
- Edge `type` strings must match bd's exact identifiers (use hyphenated forms: "parent-child", "discovered-from", "caused-by"). No camelCase, no enum aliases.
- Closed beads are excluded by default; include_closed=true opts in. Superseded beads always come along because they're referenced by supersedes edges.
- Response must serialize cleanly to JSON; no circular references, no Map/Set leaking through.
- For repos that have NO observability.db, the `specialists` array is empty (not undefined, not error). Gracefully skip.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit clean.
- [ ] bun --bun vitest run apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/graph.test.ts passes (fixture has 6 beads, 4 edges across at least 3 distinct edge types).
- [ ] Manual: curl localhost:3000/api/console/graph?project_id=gitboard returns nodes+edges+specialists.
- [ ] Manual: payload size for the current gitboard project (250+ beads) is under 250kb.
- [ ] include_closed=false vs true returns differing node counts; include_closed=false still includes any supersedes-edge targets even if closed.

## OUTPUT
- Diff list (2 new files + 1 modification + 1 type file + 1 test).
- Sample response payload pasted in the close note (truncated to ~30 lines).
- Confirmation that all 9 edge types appear in the bd source schema (run `bd link --type=<each>` smoke test before close).
- Payload size benchmark for current gitboard project.

## APPROACH NOTES
- beads-api bridge: see apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:6 for the `beadsRoutes` import + mounting. Same DB client surface is reusable in graph-dao.ts.
- bd's JSON output via `bd dep list <id> --json` shows the `dependency_type` field ŌĆö that's the source of edge type names. Confirmed in this session.
- The observability dao already returns `{jobId, repoSlug, beadId, status, updatedAt, specialist}` ŌĆö strip to the 5 fields needed for the specialists overlay.
- A clean way to fetch the FULL edge set across a project is to query the beads-api with `--json` for the project's bead list, then bulk `bd dep list <id1> <id2> ...` with --json. Verify the batch syntax in graph-dao.ts.

title: IA restructure: drop Beads surface, flatten Console tabs
description:
## PROBLEM
Current shell has three Surfaces (github, beads, console). Beads holds its own sub-tabs (feed, triage, memories), Console holds its own (observability, specialists placeholder). Operator wants a single operational surface ŌĆö drop Beads as a Surface, and put feed/triage/memories alongside observability/specialists/graph as Console's flat tab strip. Until this restructure lands, there's nowhere for the new Graph and live Specialists tabs to mount that makes IA sense.

## SUCCESS
- TopBar surface switcher renders exactly two buttons: GitHub, Console.
- Console renders a tab strip with six labels in this order: Feed, Triage, Memories, Graph, Observability, Specialists.
- Tab order is canonical (defined in types/shell.ts), not free-form in the JSX.
- Selecting Feed renders the existing BeadsRepoView in 'feed' mode; Triage ŌåÆ kanban; Memories ŌåÆ memories list. Behaviorally identical to today's Beads sub-tabs.
- Graph + Specialists tabs render placeholder panels that say "Coming in forge-f6qk.3 / forge-f6qk.5" with a small octicon. (Real content lands in later children.)
- Observability tab unchanged (still renders pages/console/Observability.tsx).
- Legacy URL deep-links resolve correctly on first load:
  - /gitboard/beads ŌåÆ Console > Feed
  - /gitboard/beads/feed ŌåÆ Console > Feed
  - /gitboard/beads/triage ŌåÆ Console > Triage
  - /gitboard/beads/memories ŌåÆ Console > Memories
  - /gitboard/console ŌåÆ Console > Observability (unchanged default)
  - /gitboard/console/graph ŌåÆ Console > Graph
- localStorage selection key 'forge-5w9:selection' continues to persist surface+tab+repo. Operators with persisted surface='beads' get auto-migrated to surface='console' with tab=feed on first load.

title: Operational Console ŌĆö flatten Beads into Console + add Graph + Specialists tabs
description:
## PROBLEM
Gitboard's IDE shell currently exposes three top-level Surfaces (GitHub | Beads | Console) but Beads and Console are conceptually the same thing ŌĆö operator-facing operational state. Console is already where observability lives; Beads is where issues live; but specialists run on beads, beads share observability surfaces, and the user mentally treats both as "what we're working on and how it's running." Keeping them as separate top-level surfaces creates redundant navigation (two clicks to flip context that's actually the same context) and hides the cross-tab observability spine that's already wired (live specialist chip, run-count chip, drawer Specialists panel).

The Graph view requested in `docs/graph/reference.html` is the missing operational primitive: see every bead and its 9-relationship-type neighborhood at a glance, with live specialist pulse on currently-running beads. It cannot live anywhere except Console because it spans every other Console tab (Feed, Specialists, Observability).

## SUCCESS
- TopBar surface switcher shows only two tabs: GitHub | Console.
- Console renders a flat horizontal tab strip with six siblings: Feed ┬Ę Triage ┬Ę Memories ┬Ę Graph ┬Ę Observability ┬Ę Specialists. No nested sub-tabs.
- /gitboard/beads, /gitboard/beads/feed, /gitboard/beads/triage, /gitboard/beads/memories URLs still resolve (set selection.surface='console', selection.tab=<mapped>) so existing bookmarks don't break.
- Graph tab renders the full-relationship operational dep-graph for the selected repo's beads. Live specialist pulse on running beads. Hover-highlights incident edges. Click-to-side-drawer for bead drill-in. Visual style matches IssueFeed tokens (no custom palette).
- Specialists tab renders the live cockpit: chain cards grouped by root bead, status filter chips, click-card ŌåÆ structured detail pane on the right. Hooked to ship an xterm specialist-feed pane later when forge-lqyc.8 lands (tracks edge).
- Side-drawer (right-slide overlay) shows bead dossier + specialist activity timeline for any bead, mounted from Graph node click + Feed row click.
- All new surfaces use the same observability hooks already wired (useAllSpecialistJobs, useSpecialistOwnership, useSpecialistHistory) ŌĆö no parallel data layer.
- tsc clean. Existing tests pass. New tests added per surface.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/types/shell.ts (Surface + Tab union restructure)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx (URL deep-link mapping for legacy /beads paths)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/TopBar.tsx (surface switcher: drop Beads, keep Console)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/MainPane.tsx (six-way tab routing under Console)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/graph.ts (NEW ŌĆö bd dep + bead state + specialist join)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/Graph.tsx (NEW ŌĆö full graph view)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/Specialists.tsx (NEW ŌĆö live cockpit, replaces placeholder)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/BeadSideDrawer.tsx (NEW ŌĆö right slide-in overlay)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/Observability.tsx (untouched as panel, mounted via new tab routing)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css (Console tab strip + graph + side-drawer classes only)
- apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/ (NEW ŌĆö per-tab integration tests)

## NON_GOALS
- No xterm specialist-feed pane in v1 ŌĆö that's a soft `tracks` dep on forge-lqyc.8. Structured detail pane is the v1 deliverable.
- No new bd CLI edge types ŌĆö use the 9 already in bd v1.0.3 (blocks, tracks, related, parent-child, discovered-from, validates, caused-by, until, supersedes).
- No bead editing from the Graph view. Click-to-drawer is read-only context; mutations stay in Feed/Triage.
- No real-time WS for the graph topology in v1 ŌĆö poll on focus + after mutations only. Graph is operational view, not realtime collaboration.
- No GitHub tab restructure. Surface=github stays as-is.

## CONSTRAINTS
- Visual contract: every new surface uses existing CSS variables (--surface-primary, --text-primary, --text-muted, --accent, --border-subtle, --row-hover). NO hardcoded hex. NO new gray-scale tokens. NO local font declarations ŌĆö Inter for chrome, JBM (var(--font-mono)) only for IDs / job-ids / numbers / percentages / bead-ids / SHA.
- Octicons (@primer/octicons-react) only ŌĆö no SVG, no emoji.
- Radius 0 across all new chrome (matches PR #11 neutralized palette).
- Bottom drawer continues to work in every Console tab ŌĆö do not break the drawer.
- Graph layout MUST use all 9 relationship types as scheduling inputs, NOT blocks-only. `blocks` edges get visual weight (heavier stroke) but do not exclusively determine column depth.
- Layered LR layout. Multi-edge between same pair = two arcs (top + bottom), never merged. Arrowhead direction is sourceŌåÆeffect for all 8 directed types; `related` has no arrowhead.
- Closed beads remain in the graph at ~45% opacity with dashed border. Superseded adds strikethrough on the title. Both stay as edge endpoints.
- forge-lqyc must NOT be edited by this epic. Tracks edge only.
- The bead side-drawer is a separate component from BottomDrawer (which is the bottom log/specialist drawer). Two distinct primitives.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Surface switcher in TopBar shows only GitHub | Console after merge.
- [ ] Clicking Console reveals 6 tabs: Feed ┬Ę Triage ┬Ę Memories ┬Ę Graph ┬Ę Observability ┬Ę Specialists, in that order.
- [ ] Old URL /gitboard/beads/feed loads with Console > Feed selected.
- [ ] Graph tab renders >= 10 nodes from current main bead set with at least 3 distinct edge types visible.
- [ ] Graph node click opens side-drawer with bead dossier.
- [ ] Specialists tab shows live cockpit list with at least 1 chain card visible during a known specialist run.
- [ ] bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit clean.
- [ ] New integration tests pass: bunx vitest run apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/.
- [ ] Manual Playwright: surface switch ŌåÆ tab switch ŌåÆ graph node click ŌåÆ side-drawer open ŌåÆ close ŌåÆ drawer toggle (Ctrl+J) still works.

## OUTPUT
- Diff statistics per child bead handoff (files changed, LoC added/removed).
- tsc + vitest tail showing green.
- One Playwright screenshot per new Console tab (Graph, Specialists, side-drawer over Graph).
- Confirmation that forge-lqyc was NOT modified.
- Confirmation that no new hex colors / non-token fonts were introduced (rg sweep over the new files).
- Residual risks list (e.g. graph perf at 200+ nodes, xterm pane landing path).

## REFERENCES
- docs/graph/reference.html ŌĆö static graph design spec (9 edge types, glyphs, agent pulse, multi-arc). USE FOR STRUCTURE/GLYPHS/EDGE ENCODING ONLY. Colors/fonts come from app tokens, not the reference.
- docs/graph/detail.md ŌĆö implementation rules (layout, arrowhead invariants, multi-edge handling, hover/click hooks)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/IssueFeed.tsx ŌĆö VISUAL ANCHOR. Match its row density, font usage, chip styling.
- forge-lqyc ŌĆö terminal stream substrate epic (soft tracks dep)
- forge-lqyc.8 ŌĆö readonly specialist-feed xterm provider (specifically the pane this epic will mount later)
- bd v1.0.3 ŌĆö confirmed supports all 9 edge types live (blocks, tracks, related, parent-child, discovered-from, validates, caused-by, until, supersedes)
- PR #11 (commit 968eb9e) ŌĆö established neutralized palette + Inter chrome that this epic must honor

title: Embedded terminal stream substrate and drawer terminal
description:
PROBLEM: Gitboard needs an embedded terminal capability without coupling the UI to one backend process type or accidentally exposing a raw shell in unsafe deployments. Operators should be able to run project-local commands from the dashboard, and the same terminal renderer should later support readonly specialist job feeds and other stream sources.
SUCCESS: Epic delivers a provider-agnostic terminal-stream substrate, a local/dev PTY-backed drawer terminal as the first write-capable provider, safety gates for shell-capable providers, and a path for specialist-feed reuse. tmux remains optional and SSH/Tailscale remains future-scoped.
SCOPE: Bottom drawer tab/store/UI; reusable xterm-based TerminalStream component; shared terminal stream protocol types; backend terminal provider/session manager; websocket bridge; safety/config surface; focused tests for the substrate and providers.
NON_GOALS: No mandatory tmux dependency for MVP; no SSH/Tailscale implementation; no production raw-shell exposure by default; no full Specialists tab redesign beyond proving readonly stream reuse.
CONSTRAINTS: Keep terminal rendering separate from process/session ownership. Design provider kinds for pty, tmux, ssh, command, and specialist-feed without implementing all providers. Shell-capable providers must be explicitly enabled outside local/dev. No command input or secrets in logs.

title: Integrate Console into shell as a third Surface tab (drop /console URL split)
description:
## PROBLEM
forge-che shipped /gitboard/console as a separate top-level route with its own minimal shell ŌĆö it is detached from the rest of the app (no sidebar, no topbar theme toggle integration, no repo-aware filtering later). Operator wants Console integrated as a third Surface tab in the existing IDE shell alongside GitHub and Beads, not a separate URL.

## SUCCESS
- TopBar surface switcher has three tabs: GitHub | Beads | Console.
- Selecting Console renders the existing ObservabilityPanel inside the MainPane (right side of the IDE shell), keeping Sidebar visible on the left.
- Console surface has its OWN tab strip (the existing tab row below the surface switcher), containing v1 tab 'Observability' (the panel we shipped). Placeholder 'Specialists' tab disabled with 'coming soon' (reserved for forge-e23).
- /gitboard/console URL still works as a deep-link: on App boot if the path ends with /console, programmatically set selection.surface='console' in shellStore THEN render normal <ShellApp/>. No more separate Console.tsx wrapper rendered standalone.
- Theme toggle stays in the topbar (already there). Sidebar stays open (the repo list is irrelevant for Console v1 but may host filters in v2 ŌĆö keep visible, no shrinking).

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/types/shell.ts
  - Surface type: 'github' | 'beads' | 'console'
  - new ConsoleTab type: 'observability' | 'specialists'
  - export CONSOLE_TABS = [{ id: 'observability', label: 'Observability' }, { id: 'specialists', label: 'Specialists' }]
  - extend TabId union with ConsoleTab
  - extend DEFAULT_TAB with console: 'observability'
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/TopBar.tsx
  - Add 'Console' button to the surface switcher (after Beads). Use a fitting octicon (GraphIcon or PulseIcon).
  - Add a tab strip rendering for the Console surface (3rd branch in the existing if/else chain): map CONSOLE_TABS; the 'specialists' tab is rendered but disabled (aria-disabled, muted color, title 'Coming soon ŌĆö forge-e23') and clicks are no-ops in v1.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/MainPane.tsx
  - When selection.surface === 'console', render the Observability panel (import the existing pages/console/Observability.tsx ŌĆö keep the file location, just stop wrapping it in its own page chrome).
  - The panel must NOT include its own outer page chrome anymore (no full-screen padding, no big 'Jobs stats' header that competes with the existing topbar). Slim it down so it fits under the topbar tab strip ŌĆö keep the 11 sections and the 7d/30d/all range toggle, but drop the OmniForge Console header and the 100vh min-height.
  - Use the same key invariant the MainPane currently uses for surface:tab:repo rerenders.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/Console.tsx
  - DELETE this file (was the standalone wrapper).
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/Observability.tsx
  - REMOVE shellStyle (no min-height: 100vh), padding wrapper, and the 'Observability / Jobs stats' big header. Keep only the 7d/30d/all toggle and the 11 metric sections. The MainPane already provides the framed layout.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx
  - REMOVE the `if (path === "/console" || ...) return <Console />;` branch.
  - ADD pre-render: if path matches /\/console$/, before returning <ShellApp/>, call useShellStore.getState().setSurface('console'). Just once, on first load, no router needed.

## NON_GOALS
- No new metrics. No charts. No new endpoints.
- Do not change observability backend/dao/route.
- Do not modify Console.tsx file rename ŌĆö just delete (its content moves into MainPane's surface switch).

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: no gitnexus_* calls.
- Match visual contract of the rest of the shell ŌĆö flat surface, JBM on numbers, octicons only, no shadow/gradients.
- Keep the surface switcher accessible: aria-selected, role tab/tablist preserved.
- LocalStorage selection persists with surface='console' ŌĆö confirm via the existing forge-5w9:selection write path.

## VALIDATION
- bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit clean
- Manual via Playwright: navigate to /gitboard, click Console surface tab ŌåÆ ObservabilityPanel renders inside MainPane with sidebar still visible; click GitHub ŌåÆ ObservabilityPanel disappears, normal GitHub view renders. Navigate to /gitboard/console ŌåÆ boots directly into Console surface.

- Files changed (Ōēż6)
- Confirmation that pages/console/Console.tsx is deleted
- Confirmation that the Console surface integrates inside the existing ShellApp
- Confirmation that /gitboard/console deep-link still works

title: Drawer drag broken at default size + add maximize toggle
description:
## PROBLEM
Operator: drag-to-resize visually starts but nothing changes. Root cause: BottomDrawer.tsx has a hardcoded `MAX_HEIGHT = 600` used BOTH inside the rendered `style={{ height: clampedHeight }}` (via local clamp) AND inside the move handler. On any viewport > 800px tall, the store's default (75vh-derived) opens the drawer AT this 600 ceiling, so dragging up immediately hits the wall and no movement is visible.

Live evidence: programmatic mousedown + window mousemove from y=423 ŌåÆ y=20 with viewport=807 ŌåÆ drawer.style.height stayed at "600px". With MAX_HEIGHT=600 in the component, every value above 600 gets clamped flat.

Also missing: maximize affordance (operator request).

## SUCCESS
1. Drag actually resizes both up and down on any viewport.
2. Component no longer carries its own MAX_HEIGHT. Source of truth = the store's setDrawerHeight clamp (already clamps to [120, window.innerHeight - 100]).
3. New "maximize" icon button in the drawer header actions (next to the X close button), using `@primer/octicons-react` `ScreenFullIcon` when not-maximized / `ScreenNormalIcon` when maximized. Click toggles between current drawer height and a maximized height (window.innerHeight - 60 ŌĆö keep topbar visible).
4. Maximize state is transient (NOT persisted to localStorage). On reload, drawer comes back at the last user-resized height.
5. After unmaximize, drawer returns to whatever height it had before maximize was hit.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawer.tsx ŌĆö remove `MAX_HEIGHT` const + remove component-local `clamp(height, MIN_HEIGHT, MAX_HEIGHT)`. Render `style={{ height }}` directly (store already clamps writes). In the resize move handler, no clamp either ŌĆö pass raw `window.innerHeight - moveEvent.clientY` to `setDrawerHeight` (store clamps).
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawerTabBar.tsx ŌĆö add a maximize toggle icon button before the X close button. Wire from BottomDrawer via new props `isMaximized: boolean; onToggleMaximize(): void`.
- BottomDrawer.tsx: hold maximize state in `useState<number | null>(null)` storing the pre-max height. When maximize fires, snapshot current height to state and call setDrawerHeight(window.innerHeight - 60). When unmaximize fires, call setDrawerHeight(snapshot) and clear state. isMaximized = `snapshot !== null`.

## NON_GOALS
- No changes to store, dao, routes, tests, tabs, styling tokens
- No changes to Ctrl+J behavior
- No persistence of maximize state

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: do NOT call gitnexus_*. Read files directly.
- Keep the existing fully-hide-on-close behavior (return null when !open).
- Keep the resize hit area + visible hairline styling untouched (it's working ŌĆö operator confirmed visible).
- Drawer body must remain scrollable when height grows.

## VALIDATION
- bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit clean
- Manual: orchestrator will Playwright-verify post-merge that drag works and maximize button toggles correctly.

## OUTPUT
- Files changed (Ōēż 2)
- Confirmation MAX_HEIGHT const removed from BottomDrawer.tsx
- Confirmation maximize button uses ScreenFullIcon/ScreenNormalIcon from @primer/octicons-react
notes:
Removed BottomDrawer local max clamp, added transient maximize toggle, preserved store clamp as source of truth.

title: Fix forge-gud9 drawer UX: full-hide on close, top-edge resize, 75vh default
description:
## PROBLEM
Operator feedback on shipped forge-gud9 drawer:
1. Closing drawer (X button or Ctrl/Cmd+J) leaves a 120px tab-bar strip visible at the bottom of the viewport. User expects the drawer to be fully hidden when closed.
2. The resize handle (.bottom-drawer-resizer) renders at the BOTTOM edge of the drawer (DOM order: tabbar ŌåÆ body ŌåÆ resizer ŌåÆ close button). User expects the resize handle on the TOP edge (between MainPane content and the drawer) ŌĆö that is the universal pattern.
3. Default drawerHeight on first open is 240px. Operator wants 75% of viewport height (~600px on typical 800px viewport).

Verified live (Playwright on http://localhost:5173/gitboard/):
- close button works (data-open flips to false) but drawer height stays at 120px because `.bottom-drawer { min-height: 120px }` clamps it
- resizer rect yŌēł803, viewport 807 ŌåÆ resizer is at bottom edge
- Ctrl+J also works (toggles data-open), same min-height residual issue

## SUCCESS
- When drawerOpen=false, the entire `<section className="bottom-drawer">` is fully hidden (display: none OR height: 0 + no contents rendered). NO 120px residual strip.
- Ctrl/Cmd+J re-opens the drawer (only way to reopen once closed ŌĆö that's the VSCode pattern, and the topbar can keep a future "Toggle Panel" button as follow-up, not in this bead).
- The resize handle is at the TOP edge of the drawer, not the bottom. Drag UP grows the drawer, drag DOWN shrinks. Existing math `window.innerHeight - moveEvent.clientY` already supports this ŌĆö just needs DOM/visual repositioning.
- Resize handle has a small visible cue (1-2px line in --bottom-drawer-border, slightly brighter on :hover via --accent, cursor: row-resize). Currently it's `background: transparent` ŌĆö invisible.
- Default drawerHeight (when no localStorage value): `Math.floor(window.innerHeight * 0.75)`, clamped to [MIN_HEIGHT, MAX_HEIGHT]. Recompute MAX_HEIGHT ŌĆö currently 600 caps below 75% of a 1080 viewport (810). Raise MAX_HEIGHT to `window.innerHeight - 100` (leave room for topbar).
- After this fix, opening on a fresh localStorage gives a drawer that fills ~75% of the viewport. Resize handle on top edge drags up/down. Close fully hides drawer including tab bar.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawer.tsx (DOM order rearrangement, conditional render of whole section when !open OR explicit display: none, resizer first child)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/shell.ts (initialDrawerHeight = 75vh-derived, MAX_HEIGHT raised; persistence + clamp logic preserved)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css (resizer visible 2px top line + hover state, .bottom-drawer-resizer position now top of drawer body)
- apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/stores/shell.test.ts (update clamp test to new MAX, ensure 75vh-on-first-load default behavior verified)

## NON_GOALS
- No new tab kinds, no new filters, no Logs/Specialists tab content changes
- No keyboard shortcut additions beyond Ctrl/Cmd+J
- No new topbar button (deferred to follow-up bead if user asks)
- Do NOT change SpecialistsTabPanel, LogsTabPanel, useSystemLogs, useAllSpecialistJobs, dao.ts, routes/specialists.ts

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: do NOT call ANY gitnexus_* MCP tool (known stall pattern ŌĆö see memory `gitnexus-context-mcp-call-stalls-explorers`). Read files directly with bash/rg/cat. Bootstrap may not be needed since node_modules persists in main repo ŌĆö but if working in a fresh worktree, run `bun install` first.
- The drawer min-height when open stays 120px (lower bound). MAX_HEIGHT can be raised but should not exceed `window.innerHeight - 100` to avoid covering topbar.
- Resize math `window.innerHeight - moveEvent.clientY` already gives drag-from-top-edge behavior; verify with manual reasoning that dragging the top edge UP increases height (yes: clientY decreases ŌåÆ innerHeight-clientY increases).
- Preserve existing localStorage keys (`forge-gud9:drawerOpen`, `:drawerHeight`, `:drawerTab`) so no regression for users who already opened the drawer once.
- When `drawerHeight` was previously saved (e.g., user dragged to 400px), keep that value; only apply the 75vh default on fresh load (`readJSON` fallback).
- For first-load 75vh: cannot evaluate `window.innerHeight` at module-load if SSR is a concern. Use a lazy initializer / function:
  ```ts
  const initialDrawerHeight = readJSON<number | null>(LS.drawerHeight, null) ?? defaultDrawerHeight();
  function defaultDrawerHeight() { return typeof window !== 'undefined' ? Math.floor(window.innerHeight * 0.75) : 600; }
  ```
- Drawer fully-hidden when closed: simplest path = early-return `null` from `<BottomDrawer />` when `!open`. Then re-mount on Ctrl+J. State persists in zustand. This drops the tab-bar-visible-when-closed affordance; that is the intended new behavior per operator.

## VALIDATION
- `bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit` exits 0
- `bunx vitest run apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/stores/shell.test.ts` passes (update test as needed)
- Manual verification post-merge by orchestrator via Playwright: localStorage cleared ŌåÆ drawer opens to ~75vh on first Ctrl+J; resize handle is visible on top edge; close fully hides drawer

## OUTPUT
- List of changed files
- tsc + vitest tail
- Confirmation: when !drawerOpen the bottom-drawer section is not in the DOM
- Confirmation: default drawerHeight is 75vh-derived for fresh users
- Confirmation: resizer is the first element inside the drawer (or positioned absolutely on top edge with z-index)
notes:
Drawer fully hides on close; resizer moved to top edge; default drawerHeight now 75vh-derived with window clamp to innerHeight-100; verified via vitest + tsc.

title: Inline specialist history in feed: run-count chip + dossier timeline
description:
## PROBLEM
gitboard's feed shows the live specialist chip on in_progress beads (9219bc9), but once a bead closes there's no trace of the specialists that ran on it. The observability.db retains the rows; the UI just doesn't surface them.

## SUCCESS
Every bead row that ever had Ōēź1 specialist run on it shows a subtle counter inline (e.g. `┬Ę 3 runs`). Clicking to expand the row reveals a vertical SPECIALIST ACTIVITY timeline in the dossier with one line per past job ŌĆö specialist ┬Ę status ┬Ę job_id ┬Ę timestamp ┬Ę last_output excerpt.

The live chip behavior from 9219bc9 stays unchanged for in_progress beads. The counter is for closed/open beads that had specialist history.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/IssueFeed.tsx (add counter inline next to existing chip slot)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/IssueDossier.tsx (add timeline section)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useSpecialistHistory.ts (new ŌĆö fetches all rows for a bead, NOT just live)
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/dao.ts (add jobsByBead variant that returns ALL rows including closed history)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts (extend /jobs?bead_id=ŌĆ” to accept &include_history=1)

## NON_GOALS
- Drawer Specialists tab (separate work ŌĆö forge-gud9)
- Editing/replaying past jobs from this view
- Cross-bead history view (that's a future "Activity" surface)

## CONSTRAINTS
- Counter style: muted color, monospace, prefixed with `┬Ę` separator like other meta-item slots in issue-classification. Format: `┬Ę 3 runs` or `┬Ę 1 run`. Hidden when count=0.
- Timeline rendered in IssueDossier under existing sections, header "SPECIALIST ACTIVITY". Most-recent at top. Each row: monospace, `status_icon  specialist  state  job_id-short  HH:MM ago  excerpt`. Click row ŌåÆ reveal full last_output in tooltip OR inline collapse.
- Existing jobsByBead query returns DESC by updated_at_ms with NO status filter (already true ŌĆö see dao.ts:9). The hook just needs to be the non-live counterpart.
- Counter MUST not fetch on every render ŌĆö share fetch result via React Query / SWR-style cache or a single hook call per row gated by visibility.
- Performance: feed renders many rows; per-row API call to /jobs?bead_id is OK because backend reads from attached SQLite in <1ms. Mitigate with a small in-memory cache (~30s TTL) in the hook.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Closed bead with past specialist rows shows `┬Ę N runs` on the row
- [ ] Closed bead with zero specialist runs shows no counter (no visual noise)
- [ ] Expanding the row reveals SPECIALIST ACTIVITY section with all past runs DESC by time
- [ ] In_progress bead still shows the live chip (no regression on 9219bc9)
- [ ] In_progress bead can ALSO show the counter if it has prior runs (live chip + counter, both visible)
- [ ] No N+1 fetch storm ŌĆö feed scroll doesn't refetch on every paint
- [ ] Existing tests still pass

## OUTPUT
Inline past-runs counter on feed rows + full activity timeline in expanded dossier. observability.db data finally surfaced for traceability without needing the drawer.

## REFERENCES
- 9219bc9: live chip + live-only filter (foundation)
- forge-gud9: drawer with cross-bead Specialists tab (complementary, not redundant)
- observability.db: specialist_jobs retains all rows; no new persistence needed
notes:
Added per-bead specialist history hook with 30s cache + pending dedupe. IssueFeed now shows inline runs chip and SPECIALIST ACTIVITY dossier timeline from cached history.

title: Unified bottom drawer (VSCode-style): Logs + Specialists tabs
description:
## PROBLEM
gitboard needs a VSCode-style bottom drawer hosting ambient cockpit views. Two efforts converge here: forge-wvlj (event-log terminal) and the in-flight specialists surface that needs a home outside the sidebar.

## SUCCESS
One shared <BottomDrawer /> with a top tab bar. Tabs:
- "Logs" ŌĆö forge-wvlj's event-log terminal view (real-time WS feed)
- "Specialists" ŌĆö feed-style listing with two sections, in_progress on top, history below. Rendered terminal-style (monospace, minimal chrome, one row = full sp ps line: job_id ┬Ę specialist ┬Ę status ┬Ę bead_id ┬Ę chain ┬Ę epic ┬Ę repo ┬Ę elapsed)
Drawer collapsible (drag-to-resize), persists open/closed + active tab + height in localStorage. Ctrl/Cmd+J toggles.

Per-bead specialist traceability lives INLINE in the feed (chips on bead rows), NOT in the drawer. The Specialists tab is for global "all running + recently completed" cockpit view.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawer.tsx (new)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawerTabBar.tsx (new)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/MainPane.tsx (mount + flex layout, fixed bottom region)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/shell.ts (add drawerOpen, drawerHeight, drawerTab)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/SpecialistsTabPanel.tsx (new ŌĆö terminal-style 2-section list)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/InFlightRail.tsx (DEPRECATED ŌĆö replaced by SpecialistsTabPanel; remove after migration)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useAllSpecialistJobs.ts (new ŌĆö fetches both live + history through new endpoint)

## NON_GOALS
- Per-bead specialist history view inside the drawer ŌĆö that lives in the feed (separate work)
- Replacing forge-wvlj's log-streaming protocol ŌĆö drawer hosts their component as-is
- Sidebar restructure (already done ŌĆö rail dropped in 9219bc9)

## CONSTRAINTS
- Terminal-style row shape: one line per job, monospace, columns: `<job_id> <specialist> <status> <bead_id> <chain_id?> <epic_id?> <repo> <elapsed>`. No card chrome, no padding wasted on decoration. The user should feel like they're looking at `sp ps` in a tmux pane.
- Two sections: "in_progress" (status IN starting/running/waiting) and "history" (status IN done/error/cancelled), each with a header line. history limited to last 50 by default, with "show more" affordance.
- Backend: extend GET /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight ŌåÆ split into in_flight + recent_history, OR add new GET /api/specialists/jobs/recent?limit=50. Reuse existing DAO + add a new `recentJobs(limit)` method querying observability.db.
- Drawer height range 120px (collapsed) ŌĆö 600px (max); default 240px
- Keyboard shortcut to toggle: Ctrl/Cmd+J (VSCode parity)
- forge-wvlj coordinates: their executor lands the Logs tab content inside this drawer chrome. If they land first with their own drawer, this epic refactors theirs into the tabbed shell.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] BottomDrawer renders at bottom of MainPane with 2 tabs (Logs, Specialists)
- [ ] Specialists tab: in_progress section on top with live rows, history section below with closed runs DESC by updated_at_ms
- [ ] Rows are terminal-style monospace single lines with all sp-ps fields
- [ ] Empty in_progress shows "no live specialists" inline; empty history shows "no recent runs"
- [ ] Ctrl/Cmd+J toggles open/closed, drawerHeight persists across reloads
- [ ] No layout shift when toggling; main content area resizes
- [ ] Logs tab placeholder integrates forge-wvlj's component when ready (clean handoff interface)
- [ ] InFlightRail.tsx removed from codebase OR marked deprecated with redirect to SpecialistsTabPanel

## OUTPUT
Working BottomDrawer with Specialists tab (terminal-style sp-ps-shape rows split by live/history sections) + Logs tab placeholder ready for forge-wvlj.

## REFERENCES
- forge-wvlj: Dashboard bottom drawer ŌĆö live event-log terminal view (Logs tab content)
- forge-zpw8: structured event-log emitter (backend for Logs tab)
- 9219bc9: rail removed from sidebar, chip restyle landed
- observability.db retention: specialist_jobs keeps closed rows, so history is a query away

## SCOPE

- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/__tests__/InFlightRail.test.tsx
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/__tests__/SpecialistChainGraph.test.tsx

## NON_GOALS
- Visual regression tests (no snapshot lib in v1)
- Mutation interaction tests (kill/resume ŌĆö out of v1)

## CONSTRAINTS
