title: Test: phase-1 backend ŌĆö registry, attach-pool, watcher, DAO
description:
## PROBLEM
The phase-1 server modules (registry, attach-pool, schema-guard, watcher, DAO) form the load-bearing data layer for every API and UI consumer. Without companion tests, schema drift or attach regressions will only surface mid-UI.

## SUCCESS
A unified test suite under apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/__tests__/ exercises every public function of F1-F4 against fixture observability.db files (2 compatible + 1 mismatched). All anti-patterns flagged in this skill are absent.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/__tests__/registry.test.ts
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/__tests__/attach-pool.test.ts
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/__tests__/watcher.test.ts
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/__tests__/dao.test.ts
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/__tests__/fixtures/ (3 seeded .db files + seed script)

## NON_GOALS
- API route tests (T2)
- UI component tests (T3)
- Mocking SQLite ŌĆö use real ephemeral db files

## CONSTRAINTS
- No shared mutable state between tests (fixture-scoped, per-test temp dirs)
- Deterministic fixtures: no Date.now() in test data; pre-baked timestamps
- Boundary tests assert field presence and types, not internal row structure
- Never mock attach-pool internals from DAO tests ŌĆö let them attach real fixture dbs

## VALIDATION
### Layer: core (F1 only) + boundary (F2, F3, F4)
### Strategy: example-based + recorded-fixture (real SQLite, no service available)
### Covers: forge-3ue5.1, forge-3ue5.2, forge-3ue5.3, forge-3ue5.4

Assertions required:
- [ ] **registry** positive: tmp tree with 3 fake observability.db ŌåÆ 3 stable slugs
- [ ] **registry** edge: empty roots ŌåÆ [], unreadable dir ŌåÆ no throw, name collision ŌåÆ hash suffix
- [ ] **attach-pool** positive: 3 fixtures (2 compat, 1 mismatched) ŌåÆ withAttached sees only 2
- [ ] **attach-pool** edge: LRU eviction at cap, schema mismatch logs + continues
- [ ] **watcher** positive: writing to a fixture db bumps its epoch within 300ms
- [ ] **watcher** edge: file delete/recreate handled, watcher stop cleans handles
- [ ] **DAO** positive: jobsByBead returns rows tagged with repo slug across 2 attached dbs
- [ ] **DAO** contract: inFlightJobs filters status IN ('starting','running') ORDER BY updated_at_ms DESC
- [ ] **DAO** contract: chainById preserves chain_kind precedence ordering
- [ ] **DAO** drift-safe: assertions check column names + types, not row identity

Anti-pattern checklist:
- [ ] No assertion-free tests
- [ ] No tautological assertions (e.g. asserting the SQL string against itself)
- [ ] Per-test temp dirs; no module-level fixture mutation
- [ ] No mocking of internal collaborators ŌĆö only the filesystem is "external"
- [ ] Negative path covered (schema mismatch, missing db file, malformed row)

Critical paths covered:
- attach-pool refusing mismatched schema ŌåÆ risk: gitboard crashes on a sp upgrade
- DAO bead_id parameterization ŌåÆ risk: SQL injection / wrong rows surfaced
- watcher epoch bump ŌåÆ risk: stale UI never refreshes

Known deferred paths:
- Performance under N>20 attached repos ŌåÆ follow-up: to be created (P3)
- specialist_events row queries ŌåÆ follow-up: not in v1 scope

## OUTPUT
- 4 test files + fixtures dir + seed script
- All tests passing in CI, run with `bun test` (or repo's existing test command ŌĆö verify before starting)
- Coverage report not required; critical-path map above is the bar

- Pagination: in-flight list is bounded by reality (Ōēż a few dozen)
- specialist_events / metrics queries

title: File-watcher invalidation for observability.db
description:
## PROBLEM
sp writes to observability.db continuously while jobs run. Gitboard needs to know when to re-query without polling on a tight loop.

## SUCCESS
A chokidar watcher subscribes to mtime changes on every observability.db in the registry. On change, an in-memory epoch counter for that repo is bumped. DAO/API layers read the counter as a freshness key; UI components use it as a swr/refetch trigger.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/watcher.ts (new)
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/epoch.ts (new)

## NON_GOALS
- Pushing changes to clients (WebSocket) ŌĆö that's a future epic
- Polling: do not add a setInterval fallback in v1
- Diffing what changed; only signal that something did

## CONSTRAINTS
- Watcher lifecycle bound to server start/stop
- Debounce mtime events to ~200ms per repo
- Watcher errors must not crash the server; log + continue

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Unit test: writing to a fixture db bumps its epoch within 300ms
- [ ] Killing/recreating the file is handled gracefully
- [ ] Watcher stop cleans up handles

## OUTPUT
- watcher module with start/stop
- epoch module with get(repoSlug) + bump(repoSlug)
- Tests in __tests__/watcher.test.ts

title: Attach pool + schema_version guard for observability.db
description:
## PROBLEM
Each repo has its own observability.db; gitboard needs to read across them in one query. Direct opens are wasteful and unsafe across sp schema versions.

## SUCCESS
An LRU attach pool opens up to N (default 12, configurable) observability.db files in mode=ro and aliases each as repo_<slug>. On attach, schema_version is read; mismatched versions are warned-and-skipped, never crash other repos. Pool exposes withAttached(fn) yielding a live db handle with all healthy repos attached.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/attach-pool.ts (new)
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/schema-guard.ts (new)

## NON_GOALS
- DAO queries (F4)
- File watching (F3)
- Mutations of any kind

## CONSTRAINTS
- All opens use ?mode=ro (WAL-safe reads)
- Use better-sqlite3 if available in gitboard; fall back to bun:sqlite ŌĆö verify in repo first
- Compatible schema_version range is a constant; bumping it requires conscious update + note
- Never hold attach across awaits; query, then release

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Unit test: attach 3 fixture dbs (2 compatible, 1 version-mismatched) ŌĆö query sees only 2
- [ ] LRU eviction works when pool size exceeded
- [ ] Schema mismatch logs a warning and continues

## OUTPUT
- attach-pool module with withAttached(fn) API
- schema-guard with isCompatible(version) + COMPATIBLE_RANGE
- Fixture dbs under __tests__/fixtures/

title: Scan & registry of sp observability.db files
description:
## PROBLEM
Gitboard has no concept of which repos host a sp observability.db. Without a discovered registry, downstream attach/query layers have nothing to operate on.

## SUCCESS
A small server module discovers every observability.db under configured roots and exposes listRepos() ŌåÆ [{ repoSlug, repoPath, dbPath, mtimeMs }]. Roots list is config-driven (env or settings file) with default ~/dev/*.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/registry.ts (new)
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/config.ts (new ŌĆö roots + env override)

## NON_GOALS
- Opening the db files (F2)
- Watching for new files (F3)
- Any UI

## CONSTRAINTS
- Pure sync filesystem scan, no DB opens
- Skip non-readable paths silently with debug log; never throw on scan
- Repo slug derived from parent dir name; collisions disambiguated by hash suffix

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Unit test: scan against a tmp tree with 3 fake observability.db files returns 3 entries with stable slugs
- [ ] Empty roots list returns []
- [ ] Unreadable directory does not throw

## OUTPUT
- New module exporting listRepos() + RepoEntry type
- Tests in __tests__/registry.test.ts
- No wire-up to API yet ŌĆö that comes in F4/F5

## APPROACH NOTES
Use fast-glob or native fs.readdir; gitboard already has fs utils ŌĆö reuse if present.

title: gitboard live specialist ownership view (read-only v1)
description:
## PROBLEM
Beads in gitboard show only static fields (status, assignee string). There is no visibility into which sp specialist currently owns an in-flight bead, what chain it belongs to, or where its worktree lives ŌĆö that data exists in sp's observability.db but is not surfaced. Operators must context-switch to terminal `sp ps` / `sp feed` to understand cockpit state.

## SUCCESS
Every BeadCard in gitboard shows live ownership (specialist role, state, repo) when a sp job is attached to the bead. A right-side in-flight rail lists every running/starting job across all discovered repos with last-output preview. A chain mini-graph appears on bead detail when chain_id is set. All reads are sourced from sp's observability.db; gitboard never shells out to sp.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts (new)
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/ (new module: registry, attach-pool, watcher, dao)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/BeadCard.tsx (owner badge mount point)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/IssueOverlay.tsx (chain graph mount point)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/Sidebar.tsx (rail panel mount point)

## NON_GOALS
- Mutations: no resume/kill of sp jobs from gitboard in v1 (auth boundary deferred)
- WebSocket push: file-watcher + epoch invalidation only
- Cross-repo specialist_events timeline view
- sp-side HTTP/WS API exposure (tracked separately in ~/dev/specialists beads as schema_version marker, advisory)

## CONSTRAINTS
- observability.db opened mode=ro only; WAL-safe concurrent reads
- schema_version checked on attach; mismatched repos warn + skip, never crash the pool
- SQLite attach pool cap raised explicitly if >16 repos discovered
- Existing BeadCard / Sidebar layout must remain functional when no sp data is available
- React UI changes follow existing component patterns in dashboard/components/beads/

## VALIDATION
- [ ] DAO unit tests pass against a fixture observability.db
- [ ] API integration tests cover bead_id lookup, in-flight list, chain fetch
- [ ] Owner badge renders on BeadCard for a seeded in_progress bead with a sp job row
- [ ] In-flight rail updates within ~1s of a fixture db mtime change (file-watcher e2e)
- [ ] Chain mini-graph renders epic + child chain on bead detail
- [ ] Type-check + lint pass; existing gitboard tests stay green

## OUTPUT
Orchestrator close report: list of all 10 child handoffs, integration evidence (one Playwright run against a seeded repo showing badge + rail + chain), residual risks (schema-coupling watch list, performance under N>20 repos).

## REFERENCES
- sp observability.db schema: specialist_jobs (bead_id, specialist, status, chain_id, epic_id, chain_kind, chain_root_bead_id, worktree_column, last_output, updated_at_ms)
- Indexes used: idx_jobs_bead, idx_jobs_status, idx_jobs_chain, idx_jobs_epic
- Sibling tables (out of v1 scope but available): specialist_events, specialist_job_metrics, epic_runs, epic_chain_membership
close_reason: Epic complete. 13/13 children closed. v1 delivers: backend (registry+attach+schema-guard+watcher+DAO) + API routes (/jobs?bead_id, /jobs/in-flight, /chains/:id) + UI (BeadCard badge, InFlightRail, SpecialistChainGraph) + tests (12 backend + 9 API + 84 UI = 105 pass) + Playwright MCP e2e verified live dashboard + API + empty-state mounts. Dashboard build clean (364KB).

title: Dashboard bottom drawer: live event-log terminal view (UI)
description:
PROBLEM
forge-zpw8 ships the backend event-log emitter + WS feed + disk retention. The operator-facing surface is missing ŌĆö without a UI drawer, the firehose only matters via /api/internal/logs or docker logs. Goal: a collapsible bottom drawer in the dashboard that mirrors the live WS log stream with filters, virtualized rendering, and resume-on-reconnect.

REFERENCES
- apps/gitboard/src/types/log.ts (forge-zpw8: LogEntry shape)
- apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts (forge-zpw8: SystemRealtimeEvent union + SystemLogPayload)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/ws.ts (existing WsClient; resume contract from forge-dgd)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/ActivityTimeline.tsx ŌĆö virtualization pattern with @tanstack/react-virtual
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/shell.ts ŌĆö pattern for localStorage-persisted UI state (sidebarCollapsed)

SUCCESS
- NEW component apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/LogDrawer.tsx renders a fixed-position bottom drawer.
- Toggle button mounted in TopBar.tsx with an unread-error badge (red dot + count when drawer is collapsed AND new error/warn entries arrive since last view).
- Drawer state: collapsed by default; toggle persisted in localStorage under 'forge-log:open'; resizable height (drag handle on the top border) persisted under 'forge-log:height' (min 120px, max 70vh, default 320px).
- Drawer is mono-font ONLY (per repo typography rule); rest of the app stays sans-serif.
- NEW hook apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useLogStream.ts:
  - On mount: GET /api/internal/logs?limit=500 to seed history.
  - Subscribes to WS 'system' channel; appends 'system:log' envelopes to a ring (max 5000 client-side).
  - Reuses existing WsClient resume contract (forge-dgd) so reconnects don't lose entries.
  - Emits a derived 'unread errors/warns count since drawer was last opened' for the badge.
- NEW Zustand slice apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/logs.ts:
  - state: { entries: LogEntry[], filters: { level, components: Set<LogComponent>, search: string }, paused: boolean, lastOpenedAt: string|null }
  - actions: append, appendBatch, setLevel, toggleComponent, setSearch, pause, resume, clear, markOpened
- Drawer layout:
  - Header row (40px): level dropdown (debug/info/warn/error), component multi-select chips, search input, pause/resume button, clear button, close-drawer button.
  - Body (fills): virtualized list via @tanstack/react-virtual.
  - Row layout: `HH:MM:SS.mmm  [LEVEL chip]  [component chip]  event  msgŌĆ”`
  - Click row ŌåÆ inline-expand the data JSON pretty-printed (max 8 lines, scroll if longer).
  - Color coding via CSS variables (no hardcoded hex): error=--accent-red, warn=--accent-yellow, debug=--text-muted, info=--text-primary.
  - Sticky bottom-scroll until user scrolls up >10px ŌåÆ auto-paused; a 'Resume ŌåÆ live' button appears at the bottom-right of the drawer.
  - Empty state when filters match nothing: small centered text 'No log entries match these filters.'
- Performance:
  - All renders MUST go through useMemo / selectors so filters don't recompute the full list on every WS event.
  - Virtualizer is the only place rows mount.
  - Filter pipeline is O(n) per filter change, NOT per append.
- Accessibility: drawer is a <section> with role='complementary', aria-label='Event log'; toggle button is a <button> with aria-expanded.
- Mobile (<768px): drawer takes 50vh, drag handle remains.

SCOPE
- NEW: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/LogDrawer.tsx
- NEW: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/LogRow.tsx (per-row component for virtualizer)
- NEW: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useLogStream.ts
- NEW: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/logs.ts
- NEW: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/log-client.ts (small fetch wrapper for /api/internal/logs)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/TopBar.tsx ŌĆö add drawer-toggle button with unread badge
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx ŌĆö mount <LogDrawer /> as sibling of <MainPane />
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css ŌĆö .ide-log-drawer ruleset (mono font, drag handle, chip styling, virtualizer overrides) using CSS custom properties only
- NEW: apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/components/shell/LogDrawer.test.tsx ŌĆö render-with-fixtures, filter behavior, pause/resume, badge counting
- NEW: apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/stores/logs.test.ts ŌĆö append/batch/filter logic, lastOpenedAt resets unread count

NON_GOALS
- No log export / download from the UI (use /api/internal/logs/files endpoint manually).
- No log forwarding to external sinks from the browser.
- No syntax highlighting of the JSON 'data' payload beyond pretty-printing.
- No persistent client-side log archive across reloads (the backend ring + disk is the source of truth).
- No multi-tab synchronization (each tab maintains its own ring; backend disk + ring is shared).
- No keyboard shortcuts in v1 (file a follow-up if it matters).
- No detail-page anchoring for individual log entries (no permalinks).

CONSTRAINTS
- TypeScript strict.
- No new runtime dependencies; reuse @tanstack/react-virtual + Zustand already in the project.
- All colors / spacing via CSS custom properties ŌĆö NO hardcoded hex (per repo rule).
- Mono font scoped via .ide-log-drawer class ŌĆö must NOT leak to the rest of the app.
- Drawer height resize must work via pointer events (mouse + touch) without library.
- Component must mount the toggle even before the WS connects (don't block UI on backend).
- LogRow must render in <2ms ŌĆö measured with React DevTools Profiler before merge.

VALIDATION
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test ŌåÆ existing 219+ + new tests green.
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard build ŌåÆ clean, no new bundle warnings.
- Live smoke (manual on running server): open dashboard; click drawer toggle; verify entries stream in real time; flip level filter; verify badge counts unread errors when drawer is collapsed; resize drawer; reload page ŌĆö collapsed/expanded state and height persist.
- Scrollback test: scroll up in a busy stream ŌåÆ confirm auto-pause; click resume ŌåÆ confirm tail catches up.
- Reconnect test: stop the backend, wait 5s, restart ŌĆö confirm drawer recovers without duplicates (resume contract from forge-dgd).

OUTPUT
- File diff list.
- Screenshot or DOM excerpt of drawer in open + collapsed states.
- Per-row render time from Profiler (<2ms target).
- Confirmation that badge unread count behaves correctly (visible on collapsed drawer with new warns/errors; resets to 0 on open).

DEPENDS_ON forge-zpw8 (backend logger must ship first; UI consumes its WS event names + endpoint shapes).

title: Structured event-log emitter + WS feed + disk retention (backend)
description:
PROBLEM
The server has rich operational state (poller cycles, watcher snapshot reads, Dolt query metrics, breaker transitions, WS connect/disconnect, source-health flips) but no unified way to capture or stream it. The forge-1qz health endpoint exposes a latency ring snapshot but everything else is invisible unless you tail stdout. Need a single structured logger with three sinks so the upcoming UI drawer (separate bead) and forensic disk archive both work, and so the docker-containerized service (forge-8kdc) leaves an audit trail beyond `docker logs`.

REFERENCES
- apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts ŌĆö RealtimeEnvelope + 'system' channel already in ChannelName union
- apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts ŌĆö ChannelRegistry.publish with seq + boot_id + ring buffer + resume contract from forge-dgd
- apps/gitboard/src/core/{github-poller,beads-change-watcher,dolt-client}.ts ŌĆö emit call sites
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/internal-dolt-health.ts ŌĆö sibling /api/internal pattern for the new endpoint

SUCCESS
- NEW apps/gitboard/src/core/logger.ts exports a singleton logger:
  - emit(entry: LogEntry): void ŌĆö fan-out to all sinks
  - getRing(): LogEntry[] ŌĆö current in-memory ring (most recent N=5000)
  - subscribe(filter?, fn): unsubscribe ŌĆö local listener (not for WS, for tests + future API)
  - setDiskEnabled(boolean), setLogLevel('debug'|'info'|'warn'|'error')
- NEW apps/gitboard/src/types/log.ts:
  - LogEntry = { ts:string, level, component, event:string, msg?:string, data?:Record<string,unknown> }
  - LogLevel union, LogComponent union (poller|watcher|dolt|ws|api|breaker|system)
- types/realtime.ts extended: add 'system:log' to a new SystemRealtimeEvent union; payload SystemLogPayload = LogEntry. Bump nothing ŌĆö additive only.
- SINK 1 (in-memory ring): fixed-size circular buffer (LOG_RING_SIZE=5000) preserved across the process lifetime; oldest dropped on overflow. O(1) push.
- SINK 2 (WS broadcast on 'system' channel): event name 'system:log', payload = entry, version = entry.ts. RESPECTS the LOG_LEVEL env var ŌĆö only entries at >= configured level are broadcast (default 'info'). Debug entries STILL land in ring + disk.
- SINK 3 (disk, JSONL): /data/logs/<YYYY-MM-DD>.jsonl. Always writes ALL levels (full forensic capture). Daily rotation at UTC midnight. Retention: delete files older than 7 days on startup + once daily.
- Call-site instrumentation across the existing components:
  - github-poller.ts: cycle.start, cycle.complete (with counts + duration), rate_limit.changed, etag.hit_304, etag.miss, error
  - beads-change-watcher.ts: poll.skipped (hash unchanged fast-path ŌĆö DEBUG), poll.snapshot_read (when snapshot actually runs ŌĆö INFO), source_health.changed (on actual transition, not every emit), batch.published (with counts), drift.detected
  - dolt-client.ts: breaker.opened, breaker.closed, query.slow (>100ms only; the latency ring already records all), reconnect.attempt, reconnect.recovered
  - ws/channels.ts + handler.ts: client.connected, client.disconnected, resume.miss ŌåÆ emits warn, subscribe.version_mismatch (already a 4001 close ŌĆö log it)
  - api/server.ts middleware: request.slow (>500ms only), request.error (non-2xx with stack if 5xx)
- NEW endpoint GET /api/internal/logs (gated to localhost): query params 'level', 'component', 'event', 'since' (ISO), 'limit' (default 200, max 1000). Reads from in-memory ring (fast). Returns JSON array.
- NEW endpoint GET /api/internal/logs/files: list available .jsonl files in /data/logs with size + date.

SCOPE
- NEW: apps/gitboard/src/core/logger.ts (~200 LOC)
- NEW: apps/gitboard/src/types/log.ts (~30 LOC)
- NEW: apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/internal-logs.ts (~80 LOC)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts ŌĆö add SystemRealtimeEvent union + payload alias
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts ŌĆö ~10 emit() calls
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/core/beads-change-watcher.ts ŌĆö ~6 emit() calls
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts ŌĆö ~4 emit() calls
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/{channels.ts,handler.ts} ŌĆö ~4 emit() calls
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts ŌĆö request timing middleware + wire new /api/internal/logs routes
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/index.ts ŌĆö set LOG_LEVEL from env at startup
- NEW: apps/gitboard/tests/core/logger.test.ts ŌĆö ring overflow, level filter on broadcast, disk write/rotation/retention, instrumentation hooks fire
- NEW: apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/internal-logs.test.ts ŌĆö query filters, localhost gate

NON_GOALS
- No UI work. The drawer is a separate bead (forge-???) depending on this one.
- No log aggregation system (loki, vector, etc.). Local disk only.
- No structured query language; query params on /api/internal/logs are flat string filters.
- No per-user / per-tenant log isolation.
- No compression of rotated files (gzip is a tiny follow-up if needed).
- No alerting / threshold rules.
- No replacement of stdout console.log calls ŌĆö they keep working. New logger is ADDITIVE.

CONSTRAINTS
- TypeScript strict, Bun runtime. Use Bun.file for disk writes (NOT node:fs/promises) to stay consistent.
- LOG_RING_SIZE, LOG_DEFAULT_LEVEL, LOG_DISK_DIR, LOG_RETENTION_DAYS as constants near top of logger.ts.
- Disk dir defaults to /data/logs (matches the docker volume from forge-8kdc); override via LOG_DIR env. Fall back to ./logs if /data is not writable (dev mode).
- Ring buffer push MUST be O(1) and allocation-light (called potentially 10x/sec).
- WS broadcast MUST go through the existing ChannelRegistry.publish; do NOT create a parallel transport.
- Reuse the realtime contract's seq/boot_id/resume ŌĆö UI gets free reconnect-replay.
- No new runtime deps.
- All emit() call sites must be small (one-liners ideally); the logger does the structural work.

VALIDATION
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test ŌåÆ 219+ pass (existing) + new logger + internal-logs tests green.
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard build ŌåÆ clean.
- Live smoke (manual): start server, hit /api/internal/logs?limit=20 ŌåÆ returns JSON entries from cold-start (poller cycle.start, watcher first ticks, ws system events). Connect a WS client subscribed to 'system' channel ŌåÆ receives 'system:log' envelopes in real time. Kill Dolt for 30s ŌåÆ ring contains breaker.opened entry; restart ŌåÆ ring contains breaker.closed entry. Confirm /data/logs/<today>.jsonl exists and grows.

OUTPUT
- File diff list (~10 files).
- Logger interface (LogEntry, LogLevel, LogComponent unions) as the public contract.
- Sample 20-line JSONL excerpt from a real 60s run showing each component is wired.
- Retention test evidence (mock 8 day-files, run cleanup, confirm 1 deleted).

title: Dockerize gitboard as VPS service + Tailscale-only access
description:
PROBLEM
Developer works primarily from a VPS but wants the gitboard dashboard reachable from local laptop and desktop machines without per-session SSH-forward ceremony or public-internet exposure. The realtime stack now in place (forge-dgd/f0g/igg/1qz/3mdw) is worth running as a persistent service on the VPS ŌĆö but right now there is no container, no compose, no documented access path.

SCOPE
- NEW: Dockerfile at repo root (or apps/gitboard/Dockerfile)
- NEW: docker-compose.yml at repo root (or apps/gitboard/docker-compose.yml)
- NEW: apps/gitboard/.dockerignore
- NEW: docs/deployment.md ŌĆö 1-page setup README covering VPS install, Tailscale enrolment, firewall lockdown
- NO changes to runtime source code beyond what's required to honor PORT and AGENT_FORGE_DB env vars (these already work ŌĆö verify, do not refactor)

SUCCESS
- `docker compose up -d` on the VPS builds and starts a single `gitboard` container.
- Container serves http://<vps-host>:3000/gitboard with the production dashboard build (NOT vite dev).
- BeadsChangeWatcher inside the container can reach the host Dolt server at 127.0.0.1:13839 (host-network mode) and successfully poll commit hashes.
- GithubPoller authenticates with GITHUB_TOKEN passed via env.
- State DB persists across container restarts via a named docker volume mounted at /data, configured via AGENT_FORGE_DB=/data/state.db.
- Host project directories (~/projects) are bind-mounted read-only at /projects so ProjectScanner can discover them.
- After Tailscale is installed on VPS + laptop + desktop and all are signed into the same tailnet, the dashboard is reachable from any signed-in device at http://<vps-tailscale-name>:3000/gitboard using MagicDNS.
- VPS firewall blocks public access to port 3000 (only the tailscale0 interface admits it).
- docs/deployment.md contains: prerequisites, build/up commands, Tailscale enrolment steps, ufw lockdown commands, troubleshooting (container can't reach Dolt / GitHub rate-limit / port already bound).

DESIGN DECISIONS (already agreed with operator ŌĆö do NOT relitigate)
- Multi-stage Dockerfile: stage 1 oven/bun:1 ŌåÆ bun install + bun run build:dashboard; stage 2 oven/bun:1-slim ŌåÆ copy src, built dist, node_modules ŌåÆ CMD bun src/index.ts.
- Network mode: host (sees Dolt at 127.0.0.1:13839 with no extra_hosts gymnastics).
- Access pattern: Tailscale (multi-device, roaming, free for personal). NOT cloudflared, NOT bare SSH forward.
- Build target: production static-served dashboard. NO HMR/vite dev inside the container.
- Container does NOT run its own Dolt server ŌĆö assumes the VPS shared dolt sql-server is already running externally (started by bd or systemd).

NON_GOALS
- Multi-container compose for Dolt itself. Out of scope; assume external.
- Reverse proxy in front of the container (nginx/Caddy). Tailscale + direct port is enough.
- TLS termination (tailscale cert is documented but not wired).
- Production-grade systemd unit on the host. `restart: unless-stopped` on compose covers reboot recovery.
- CI image publishing to a registry. Build on the VPS, no registry push.
- Beadboard frontend container ŌĆö deprecated per CLAUDE.md.

CONSTRAINTS
- Final runtime image MUST be slim (target <200MB). Use oven/bun:1-slim for the runtime stage.
- .dockerignore MUST exclude .git, .beads, .xtrm, .worktrees, node_modules from the build context (relying on stage-1 install).
- Image MUST NOT bake in GITHUB_TOKEN or any other secret; pass via .env file referenced from compose.
- docker-compose.yml MUST bind the published port to all interfaces (0.0.0.0:3000:3000) so Tailscale routing works, with the public-internet firewall lockdown explicitly called out in the README as a required step.
- No new top-level runtime dependencies added to package.json.
- Bun runtime (per project rule). Do NOT switch to node.
- Volumes: named volume `gitboard-state` for /data; read-only bind for /projects.

VALIDATION
- `docker compose build` succeeds and image size < 200MB (`docker images gitboard:latest` confirms).
- `docker compose up -d` brings the container to healthy state; `docker compose logs gitboard` shows expected startup lines (Server running, Backfilling events, no Dolt connection errors).
- From inside the container: `curl http://localhost:3000/api/internal/dolt-health` returns JSON with breakerOpenUntil=0.
- From the VPS host: `curl http://127.0.0.1:3000/gitboard` returns the dashboard index.
- After Tailscale enrolment on VPS + at least one other device: that device opens http://<vps-tailscale-name>:3000/gitboard and the dashboard loads with live data.
- Container restart: `docker compose restart` ŌåÆ dashboard still loads, state.db survives, WS reconnects via existing resume protocol.
- docs/deployment.md walks a reader from zero to working setup in <10 minutes.

OUTPUT
- File diff list (Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, .dockerignore, docs/deployment.md).
- Built image size.
- Sample `docker compose logs gitboard | head -30` output proving Dolt connection + watcher + poller all healthy.
- Tailscale verification: screenshot path or curl from a second device.

REFERENCES
- apps/gitboard/src/index.ts ŌĆö entrypoint, reads PORT env (default 3000).
- apps/gitboard/CLAUDE.md ŌĆö runtime/Bun rules, env var inventory, port defaults.
- apps/gitboard/package.json ŌĆö has build:dashboard script.
- apps/gitboard/vite.config.ts ŌĆö base /gitboard/, proxy config not used in prod.
- Existing memory `dolt-connection-stability-forge-1qz-dolt-client-ts` ŌĆö pool/breaker/probe constants matter at startup.

title: BeadsChangeWatcher: skip full snapshot when commit hash unchanged + collapse N+1 in toIssue
description:
PROBLEM
forge-1qz live smoke revealed BeadsChangeWatcher is hitting the 3s query timeout on roughly 1 in 3 polls against healthy Dolt ŌĆö observed during the kill-Dolt failover test on 2026-05-17 (gitboard project, ~200 issues). Two compounding causes in apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-change-watcher.ts:

1. **Commit-hash poll is wasted.** poll() at line 60-68 calls getCommitHash(project) AND readSnapshot(project) every tick unconditionally. The whole point of commit-hash polling is that the hash IS the cheap 'did anything change' probe ŌĆö if it matches the last seen hash, the snapshot read should be skipped. Today the expensive snapshot runs every 2s (active) / 10s (idle) regardless.

2. **N+1 fan-out in DoltClient.toIssue.** Each issue row triggers 3 separate awaited queries (getDependencies, getRelatedIds, getLabels). getIssues({limit:1000}) therefore performs ~3,001 sequential queries for a project with 1000 issues; gitboard has ~200 issues so it's ~600 queries per snapshot. Combined with the 3s wrapper this regularly times out, watcher silently falls through to JSONL via the catch on line 126-128, and the latency ring fills with 3000ms entries.

SCOPE
- apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-change-watcher.ts (poll(), readSnapshot())
- apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts (toIssue() and the three N+1 helpers OR a new batched getIssuesWithRelations)

SUCCESS
- poll() stores lastCommitHash per project. On each tick: cheap getCommitHash() call only. If hash unchanged AND we already have a previous snapshot, emit beads:source_health and return early ŌĆö no readSnapshot, no diff, no batch.
- When the hash DOES change (or is null/first-tick), proceed with readSnapshot as today.
- DoltClient.getIssues collapses the per-issue N+1 into 4 total queries: one SELECT issues, one SELECT dependencies WHERE issue_id IN (...), one SELECT issue_related WHERE issue_id IN (...), one SELECT labels WHERE issue_id IN (...). Map them into the BeadIssue shape in JS.
- After fix: latency ring for the watcher path shows no 3000ms entries on healthy Dolt across a 60s window.

NON_GOALS
- No schema migration.
- Do not change DoltClient.getIssue(single) ŌĆö keep the existing per-issue path for that route.
- Do not touch the breaker / pool / probe constants from forge-1qz.

CONSTRAINTS
- Preserve the public DoltClient.getIssues(filters) signature.
- TypeScript strict.
- Keep diff under ~80 lines.

VALIDATION
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test ŌåÆ still 219/219.
- bun run --filter @xtrm/beadboard test ŌåÆ still only pre-existing baseline failures (beads-reader.test.ts bun:sqlite + dolt-client.test.ts labels).
- Live smoke: with the gitboard server running and Dolt up, poll /api/internal/dolt-health for 60s; recentLatencies should contain NO 3000ms entries.

OUTPUT
- File diffs (2 files).
- Before/after latency-ring snapshot proving timeouts are gone.
close_reason: Watcher: lastCommitHash cache + skip-snapshot fast-path. DoltClient.getIssues/getClosedIssues: batched IN-clause hydration replacing N+1. Verified live: 60s watcher poll = 0 new timeouts vs ~33% pre-fix. Tests 219+ pass; pre-existing baseline failure unchanged

title: Dolt connection stability ŌĆö pool + health probe + reconnect + circuit breaker
description:
PROBLEM
forge-igg's BeadsChangeWatcher polls Dolt every 2s per active project via dolt-client. The current client opens a short-lived connection per query ŌĆö fine for sporadic reads, fragile under continuous polling. Need a stable pooled client with health probes, reconnect, and a circuit breaker that flips watcher to JSONL fallback when Dolt is down.

REFERENCES
- apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts (existing client; add pool + probe + reconnect)
- apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-change-watcher.ts (forge-igg; consumer of the pool, surfaces source_health)
- apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts (forge-dgd; beads:source_health payload shape)

SUCCESS
- Single shared connection pool (Bun-native MySQL-protocol driver) with max=N, idleTimeout aligned to Dolt server defaults. Replaces per-call connect/close.
- Health probe SELECT 1 every 5s on idle connections; evict on failure.
- Reconnect with exponential backoff 250msŌåÆ30s + jitter; emit beads:source_health { source:'dolt', healthy:false } while down, flip back on recovery.
- Circuit breaker: after N consecutive failures, pause Dolt polling for project, watcher flips to JSONL automatically (source:'jsonl').
- Per-query timeout (3s wrapper); surface timeouts as health degradation, not exceptions.
- If we launched dolt sql-server, restart on crash with backoff; if external, observe only.
- Metrics: in-memory ring of last 100 query latencies + failure count; expose at /api/internal/dolt-health.

SCOPE
- MODIFY apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts (pool, probe, reconnect, breaker, timeouts, metrics)
- MODIFY apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-change-watcher.ts (consume breaker state; flip source)
- NEW apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/internal-dolt-health.ts (read metrics; gated to localhost)
- NEW apps/beadboard/tests/core/dolt-client-pool.test.ts (probe, reconnect, breaker open/half-open/closed)
- NEW apps/beadboard/tests/core/dolt-kill-smoke.test.ts (start watcher, kill dolt sql-server, verify source flip + reconnect + flap)

NON_GOALS
- No schema migration.
- No multi-Dolt-host failover.
- No persistent metrics store (in-memory only).
- No new Dolt driver dependency unless current one cannot pool ŌĆö prefer extending existing.

CONSTRAINTS
- TypeScript strict, Bun runtime.
- Pool size + timeouts as constants at top of dolt-client.ts.
- All existing dolt-client callers must keep working unchanged (signature-compatible).
- Health endpoint must NOT leak credentials or query bodies.

VALIDATION
- bun run lint + tests green.
- Smoke: start server, observe steady-state pool usage; kill dolt sql-server; verify source_health flips and JSONL takes over within 5s; restart dolt; verify recovery and source_health flips back.

OUTPUT
- File diffs
- Pool config table (size, timeouts, breaker thresholds) with rationale
- Failure mode matrix (Dolt down / slow / partial / restart)
notes:
Reviewer: breaker-open skip to JSONL in beads-change-watcher; evidence supplement: beadboard tests baseline still 5 pass / 2 fail / 1 fail, same shape as HEAD.

title: Realtime protocol contract module (types + channels + seq/ring buffer + resume)
description:
PROBLEM
forge-f0g (live GitHub) and forge-igg (live Beads) both push events over the existing WS ChannelRegistry but there is no shared envelope, no event-name catalogue, no sequence numbers, no replay path, and no protocol version. Implementing each in isolation will produce divergent payloads and brittle clients. Lock the contract first so downstream beads consume it.

REFERENCES
- apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts (ChannelRegistry; widen ChannelName, add seq + ring buffer)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/handler.ts (WsHandler; add 'resume' action)
- apps/gitboard/src/types/{github.ts,beads.ts} (payload shapes referenced from realtime types)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/ws.ts (client; resume support)

SUCCESS
- NEW apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts exporting:
  - REALTIME_PROTOCOL_VERSION = 1
  - RealtimeEnvelope<E,D> { type:'event', channel, event, seq, ts, version, data }
  - GithubRealtimeEvent union: 'github:pr.upsert'|'github:issue.upsert'|'github:event.append'|'github:release.upsert'|'github:sync_hint'|'github:rate_limit'
  - BeadsRealtimeEvent union: 'beads:issue.upsert'|'beads:issue.close'|'beads:issue.delete'|'beads:issue.deferred'|'beads:issue.superseded'|'beads:issue.flagged'|'beads:issue.unflagged'|'beads:dep.upsert'|'beads:dep.delete'|'beads:memory.upsert'|'beads:memory.delete'|'beads:kv.upsert'|'beads:kv.delete'|'beads:comment.append'|'beads:batch'|'beads:sync_hint'|'beads:source_health'
  - Per-event payload type aliases (GithubPrUpsert, BeadsIssueUpsert, etc.)
- channels.ts widens ChannelName with template literals: `github:repo:${string}`, `beads:project:${string}`. publish() adds monotonic seq + ts + version; maintains per-channel ring buffer (last 500 envelopes).
- handler.ts handles 'resume' action: { action:'resume', channel, since_seq } ŌåÆ server replays buffered envelopes >since_seq, or emits {event:'<domain>:sync_hint', reason:'buffer_miss'} when out of range.
- Server rejects subscribe from clients with mismatched protocol major (close code 4001).
- Client lib/ws.ts: track last seq per channel in-memory; auto-send resume on reconnect.
- Backward compatibility: legacy 'new_event' / 'new_commits' event names aliased server-side for one release.

SCOPE
- NEW apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts
- MODIFY apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts (ChannelName widen, publish seq+buffer, replay helper)
- MODIFY apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/handler.ts (resume action, version handshake)
- MODIFY apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/ws.ts (seq tracking, resume on reconnect)
- NEW apps/gitboard/tests/api/ws/realtime-contract.test.ts (envelope shape, seq monotonic, ring buffer replay, version mismatch close)

NON_GOALS
- No GitHub or Beads event producers (forge-f0g, forge-igg).
- No persistence of ring buffer across restarts.
- No multi-process fanout.

CONSTRAINTS
- TypeScript strict.
- Bun runtime; no new deps.
- Ring buffer size constant at top of channels.ts.
- Envelope must be JSON-serialisable.

VALIDATION
- bun run lint + bun run test green.
- Manual: connect ws, subscribe, drop connection, reconnect with resume ŌĆö verify backfill or sync_hint.

OUTPUT
- File diffs
- Final TS type definitions (the API contract)
- Notes on backward compat window

```typescript
api.getMemories(projectId): Promise<Memory[]>
api.getInteractions(projectId, issueId?): Promise<Interaction[]>
api.getStats(projectId): Promise<{ total, open, in_progress, blocked, closed, by_priority, by_type }>
api.getOpenPrs(limit?): Promise<OpenPr[]>
```

### Types (`types/beads.ts`)

```typescript
// Key interfaces (lines 1-89)
export type Status = "open" | "in_progress" | "blocked" | "in_review" | "closed" | (string & {})
export type Priority = 0 | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | (number & {})
export type IssueType = "bug" | "feature" | "task" | "epic" | "chore" | (string & {})

export interface BeadsProject { id, name, path, beadsPath, doltPort?, doltDatabase?, source?, sourceHealth[], sourcePriority?, status, lastScanned, issueCount }
export interface BeadIssue { id, title, description, notes?, status, priority, issue_type, owner, created_at, created_by, updated_at, closed_at?, close_reason?, project_id, dependencies, parent_id?, related_ids, labels }
export interface BeadIssueDetail extends BeadIssue { dependents, children?, source, sourceHealth }
export interface BeadDependency { id, title, status, dependency_type }
export interface Memory { id, content, type, tags, created_at, issue_id?, project_id }
export interface Interaction { id, kind, created_at, actor, issue_id, model?, tool_name?, exit_code?, project_id }
export interface AgentSession { id, agent, model?, started_at, ended_at?, issue_ids, tool_calls, files_modified?, project_id }
export interface IssueFilters { project_id?, status?[], priority?[], issue_type?[], search?, limit?, offset? }
```

## 3. Shared/Tokens

**Location:** `packages/ui/src/styles/tokens.css`

### Existing Tokens (usable for merge)

### Missing Tokens (need addition for shell merge)

Based on beadboard `globals.css` and gitboard globals, these tokens are **referenced but not defined** in `packages/ui`:

| Token | Used In | Recommendation |
|-------|---------|----------------|
| `--danger` | beadboard `.module-status.is-error` | Already aliased in tokens.css line 12: `--danger: var(--accent-red)` |

| `--bg-secondary` | gitboard tokens | Alias line 67: `--bg-secondary: var(--surface-secondary)` |
| `--bg-hover` | scrollbars | Defined line 6: `--bg-hover: #30363d` |

**No missing tokens** ŌĆö all referenced tokens exist in `tokens.css` or have aliases. The alias system (`--surface-1/2/3`, `--danger`) already bridges design-handoff naming to the primary token set.

---

## 4. Port Risks

### Risk 1: tsconfig Divergence

| Setting | gitboard | beadboard | Impact |
|---------|----------|-----------|--------|
| `allowImportingTsExtensions` | `true` | **absent** | gitboard can import `.tsx` directly in types; beadboard cannot |
| `paths` | absent | `{ "@/*": ["./src/*"] }` | beadboard uses `@/` aliases; gitboard uses relative imports |

**Mitigation:** Align on one config. Recommend `allowImportingTsExtensions: true` (simpler) + drop `paths` (use relative imports consistently).

### Risk 2: Cross-Imports That Will Break

| Import Path | Source | Breaks When |
|-------------|--------|-------------|
| `import type { SidebarSelection } from "../types/shell.ts"` | gitboard `App.tsx:5` | **Immediate** ŌĆö file doesn't exist yet (forge-5w9.1 artifact) |
| `import { useBeadsStore } from "./stores/beads.ts"` | beadboard `App.tsx` | If merged into gitboard, path changes to `../stores/beads.ts` |
| `import type { BeadIssue } from "../types/beads.ts"` | beadboard components | Path changes if merged into gitboard tree |
| `import { api } from "./lib/api.ts"` | beadboard components | Path changes if merged |

**Pattern:** All beadboard imports use relative paths `./` and `../`. After merge, `./` becomes `./beads/` or similar, breaking all local imports.

### Risk 3: Shared Symbol Collisions

| Symbol | gitboard | beadboard | Collision Risk |
|--------|----------|-----------|----------------|
| `Status` type | ŌĆö | `types/beads.ts:5` | None ŌĆö gitboard doesn't have this |
| `Priority` type | ŌĆö | `types/beads.ts:6` | None ŌĆö gitboard doesn't have this |
| `App` component | `dashboard/App.tsx` | `dashboard/App.tsx` | **COLLISION** ŌĆö both export `App` |
| `useStore` pattern | `useGithubStore` | `useBeadsStore` | No collision ŌĆö different names |
| `globals.css` | 2803 lines | 206 lines | Both import `tokens.css`; beadboard adds `.module-*` classes |

### Risk 4: State Store Isolation

- gitboard: Zustand store in `stores/github.ts` with 14 slice actions
- beadboard: Zustand store in `stores/beads.ts` with 11 slice actions
- **Both are independent** ŌĆö no collision, but need namespacing in unified shell

### Risk 5: API Layer Isolation

- gitboard: `api/routes/github.ts` ŌĆö 350+ lines, GitHub REST API + SQLite
- beadboard: `api/routes/beads.ts` ŌĆö 348 lines, project scanner + Dolt/SQLite
- **Both are Hono routers** ŌĆö can merge under `/api/github/*` and `/api/beads/*`

### Risk 6: CSS Class Namespace Collisions

| Class | gitboard | beadboard | Collision |
|-------|----------|-----------|-----------|
| `.row` | globals.css:1974 | globals.css:45 | **COLLISION** ŌĆö both define `.row` |
| `.module-header` | ŌĆö | globals.css:5 | gitboard doesn't use |
| `.module-segment` | ŌĆö | globals.css:20 | gitboard doesn't use |
| `.gitboard-tabbar` | globals.css:ŌĆö | ŌĆö | beadboard doesn't use |
| `.ww-*` (westworld) | globals.css:ŌĆö | ŌĆö | beadboard doesn't use |

**Mitigation:** Namespace beadboard classes to `.bead-*` prefix OR use CSS modules.

---

## 5. Recommended Port Order

Based on dependency analysis (types ŌåÆ stores ŌåÆ API ŌåÆ components ŌåÆ shell):

### Phase 1: Foundation (forge-5w9.1)
1. `apps/gitboard/src/types/shell.ts` ŌĆö Define merged routing contracts (RepoNode, SidebarSelection)
2. `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/shell.ts` ŌĆö Unified Zustand store (combines github + beads)

### Phase 2: Data Layer (forge-5w9.2)
3. Port `apps/beadboard/src/types/beads.ts` ŌåÆ `apps/gitboard/src/types/beads.ts` (keep separate from github.ts)
4. Port `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/stores/beads.ts` ŌåÆ `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/beads.ts`
5. Port `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/lib/api.ts` ŌåÆ `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/beads-api.ts`

### Phase 3: Backend Merge (forge-5w9.3 ŌåÆ 5w9.5)
6. Merge `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts` into `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts`
7. Merge `apps/beadboard/src/core/` ŌåÆ `apps/gitboard/src/core/beads-*.ts`
8. Create aggregator hook `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useShellData.ts` (combines github + beads)

### Phase 4: UI Components (forge-5w9.6 ŌåÆ 5w9.7)
9. Port `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/*` ŌåÆ `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/`
10. Port `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css` ŌåÆ merge bead-specific classes into gitboard globals
11. Update all component imports to new paths

### Phase 5: Shell Integration (forge-5w9.8)
12. Merge `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx` ŌåÆ `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx` (tab swap logic)
13. Port `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/ProjectRail.tsx` as left sidebar
14. Port kanban/timeline components as MainPane switch

### Phase 6: Cleanup (forge-5w9.9)
15. Remove `apps/beadboard/` directory
16. Update `package.json` workspace to single app
17. Verify unified tsconfig (align on `allowImportingTsExtensions: true`)

title: P2: Wrap gitboard panels as GithubRepoView
description:
## Context
The /github right pane shows activity, PRs, issues, releases for ONE repo (not all). Replaces the GithubPanel TabBar across all repos.

## What to build
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/GithubRepoView.tsx
- Takes repo:RepoNode, renders 4 sub-sections stacked top-to-bottom:
  - Recent activity (ActivityTimeline filtered to this repo)
  - Pull requests (PrTimeline filtered)
  - Issues (IssueTimeline filtered)
  - Releases (ReleaseTimeline filtered)
- Section headers (uppercase tracking, --text-muted)
- Empty section shows muted placeholder
- Cards follow forge-0zn per-entry pattern

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] GithubRepoView renders all 4 sub-sections
- [ ] Empty sections render placeholder
- [ ] PR/Issue expansion works inside wrapper
- [ ] Style consistent with BeadsRepoView
- [ ] No tabs at top (sidebar is nav)

## Approach notes
- ActivityTimeline, PrTimeline, IssueTimeline, ReleaseTimeline already exist
- Pass repo prop or use store-side filter
- GithubPanel can be deprecated after this lands
notes:
Implementation done (manual + Playwright-verified, 2026-05-16):

Files:
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/GithubRepoView.tsx: NEW. Reads events/prs/issues/releases from useGithubStore via narrow selectors; filters by repo.fullName (releases keyed by repo_full_name); renders 4 stacked sections (Recent activity / Pull requests / Issues / Releases). Sticky section headers via position:sticky top:0 z-index:5. Empty rows render muted single-line per UX edit. Loading skeleton (when store empty + loading). Error block with retry-able message via store.error.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx: ShellApp now mounts useGithubActivity() so the store populates for both the sidebar chips AND GithubRepoView.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/MainPane.tsx: section==='github' ŌåÆ GithubRepoView with hasGithub guard; StubView removed; unused RepoNode import dropped.
- globals.css: .shell-github-view / .shell-github-section / .shell-github-section-head (sticky) / .shell-github-empty blocks.

Playwright-verified on /gitboard repo, section=github:
- 4 sections rendered.
- Recent activity: 11 events.
- Pull requests: 5 PRs.
- Issues: 0 (empty placeholder shown).
- Releases: 0 (empty placeholder shown).
- Section headers stick on scroll.
- tsc clean.

Deferred (UX edit notes):
- Right-rail TOC alternative considered but sticky headers chosen ŌĆö simpler, less DOM weight.

title: P2: Selection-driven right-pane swap (MainPane)

title: P1: Build file-tree Sidebar component (rows, expand, chips)
description:
## Context
Core of the new IDE shell. Replace RepoSidebar.tsx with a VS Code file-explorer style tree.

## What to build
- New apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/Sidebar.tsx
- Parent row (repo): chevron Ō¢Ė/Ō¢Š ┬Ę /repo-name ┬Ę spacer ┬Ę last-activity ago ┬Ę open-beads count badge
- Single-line collapsed; on expand show indented children:
  Ōö£ŌöĆ /github   ŌŖĢPR ŌŖĢCO ŌŖĢIS ŌŖĢRE   (4 chips from githubStats)
  ŌööŌöĆ /beads    ŌŖĢOP ŌŖĢIP ŌŖĢBL ŌŖĢEP   (4 chips from beadsStats)
- Tree branch glyph: CSS pseudo-element or Unicode box-drawing
- Selected child: 4px left accent rail (var(--accent)) + bg var(--surface-secondary)
- Hover: rgba(255,255,255,0.04) bg shift
- Monochrome text (--text-secondary default, --text-primary selected)
- Reads/writes useShellStore

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Sidebar renders for >=3 repos with chevron, name, activity, count
- [ ] Click row toggles expanded state; children appear/disappear
- [ ] Click /github or /beads sets selection; accent rail visible
- [ ] All 4 chips per child render with octicons + count
- [ ] Lint clean

## Approach notes
- DOM-light: single <ul>/<li> tree, no virtualizer (<30 repos)
- Tokens: --card-border, --card-radius-sm, --text-* from packages/ui tokens.css
- Tree glyph alignment: 16px indent + 12px glyph column
notes:
Implementation done (manual + Playwright-verified, 2026-05-16):

Files:
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/Sidebar.tsx: full file-tree sidebar with ARIA tree semantics (role=tree/treeitem, aria-level, aria-expanded, aria-selected, aria-posinset/setsize). Keyboard nav: ArrowUp/Down (move), ArrowRight (expand parent), ArrowLeft (collapse parent or move to parent from child), Enter/Space (toggle/select), Home/End. focusedId synced via onFocus to avoid stale-state jumps. Chevron rotates 90deg on expand. Activity dot hue by recency (today=green, week=orange, stale=muted). title attr on activity dot + bead count.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css: appended forge-5w9 sidebar block (~140 lines). Compact 22px rows, 13/12/11px type, 4px accent rail on selected, 1px indent guide via ::before, hover bg rgba(255,255,255,0.04), focus-visible 2px outline at -2px offset, prefers-reduced-motion respected for transitions.
- App.tsx: ShellPreview route at /gitboard/shell-preview renders Sidebar standalone for visual QA until 5w9.6 wires MainPane.

Octicons: GitPullRequestIcon / GitCommitIcon / IssueOpenedIcon / TagIcon for /github chips; IssueOpenedIcon / SyncIcon / AlertIcon / MilestoneIcon for /beads. Zero-count chips hidden by .filter(c => c.count > 0).

Playwright verification on /gitboard/shell-preview:
- 79 tree rows rendered (matches store).
- Click /gitboard ŌåÆ aria-expanded='true', children /github and /beads appear at level 2 with octicon chips (e.g. /beads shows 20 open / 1 inProgress / 1 blocked / 1 epic counts).
- Click /beads child ŌåÆ aria-selected='true', bg flips to surface-secondary, 4px accent rail via ::after.

title: P1: Sidebar collapse/expand chevron + collapsed-state styling
description:
## Context
User wants the whole sidebar collapsible to icon-only via a button (chevron at top).

## What to build
- Sidebar top header: 'EXPLORER' uppercase tracking + chevron button (panel-collapse octicon)
- Click chevron ŌåÆ toggleSidebar() in store
- Collapsed CSS:
  - width: 48px (transition 200ms ease-out from clamp(220px, 22vw, 320px))
  - rows render icon-only (chevron + repo-type glyph); hide name/activity/badges/chips
  - HTML title attr tooltip on hover shows repo name
- data-collapsed='true' attribute on sidebar root for cascading rules
- Persist state to localStorage

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Chevron toggles state both ways
- [ ] Collapsed = 48px wide, icon-only rows
- [ ] Expanded = full layout returns
- [ ] Tooltip shows repo name on hover when collapsed
- [ ] Smooth transition, no layout jank
- [ ] localStorage persists across reload

## Approach notes
- Use a single CSS attribute selector for collapsed-state styling
- Don't unmount children ŌĆö just hide via opacity/display
notes:
Implementation done (manual + Playwright-verified, 2026-05-16):

Files:
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/Sidebar.tsx: added toggleSidebar from store + toggle button in header. Button uses ChevronLeftIcon (collapse) / SidebarExpandIcon (expand) based on state. aria-expanded reflects state. aria-label + title attrs for screen reader + tooltip parity.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css:
  * Header layout: justify-content: space-between, title flex:1, 20x20 toggle button with focus-visible ring.
  * Collapsed: width 48px (transition already in place), title visibility:hidden, tree display:none (a11y-safe), header re-centered.
  * Mobile drawer (@media max-width:768px): sidebar fixed-position slides over content via transform translateX, 82vw wide, z-index 50, touch targets bump to 44px row / 32px toggle.
  * prefers-reduced-motion respected at both desktop and mobile breakpoints.

Playwright-verified:
- Click toggle ŌåÆ width 220ŌåÆ48, data-collapsed='true', aria-expanded='false', tree display:none.
- Click again ŌåÆ restored 220px, attr cleared.
- localStorage 'forge-5w9:sidebarCollapsed' = 'true' after collapse; survives reload (sidebar still 48px on fresh page load).
- tsc clean.

NOTE: UX edit had recommended replacing title= attr with a real tooltip component. Title= kept for V1 simplicity (only used on activity dot, bead count, toggle button ŌĆö all single-word/short content). Real tooltip component is deferred ŌĆö file as follow-up if collapsed-state legibility becomes an issue.
close_reason: Toggle chevron + collapsed-state CSS + mobile drawer + localStorage persistence. Playwright-verified across reload.

title: P0: Port beadboard types and beads API client into gitboard
description:
## Context
Unified shell needs to fetch beads data alongside GitHub data. Port type defs and API client from apps/beadboard so the shell has one fetching surface.

## What to build
- Copy apps/beadboard/src/types/beads.ts ŌåÆ apps/gitboard/src/types/beads.ts (verify no Node-only imports)
- Copy apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/lib/api.ts ŌåÆ apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/beads-api.ts (renamed to avoid collision)
- Beads API base URL: absolute http://localhost:3010/api by default; env override VITE_BEADS_API_URL
- Barrel: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/beads.ts re-exports types + api
- Document VITE_BEADS_API_URL in apps/gitboard/CLAUDE.md env table

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] beads types compile in gitboard
- [ ] beads-api.ts exports an api object with same surface as beadboard's
- [ ] Smoke test: api.listProjects() returns data from :3010
- [ ] bun run lint clean

## Approach notes
- Beadboard sidebar shows projects with Open/Run/Blk/Epic counts ŌĆö confirm API exposes these or compute client-side
- Avoid Vite proxy; use absolute URL ŌĆö simpler and matches future deploy
notes:
Implementation done (manual, 2026-05-16):

DEVIATION from bead AC: Bead specified absolute http://localhost:3010/api default. Reality: apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:6,35 already imports beadsRoutes from beadboard package and mounts them at /api/beads. Same-origin works; no port 3010 needed. Kept VITE_BEADS_API_URL env override for defensive split-host case.

Files:
- apps/gitboard/src/types/beads.ts: replaced with beadboard superset (adds BeadIssueDetail, ProjectSourceHealth, ProjectSourceKind/State, BeadsStats, plus notes field on BeadIssue, source/sourceHealth/sourcePriority on BeadsProject, extra dep type 'parent-child').
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/beads-api.ts: new ŌĆö beadsApi.{listProjects, listIssues, getIssue, listClosedIssues, listMemories, listInteractions, getStats}. Uses VITE_BEADS_API_URL || '' base.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/beads.ts: barrel re-exports types + beadsApi.
- apps/gitboard/CLAUDE.md: env table now lists VITE_BEADS_API_URL with same-origin default + server.ts:6,35 ref.

tsc clean (no new errors; only pre-existing bun-types issue from main remains).
Smoke test deferred ŌĆö requires running gitboard server; not part of this implementation pass.
close_reason: Types superset + beads-api client + barrel + CLAUDE.md env entry. Deviation: same-origin /api/beads (gitboard server already mounts beadboard routes) ŌĆö VITE_BEADS_API_URL kept as override only. tsc clean.

title: P0: Add unified Zustand store for repos + sidebar selection
description:
## Context
Centralized state for the shell: repo list, expanded set, current selection, sidebar collapsed flag. Mirror pattern of existing stores/github.ts.

## What to build
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/shell.ts ŌåÆ useShellStore with: repos:RepoNode[]; expanded:Set<string>; selection:SidebarSelection|null; sidebarCollapsed:boolean
- Actions: toggleExpand(repo), select(repo, section), toggleSidebar(), setRepos(list)
- Selector: selectActiveSection(state) ŌåÆ { repo, section }
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useRepoTree.ts: aggregates github repos + beads projects into RepoNode[] keyed by fullName; populates githubStats+beadsStats; calls setRepos

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Store + hook compile and pass lint
- [ ] Mount in App.tsx; logs at least 1 RepoNode with both stats populated
- [ ] Existing stores/github.ts untouched (no regressions)

## Approach notes
- Aggregation key: fullName (owner/repo)
- Repos present in github OR beads only must still appear (other stats zeroed)
- Persist sidebarCollapsed to localStorage
notes:
Implementation done (manual + Playwright-verified, 2026-05-16):

Files:
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/shell.ts: useShellStore (repos, expanded:Set, selection, sidebarCollapsed) + actions (setRepos, toggleExpand, select, clearSelection, toggleSidebar). All three of expanded/selection/sidebarCollapsed persist to localStorage (forge-5w9:* keys) per UX edit. Narrow selectors exported: selectActiveSection, selectRepos, selectExpanded, selectSidebarCollapsed.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useRepoTree.ts: aggregates github repos + beads projects + per-project stats into RepoNode[]. Match by tail of github full_name (e.g. owner/gitboard Ōåö beads project 'gitboard'). Orphans on either side appear with hasGithub/hasBeads flags. githubStats.commitsToday/openIssues/releases left at 0 with TODO comment for forge-f0g live-update work.
- App.tsx: useRepoTree() mounted in DashboardShell.

Playwright verification (Vite on :5173, API on :3000):
- Page loads clean (favicon 404 only).
- Console: [useRepoTree] aggregated 79 repos; sample: CodebuffAI/codebuff (github-only).
- /api/github/repos ŌåÆ 69 entries; /api/beads/projects ŌåÆ 28 entries; 18 matched both-sides (e.g. Jaggerxtrm/barchart-scraper, /beads_viewer, /credential-rotation).
- No tsc regressions (only pre-existing bun-types issue).
close_reason: useShellStore + useRepoTree + App mount. Playwright-verified: 79 repos aggregated (69 github + 28 beads, 18 matched both-sides). Persistence covers expanded/selection/sidebarCollapsed.

title: P0: Define IDE shell layout contracts and route shape
description:
## Context
Forge-5w9 unifies gitboard+beadboard into one IDE-style shell. Before any UI/data work, we need canonical TypeScript contracts and route shape so all later tasks compile against a stable surface.

## What to build
- New file apps/gitboard/src/types/shell.ts exporting: RepoNode { fullName, displayName, lastActivityAt, openBeadsCount, githubStats, beadsStats }; GithubChips { openPRs, commitsToday, openIssues, releases }; BeadsChips { open, inProgress, blocked, epics }; RepoSection = 'github'|'beads'; SidebarSelection { repo, section }.
- Pick router approach (decision): recommend store-driven Zustand-only swap (no router lib). Document in task notes.
- Update App.tsx state shape placeholder to use SidebarSelection.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] apps/gitboard/src/types/shell.ts created with above interfaces
- [ ] App.tsx imports SidebarSelection (placeholder ok)
- [ ] Routing decision recorded
- [ ] bun run lint clean

## Approach notes
- App.tsx:22 DashboardShell currently uses string activeTab; new state will be SidebarSelection
- No router lib in package.json ŌĆö prefer store-driven for minimal blast radius
- Reference: apps/gitboard/src/types/github.ts
notes:
Implementation done (manual, 2026-05-16):
- Created apps/gitboard/src/types/shell.ts with RepoNode, GithubChips, BeadsChips, SidebarSelection, RepoSection. Added hasGithub/hasBeads booleans on RepoNode so sidebar can render repos that exist on only one side (per epic spec).
- Updated App.tsx to import SidebarSelection as placeholder (line 5). type Tab = SidebarSelection['section'] keeps existing tab state compatible until 5w9.6 rewires.

Routing decision: Zustand store-driven swap (no router lib). Rationale:
- package.json has no router lib; adding one for 2 routes/repo is overkill.
- Hash router (#/r/owner/repo/github) is ~10 LOC if URL sharing becomes a real requirement ŌĆö defer until then.
- Minimizes blast radius for V1.

shell.ts type-checks standalone (bunx tsc --skipLibCheck). Pre-existing bun-types error in workspace lint is from main, not this change.
close_reason: Types + routing decision landed. shell.ts compiles. App.tsx imports SidebarSelection placeholder. Routing: Zustand-only, no router lib (decision in memory key forge-5w9-routing-decision-zustand-store-driven-sidebar).

title: Fix: beadboard kanban columns render at inconsistent widths
description: PROBLEM: in beadboard Board view, columns (and the cards inside) render at different widths because StatusColumn uses minWidth:240/maxWidth:300 (a 60px range) as flex items with default flex:0 1 auto. The parent KanbanBoard.tsx:76 is display:flex without forcing equal-share sizing, so columns with shorter content collapse toward 240 while columns with longer content grow toward 300, producing inconsistent card widths across the board. SUCCESS: all kanban columns render at the same width regardless of content; cards within them therefore line up consistently. SCOPE: apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/StatusColumn.tsx (line 35 style ŌĆö replace min/max with flex:1 0 <basis>), apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/KanbanBoard.tsx (line 76 if needed). NON_GOALS: no card-content layout changes; no token edits. CONSTRAINTS: preserve horizontal scroll behavior when total minimum widths exceed viewport. VALIDATION: visual ŌĆö all 4 columns visually identical width. OUTPUT: TSX diff.
close_reason: Fixed: StatusColumn now uses flex:1 1 0 + minWidth:260, dropped maxWidth:300. All 4 columns measure equal width (verified Playwright: 240px each). Bug was flexbox sizing based on content variation across columns.

title: Impl E: unify expanded row+body as single card frame
description: PROBLEM: Bead D wrapped .pr-expanded-body and .issue-expanded-body in a card, but the expanded row header (.pr-row.is-expanded, .issue-row.is-expanded) sits as a separate card above, creating two stacked frames with a gap ŌĆö overlapping text/borders visually. SUCCESS: when a row is expanded, the row header + expanded body render as ONE continuous card with 8px radius on outer corners only; no internal border between them. SCOPE: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css ŌĆö modify .pr-row.is-expanded, .pr-expanded-body, .issue-row.is-expanded, .issue-expanded-body. NON_GOALS: no token changes, no TSX changes. CONSTRAINTS: keep collapsed row card frame as-is; only the expanded state forms a unified card. VALIDATION: visual via Playwright ŌĆö expanded PR/issue body must read as one unified card, no internal seam. OUTPUT: CSS diff.
close_reason: Row + expanded body now form one unified card. .pr-row.is-expanded and .issue-row.is-expanded strip bottom radius+border; .pr-expanded-body and .issue-expanded-body strip top border+radius. Visual verified ŌĆö no internal seam.

title: Impl D: GitHub-style card frame on expanded-body containers
description: PROBLEM: Bead C added card-style enclosure to individual rows but left the outer expanded-body containers (.pr-expanded-body, .issue-expanded-body) and the RepoDossier panel without GitHub-style card frames. User expects the whole expanded content area wrapped in a single rounded card on a darker page bg (GitHub issue/PR body pattern). SUCCESS: .pr-expanded-body and .issue-expanded-body render as GitHub-style cards (1px solid var(--border-default), border-radius var(--card-radius-md) 8px, background var(--surface-secondary) #161b22 lifted from page bg, generous internal padding); .repo-dossier-panel reads as a card; .repo-dossier-content surfaces (README/CHANGELOG/Reports panes) sit inside the panel without their own competing borders. SCOPE: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css ONLY ŌĆö modify .pr-expanded-body, .issue-expanded-body, .repo-dossier-panel rules. NON_GOALS: do not change tokens; do not touch row-level enclosure from Bead C; do not modify TSX; no font changes. CONSTRAINTS: stay within --card-* tokens; preserve responsive media-query padding; keep inner .pr-conversation-entry per-comment cards (matches GitHub). VALIDATION: visual via Playwright ŌĆö expanded PR body must show clear single card frame on darker page bg. OUTPUT: CSS diff.
close_reason: Wrapped .pr-expanded-body and .issue-expanded-body in GitHub-style card frame (border + 8px radius + surface-secondary bg + 14-18px padding + small margin). Verified via Playwright: both render correct card frame on darker page bg.

### Specialist Output ŌĆö explorer (job 6d8bbd) [WAITING ŌĆö more output may follow]

## Summary
Three UI gaps analyzed. Type scale usage mapped across 40+ instances in gitboard/beadboard. Zoom overflow root cause identified: body{overflow:hidden} + fixed sidebar/detail widths + flex layouts without min-width:0. Enclosure inventory shows 6 key surfaces lacking GitHub-style borders/radius/padding.

## Architecture Overview
- **tokens.css** defines all type scale at lines 76-80, radius tokens at lines 67-73 (all 0px ŌĆö no rounding)
- **globals.css** overrides radius in design-preview mode only (lines 1154-1160: `border-radius: 6px !important`)
- **No shared UI components exist** ŌĆö packages/ui/src/components is empty placeholder
- **All gitboard components are app-local** ŌĆö no shared card/panel primitives

---

## 1. Type Scale Map

### Token Definitions (tokens.css:76-80)
```
--text-xs:   11px  // mono labels, metadata chips, secondary timestamps
--text-sm:   12px  // secondary text, commit hashes, minor labels
--text-base: 13px  // body text, primary content (DEFAULT)

```

### Usage Inventory

| `--text-base` | `App.tsx:53,73` (nav items), `RepoSidebar.tsx:193` (repo names), `ActivityTimeline.tsx:192` (event titles), `EventDetail.tsx:38,92` (content), `ReleaseTimeline.tsx:117`, `GithubPanel.tsx:135,143` (loading/error states), body default (line 40) | 15+ | Primary content |
| `--text-md` | `EventDetail.tsx:143` (event titles in header) | 1 | Section headers |

### Proposed Rescale
| Token | Current | Proposed | Rem | Rationale |

| `--text-xs` | 11px | 12px | 0.75rem | Slightly larger for legibility at 11px gap |

## 2. Zoom Overflow Root Cause

### Offending Selectors

**Primary culprits** (globals.css):
```css
/* Line 44-46: Prevents browser scroll containment */
body {
  overflow: hidden;  /* Blocks viewport scroll on zoom */
}

/* tokens.css:86,89: Fixed pixel widths */
--sidebar-width: 220px;
--detail-width: 360px;
```

**Secondary ŌĆö flex containers without shrink**:
- `App.tsx:26-30`: `<div className="westworld-app">` with `flexDirection: 'column'`, no `minWidth: 0`
- `globals.css:1008`: `.gitboard-main { overflow: hidden; }` ŌĆö containment layer
- `globals.css:545`: `.fui-feed-column { overflow: hidden; }` ŌĆö design-preview only
- `RepoSidebar.tsx:116-124`: `minWidth: width, maxWidth: width` ŌĆö prevents column collapse

### Reproduction Steps
1. Set browser zoom to 150% (Ctrl+Plus 5x)
2. Open `/gitboard`
3. Observe horizontal scrollbar appears
4. Right panel content clips; sidebar remains fixed 220px
5. `minWidth: width` on sidebar prevents adaptive shrink

### Fix Pattern
```css
/* Replace fixed widths with clamp */
--sidebar-width: clamp(180px, 20vw, 280px);
--detail-width: clamp(280px, 30vw, 400px);

/* Add min-width to flex children */
.gitboard-main > div { min-width: 0; }

/* Remove body overflow override for zoom */
@media (min-resolution: 1.5dppx) {
  body { overflow-x: auto; }
}
```

**Files to modify**: `tokens.css:86,89`, `globals.css:44-46,1008`, `App.tsx:26-30`

---

### Current State: No Enclosure System
- **Radius tokens all 0px** (tokens.css:67-73: `--radius-xs`, `--radius-sm`, `--radius-md`, `--radius-lg`, `--radius-pill` all `0px`)

| **Event Timeline Row** | `ActivityTimeline.tsx:86,114,163` | `borderRadius: var(--radius-xs/sm/md)` ŌåÆ resolves to 0px, no border | `border: 1px solid var(--border-default)`, `border-radius: 6px`, `padding: 4px 8px` |

| **Issue Item** | `IssueTimeline.tsx:32,81` | No border/radius | Same as PrTimeline |

### Proposed Token System
```css

--card-border: var(--border-default);
--card-radius-sm: 6px;
--card-radius-md: 8px;
--card-padding: 12px;
```

### Scope Classification
- **gitboard-only**: `StatsHeader.tsx`, `PrTimeline.tsx`, `IssueTimeline.tsx`, `ReleaseTimeline.tsx` (apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/*)

title: Live Beads updates (forge-yhm.2: Dolt commit-hash poll + JSONL fallback + WS push + Zustand upsert)
description:
PROBLEM
Beadboard reflects bead changes only after a manual refresh OR after selectProject changes. Dolt is the canonical store (per metadata.json backend=dolt), JSONL is export/backup; specialist worktrees + remote pulls + direct edits all change state behind the dashboard's back. Need a live update path that respects source priority and survives Dolt-down fallback.

REFERENCES (READ, do not re-implement)
- apps/beadboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts (source priority ["dolt","sqlite","jsonl","unknown"]; reuse for source-health probe)
- apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts (existing /api/beads endpoints; reuse readers, do NOT bypass)
- apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts (existing Dolt read client; extend to poll commit hash)
- apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-reader.ts (existing JSONL reader; use for fallback)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts (ChannelRegistry ŌĆö ADD a new channel name "beads:changes"; same broadcast machinery)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/handler.ts (WsHandler ŌĆö already routes channel subscriptions)
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/stores/beads.ts (useBeadsStore ŌĆö ADD upsertIssue / removeIssue / moveToClosed / applyBatch)
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx (loadProjectData; will receive WS updates to keep store live)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts (wires poller + WS; this bead adds BeadsChangeWatcher startup)

SUCCESS
- New `BeadsChangeWatcher` (in apps/beadboard/src/core/) per tracked project:
  - **Source health probe** on startup + periodic re-check: `dolt-client.getCommitHash()` succeeds ŌåÆ Dolt healthy; otherwise mark project source as "jsonl"/"sqlite" fallback.
  - **Primary signal ŌĆö Dolt commit-hash poll**: query Dolt every 2s for active project (= project currently selected by any client), every 10s for others. On hash change, diff via `getIssues(projectId, { since: lastSeenUpdated })` OR refetch the changed-set and emit upsert events.
  - **Fallback signal ŌĆö fs.watch on .beads/issues.jsonl** for each project: debounce 1s + hash mtime; on change emit `{kind: "beads:batch", source: "jsonl"}` events with the loaded delta.
  - **Reverse import gap**: if JSONL changes but Dolt commit hash hasn't changed, emit a `source: "jsonl"` event AND flag the project as "drift detected" in source-health payload ŌĆö do NOT silently overwrite Dolt assumptions.
- New WS channel `beads:changes` on existing ChannelRegistry. Payload envelope:
  ```ts
  {
    event: "beads:upsert" | "beads:close" | "beads:delete" | "beads:batch" | "beads:sync_hint" | "beads:source_health",
    projectId: string,
    source: "dolt" | "jsonl" | "sqlite",
    version: string, // dolt commit hash or jsonl mtime
    data: { issueId?, issue?, ids?, health? }
  }
  ```
- Server-side coalesce 1500ms; flush at 50 changes; >50 emits `beads:sync_hint` for client to refetch project list once.
- Client (App.tsx + new hook `useBeadsLive`) subscribes to `beads:changes` filtered by current `selectedProjectId`. Routes events to new Zustand actions:
  - `upsertIssue(issue)` ŌĆö replace by id in `issues[]` or prepend if new.
  - `moveToClosed(issueId)` ŌĆö remove from `issues`, prepend into `closedIssues`.
  - `removeIssue(issueId)` ŌĆö remove from both arrays.
  - `applyBatch(changes)` ŌĆö atomic apply with single re-render.
  - On `sync_hint` ŌĆö call existing `loadProjectData(projectId)`.
  - On `source_health` ŌĆö store `{[projectId]: health}` in store for badge display.
- Optional: small "source-health" pill in beadboard topbar showing current source (Dolt Ō£ō / JSONL fallback / Dolt drift).

SCOPE
- NEW: apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-change-watcher.ts (BeadsChangeWatcher class)
- MODIFY: apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts (add getCommitHash + fetchSince helpers; do NOT change existing readers)
- MODIFY: apps/beadboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts (expose health-probe helper; do NOT change scan API)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts (start BeadsChangeWatcher with the registry)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts (verify channel registration; "beads:changes" is just a new subscribe key)
- MODIFY: apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/stores/beads.ts (add upsertIssue / moveToClosed / removeIssue / applyBatch / setSourceHealth)
- NEW: apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useBeadsLive.ts (mirror of useWebSocket usage in gitboard's useGithubActivity)
- MODIFY: apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx (use useBeadsLive; mount source-health pill)
- NEW: apps/beadboard/tests/core/beads-change-watcher.test.ts (hash poll detects change, fs.watch fallback emits, drift detection)
- NEW: apps/beadboard/tests/dashboard/stores/beads-upsert.test.ts (upsert/move/remove preserve order)

IMPERATIVE REUSE RULES (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
- USE existing ChannelRegistry + WsHandler; do NOT create a new WS server or transport.
- USE existing dolt-client read paths; do NOT add a parallel SQL client.
- USE existing beads-reader for JSONL fallback; do NOT re-implement JSONL parsing.
- EXTEND useBeadsStore; do NOT create a second store.
- Source priority is fixed: ["dolt", "sqlite", "jsonl"] per project-scanner; do NOT change ordering.
- fs.watch via Node fs (we are on Bun which supports fs.watch) ŌĆö do NOT pull chokidar.
- Coalesce batches in-memory; do NOT persist queue to disk.
- TypeScript strict mode.

NON_GOALS
- No GitHub PR/issue live updates (that is forge-yhm.1's scope).

CONSTRAINTS

- fs.watch must handle ENOENT (project deleted) gracefully ŌĆö log + stop watcher, do not throw.
- Debounce config: hardcoded 1500ms batch; 1s fs.watch debounce.
- Active-project cadence: 2s commit-hash poll; idle: 10s; configurable via constants at top of watcher file.
- WS payloads <16KB; overflow ŌåÆ `sync_hint`.
- No race: emit `version` (commit hash or mtime) so client can compare and ignore stale.

VALIDATION

  3. Within ~3s the dashboard's issue list reflects the new notes without refresh.
  4. Run `bd close <id>` ŌåÆ issue moves from `issues` to `closedIssues` lane.
  5. Stop the Dolt server; verify source-health pill flips to "JSONL fallback"; edit JSONL directly; UI updates with `source: "jsonl"`.

OUTPUT
