title: Live GitHub PR/Issue updates (forge-yhm.1: server poll + WS push + client upsert)
description:
PROBLEM
Today's GitHub poller only polls /users/<me>/events, which misses issues + PRs created by collaborators. forge-1kv (just shipped) and recent collaborator activity (Rico1109 #257) only surface after a manual one-shot backfill. Dashboard cannot reflect remote changes without page refresh.

REFERENCES (READ, do not re-implement)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts (existing GithubPoller class; pollUser, backfillPrsAndIssues, setInterval at line 595 ŌĆö extend, don't fork)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/github-store.ts (insertPr/insertIssue/upsertRepo ŌĆö use existing helpers)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts (ChannelRegistry ŌĆö already broadcasts on "github:activity")
- apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/handler.ts (WsHandler connection lifecycle)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts (existing useWebSocket subscription; wsHandler handles "new_event"/"new_commits" ŌĆö add "new_pr"/"new_issue")
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/github.ts (Zustand store; add upsertPr / upsertIssue / markRepoUnread is already there)
- apps/gitboard/src/types/github.ts (GithubPr, GithubIssue shapes)

SUCCESS
- New `RepoPollerLoop` class (or extension on GithubPoller) periodically fetches `/repos/:owner/:repo/issues?state=all&since=<lastIssueUpdated>&per_page=100` and `/repos/:owner/:repo/pulls?state=all&sort=updated&direction=desc&per_page=100` per tracked repo.
- ETag cache per (repo, endpoint): send `If-None-Match`, skip parse on 304.
- Watermark tracking: per repo store `last_issue_updated_at` and `last_pr_updated_at`; stop iterating pages once a row predates the watermark.
- Adaptive cadence:
  - **1 min** for "active" repos = (repo has push event in last 1h) OR (repo currently in `filter.repos` from any connected client) OR (manually toggled active in UI later)
  - 10 min default
  - 30 min for repos with no activity in 24h
  - Backoff: pause polling for the next minute when `X-RateLimit-Remaining < 500`; resume below 80% budget consumed
- Detection: when an inserted PR or issue is NEW (insert returned true) OR has updated since cached version, build a normalized payload and broadcast on `github:activity` channel: `{ event: "new_pr" | "new_issue", data: { repo, number, title, state, author, url, updated_at } }`
- Client wsHandler in useGithubActivity adds two cases:
  - `new_pr`: call `useGithubStore.getState().upsertPr(pr)` and `markRepoUnread(pr.repo)`
  - `new_issue`: call `useGithubStore.getState().upsertIssue(issue)` and `markRepoUnread(issue.repo)`
- Coalesce: server-side buffer events 1500ms; if buffer >50 items, emit a single `{ event: "sync_hint", repos: [...] }` payload that causes client to refetch the list once instead of upserting individually.
- New Zustand actions: `upsertPr(pr)`, `upsertIssue(issue)` ŌĆö replace existing element by `(repo, number)` key or prepend if new; preserve current sort.

SCOPE
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts (add RepoPollerLoop, ETag cache, watermarks, adaptive cadence, broadcast on insert)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/core/github-store.ts (add `last_issue_updated_at` / `last_pr_updated_at` to repo row, OR a side table `repo_poll_state`; expose helper getters/setters)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts (wire poller's broadcast ŌåÆ registry channel "github:activity")
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts (broadcasts already exist; verify; no API change unless event type union widens)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/github.ts (add upsertPr, upsertIssue actions, sync_hint handler)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts (handle new_pr / new_issue / sync_hint in wsHandler)
- NEW: apps/gitboard/tests/core/github-poller-loop.test.ts (smoke: poll cycle inserts new PR, broadcasts payload; ETag 304 path; rate-limit backoff)
- NEW: apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/stores/github-upsert.test.ts (upsert by composite key, preserve order)

IMPERATIVE REUSE RULES (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
- EXTEND existing GithubPoller class; do NOT create a parallel poller class file.
- USE existing ChannelRegistry + WsHandler; do NOT add a new WS channel or transport.
- USE existing `insertPr` / `insertIssue` helpers; do NOT bypass and write SQL directly elsewhere.
- USE existing `useGithubStore` + `useWebSocket` hooks; do NOT create parallel state or a second WS client.
- ETag stored in-memory Map at first; add DB persistence ONLY if scope explicitly says so (it doesn't).
- Cadence "active" definition is described above ŌĆö do NOT add a config UI or env var yet (hardcoded thresholds OK).
- No external libraries (gh REST via existing `githubApi` helper).

NON_GOALS
- No beads change stream (forge-yhm.2 covers that).
- No issue/PR detail re-poll for selected items (separate concern; mention in OUTPUT as residual).
- No `/console` integration.
- No persistence of ETag/watermark across server restarts (in-memory OK).
- No GraphQL migration.
- No UI controls for cadence (hardcoded thresholds).

CONSTRAINTS
- TypeScript strict mode.
- Bun runtime (`Bun.spawnSync`, `bun:sqlite`).
- Hono for routes (no change).
- Reuse `getGithubToken()` from poller; never read env directly elsewhere.
- All HTTP requests must check `X-RateLimit-Remaining` and short-circuit polling if <500.
- WS payload size <16KB per message; batches that exceed should split into `sync_hint`.
- Maintain backward compatibility with existing "new_event" and "new_commits" payloads ŌĆö they keep working.

VALIDATION
- `bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test` green (existing 208 + 2 new = 210).
- `bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard build:dashboard` green.
- Smoke (orchestrator runs):
  1. Open an issue on `Jaggerxtrm/xtrm-tools` via `gh issue create` after server is running.
  2. Within ~1 min the dashboard's Issues tab shows it without page refresh.
  3. Open a PR similarly; PR row appears in PR tab.
  4. Observe `X-RateLimit-Remaining` in server logs; confirm adaptive cadence.

OUTPUT
- File diff list (estimate 6 modified + 2 new tests)
- New WS event payload shapes (typed)
- Adaptive cadence thresholds with rationale
- Residual risks (e.g. detail refetch not implemented, ETag not persisted)

title: SCOPE 2 (parallel perspective): forge-yhm live updates
description:
PROBLEM
Currently the dashboard only learns about new GitHub PRs/Issues via the poller's initial backfill (one-shot at startup), and never reflects beads changes between manual refreshes. User wants live updates covering BOTH the GitHub side AND the beads side. Beads has two sources: Dolt server (canonical, sometimes drifts) and .beads/issues.jsonl (backup). Need to pick the right canonical change-source.

QUESTIONS (please answer ALL with reasoning + concrete recommendation)

1. **Beads source-of-truth**: Is Dolt the true canonical, with JSONL the backup? When/how do they diverge? If a session edits JSONL directly (e.g. specialist worktree), is that reflected back to Dolt?

2. **Change detection ŌĆö beads side**:
   a. Dolt SQL polling against `commit_hash`/`commit_log` for change deltas?
   b. Dolt replication (binlog/CDC) ŌĆö does Dolt expose one?
   c. fs.watch on .beads/issues.jsonl per project (cheap, lossy on rename)?
   d. Hook the `bd` CLI to emit events?
   Rank with tradeoffs.

3. **Change detection ŌĆö GitHub side**: Currently poller hits user-event feed only. To catch issues opened by collaborators we need per-repo /issues + /prs polling on a separate cadence. What's the right interval given GH REST rate-limit (5000/hr)? Use ETag/conditional requests to save quota?

4. **WS channel architecture**: One unified `forge:live` channel with typed payloads `{kind: "github:pr" | "github:issue" | "beads:issue" | "beads:close"}`, OR separate channels per source (`github:activity` + `beads:changes`)? Tradeoffs for routing, subscription complexity, multi-project filtering.

5. **Granularity & cadence**: Push every row mutation immediately, or coalesce in 1ŌĆō2s batches? What if 50 beads close at once from `xt end`?

6. **Client mutation strategy**: When `new_issue` arrives, do we (a) prepend to store and re-render, (b) refetch the whole issue list, (c) refetch only that one issue's detail? Same question for beads ŌĆö Zustand `useBeadsStore` currently holds whole arrays.

7. **forge-yhm scope split**: Should we ship this as one unified bead, OR split into:
   - forge-yhm.1 GH periodic poll + WS push (smaller, well-understood)
   - forge-yhm.2 Beads change stream (riskier, dolt-dependent)
   Recommend the split with rationale.

8. **Premortem**: 30 days after shipping, what kills this design? Rate-limit exhaustion? WS reconnect storms? Stale Dolt port? Race conditions between push and poll? List the top 3 failure modes and the cheapest mitigations.

OUTPUT
- Numbered answers to all 8 with explicit recommendation per question
- Final architectural recommendation in 5 lines max
- Risks ranked

notes:
### Specialist Output ŌĆö overthinker (job 7f4eed) [WAITING ŌĆö more output may follow]

## Summary

Read repo evidence. No file changes. No bd claim/close possible: no bash tool exposed. GitNexus/Serena tools unavailable in harness, so used read/grep/ls only.

## Phase 1 - Initial Analysis

Goal: live dashboard updates for GitHub + beads.

Local facts:
- GitHub poller exists in `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts`.
- Current loop polls only `/users/:username/events` every 5 min.
- PR/issues full backfill runs only inside `backfill()`, not periodic.
- WS infra exists: `github:activity` channel in `apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts`.
- Client already subscribes to `github:activity` in `useGithubActivity.ts`.
- Beads API reads sources by priority: Dolt ŌåÆ SQLite ŌåÆ JSONL in `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts`.
- Scanner reports same priority in `apps/beadboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts`.
- `.beads/export-state.json` stores `last_dolt_commit`, issue count, timestamp.
- `.beads/README.md` says Beads stored in Dolt, syncs with commits, JSONL present as export/backup.

Core problem: need change producer, event envelope, client merge path.

## Phase 2 - Devil's Advocate

Assumption: Dolt always canonical. Risk: local Dolt server stale/down/wrong DB. Evidence: `.beads/dolt-server.log` has many `table not found` errors. Need fallback health.

Assumption: JSONL watch catches all. Risk: Dolt writes may not export JSONL immediately; fs.watch misses rename/burst; worktrees skipped by scanner.

Assumption: GitHub events enough. False. User event feed misses collaborator-created issues/PRs unless authenticated user involved.

Assumption: push every change. Risk: `xt end` closes many beads; WS flood + React re-render storm.

Assumption: client can mutate arrays. Risk: filters/sorting/source transitions make prepend wrong. Need idempotent upsert + periodic reconciliation.

## Phase 3 - Synthesis

Best architecture: one live bus, two producers.

- GitHub producer: keep user events poll. Add per-tracked-repo issue/PR updater with watermarks + ETag.
- Beads producer: poll canonical Dolt commit hash when healthy. Use JSONL watch as fallback/supplement. Do not rely on CLI hook first.
- WS: unified `forge:live` channel, typed payloads, project/repo filters.
- Server coalesces changes in 1ŌĆō2s batches.
- Client applies cheap idempotent upserts for visible rows, refetches detail when selected row affected, full list reconciliation on reconnect/source drift.

## Phase 4 - Final Refined Output

## Final Answer

### 1. Beads source-of-truth

**Recommendation: Treat Dolt as canonical when healthy; JSONL as backup/export; SQLite as middle fallback.**

Reason:
- `ProjectScanner` priority: `["dolt", "sqlite", "jsonl", "unknown"]`.
- `beadsRoutes` reads sources in same priority.
- `.beads/README.md` says issues are ŌĆ£Stored in Dolt databaseŌĆØ.
- `.beads/export-state.json` records `last_dolt_commit`, timestamp, count. Looks export marker, not primary DB.

Divergence:
- Dolt server down/stale/wrong DB.
- JSONL export lag after Dolt mutation.
- Worktree/specialist edits touching JSONL directly.
- Git hook sync not run yet.
- Manual file edits bypass `bd`.

Direct JSONL edit reflected to Dolt? **No evidence in repo. Assume no automatic reverse import.** Needs `bd` import/sync path or explicit CLI action. Direct JSONL writes should be treated as divergent backup state, not canonical mutation.

---

### 2. Change detection ŌĆö beads side

**Recommendation rank:**

1. **Dolt SQL polling against commit hash / log**
   - Best canonical signal.
   - Cheap: poll `HEAD`/commit hash every 1ŌĆō2s per active project, 5ŌĆō15s idle.
   - On hash change, query updated issues by `updated_at`/diff if available.
   - Needs schema probe because local code has no commit-log implementation yet.

2. **fs.watch on `.beads/issues.jsonl`**
   - Good fallback and wake-up signal.
   - Cheap, easy.
   - Lossy on atomic rename/burst. Must debounce then refetch.
   - Useful when Dolt unreachable or JSONL-only project.

3. **Hook `bd` CLI to emit events**
   - Precise for local `bd` commands.
   - Bad coverage: misses direct DB changes, remote pulls, specialists without wrapper, manual edits.
   - Good later as optimization, not foundation.

4. **Dolt replication/binlog/CDC**
   - Do not pick now.
   - No local evidence of exposed CDC path.
   - Highest complexity.

Concrete beads design:
- `BeadsChangeWatcher`
  - source health probe.
  - if Dolt healthy: poll commit hash.
  - if hash changed: refetch changed issue summaries/details or list.
  - always watch JSONL as fallback trigger.
  - debounce 1ŌĆō2s, publish batch.

---

### 3. Change detection ŌĆö GitHub side

**Recommendation: Add per-repo polling on separate cadence: 10 min default, staggered, ETag + watermarks.**

Current:
- `/users/:username/events` every 5 min.
- Backfill PR/issues one-shot only.

Need:
- For each tracked repo:
  - `/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues?state=all&since=<last_issue_updated>&per_page=100`
  - `/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls?state=all&sort=updated&direction=desc&per_page=100`
  - stop when `updated_at <= watermark`.

Rate math:
- 2 requests/repo/cycle.
- 10 min cadence = 12 requests/repo/hour.
- 50 repos = 600/hour.
- 100 repos = 1200/hour.
- Under 5000/hour with room for detail calls.
- 5 min cadence doubles cost: 100 repos = 2400/hour, still possible but less safe.

Use ETag:
- Store ETag per URL+params.
- Send `If-None-Match`.
- On 304, skip parsing/upsert.
- Still implement hard budget using `X-RateLimit-Remaining`.

Adaptive cadence:
- Active repo: 2ŌĆō5 min.
- Normal: 10 min.
- Idle/no changes 24h: 30 min.
- Low rate remaining: backoff to 30ŌĆō60 min.

---

### 4. WS channel architecture

**Recommendation: one unified `forge:live` channel with typed payloads.**

Payload:
```ts
{
  kind: "github:pr" | "github:issue" | "github:event" | "beads:issue" | "beads:close",
  projectId?: string,
  repo?: string,
  op: "upsert" | "delete" | "close" | "batch" | "sync_hint",
  data: unknown,
  version?: string,
  source?: "dolt" | "sqlite" | "jsonl" | "github"
}
```

Why unified:
- One client subscription.
- One reconnect/backoff path.
- One batch envelope.
- Easy multi-source dashboard.

Separate channels downside:
- `github:activity` + `beads:changes` duplicates reconnect, filter, handler code.
- Cross-source views need multiple subscriptions.

Caveat:
- Keep internal producer names separate.
- Channel can support filters: projectId/repo/kinds during subscribe later.

---

### 5. Granularity & cadence

**Recommendation: coalesce 1ŌĆō2s batches, never push every row immediately by default.**

Why:
- `xt end` can close 50 beads.
- React/Zustand full-array writes can rerender hard.
- fs.watch may emit burst events.

Batch shape:
```ts
{
  kind: "beads:batch",
  projectId,
  changes: [
    { op: "upsert", issueId, status, updated_at },
    { op: "close", issueId, closed_at }
  ],
  sourceVersion: "dolt-commit-hash"
}
```

Rules:
- If one change: still send single or batch; client handler same.
- Max batch delay: 2s.
- Max batch size: e.g. 100; overflow sends `sync_hint` causing refetch.
- On source version jump with unknown diff: send `sync_hint`.

---

### 6. Client mutation strategy

**Recommendation: hybrid. Upsert list rows, refetch selected detail, full refetch on uncertain batch/reconnect.**

GitHub:
- `new_event`: prepend/upsert existing `events`, already supported by `prependEvent`.
- `github:pr` / `github:issue`: add store actions `upsertPr`, `upsertIssue`.
- If selected PR/issue changed: refetch detail endpoint.
- Do not full-refetch list for every event.

Beads:
- Add `upsertIssue`, `removeIssue`, `moveToClosed`, `applyBatch` to `useBeadsStore`.
- If issue changes status from open ŌåÆ closed: remove from `issues`, upsert into `closedIssues`.
- If selected issue changed: refetch `/api/beads/projects/:id/issues/:issueId`.
- If `sourceVersion` gap, overflow, reconnect: refetch whole visible project lists.

Reason:
- Current store holds whole arrays; full list refetch per mutation is safe but wasteful.
- Pure local mutation risks filter/sort drift.
- Hybrid gives responsive UI + eventual consistency.

---

### 7. forge-yhm scope split

**Recommendation: split.**

- `forge-yhm.1`: GitHub periodic repo PR/issues poll + WS push.
- `forge-yhm.2`: Beads change stream + source-health + Dolt/JSONL fallback.

Rationale:
- GH path is bounded, existing DB/store/routes already support PR/issues.
- Beads path has source-truth risk, Dolt health, JSONL drift, debounce, worktree questions.
- Split gives usable live GitHub sooner.
- Split lets beads watcher ship after schema/source probing.
- Review/test scope smaller.

---

### 8. Premortem ŌĆö top 3 failure modes

#### 1. Rate-limit exhaustion
Cause:
- Too many repos.
- Detail endpoint fanout.
- No ETag/backoff.

Cheap mitigation:
- 10 min default cadence.
- ETag cache.
- Watermarks.
- Repo staggering.
- Stop polling when `X-RateLimit-Remaining < 500`.
- UI source-health badge.

#### 2. Beads source drift / stale Dolt port
Cause:
- Dolt server points to wrong DB.
- JSONL updated but Dolt stale.
- Direct JSONL edits not imported.

Cheap mitigation:
- Source health model visible in payload/UI.
- Compare Dolt count/hash vs JSONL mtime/export-state.
- Fallback to JSONL only when Dolt unhealthy.
- Emit `source: "dolt" | "jsonl"` in WS payload.
- Periodic full reconciliation.

#### 3. WS reconnect/batch race
Cause:
- Client disconnects during batch.
- Poll writes DB then WS push lost.
- Client applies stale event after refetch.

Cheap mitigation:
- Every payload carries `sourceVersion` + `updated_at`.
- Client idempotent upsert by id and newer timestamp.
- On reconnect: full refetch visible lists.
- Exponential backoff already exists; add jitter.
- Server sends `sync_hint` after overflow/gap.

---

## Risks

1. **Dolt watcher uncertainty** ŌĆö local code has no commit-log probe yet. Need schema/research pass.
2. **JSONL reverse-sync assumption** ŌĆö direct JSONL edits likely not reflected to Dolt.
3. **GitHub polling cost** ŌĆö safe with ETag/watermarks, risky without.
4. **Client store churn** ŌĆö needs upsert actions to avoid full-array thrash.
5. **WS event loss** ŌĆö fix by reconnect refetch + sourceVersion.

## Verification

Evidence read:
- `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/github.ts`
- `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts`
- `apps/beadboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts`
- `apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts`
- `.beads/README.md`
- `.beads/export-state.json`

## Beads

Could not run:
- `bd update forge-7ff --claim`
- `bd close forge-7ff --reason="done"`

Reason: no bash tool exposed in current harness.

{

  "summary": "Completed 4-phase Overthinker analysis for forge-yhm live updates. Recommended split delivery, Dolt-first beads watcher with JSONL fallback, repo-level GitHub polling with ETag, unified forge:live WS channel, batched payloads, hybrid client mutation strategy.",

   d. Hook the `bd` CLI to emit events?

3. **Change detection ŌĆö GitHub side**: Currently poller hits user-event feed only. To catch issues opened by collaborators we need per-repo /issues + /prs polling on a separate cadence. What's the right interval given GH REST rate-limit (5000/hr)? Use ETag/conditional requests to save quota?

4. **WS channel architecture**: One unified `forge:live` channel with typed payloads `{kind: "github:pr" | "github:issue" | "beads:issue" | "beads:close"}`, OR separate channels per source (`github:activity` + `beads:changes`)? Tradeoffs for routing, subscription complexity, multi-project filtering.

5. **Granularity & cadence**: Push every row mutation immediately, or coalesce in 1ŌĆō2s batches? What if 50 beads close at once from `xt end`?

6. **Client mutation strategy**: When `new_issue` arrives, do we (a) prepend to store and re-render, (b) refetch the whole issue list, (c) refetch only that one issue's detail? Same question for beads ŌĆö Zustand `useBeadsStore` currently holds whole arrays.

7. **forge-yhm scope split**: Should we ship this as one unified bead, OR split into:
   - forge-yhm.1 GH periodic poll + WS push (smaller, well-understood)
   - forge-yhm.2 Beads change stream (riskier, dolt-dependent)
   Recommend the split with rationale.

8. **Premortem**: 30 days after shipping, what kills this design? Rate-limit exhaustion? WS reconnect storms? Stale Dolt port? Race conditions between push and poll? List the top 3 failure modes and the cheapest mitigations.

OUTPUT
- Numbered answers to all 8 with explicit recommendation per question
- Final architectural recommendation in 5 lines max
- Risks ranked
notes:
### Specialist Output ŌĆö overthinker (job 2bee39) [WAITING ŌĆö more output may follow]

## Summary

forge-yhm should split. GH live path small. Beads stream risky because Dolt/JSONL truth split needs source contract.

## Status

Read-only scope complete. No files changed. `bd` claim/close not run: no bash tool available in harness.

## Phase 1 ŌĆö Initial Analysis

### 1. Beads source-of-truth

**Recommendation: Dolt canonical, JSONL backup/export.**

Evidence:
- `.beads/metadata.json` says `"backend": "dolt"`, `"dolt_mode": "server"`.
- `.beads/export-state.json` records `last_dolt_commit`.
- Beadboard source priority already says `["dolt", "sqlite", "jsonl", "unknown"]`.
- Docs/spec in repo references Dolt > SQLite > JSONL priority.

Divergence cases:
- Dolt server unavailable/stale port ŌåÆ UI falls back to SQLite/JSONL.
- JSONL export lags Dolt commit.
- Specialist worktree edits `.beads/issues.jsonl` directly ŌåÆ not reflected in Dolt unless explicit import/reconcile exists.
- Current scanner skips `.worktrees`, so worktree JSONL edits may be invisible.

**Concrete recommendation:** Treat Dolt as canonical when reachable and healthy. Treat JSONL as read-only fallback plus drift signal. Do not assume JSONL direct edits sync back to Dolt.

---

### 2. Change detection ŌĆö beads side

**Ranking:**

1. **Dolt SQL polling on commit/log state**
   - Best canonical signal.
   - Poll `dolt_log`/current commit hash or equivalent. On change, diff issue rows or refetch changed IDs.
   - Also check uncommitted working set if `bd` can mutate without commit.
   - Risk: schema/table names need live Dolt validation.

2. **fs.watch on `.beads/issues.jsonl`**
   - Cheap fallback.
   - Catches backup export and direct JSONL edits.
   - Lossy on rename/atomic replace; use debounce + stat/hash reread.
   - Watch main repo and known worktree beads paths if scanner supports them.

3. **Hook `bd` CLI to emit events**
   - Great latency.
   - Bad coverage: misses direct Dolt SQL, JSONL edits, old bd versions, specialist tool bypass.
   - Better future optimization, not canonical detector.

4. **Dolt binlog/CDC**
   - Do not ship first.
   - Dolt CDC support unclear from local repo evidence.
   - Operational burden high vs polling.

**Concrete recommendation:** Implement hybrid: Dolt commit polling primary, JSONL watcher fallback/drift detector. Leave bd event hook optional later.

---

### 3. Change detection ŌĆö GitHub side

Current poller uses user events, so collaborator-created issues can be missed.

**Recommendation: add per-repo polling:**
- `/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues?state=all&since=<lastSeenUpdatedAt>&per_page=100`
- `/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls?state=all&sort=updated&direction=desc&per_page=100`

Cadence:
- Default **5 min** for normal tracked repo count.
- Formula: `repos * endpoints * 12 req/hr` at 5 min.
  - 50 repos ŌåÆ 1,200 req/hr.
  - 100 repos ŌåÆ 2,400 req/hr.
  - 200 repos ŌåÆ 4,800 req/hr, too close.
- Use dynamic cadence:
  - Ōēż100 repos: 5 min.
  - 101ŌĆō200 repos: 10 min.
  - >200 repos: 15ŌĆō30 min or active repos only.

ETag:
- Use `If-None-Match`.
- Store ETag per `(repo, endpoint, query)`.
- 304 responses save payload and should reduce primary rate pressure.
- Still enforce local budget because detail calls and retries cost.

**Concrete recommendation:** Budget to 60% of 5000/hr max, jitter polls, back off below 500 remaining.

---

### 4. WS channel architecture

**Recommendation: separate channels, shared envelope.**

Use:
- `github:activity`
- `beads:changes`

Payload envelope:
```ts
{
  kind: "github:pr" | "github:issue" | "beads:issue" | "beads:close",
  projectId?: string,
  repo?: string,
  id: string,
  action: "created" | "updated" | "closed" | "deleted",
  data?: unknown,
  changedAt: string
}
```

Tradeoffs:
- Separate channels reduce unnecessary subscriptions.
- Beadboard need not receive GitHub traffic.
- Gitboard need not receive Beads traffic.
- Multi-project filtering stays payload-level.
- Unified `forge:live` easier for console shell later, but noisy now.

**Concrete recommendation:** Ship separate channels now. Add `forge:live` bridge only when console needs one combined feed.

---

### 5. Granularity & cadence

**Recommendation: coalesce 1ŌĆō2s batches.**

Why:
- `xt end` can close 50 beads.
- Per-row WS spam causes render storms.
- Batches let client dedupe and refetch once.

Batch shape:
```ts
{
  kind: "beads:batch",
  projectId: "...",
  changes: [
    { kind: "beads:close", id: "forge-123", action: "closed" }
  ],
  highWatermark: "doltCommitOrJsonlHash"
}
```

Policy:
- Debounce 1000ms.
- Flush at 100 changes.
- Flush immediately for manual single action if no batch pending.
- Include watermark to avoid stale push vs poll races.

---

### 6. Client mutation strategy

**Recommendation: upsert row, refetch detail only when needed, full refetch only for large/ambiguous batches.**

GitHub:
- `github:pr` / `github:issue` with full row ŌåÆ upsert into `prs`/`issues`.
- Mark repo unread.
- Async refetch repo stats/summary.
- If selected item matches ŌåÆ refetch one detail endpoint.

Beads:
- Add Zustand helpers: `upsertIssue`, `removeIssue`, `upsertClosedIssue`, `applyIssueBatch`.
- If payload has full row ŌåÆ mutate arrays.
- If close event only has ID ŌåÆ move/remove then fetch issue detail.
- If batch > 25 or watermark gap ŌåÆ refetch current project issue list + closed list once.

Avoid:
- Full list refetch per event.
- Blind prepend without dedupe/filter checks.

---

### 7. forge-yhm scope split

**Recommendation: split.**

- `forge-yhm.1` ŌĆö GH repo poll + WS push.
  - Known API.
  - Existing DB/store supports PRs/issues.
  - Existing WS channel exists.
  - Lower risk.

- `forge-yhm.2` ŌĆö Beads change stream.
  - Needs Dolt commit polling validation.
  - Needs JSONL fallback and drift semantics.
  - Needs source-health model.
  - Needs batch handling.

**Concrete recommendation:** Ship GH first, then Beads. Keep shared WS envelope design so second bead plugs in cleanly.

---

### 8. Premortem ŌĆö top 3 failures

#### 1. GitHub rate-limit exhaustion
Cause:
- Too many repos, too-fast polling, detail fetch storms.

Mitigation:
- ETags.
- Per-repo adaptive cadence.
- Global request budget.
- Backoff below 500 remaining.
- Poll active/tracked repos only.

#### 2. Stale Dolt source / wrong source chosen
Cause:
- Scanner marks Dolt ŌĆ£availableŌĆØ from config port, not actual query.
- Dolt server down while JSONL newer.
- Worktree JSONL edits invisible.

Mitigation:
- Health probe with actual SQL.
- Show source + freshness in UI.
- Compare Dolt commit vs `export-state.json`.
- Watch JSONL as drift signal, not canonical overwrite.
- Add ŌĆ£source degradedŌĆØ event.

#### 3. WS storms / stale client state
Cause:
- Bulk close emits 50 events.
- reconnect resubscribe loops.
- Push arrives before DB transaction visible.
- Client mutates filtered arrays wrong.

Mitigation:
- Batch 1ŌĆō2s.
- Watermarks.
- Client dedupe by stable ID.
- Refetch-on-gap.
- Exponential reconnect with jitter.
- Server heartbeat + max connection limits.

## Phase 2 ŌĆö Devil's Advocate

Dolt commit polling can miss changes if bd writes without commit. Need working-set probe or `updated_at` fallback.

JSONL watcher can report backup export after Dolt already changed. If treated as source, stale overwrite risk. Must tag source and compare freshness.

bd hook seems attractive but brittle. Multi-process agents and direct file edits bypass it.

Per-repo GH polling may explode for large repo lists. Need budget gate before implementation, not after.

Separate WS channels create duplicate subscription logic. But existing client already subscribes by channel, so lower migration cost than unified channel.

Incremental client mutation risks filter bugs. Need refetch threshold and selected-detail refetch.

## Phase 3 ŌĆö Synthesis

Best design is source-layered, not source-confused.

- GitHub: poll authoritative REST lists on cadence, store deltas, publish typed WS events.
- Beads: Dolt canonical when live; JSONL fallback and drift detector; batch changes.
- WS: separate operational channels, common event envelope.
- Client: optimistic incremental arrays with refetch-on-gap safety.

This keeps live feel without making WS canonical. DB/source remains truth. WS means ŌĆ£something changed; here is row or ID.ŌĆØ

## Phase 4 ŌĆö Final Refined Output

## Final Answer

1. Dolt canonical. JSONL backup/export. Direct JSONL edits do not sync to Dolt without explicit import.
2. Beads detection rank: Dolt commit polling, JSONL watch fallback, bd hook later, Dolt CDC last.
3. GitHub: add per-repo issues + pulls polling, 5 min default, adaptive by repo count, ETag required.
4. WS: keep separate `github:activity` + `beads:changes`, shared typed envelope.
5. Cadence: 1ŌĆō2s coalesced batches, flush cap, handle `xt end` bulk closes as one batch.
6. Client: upsert rows, refetch selected detail, full refetch only for large/gapped batches.
7. Split forge-yhm into GH first and Beads second.
8. Biggest risks: GH rate-limit, stale Dolt/source drift, WS/client state storms.

Final architecture in 5 lines:
- GH poller watches user events plus repo PR/issue updates with ETags.
- Beads watcher polls Dolt commit state and watches JSONL fallback.
- Server publishes typed batched events on source-specific WS channels.
- Client upserts small deltas and refetches on watermark gaps/big batches.
- Dolt remains canonical; JSONL never overwrites Dolt without explicit reconcile.

## Risks

1. **High:** Dolt/JSONL drift hides true issue state. Mitigate health probes + source freshness UI.
2. **High:** GitHub rate limit burns under many repos. Mitigate ETag + adaptive scheduler.
3. **Medium:** WS reconnect/render storms. Mitigate jitter + batches + dedupe.
4. **Medium:** Incremental store mutations break filtered views. Mitigate threshold refetch.
5. **Low:** bd hook misses clients. Use only optional latency booster.

## Verification

- `.beads/metadata.json`
- `.beads/export-state.json`
- `docs/beadboard-inline-feed-spec.md`

## Beads

- Claim/notes/close not executed: no bash tool exposed.
- No files changed.
- No commit/push needed.

description:

apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/KanbanBoard.tsx + StatusColumn.tsx + BeadCard.tsx render columns with oversized cards and pill-style chips; visual density doesn't match the Feed rows shipped in forge-l85 / forge-wky. SUCCESS

NON_GOALS

- No charts, no progress bars, no sparklines, no lifecycle ribbons.
- Don't rewrite the layout from scratch ŌĆö adapt incrementally.

CONSTRAINTS
- Preserve existing tests where they pertain to PrTimeline structure (or update them in lockstep).
- Markdown/HTML/XML rendering must remain XSS-safe (existing SafeMarkdown pattern).
- Section labels: 11-12px, uppercase tracking ~0.04em, Inter, accent-tinted octicon.

VALIDATION

OUTPUT

title: Octicon consistency pass across gitboard + beadboard
description:
PROBLEM
Mixed icon families across gitboard + beadboard: some files use @primer/octicons-react, others use unicode glyphs or custom SVGs. The xtrm-console contract requires one icon family ŌĆö Octicons ŌĆö everywhere.

REFERENCES
- Already in use: @primer/octicons-react (RepoSidebar, EventIcon, etc.)
- Design source icon set: /tmp/xtrm-design/xtrm-console/project/src/icons.jsx (octicon paths)
- bd remember: xtrm-console-visual-contract

SUCCESS
- Every visible glyph rendered via @primer/octicons-react components.
- No raw unicode glyphs in JSX text content used as icons (e.g. "Ō£ō", "ŌŚÅ", "ŌåÆ", etc. used decoratively).
- No custom inline-SVG icons in components; if a needed glyph is missing from Octicons, document the exception.

SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/*.tsx
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/*.tsx
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx + apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx
- packages/ui/src/components/* if any icon-like components live there

NON_GOALS
- No font changes (forge-m38).
- No CSS token changes (forge-93p is landed).
- No layout changes.

CONSTRAINTS
- Match design's icon usage in meta lines: clock, person, branch, comment, file, diff, tag, type marks (bug/feature/task/epic/chore/decision/PR variants/issue variants).
- Sizing: design uses 12px for inline meta icons, 14px for row primary icons, 16px for headers.
- Preserve existing accessibility (aria-labels on icon buttons).

VALIDATION
- grep "ŌĆó|ŌŚÅ|ŌåÆ|Ō£ō|Ō£Ś|ŌÅĄ|ŌÅĘ|ŌŚ»" in JSX text content of scoped files ŌĆö should return 0 matches for decorative use (any remaining must be text content, not icons).
- grep for raw <svg> in scoped files ŌĆö should only appear via Octicon components.

OUTPUT
- Manifest of icon swaps per file.
- Any unmatchable glyph noted with the chosen Octicon substitute (or kept).

title: Typography pass: less mono in UI text
description:
PROBLEM
JetBrains Mono is overused in UI text (titles, tabs, meta lines, labels). The xtrm-console contract restricts mono to IDs, SHAs, branch refs, diff numbers, and file paths ŌĆö everything else uses Inter.

REFERENCES

SUCCESS
- Mono font-family used only on these surfaces: bead IDs (forge-xxx), PR numbers (#412), SHA hashes, branch refs (origin/main), diff numbers (+142/-28), file paths, code blocks, command hints.
- Everything else (titles, tabs, meta lines, dossier prose, kv keys, segment labels, state tokens, day headers) uses Inter.
- Hierarchy still readable ŌĆö restore Inter where mono was masking intended emphasis.

SCOPE

- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css
- All *.tsx in apps/{gitboard,beadboard}/src/dashboard/components/* that set font-family inline

NON_GOALS
- No icon changes (forge-hl1).
- No layout reflow.
- No new tokens unless typography demands them.

CONSTRAINTS
- Don't add new font imports beyond Inter and JetBrains Mono.
- Preserve existing accessibility/contrast.
- Avoid mass replace ŌĆö selectively change font-family in identified mono-leaking surfaces.

VALIDATION
- grep "JetBrains Mono\|monospace\|font-family.*mono" across scoped files ŌĆö every remaining hit must be on one of the allowed surfaces (ID/SHA/branch/diff/path/code).

OUTPUT

- [ ] bun run --cwd apps/beadboard build:dashboard passes.
- [ ] /gitboard returns 200 and renders the Git view.
- [ ] /beadboard returns 200 and renders the Beads view.
- [ ] Visual contract checklist is reviewed.
- [ ] Follow-up issues are created for remaining drift.

## Approach notes

This task is validation only unless tiny style regressions are found. No architecture work.

Until a true shared shell exists, /gitboard and /beadboard remain separate routes. Their topbars and sidebars should still feel like the same product without embedding one app inside another.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] /gitboard and /beadboard topbars use the same height, typography, border, background, and tab style.
- [ ] Sidebars use the same width, square rows, and border treatment.
- [ ] No duplicate shell chrome is introduced.
- [ ] Both dashboards build and routes return 200.

## Code Sanity Verdict
- Verdict: FINDINGS
- Scope reviewed: `HEAD 591c7a7` in `apps/beadboard/src/types/beads.ts`, `apps/beadboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts`, `apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-reader.ts`, `apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts`, `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts`, `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/lib/api.ts`
- Confidence: medium

## Findings
- Location: `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts:108,124`
  - Issue: `new BeadsReader({} as any)` fake-constructs DB reader for pure JSONL routes.
  - Why it matters: `as any` hides contract break. Future constructor change or accidental DB access blows up at runtime, while type system gives false safety.
  - Suggested executor instruction: Split JSONL parsing helpers out of `BeadsReader`, or make `db` optional and stop passing fake object.

- Location: `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts:80-83,191-217` plus `apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts:57-67,113-132` and `apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-reader.ts:19-29`
  - Issue: issue-detail path returns source record with `project_id: ""`.
  - Why it matters: list route stamps `project_id`, detail route does not. API shape becomes inconsistent; downstream client can see empty project id even when route path already knows it.
  - Suggested executor instruction: Normalize detail payload before return: set `project_id` from route param in both Dolt/sqlite paths.

- Location: `apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-reader.ts:37-56,59-80`
  - Issue: JSONL parsers do `JSON.parse` per line with no per-line guard and then cast raw data straight to `Memory`/`Interaction`.
  - Why it matters: one malformed line drops whole file to empty array via outer catch, so bad record erases all good records. Also schema drift stays silent.
  - Suggested executor instruction: Parse line-by-line with try/catch, skip bad rows, and validate minimum fields before cast.

## Non-findings
- Did not flag fallback order; current `dolt -> sqlite -> jsonl -> unknown` chain looks sane from reviewed code.
- Did not validate build or clean worktree; `git status` in this session already showed unrelated dirty bead/serena files, and no build run here.

## Handoff
Fix 3 runtime/type holes above, then rerun reviewer. No broad refactor needed.

## Machine-readable block

title: github WS parity: publish github:rate_limit when poller detects throttle changes
description:
## PROBLEM
\`github:rate_limit\` event type and \`GithubRateLimit\` payload (\`{ limit, remaining, reset_at }\`) are declared in \`apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts\` but never published. The poller already tracks rate-limit state internally ŌĆö observed in \`apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts\` lines ~239-252 with \`emit(makeLogEntry(\"poller\", \"rate_limit.changed\", ...))\` ŌĆö but those changes go only to the log file, not to the WS bus. Dashboard cannot show a "GitHub API throttled" banner without push notification.

## SCOPE
- Wherever rate-limit state changes in github-poller.ts (pausedUntil reset, remaining threshold cross), call \`this.publishGithubEvent(\"github:rate_limit\", { limit, remaining, reset_at }, ...)\`.
- Add a small lightweight throttle so we don't spam the WS bus with one-per-request updates ŌĆö publish only on threshold cross or paused-state transition.

## OUTPUT
- Dashboard can subscribe and surface "GitHub rate-limit at X%" indicator in real time.

title: [future] Reduce specialists refresh tail latency under cold-start contention
description:
## CONTEXT (deferred for future exploration)
After forge-9qmk / forge-ijzx / forge-cspw landed, head-of-line blocking is largely fixed (cold parallel <50ms, graph stays <300ms in all observed waves). But there is a residual tail-latency spike on the SPECIFIC specialists endpoint on the second wave after cold start (measured 1597ms in dev 2026-05-21).

Root cause: even with chunked dao queries (yield every 5 repos), the HTTP response for a stale-returning specialists request can be queued behind the in-flight chunked refresh work. Adding request-level dedup eliminates DUPLICATE refresh work but doesn't necessarily eliminate the spike, because the FIRST refresh's chunks still run on the event loop.

## OPTIONS TO EXPLORE
1. Tighter chunking: yield after every 1-2 repos instead of every 5. May be enough.
2. Request-level dedup: single inFlightRefreshPromise so concurrent cache-misses share one refresh. Useful even if not sufficient alone.
3. Worker thread for observability dao queries: zero contention on main event loop. Biggest lift but biggest win.
4. Server-sent freshness: WS-push 'specialists:refreshed' so the dashboard doesn't poll a stale endpoint.

## DECISION
Leaving as-is for now. Graph (the user-visible pain) is fixed. Specialists spike happens once per cold start then settles. Revisit if the spike starts mattering for real workflows.

## REFERENCES
- forge-9qmk (listRepos cache)
- forge-ijzx (attach-pool yield)
- forge-cspw (dao chunking)
- bd memory: when-writing-bead-ac-for-performance-ha-work

To tune into a running specialist, an operator has to manually open the drawer, switch to Specialists tab, find the job in the list. Quick-access from the chip is a 3-click ŌåÆ 1-click win.

## SUCCESS
- Clicking a running/error/cancelled specialist chip on any bead row in IssueFeed dispatches into useShellStore: drawer.open() + drawer.setTab('specialists') + drawer.setSpecialistsSelectedJobId(jobId).
- Drawer animates open (or stays open if already open), switches to Specialists tab, and the row for that job is selected ŌĆö which (once .2 lands) auto-transitions to feed view streaming live output.
- No new component chrome ŌĆö pure wiring through existing primitives. Chip styling unchanged.
- Cursor changes to pointer on running chips; non-running chips (done excluded per memory) stay as-is.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/SpecialistOwnerBadge.tsx ŌĆö onClick handler dispatching shell-store actions
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/shell.ts ŌĆö confirm action signatures match (drawer.open, setTab, setSpecialistsSelectedJobId from .1)
- CSS: add cursor: pointer + subtle hover state on .specialist-owner-badge (only when interactive)

## NON_GOALS
- Redesigning the chip (separate scope; visual contract is locked per memory specialist-chip-visibility-rule)
- Adding interactivity to 'done' chips (deliberately hidden per memory)
- Right-side-drawer or popover variants (drawer is the chosen home per epic)
- Implementing the feed view itself (that's .2)

## CONSTRAINTS
- Click target = whole chip, but must NOT swallow row click if the chip is inside a clickable bead row ŌĆö call stopPropagation on the chip onClick.
- Must work for chips on closed beads too (showing past error/cancelled states per visibility rule) ŌĆö operator may want to inspect a past failure.
- If jobId is null/undefined (data inconsistency), no-op silently.
- Drawer open behavior must respect existing keyboard shortcut state (don't conflict with Ctrl/Cmd+J).

## VALIDATION
- [ ] Click a running chip on a feed row ŌåÆ drawer opens, Specialists tab active, that job's row selected.
- [ ] If .2 has landed: feed view auto-starts streaming for that job.
- [ ] Clicking the chip does NOT open the bead row expansion or BeadSideDrawer (no leak).
- [ ] Done chips remain non-interactive (per memory rule they're not even rendered, but verify no regression).
- [ ] Keyboard shortcut Ctrl/Cmd+J still toggles drawer open/closed normally.

## OUTPUT
Changed files, gif of chip ŌåÆ drawer ŌåÆ selection flow, confirmation that done-chip visibility rule is preserved.

title: Test coverage: Console epic integration + unit tests
description:
## PROBLEM
The Console epic adds 4 new surfaces (Graph backend, Graph frontend, BeadSideDrawer, Specialists tab) and restructures the IA. Without dedicated integration + unit tests, regressions land silently. Each child landed its own focused validation but cross-surface integration (e.g. Graph node click ŌåÆ BeadSideDrawer open ŌåÆ close ŌåÆ state preserved) needs explicit coverage. This task is the safety net after all 5 implementation children close.

## SUCCESS
- Integration test suite for Console IA: surface switch persistence, tab switch persistence, /gitboard/beads/* legacy URL mapping, localStorage migration of legacy beads-surface selection.
- Integration test suite for Graph tab: layout determinism (same input ŌåÆ same positions), edge type rendering count, pulse on running specialist, hover-highlight DOM mutation, click-emit beadId.
- Integration test suite for BeadSideDrawer: open/close lifecycle, ESC handler, backdrop click, mobile full-screen breakpoint, Open-in-Feed switches tab + scrolls.
- Integration test suite for Specialists tab: chain grouping correctness, filter chip toggles, detail pane fetch on selection, polling pause on visibility change.
- Unit tests for the pure layout function (apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/pages/console/graph/layout.ts) ŌĆö see forge-f6qk.3 for the contract.
- Unit tests for chain aggregation logic (the function in useChains.ts that derives chains from /jobs/in-flight + /jobs/recent).
- All new tests pass via bun --bun vitest run apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/.
- bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit stays clean.
- Total new test file count: ~6-8 files. Total new test count: 25-40.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/ia-restructure.test.tsx (NEW) ŌĆö surface/tab unions, URL mapping, localStorage migration
- apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/graph/layout.test.ts (already in forge-f6qk.3 ŌĆö extend if needed)
- apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/graph/Graph.test.tsx (NEW) ŌĆö fetch + render + hover + click integration
- apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/BeadSideDrawer.test.tsx (NEW) ŌĆö open/close, ESC, backdrop, mobile
- apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/Specialists.test.tsx (NEW) ŌĆö card list, filter, detail pane, polling
- apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/hooks/useChains.test.ts (NEW) ŌĆö chain aggregation pure logic
- apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/graph.test.ts (already in forge-f6qk.2 ŌĆö extend if needed)
- Fixtures: apps/gitboard/tests/fixtures/console-graph.json ŌĆö sample 20-bead 30-edge fixture for layout + render tests

## NON_GOALS
- No e2e Playwright tests in this task ŌĆö those are operator-side smoke at session close.
- No performance benchmark tests (the 200ms graph render target is checked manually).
- No mutation tests / property-based tests.
- No backend graph endpoint tests beyond what forge-f6qk.2 already includes.
- No visual regression / pixel-diff tests.

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: do NOT call gitnexus_* tools.
- Tests must run under `bun --bun vitest run` (the bun:sqlite gating rule from forge-che).
- For tests that need observability.db data, use the existing fixtures pattern from apps/gitboard/tests/__tests__/fixtures/observability/.
- No new test runners. Vitest only.
- Mock API endpoints with msw or simple fetch stubs ŌĆö pick the lightest path consistent with existing tests.
- React component tests use happy-dom (already configured).
- Each test file < 200 lines. If larger, split.

## VALIDATION
- [ ] bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit clean.
- [ ] bun --bun vitest run apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/pages/console/ passes (all new tests green).
- [ ] bun --bun vitest run apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/hooks/useChains.test.ts passes.

## OUTPUT

- Vitest run tail showing N tests passed.
- Coverage report excerpt for the new surfaces.
- Confirmation no existing tests regressed.

## APPROACH NOTES
- Reuse fixture format from apps/gitboard/tests/__tests__/dao.test.ts for sqlite-backed tests.
- The chain aggregation logic in useChains.ts should be exported as a pure function so it's testable without React mounting.
- For Graph SVG tests, the layout function returns positioned nodes ŌĆö assert positions (deterministic), not pixel renders.
- For pulse/animation tests, assert the CSS class is applied (or the React state flag is set), not the rendered animation frame.
- Use vitest's vi.useFakeTimers() for the 5s polling assertions in Specialists.test.tsx.

title: Plan future SSH/Tailscale provider
description:
PROBLEM: SSH/Tailscale remote terminals are useful for VPS workflows but have larger auth, allowlisting, and safety risks than the local PTY MVP.
SUCCESS: Future SSH/Tailscale provider is scoped as a design plan with explicit exclusions, risks, and safety requirements, not implemented in this epic.
SCOPE: Design notes for host profiles, auth/storage model, allowlisting, production gating, Windows client implications, and how SSH provider would fit TerminalProviderKind.
NON_GOALS: No SSH implementation; no credential storage implementation; no UI for managing profiles; no production enablement.
CONSTRAINTS: Treat remote shell as high-risk. Require explicit allowlists and operator opt-in. Keep compatibility with TerminalStream/protocol abstractions.
VALIDATION: Documented plan names risks, non-goals, and follow-up issue shape sufficient for later implementation planning.
OUTPUT: SSH/Tailscale provider plan and follow-up beads only if needed.
acceptance_criteria: Document host profile model; document auth/storage risks; document allowlisting requirements; document production safety gates; document Windows client implications; explain protocol/provider integration; explicitly exclude SSH implementation from MVP.

title: Add readonly specialist feed provider
description:
PROBLEM: Future Specialists UI should reuse TerminalStream for live job following rather than building another log renderer. Specialist feed streams are readonly and should not require shell permissions. This is a follow-up after the local PTY MVP proves the renderer/protocol shape.
SUCCESS: A readonly specialist-feed provider can stream output equivalent to sp feed <job_id> into TerminalStream with ANSI support and clear completion/error states.
SCOPE: Provider adapter for specialist job feed output; protocol capability wiring for readonly streams; minimal UI/test hook proving TerminalStream can consume it.
NON_GOALS: No full Specialists tab redesign; no interactive resume/steer input box; no shell/PTY access; no tmux/SSH; not required to ship the local PTY MVP.
CONSTRAINTS: Provider must be readonly. Keyboard input is ignored or blocked. It must not inherit shell-capable safety enablement requirements, but still must validate job IDs and permissions/scope as appropriate.
VALIDATION: Tests or smoke prove job_id validation, output streaming, readonly input blocking, ANSI rendering path, completion and error states.
OUTPUT: Provider implementation or prototype integration, test evidence, follow-up notes for Specialists tab embedding.
acceptance_criteria: Provider accepts job_id; streams specialist feed output into TerminalStream; runs in readonly mode; keyboard input is ignored or blocked; ANSI output renders correctly; completion/error states are displayed; invalid job IDs are rejected safely; can later be embedded inside Specialists tab job detail view.
close_reason: Done: readonly specialist-feed provider streams specialists feed <job> --follow into TerminalStream protocol, validates job IDs, blocks input/resize, and has protocol/provider/bridge tests.

title: Evaluate optional tmux provider
description:
PROBLEM: tmux may improve persistence/detach behavior for VPS/Tailscale workflows, but making it an MVP dependency could add platform and operational complexity.
SUCCESS: A bounded evaluation recommends whether tmux should remain a future provider, become a config-enabled optional mode, or be rejected for now.
SCOPE: Design notes/prototype only for tmux provider semantics, persistence, detach/attach, cleanup, server restart/browser reload behavior, and VPS/Tailscale usefulness.
NON_GOALS: No production tmux implementation unless explicitly spun into follow-up issues; no SSH provider; no TerminalStream rewrite.
CONSTRAINTS: tmux is optional and must not block local PTY MVP. If recommended, define config gates and cleanup model. Consider Windows client to Linux VPS/Tailscale usage from the operator perspective.
VALIDATION: Document raw PTY vs tmux tradeoff matrix and recommendation with risks and required follow-up issues if accepted.
OUTPUT: Decision note in bead or docs plus any filed implementation follow-ups.
acceptance_criteria: Document raw PTY vs tmux tradeoffs; cover detach/attach behavior; cover browser reload/server restart behavior; cover orphan cleanup behavior; cover VPS/Tailscale usefulness; recommend keep as future provider, add as config-enabled mode, or reject for now; file implementation child issues if accepted.

title: Deploy gitboard to VPS with Tailscale-only access
description:
## PROBLEM
forge-8kdc shipped the deploy scaffold (Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, docs/deployment.md) but nothing is actually running anywhere yet. Operator wants gitboard reachable from any device on the Tailscale tailnet, not just localhost during dev. Currently every session relies on running `bun run dev` locally ŌĆö no persistent service, no remote access, no auto-restart on reboot.

## SUCCESS
- A Tailscale-enrolled VPS runs gitboard via docker compose, restarts automatically on reboot.
- Dashboard reachable from any tailnet device at `http://<vps-tailnet-name>:3000/gitboard`.
- Public internet cannot reach :3000 (ufw / firewall enforces).
- GitHub poller works against operator's repos via the deployed token.
- Per-repo `.specialists/db/observability.db` files mounted via `~/projects` bind so Console / drawer surfaces still populate.
- Beadboard backend reachable on same port (no separate service).

## DECISIONS TO MAKE BEFORE DISPATCH
1. **Host choice**: existing VPS (which?) or new? Tailscale already installed there?
2. **Dolt strategy**: docs assume host Dolt at `127.0.0.1:13839` (gkpr's design). Is the VPS already running a shared Dolt server, or does this VPS need a fresh local Dolt instance? If fresh, do we ship beads state there or treat as ephemeral?
3. **Project bind mount**: `~/projects` on VPS doesn't exist by default ŌĆö we need to either clone repos to the VPS or accept that VPS-side observability dbs only build up when specialists run against the VPS. Most likely: VPS is a passive observer + dashboard, while specialist runs continue on dev laptop. In that case the observability surface won't show local laptop runs unless we sync the dbs.
4. **Tailscale model**: install Tailscale on VPS host (docs default) OR run a Tailscale sidecar container next to gitboard? Sidecar simplifies host setup but adds complexity. Host-level is cleaner for a single-service VPS.
5. **CI image build**: build locally + push to ghcr.io, or `docker compose build` on the VPS every deploy? Push-to-registry adds CI cost but speeds redeploys.
6. **Secrets**: `.env` with GITHUB_TOKEN ŌĆö manual scp, or use tailscale's `tsnet` + serve only on tailnet, or sops-encrypted .env in git?

## SCOPE (when eventually dispatched)
- Verify Tailscale is on the chosen VPS (`tailscale status`)
- Verify Docker + Compose plugin (`docker compose version`)
- Decide Dolt question ŌĆö if VPS-local: provision `~/.beads/shared-server/` + start Dolt; if remote: configure DOLT_HOST env in compose
- scp `.env` with GITHUB_TOKEN (mode 600, owner root)
- `docker compose build && docker compose up -d`
- `sudo ufw deny 3000/tcp && sudo ufw allow in on tailscale0 to any port 3000 proto tcp`
- Verify from a tailnet device: open `http://<vps-tailnet-name>:3000/gitboard` and confirm Console + Beads load
- Add a systemd or compose-restart sanity (reboot the VPS, confirm container comes back)
- Document the actual VPS hostname + tailnet name in a private deploy notes file (NOT this bead ŌĆö secrets)

## NON_GOALS
- Multi-VPS clustering
- Reverse proxy (Caddy/Traefik) ŌĆö Tailscale is the access layer for v1
- TLS termination ŌĆö Tailscale handles transport
- Public-internet exposure under any circumstance
- Real-time observability sync between laptop and VPS ŌĆö accept that VPS-side observability.db files only reflect VPS-side specialist runs (separate problem)

## OPEN QUESTIONS (block dispatch until answered)
- Which VPS? (hostname / provider)
- Where will the `.beads` SSOT live ŌĆö laptop, VPS, or both via Dolt replication?
- Should the VPS itself ever dispatch specialists, or is it dashboard-only?
- Acceptable downtime window for the initial deploy?

## REFERENCES
- forge-8kdc (closed) ŌĆö scaffold (Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, docs/deployment.md). Already on main.
- docs/deployment.md ŌĆö current docs, may need updates after first real deploy
- memory `forge-8kdc-docker-multi-stage-bun-image-host` ŌĆö multi-stage Bun, host networking, named gitboard-state volume

## OUTPUT (when eventually dispatched)
- VPS hostname + tailnet name in private notes (NOT in this bead)
- Confirmation that `http://<vps-tailnet-name>:3000/gitboard` loads from a tailnet device
- Confirmation that port 3000 is blocked on public iface, allowed on tailscale0
- Restart test: `sudo reboot` ŌåÆ container comes back without manual intervention
- Updated docs/deployment.md with any deviations encountered
close_reason: Deployed gitboard to VPS as native systemd user service (~/.config/systemd/user/gitboard.service, linger enabled) bound to tailscale IP 100.113.49.52:3030 ŌĆö tailnet-only access from laptop/desktop, no public exposure. Tried docker first per the original plan but it caused multiple real problems (PTY spawned container shells not host shells, new bridge had no NAT, host.docker.internal didn't resolve with host networking) ŌĆö native run fixed all four user complaints (graph load, terminal, slow pages, missing repos). Code fixes shipped: beadboard scanner shared-server fallback + depth-0 fall-through for nested layouts; maxDepth 1->3 in beads route; DOLT_HOST env override in beads route + graph-dao. Follow-up forge-nrwc filed for documentation updates.

title: P3: Deprecate apps/beadboard frontend
description:
## Context
After parity is verified on the unified shell, beadboard's standalone frontend must go to prevent drift. Backend on port 3010 stays.

## What to build
- Verify all beadboard functionality reachable via unified gitboard /beads view
- Pick A or B:
  - (A) Replace apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/* with a single index.html redirecting to gitboard /r/<defaultRepo>/beads
  - (B) Delete apps/beadboard frontend entirely; keep apps/beadboard/src/api + src/core (backend) if a separate api server exists, else move backend into gitboard
- Update workspace package.json + bun.lock
- Update beadboard CLAUDE.md to point at gitboard

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] No frontend code remains in apps/beadboard (or it redirects)
- [ ] bun install clean after removal
- [ ] CI doesn't reference deleted frontend
- [ ] Docs updated

## Approach notes
- Backend on :3010 must keep running ŌĆö unified gitboard depends on it
- Check apps/beadboard package.json scripts and remove dashboard build entries

title: Console: stats from observability.db
description:
PROBLEM
xt specialists run produce a local observability.db (SQLite) with timings, token spend, payload sizes, status, model. No UI surface; users have to run sp ps or query SQL directly.

REFERENCES
- Local DB likely lives at .specialists/observability.db or .xtrm/observability.db (VERIFY before implementing ŌĆö first step of the SCOPE)
- KPI skill: bd memories using-kpi (background: KPI analysis on observability)
- Existing stat-tile primitive: TriageStatCard / TriageStatGrid in apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx
- bun:sqlite for read-only access
- apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts mounts /api/console (per forge-z6j)

SUCCESS
- New read-only API: /api/console/observability/jobs (recent N jobs), /api/console/observability/roles (per-role: count, avg duration, avg tokens, p50/p95), /api/console/observability/spend (per-day token + cost rollup).
- Observability module in /console renders: spend last 7/30 days ┬Ę per-role table ┬Ę recent jobs list (id, role, bead, status, duration, tokens) ┬Ę waiting jobs (status='waiting').
- All read-only; no kill/resume actions in v1.

SCOPE
- VERIFY (step 1): exact path of observability.db on disk; confirm tables/columns. Likely tables: jobs(id, role, bead, status, started_at, ended_at, model), turns(job_id, tokens_in, tokens_out, cost_usd). VERIFY-then-implement.
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/observability.ts (new)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/observability-store.ts (new ŌĆö bun:sqlite reader with prepared statements)
- apps/console/src/.../Observability.tsx (new ŌĆö or beadboard tab if /console not yet)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/client.ts (getObservabilityJobs / getRoles / getSpend)
- apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/observability.test.ts (new)

NON_GOALS
- No write actions (no killing jobs, no editing rows).
- No re-running of past jobs.
- No reading other plugins' DBs.

CONSTRAINTS
- Read-only mode (open DB with readonly: true).
- DB path resolved via env or config ŌĆö do not hardcode.
- If DB does not exist: API returns empty arrays / zeros, not 500.
- Numbers shown in human-readable form (1.2k tokens, $0.034) but raw values available in response JSON.

VALIDATION
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test green; new endpoint test handles missing DB gracefully.
- /api/console/observability/jobs returns Ōēź0 items.
- Manual: KPIs render on Observability tab; numbers match a direct sqlite3 query.

OUTPUT
- Confirmed DB path + schema (the actual verification finding), API contract, files changed, screenshot of Observability tab.
notes:
Added console route, observability summary API, client hook/page, and initial tests. tsc baseline still noisy from repo-wide preexisting JSX/type-dep issues; need focused verification once deps/tooling resolved.

title: Console: Specialists tab ŌĆö live job list + chain history
description:
PROBLEM
sp ps shows the live state of specialist jobs in terminal ŌĆö running/waiting/done, role, bead, worktree, durations. There's no UI surface; users have to run sp ps and parse output manually.

REFERENCES
- sp ps shape (already observed): jobs grouped by worktree; each job has id (6-char hex), role, status (running|waiting|done|cancelled), bead, elapsed, turns, tokens, payload size, last action text
- Per-turn output already auto-appended to bead notes (CLAUDE.md memory: per-turn output auto-appends to bead notes for ALL specialists)
- sp result <id>, sp feed <id>, sp ps <id> for single-job detail
- Existing stat / row primitives in /console

SUCCESS
- New /api/console/specialists/jobs returns flat array of jobs with: { id, role, status, bead, elapsed_ms, turns, tokens_total, last_message, worktree, payload_kb }.
- Specialists module in /console renders: chains tree grouped by root bead ŌåÆ exec/code-sanity/security-auditor/reviewer; rows show role ┬Ę status badge ┬Ę bead link ┬Ę last action excerpt ┬Ę elapsed; click row ŌåÆ expands to show full bead-notes turn log (which already contains the handoff messages).
- Filter chips: All ┬Ę Running ┬Ę Waiting ┬Ę Done.
- Refreshes every 5s while module is visible (clearable interval; not polling when hidden).

SCOPE

- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/specialists.ts (new GET /api/console/specialists/jobs)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/specialists-runtime.ts (new ŌĆö invoke sp ps --json with execFile, 2s timeout)

- apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/specialists.test.ts (new ŌĆö mock sp output)

- No editing of bead notes from this view.

CONSTRAINTS

- Timeout the subprocess (2s). If it overruns, return last cached snapshot.

VALIDATION

title: Beadboard: replace remaining bv parity gaps with native scoring
description:
PROBLEM
The Triage tab currently scores issues with a small client-side heuristic: priority weight (P0=1 ... P4=0) + staleness (daysIdle/30 capped at 1) + open-load (openCount/100 capped at 1). bv --robot-triage uses a richer ranker: pagerank centrality, betweenness, blocker_ratio, time_to_impact, urgency aging curve, risk signals (fan_variance, activity_churn, cross_repo_risk, status_risk, composite). The dashboard does NOT shell out to bv at runtime, so the heuristic gap is intentional today, but rank quality on large boards (Ōēź500 issues) is noticeably weaker than bv.

REFERENCES
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx ŌĆö getTriageSignals, buildTriageView
- bv --robot-triage JSON shape (already inspected): score breakdown includes pagerank/betweenness/blocker_ratio/staleness/priority_boost/time_to_impact/urgency/risk
- bd dependency edges available client-side from issue.dependencies

SUCCESS
A DECISION bead. Two acceptable outcomes:

(a) PORT: implement pagerank + betweenness over the loaded dependency graph (Map<id, BeadIssue> ŌåÆ adjacency), feed into the score blend at weights {pagerank: 0.25, betweenness: 0.10, blocker_ratio: 0.10, priority: 0.20, staleness: 0.15, urgency: 0.15, risk: 0.05}. Add risk_signals.status_risk computed from status (blocked=0.6, in_progress=0.3, open=0.1, closed=0). No external deps; pure JS graph algorithms (~150 lines).
OR
(b) ACCEPT: keep current heuristic, document the parity gap as intentional in the bead description, and add a 'View in bv' hint in the Triage panel (link to copy-pasteable 'bv --robot-triage' command).

Either way, the dashboard never executes 'bv' as a subprocess; bv stays a design reference + CLI for power users.

SCOPE (if (a) is chosen)
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx ŌĆö replace getTriageSignals body
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/lib/graph.ts (new ŌĆö pagerank + betweenness)
- apps/beadboard/tests/dashboard/lib/graph.test.ts (new ŌĆö unit tests for both algorithms on small graphs)

NON_GOALS
- Do not import bv as a library.
- Do not shell to bv.
- Do not change Velocity / Project Health tiles.

CONSTRAINTS
- Algorithms must be O(V┬ĘE) or better; iteration cap 25 for pagerank with damping 0.85; reject any algorithm needing external libraries.
- Score normalization must keep the existing top-5 cap.

VALIDATION (if (a))
- Unit tests for pagerank on hand-drawn graphs (triangle, star, chain).
- Unit tests for betweenness on a 5-node graph with a known bridge.
- Triage top-3 on the local board includes the previously-bv-flagged issues (regression sample = top 3 from bv --robot-triage at time of land).

OUTPUT
- Decision: (a) PORT or (b) ACCEPT, with rationale.
- If (a): files changed, tests added, top-3 comparison.
- If (b): bead description updated with parity gap rationale.
acceptance_criteria:
Decision recorded as either:
(a) PORT ŌĆö pagerank + betweenness implemented in apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/lib/graph.ts; getTriageSignals uses new weight blend; unit tests pass for pagerank (triangle/star/chain) and betweenness (5-node with known bridge); Triage top-3 overlaps bv --robot-triage top-5 on the regression sample
(b) ACCEPT ŌĆö bead description updated with parity-gap rationale; Triage panel surfaces 'Run bv --robot-triage' copy-pasteable hint; no code changes
Either way: dashboard never executes 'bv' as a subprocess
