value: Gitboard Beads Feed UI rules: active issues render blocker tree with single primary blocker placement to avoid duplicate rows; epic relation propagates purple markers through nested descendants; right column format is 'P<n> TYPE status date / deps / PR / agent' with only priority/type colored by type (bug red, epic purple, feature royal blue).
Terminal background preference: use #141414/app surface tone rather than pure black for visual consistency with Gitboard.

forge-5w9 reviewer waiver (forge-lec): getOpenPrs intentionally NOT ported into beadsApi. Rationale: it's a /api/github/prs endpoint, not /api/beads. gitboard's own apiClient.getPrs covers github PR fetches. prByIssueId is OPTIONAL in BeadCard/StatusColumn/KanbanBoard component props ŌĆö never wired = no runtime regression. Future PR-link feature should reshape apiClient.getPrs() result into OpenPr at the wiring layer, not duplicate the fetcher in beads-api. Reviewer score 89/PARTIAL ŌĆö accepted with this waiver.

forge-f6qk.6 console epic test coverage: 6 new test files (ia-restructure, Graph, BeadSideDrawer, Specialists, useChains, fixtures/console-graph.json) + 13 tests. Pattern: tests fork off origin/main but need post-integration state ŌĆö workflow is push xt/integration branch, dispatch with --force-stale-base, executor first git fetch origin <branch> && git reset --hard origin/<branch>. Reviewer pitfall: diff source defaults to main; for branches reset to non-main, rebut with explicit base (git diff --name-only origin/<branch>..feature/<x>).

Graph chip Feed-parity restyle (forge-1x6c): chips use 2-line grid layout matching IssueFeed rows ŌĆö top: id / title (mono id, low-alpha '/' sep, italic title); bottom: Pn type state agent (Pn+type coloured by TYPE_CONFIG palette duplicated from IssueFeed.tsx: bug #ff4d5e, feature #4169e1, task --text-muted, epic rgba(163,113,247,0.95)). NODE_W=260, NODE_H=44 (was 220/26). Drop priority-rail border-left; status text replaces glyph. BeadNode.tsx + Graph.tsx NodeChip + Graph.tsx OrphanRow all use identical g-node-identity / g-node-class structure. Palette duplicated in graph instead of shared ŌĆö flag in code so drift is visible if Feed palette changes.

Beads source health is Dolt-first: JSONL must be shown/logged as degraded backup only. /connection should classify missing config, dead pid, unreachable server, and query failures distinctly while still allowing fallback reads.
Audit forge-fje bounded row-normalization failed: build:dashboard missing vite in current scope and required reviewed_job_id not injected

gitboard specialist live status fix: useSpecialistOwnership must poll mounted bead cards, and specialists router default DAO must refresh observability DB discovery because worktree orchestrators can create/write .specialists/db/observability.db after server startup.

sp's observability.db at <repo>/.specialists/db/observability.db is the right join source for beadŌåöspecialist UI in gitboard (not 'sp ps' shell-out). specialist_jobs has bead_id+specialist+status+chain_id+epic_id+chain_kind+worktree_column+last_output, indexed by bead_id and chain_id. Open mode=ro, pin schema_version, mutations stay out of v1.

forge-5w9.6 MainPane swap: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/MainPane.tsx reads useShellStore selection. key={repo:section} on <main> forces clean remount on swap. EmptyState shows 3 most recently active repos as clickable cards. /gitboard default route uses ShellApp (Sidebar + MainPane). /gitboard/legacy preserves old TabBar shell. Per-(repo,section) state preservation deferred ŌĆö currently lost on swap via key-remount.

Gitboard PR/issue expanded view style follows GitHub per-entry pattern, NOT a single outer wrapping card. Each .pr-conversation-entry (commit, comment, review, body) renders as its own discrete card with --card-border, --card-radius-sm or -md, --surface-secondary bg. .pr-expanded-body itself has NO border/bg ŌĆö it's just a flat container. Body/comment/review entries get larger padding + --card-radius-md; commit/event entries stay smaller with --card-radius-sm. This iteration corrected an earlier mistake where the whole expanded view was wrapped in one outer card frame.

Design handoffs from Claude Design (Anthropic) use --surface-1/2/3 and --danger token names, but this repo's packages/ui/src/styles/tokens.css uses --surface-primary/secondary/tertiary and --accent-red. When porting design selectors that reference --surface-1/2/3 or --danger, either rename the selector to use the repo's tokens OR add aliases at the top of tokens.css. Failure to do this silently breaks any element using those vars (background collapses to transparent). Source: forge-pq6 structural alignment broke beadboard until forge-qct alias commit.
forge-7ap: canonical tokens stay in packages/ui; gitboard active chrome and beadboard chrome should reference vars only, with preview sandbox styles treated separately

When a bead has a strict acceptance criterion (e.g. 'a PR that does X without Y is reviewer FAIL'), put it in the bead's NOTES section as an explicit FAIL gate, not just as a constraint. The reviewer reads NOTES and uses the gate phrasing to hold the line against under-scoped fixes ŌĆö even when the executor self-reports 'partial' and tries to close. This is what saved forge-5i43 from landing as TTL-caching-only without async Dolt ATTACH: the NOTES contained 'A PR that only adds caching without addressing the Dolt sync-init blocking is reviewer FAIL', and the reviewer cited that exact line. Pattern: put the negative gate (the disqualifier) before the positive AC list, so it anchors the verdict.

forge-0w4 backend gotchas: (1) /api/beads/projects/{id}/issues/closed returns 404 on Dolt-backed projects ŌĆö fetch ALL via /issues?limit=1000 and split client-side. (2) /api/github/events default limit is 50 (most-recent across ALL repos), /api/github/prs is 100 ŌĆö client-side filter-by-repo on the global store returns 0 for most repos. Solution: per-repo apiClient.getEvents({repos:[fullName]}) + getPrs({repo}) + getIssues({repo}) + getReleases({repo}) in a local hook (useGithubRepoData in MainPane.tsx).

Markdown table parsing: pipe-row + separator row (|---|---|) is the trigger. Reject early if next line isn't dashes-with-pipes pattern. parseTableCells strips leading/trailing | then splits on | and trims. Fenced code blocks: detect (```|~~~) + optional lang, consume lines until matching fence.

gitboard UI architecture: packages/ui/components is empty placeholder; all gitboard styling is app-local via inline styles + globals.css. Radius tokens all 0px by design. Three structural gaps in current shell: (1) type scale 11-18px is dense, (2) body{overflow:hidden}+fixed sidebar/detail widths cause horizontal zoom overflow, (3) no enclosure system on cards. Three executor beads touch tokens.css so must sequence AŌåÆBŌåÆC.

forge-8kdc docker: multi-stage Bun image, host network mode (Dolt at 127.0.0.1:13839), named docker volume gitboard-state mounted at /data, ~/projects bind-mounted read-only, GITHUB_TOKEN via .env file, Tailscale-only access pattern documented in docs/deployment.md including ufw lockdown commands.

Theme gotcha: gitboard's --accent-blue is defined ONCE in :root (#7ed7e5/#8ed2dc pale cyan) and NOT overridden in [data-theme=light]. Anything using it directly will wash out on the white background. PR#10 left this asymmetry. When building a new themed UI element that needs a vibrant accent on BOTH themes, define your own component-scoped token at :root + override in .ide-shell[data-theme='light'] (pattern: see --specialist-chip-accent in globals.css). Theme-aware tokens that ARE properly overridden: --text-muted, --text-secondary, --text-primary, --accent-dim, --accent-soft, --bg-hover.

Drawer resize/maximize tuning: the store's setDrawerHeight clamp upper bound (window.innerHeight - OFFSET) MUST equal the component's MAXIMIZED_OFFSET, OR clicking maximize is a silent no-op (it asks for a height the store rejects). Standardized OFFSET=24 ŌĆö leaves a 24px hairline at top while keeping topbar visible. Also: BottomDrawer.tsx MUST NOT carry its own MAX_HEIGHT constant ŌĆö leads to silent drag failure when default open height exceeds the local cap (operator-reported 'drag does nothing' on viewport > 800px). Store is single source of truth for height bounds.

Graph forge-2a8a.2 pivot: bead spec said 'single ReactFlow viewport hosts all clusters' but operator rejected during review (one mass, no page structure). Final shape = one ReactFlow per cluster (ClusterPane), CSS-grid outer layout (.g-clusters), bare zone tint (no card border), bottom orphans strip. BeadNode has 6 hidden handles (lt/ls/tt/ts/bt/bs/rt/rs ŌĆö left/top/bottom/right ├Ś target/source); buildClusterFlow picks handle pair by ╬öx/╬öy so bezier never wraps over chips (same-column ŌåÆ top/bottom). Page scroll lives at .ide-main-content; .g-app is min-height:100% with no overflow. React Flow props for non-trapping wheel: zoomOnScroll=false, preventScrolling=false, panOnScroll=false. Refit on toggle via key={cluster.id:nodes.len:edges.len} on <ReactFlow>.

Gitboard expanded-body card pattern: wrap .pr-expanded-body and .issue-expanded-body with --card-border, --card-radius-md (8px), --surface-secondary bg, 14-18px padding, small 4-6px margin. This creates GitHub-style 'card floating on darker page' effect. Page bg --surface-primary (#0d1117) is darker than --surface-secondary (#161b22), so the card pops. Watch for later .pr-expanded-body override rules in globals.css (~line 1974) ŌĆö must keep them aligned with first rule (~line 1819).
Beadboard kanban dossier markdown should strip raw HTML tags and whitelist link protocols before rendering inline.
Beadboard project summaries now carry source health and priority; issue detail endpoint falls back from Dolt to sqlite when live Dolt unavailable.

Codex (chatgpt-codex-connector) leaves inline review comments via PR review API; gh pr view --json reviews shows only review body, fetch inline findings via gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>/pulls/<n>/comments

forge-f6qk.2 graph backend: GET /api/console/graph mounted in api/server.ts; new files core/graph-dao.ts + api/routes/graph.ts + types/graph.ts + tests/api/routes/graph.test.ts. resolveProject returns null on missing project_id (NOT first-project fallback) ŌĆö empty payload with 'project' note. 9 edge types verbatim in types/graph.ts:3 union. Live specialists filter to {starting,running,waiting}. Reviewer rebuttal pattern: cite the actual union enumeration as structural proof rather than expecting manual smoke.

gitboard already mounts beadboard's /api/beads/* routes via apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:6,35 (imports beadsRoutes from '../../../beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts'). Cross-package import. forge-5w9.9 deprecation MUST move beadboard's api/routes/beads.ts + core/beads-*.ts into apps/gitboard before deleting beadboard, or gitboard's beads endpoints break. beadboard backend on port 3010 is NOT a runtime dependency for gitboard ŌĆö gitboard serves beads itself on its own port (3000).

CSS grid implicit-column trap: display:grid WITHOUT grid-template-columns creates an implicit single-column grid sized to fit-content, not fill the parent. Children shrink to the width of the LARGEST row content, not to the container. Symptom: percentage widths on grandchildren resolve inconsistently because the parent row is variable width. Fix: always specify grid-template-columns (e.g. minmax(0, 1fr)) on a single-column list grid, OR use display:flex; flex-direction:column. Source: forge-l85 beadboard .module-list inconsistent dossier width.
GitHub Contents API: GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{path} returns base64 file content for files OR array of entries for dirs. Decode with Buffer.from(content, 'base64'). 404 means missing file.

Terminal frontend can receive not_found when reattaching stale in-memory PTY sessions after backend restart; TerminalTabPanel should clear stored token/session/output and open a fresh session automatically.

Real-world sp observability.db quirks discovered during live e2e (forge-xu1h test): (a) the slug derivation MUST strip the '.specialists/db' suffix from observability.db's parent ŌĆö basename naively gives 'db' for every repo. (b) sp's schema_version values vary wildly across repos (0, 11, 68, 84, 26997) ŌĆö a strict COMPATIBLE_RANGE rejects everything. Best contract check is table-shape presence (specialist_jobs exists), not version number. (c) Real observability.db paths live at both <repo>/.specialists/db/observability.db (modern) AND <repo>/.specialists/observability.db (legacy specialists repo) ŌĆö the scan walks recursively which handles both.

BottomDrawer resize must be defensive: (1) setPointerCapture in try/catch ŌĆö React StrictMode double-mount can leave the pointer with no active ID by the time the second invocation runs, throwing NotFoundError; (2) on catch, fall back to document.addEventListener for pointermove/pointerup so drag still works; (3) call setPointerCapture BEFORE preventDefault ŌĆö preventDefault on pointerdown can invalidate implicit pointer activation; (4) .bottom-drawer-resizer needs touch-action: none in CSS or trackpad/touchscreen gestures convert drag into scroll and cancel the pointer before pointermove fires.

graph-dao readIssues used to fetch limit:1000 unfiltered from Dolt, then filter status at app level. On projects with >1000 closed issues (specialists: 65 open + 2071 closed) the open ones were silently truncated ŌĆö graph showed ~7 of 65 expected open nodes. Always push status filter to SQL when the consumer only renders non-closed (forge-afac fix). Pattern applies to any 'fetch then filter' code that has a row cap below the realistic dataset size.

Specialist chip visibility rule (per operator design feedback): chip surfaces ACTIONABLE state only. Show running/starting/waiting (live in-flight) + error/cancelled (failure traceability ŌĆö operator needs to know which specialist died). HIDE 'done' ŌĆö a successful past run is history, not an actionable signal, and belongs in the per-bead dossier timeline (forge-4hmt). Don't bloat the feed with success badges. Rule lives in SpecialistOwnerBadgeForBead.VISIBLE_STATES + useSpecialistOwnership fallback chain (live ŌåÆ error ŌåÆ cancelled, never done).

terminal-stream-epic-design: For Gitboard embedded terminal, keep TerminalStream renderer separate from TerminalSessionProvider ownership; use versioned generic envelope with terminal-specific payloads; land protocol + safety gates before PTY; keep tmux/SSH/specialist-feed as follow-ups/non-MVP unless explicitly enabled.
xtrm-console typography pass: keep JetBrains Mono only on IDs, SHAs, branch refs, diff numbers, file paths, code blocks, and command hints; Inter everywhere else in dashboard chrome/meta text.
gitboard and beadboard dashboard builds may need bun install when vite bin missing in workspace

forge-f6qk.4 bead side-drawer: new BeadSideDrawer + useBeadSideDrawer hook; mounted in App.tsx as sibling of MainPane; IssueFeed row-click opens drawer (inline expand preserved); ESC + backdrop + X-button all fire optional onClose callback. z-index 50 (BottomDrawer is 40). Scope discipline pattern: when children fork off main without prior sibling's diff, executor re-does sibling's work ŌĆö orchestrator must steer/resume with explicit revert: git checkout main -- <files>.

Unified expanded-row card pattern: when .pr-row.is-expanded (or .issue-row.is-expanded), strip bottom radius+border on row and zero its margin-bottom; on the .pr-expanded-body (or .issue-expanded-body), strip top border+radius. Result: row+body render as a single card with full rounded outer corners only. Required because the row and body are separate DOM siblings ŌĆö without this they look like two stacked cards.

Wave-8 T3 Playwright deferred: forge-3ue5.13 not closed in v1 auto-mode run. Trigger condition: when repo-wide vitest infra (@vitejs/plugin-react workspace dep) is fixed, dispatch T3 executor with test-only scope discipline. Bead state preserved with notes.

When sp reviewer or sp merge complains about huge scope ('540 files changed') vs a much smaller claimed scope, suspect a stale local main: the merge-base used by the reviewer is whatever's in your local refs, and if local main is far behind origin/main (or behind a recently-merged PR), the cumulative diff balloons. Workaround: ignore the scope-breach finding when you can confirm the executor's own diff (git log <branch> ^HEAD --oneline) is small, and patch-apply the executor's actual commits directly. Source: forge-5wc and forge-pq6 reviewers both flagged 540-file scope while real executor diffs were 2 and 3 files.

forge-5w9.3 shell store shape: useShellStore (zustand) holds repos:RepoNode[], expanded:Set<string>, selection:SidebarSelection|null, sidebarCollapsed:boolean. All three of expanded/selection/sidebarCollapsed persist to localStorage under forge-5w9:expanded/selection/sidebarCollapsed keys. Narrow selectors (selectActiveSection, selectRepos, selectExpanded, selectSidebarCollapsed) avoid re-renders. Aggregator hook useRepoTree() in apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useRepoTree.ts: matches beads projects to github repos via TAIL of full_name (e.g. Jaggerxtrm/foo Ōåö beads project 'foo'). 18 matched out of 69 github + 28 beads on this machine.

On this VPS (159.195.49.110), custom Docker bridge subnets created by ad-hoc compose projects (e.g. 172.29.0.0/16) have NO outbound NAT ŌĆö TCP egress hangs silently with no error, even though DNS resolution works. Existing pre-configured bridges (mercury_*, infra_*) work fine. When adding a new docker compose stack here, either (a) attach to an existing working network, or (b) use network_mode: host with interface-scoped bind (HOST=<iface-ip>). The shared-VPS context means: never assume a fresh bridge will work, and never set blanket ufw rules ŌĆö neighbours' services depend on existing iptables/nftables state we can't see without sudo.

forge-xdxg: terminal bridge removed connection ownership checks; attach/input/resize/exit now authorize by client-held sessionId+streamId only, while TerminalTabPanel persists same identifier in localStorage. Same-origin tab/script can hijack live PTY session until 30s cleanup.

gitboard realtime WebSocket server expects client messages with action=subscribe/unsubscribe (not type). If live views only refresh on navigation, check shared WsClient message shape and mounted view subscriptions to beads:changes.

Observability persistence note (per forge-gud9 design): specialist_jobs rows in observability.db are RETENTION-FRIENDLY ŌĆö sp keeps closed/error rows after a chain finishes. To surface a per-bead specialist history view, the only new API surface needed is GET /api/specialists/jobs?bead_id=ŌĆ”&include_history=1 returning all rows (live+closed) DESC by updated_at_ms. No new write path or migration needed; data is already there.
forge-3ue5.8 review 5de5b7: owner badge/hook/tests implemented; requirements mostly met; PARTIAL due to missing storybook/visual evidence and failing test runtime deps.

forge-f6qk.5 specialists tab live cockpit: replaces .1 placeholder Specialists.tsx with two-pane layout (60/40 split, card list + structured detail pane). New pages/console/specialists/{ChainCard,ChainDetailPane,FilterChips}.tsx + hooks/useChains.ts + useChainDetail.ts. Polling pauses on visibilitychange. Open-bead handler uses dynamic-import attempt of useBeadSideDrawer with fallback to /gitboard/console/feed scroll-into-view via data-bead-id. Wrapped try/catch + TODO for direct import once .4 lands. Integration: at merge into xt/1ers, take --theirs on Specialists.tsx (.5 cockpit overrides .1 placeholder).

CSS grid + position:absolute child gotcha: an absolutely-positioned child of a 'display: grid' container is REMOVED from grid flow, so its template column gets filled by the NEXT in-flow child. If your design has N grid-template-columns but you include a position:absolute decoration as one of the spans, you actually have N-1 in-flow children filling N columns ŌĆö everything shifts left and columns visually misalign (e.g. id renders in the rail slot, title renders in the id slot). Two fixes: either drop the absolute span (use a ::before pseudo instead) or keep it in-flow with grid-column:1. Source: forge-pq6 beadboard rows where <span className='rail' /> with position:absolute caused id+title to overlap until forge-xsk.

forge-5w9 visual debt: shell ships functionally complete but operator reaction was 'worst-ass looking app'. Root causes: (1) appended beadboard's globals.css wholesale instead of designing fresh ŌĆö beadboard color/density bleeds into gitboard; (2) MainPane has no top app chrome (stripped topbar); (3) BeadsRepoView shows double headers (my section-header + beadboard's BOARD OVERVIEW); (4) Sidebar octicons too small/dark ŌĆö chip counts run together unreadable; (5) most repo rows empty (no activity dot color, no count) = text-wall. Next visual pass should design from scratch via ui-ux-pro-max skill, NOT inherit beadboard CSS. Functional shell to refine: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/{components/shell,components/beads/BeadsRepoView,components/github/GithubRepoView,styles/globals.css}.
Graph/specialists cache should key off repo epoch plus includeClosed and use per-key in-flight dedupe; keep TTL very short for live overlays.
bv triage score uses pagerank, staleness, priority_boost, time_to_impact, urgency, and risk; top picks can be mirrored client-side with open issue idle time, priority cost, and open load.

Reuse existing .pr-body-text + .pr-rich-text CSS for any markdown body rendered via renderPrBodyText ŌĆö those classes carry the white-space:pre-wrap, font-size, line-height baseline that browser defaults need to look right. Custom .release-body-text style produced unstyled markdown output (no h3/list/p spacing).

github releases ingestion pattern (forge-nwdr): the github_releases table is now populated by pollReleases() inside the standard pollRepo() loop ŌĆö separate from the user-events stream that previously bounded release visibility to ~300 events / ~90 days. Watermark is published_at (drafts have null published_at and are skipped). ETag-gated like pollPullRequests; max 5 pages * 100 = 500 releases per cycle per repo. Result: 239 releases across 13 repos vs the prior 2 ReleaseEvent rows in github_events. Publishes github:release.upsert on github:activity for new/changed rows. The same pattern applies to any future github entity we add: dedicated table + per-repo poll method + watermark+etag in github_repo_poll_state + publish under github:activity. Three github WS event types remain declared-but-unpublished: github:event.append, github:rate_limit, and (until this PR) github:release.upsert.

forge-5w9 routing decision: Zustand store-driven sidebar selection swap, no router lib. Rationale: no router lib in package.json; only 2 routes per repo; finite repo list; hash router is ~10 LOC future-add if URL sharing becomes needed. SidebarSelection type lives in apps/gitboard/src/types/shell.ts.

Test-bead executor scope discipline lesson from T1: when dispatching executor on a test-only bead, the contract MUST say 'test files + fixtures ONLY ŌĆö never edit production code. If a test fails because production code is wrong, REPORT do not edit.' Without this hard rule, executor expands scope to make tests pass. Also: never tell executor 'git checkout origin/main -- <file>' unless the file exists on origin/main ŌĆö when local main is ahead of origin (common in auto-mode), this command deletes the file. Use 'git checkout main -- <file>' for local refs.

Watcher storm root cause (forge-h830): beads-change-watcher polled every 2s ├Ś 25 projects against Dolt, was the dominant qps source on the shared 3308 server. Once forge-7cyq fixed WS subscribe contract, all that traffic actually reached the dashboard ŌåÆ UI thrash. Three independent issues stacked: (1) L96 deleted lastCommitHash for stable projects, defeating the fast path; (2) getCommitHash created+leaked a fresh DoltClient per poll without breaker check; (3) the 2s polling cadence is wasteful when fs.watch already provides ~1s real-time signal. Combined fix: keep cached hash on stable polls, breaker-check + disconnect in finally, bump ACTIVE_POLL_MS 2sŌåÆ30s. Plus client-side: coalesce WS refetch in useGraphData/useChains with REFETCH_COALESCE_MS = 1_500. Net: ~6├Ś reduction in WS batch volume + UI thrash localised to the still-uncoalesced BeadsRepoView (separate follow-up bead).
PR detail view now uses section-label + file-row patterns; linked beads extracted from forge-* refs

When briefing executor for additions to existing apps, ENFORCE 'reuse existing primitive X at file:path; do NOT scaffold new chrome' in CONSTRAINTS ŌĆö and list the specific component to wrap or extend. Past gap: forge-z6j.1 said 'reuse RepoSidebar/IssueFeed/PrTimeline' in soft NON_GOALS/CONSTRAINTS prose; executor still built parallel primitives. Phrase as imperative: 'IMPORT and MOUNT <Component> from <file>; do not write a replacement.'

Dashboard WS subscription mismatch (forge-7cyq fix): apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/ws.ts WsClient.subscribe sent {type:'subscribe'} but apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/handler.ts handleMessage routes on msg.action ŌĆö so EVERY subscribe was silently dropped server-side. beads:changes, github:*, specialists:activity all looked subscribed (the connection succeeded) but no events were ever delivered. The whole live-update story was secretly running on HTTP polling fallbacks. Fixed by switching client to {action:'subscribe'} matching the server contract. The handler also accepts action:'resume' and action:'unsubscribe' ŌĆö never 'type'. If you ever add a new WS message verb, use action, not type.

forge-5w9 security finding patched (forge-bvq): added encodeURIComponent on every path-segment interpolation of projectId/issueId in apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/beads-api.ts. Risk was path-confusion (no XSS sink). LOW severity, fix applied same session as audit.

forge-f6qk.1 IA restructure: Surface union narrowed to github|console; ConsoleTab expanded to 6-tab strip via CONSOLE_TABS in types/shell.ts; legacy persisted surface='beads' migrated via (raw.surface as string) === 'beads' check in App.tsx (TS2367 fix); placeholders Graph.tsx and Specialists.tsx use DotFillIcon + 'Coming in forge-f6qk.{3,5}' copy with ide-empty/ide-console-placeholder classes. Pattern: reviewer first-passed against EPIC validation instead of child bead ŌĆö rebut by citing child bead VALIDATION explicitly.

Dolt connection stability (forge-1qz): dolt-client.ts uses mysql2/promise createPool with health-probe (SELECT 1 every PROBE_INTERVAL_MS), per-query QUERY_TIMEOUT_MS=3s wrapper, and a circuit breaker (BREAKER_THRESHOLD consecutive failures opens it; probe success closes it). The probe-then-retry pattern IS the half-open mechanism ŌĆö no separate state machine. BeadsChangeWatcher.readIssues checks isBreakerOpen() and skips Dolt for JSONL when open. Health metrics exposed at /api/internal/dolt-health (no credentials/query bodies leaked).

Realtime envelope version is STRING not number ŌĆö initial forge-dgd shipped with version:number + Date.parse coercion, which silently fell back to REALTIME_PROTOCOL_VERSION for non-numeric strings (e.g. Dolt commit hashes), breaking dedupe for beads. Caught during forge-f0g review. Now: version is the source's row updated_at ISO (github) or commit hash (beads), passed through verbatim. Default for unversioned publishes is String(REALTIME_PROTOCOL_VERSION).

sp merge fails with bookkeeping conflicts when the specialist's executor branch is based on a snapshot where .beads/ files don't exist as currently tracked. Workaround: git cherry-pick -X theirs <commits>, then 'git checkout HEAD -- .beads/' to restore bookkeeping, then 'git rm --cached' any '.beads~<hash>' conflict-marker entries before committing. The executor's design changes land clean; only .beads/ infra needs preservation. Source: forge-93p merge session.

Gitboard Beads Feed now uses compact multiline rows with relationship groups on the second line; Beads tabs are Feed/Triage/Memories only; Feed sections are in_progress, collapsible open expanded by default, and collapsed closed.

epoch.ts onBump pattern (forge-7cyq): observability fs-watch ŌåÆ epoch.bump(repoSlug) is the canonical 'observability state changed' signal. epoch.ts exposes a Set<BumpListener> + onBump(cb): ()=>void. Listener errors are caught and console.error-logged (not silenced) so dead push paths surface. api/server.ts registers exactly one listener inside createApp() that calls registry.publish('specialists:activity', 'specialists:sync_hint', {reason, repo_slug}, epoch). createApp disposes the previous listener before re-registering ŌĆö pattern mirrors currentObservabilityWatcher cleanup. Use this same hook for any future observer of observability changes (rate-limited metrics, alerting, etc.).

Reviewer rebuttal pattern: when reviewer flags 'unmet: parameterized queries' but actual interpolation is only schema identifiers (table names, column aliases), rebut by citing SQLite's hard limitation that table names/aliases cannot be parameter-bound. The contract constraint about 'parameterize bead/chain/epic ids' specifically named user-data ids ŌĆö schema identifiers from PRAGMA database_list and registry-controlled slugs are not in that category. Cite file:line of the ? placeholders and the db.prepare(query).all(...params) call. Rebuttal accepted on forge-3ue5.4 (81ŌåÆ94).

When writing bead AC for performance/HA work, list BEHAVIORS not named implementations. forge-5i43's AC said 'Dolt sync-init must move off the request path' ŌĆö reviewer correctly enforced THAT specific gate, but a sibling sync-FS scan in listRepos() (called on every /api/specialists/jobs/* request before the cache check) slipped through and stayed as a 1.5s/request bottleneck post-merge. Better AC phrasing: 'no synchronous filesystem walks, ATTACH probes, or other O(repos) sync work on the request path ŌĆö name the symbols if you must, but the gate is the behavior, not the named code path.' Cost of the miss: a full follow-up bead (forge-9qmk) and another reviewer round.

BeadsRepoView currently full-reloads issues/memories/interactions on every beads:sync_hint via reloadKey; useGraphData/useChains already show 1.5s trailing coalescing pattern that could be extracted into shared hook.
xtrm console now uses /console as the primary shell, backed by /api/console/repos for merged GitHub/Beads repo records; /gitboard and /beadboard remain legacy mounted routes during migration.

forge-ci9 sidebar third-level nav: SidebarSelection is { repo, section, leaf }. Expanded set tracks BOTH repo (key=fullName) AND section (key='fullName::github' or '::beads'). Leaves drive MainPane to a single full-content view per click. Per-leaf component mapping in MainPane.tsx: activityŌåÆActivityTimeline, prsŌåÆPrTimeline, issuesŌåÆIssueTimeline, releasesŌåÆReleaseTimeline, kanban/issuesŌåÆBeadsRepoView. Black bg via #000 !important on body/.shell-app/.shell-main/.shell-sidebar. Collapse-toggle works because title is conditionally rendered (omitted when collapsed) so flex layout doesn't push the 20px toggle out of the 48px width.

forge-yy27 production-bug fix root causes: (1) useBeadsLive read projectId from envelope top-level instead of msg.data ŌĆö live sync silently dead until unwrap. (2) test mock used url.includes('page=N') which COLLIDES with 'per_page=100' substring causing infinite loops in pagination tests ŌĆö always use new URL(url).searchParams.get('page') for accurate page matching. (3) getIssues/getPrs in github-store default LIMIT=100 ŌĆö pagination tests must pass {limit: 200} or higher. (4) vitest happy-dom is the canonical jsdom-like env in this repo (jsdom package not installed). Use @vitest-environment happy-dom docblock, not jsdom.

Live e2e proof for forge-3ue5 (gitboard ownership view): with sp jobs running in 2+ repos, /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight returns 6 rows tagged by repoSlug (gitboard, xtrm-tools), and InFlightRail renders them grouped by repo with elapsed time. Screenshot: apps/gitboard/.playwright-mcp/e2e-rail-live.png

When dispatching a 'structural alignment' executor that rewrites a row/dossier layout, verify field completeness in the new output before merging. forge-pq6 silently dropped IssueDossier's Dependencies, Children, Labels, Agent and replaced the markdown renderer with stripHtml(line)ŌåÆ

; the regression wasn't visible until a user expanded a bead. Reviewer-PASS on the structural shape doesn't catch dropped data because the contract names the shape, not the dossier sections. Mitigation: in structural-alignment bead contracts, list every section/affordance that must survive the refactor under a 'PRESERVE' bullet.

Playwright MCP tests default to headless Chromium ŌĆö Firefox/WebKit-specific quirks (PointerEvent setPointerCapture, preventDefault ordering, touch-action support) are NEVER caught by Playwright synthetic OR real-pointer tests if browser type isn't explicitly switched. Verify the operator's actual browser via lsof -i:<dev-port> before iterating on cross-browser UI bugs. Battle-tested cross-browser drag pattern (used by react-resizable etc): onMouseDown + document.addEventListener (NOT window, NOT pointer capture) + document.body.style.userSelect='none' during drag.

Beads watcher (forge-igg) diffs 4 entity snapshots per Dolt commit-hash tick (issues, deps, memories, kv) and emits per-entity events plus semantic aliases (issue.deferred/superseded/flagged/unflagged) derived from field transitions. JSONL fallback only covers issues.jsonl ŌĆö memories/kv/deps degrade to stale while Dolt down, surfaced via beads:source_health {drift:true, scope:...}. Watcher version = Dolt commit hash (string); no Bun.sleep ŌĆö use setTimeout for Node-test compat.

Realtime envelope uses boot_id (uuid per server start), NOT process-start timestamp, so resume can detect server restarts. Client sends {since_seq, boot_id}; server replays from ring buffer only if boot_id matches AND seq in range, else emits domain sync_hint. Process timestamp would conflate clock skew with restart; uuid is explicit. Buffer is in-memory (500 envelopes/channel), wiped on restart ŌĆö full state is rebuilt via sync_hint, not persisted.
Octicon consistency pass: replace decorative glyphs in dashboard meta lines with LinkExternalIcon/CheckIcon/ChevronDownIcon; keep design-preview svg diagram as non-icon exception.
Beadboard feed now uses shared module-header/row spine structure; filter toggle wired to local open-only state.

Beadboard kanban column-width inconsistency root cause: StatusColumn used minWidth:240/maxWidth:300 as flex children with default flex:0 1 auto, so flexbox sized each column based on internal content ŌĆö short-titled columns settled at minWidth, longer-content columns grew toward maxWidth. Fix: flex:'1 1 0' + minWidth:260 (no maxWidth). Now all columns share parent space equally and minWidth only kicks in when total < parent width (triggers horizontal scroll instead of squashing).
forge-9qmk review: listRepos TTL cache uses Date.now refreshAt; tests assert readdirSync one-scan and TTL rescan with fake timers.
review forge-93p fail when gradients/hex remain in app globals despite token centralization
Shell-provider gate review: require explicit evidence for specialist-feed permission separation; status/policy fields alone insufficient.
terminal e2e review: verify base branch naming (master vs main) before hard-gating scope checks

gitboard sidebar Beads source chip: useRepoTree polls /api/beads/projects/:id/connection and maps connected->dolt, jsonl_fallback->jsonl; /connection now validates Dolt by running a tiny getIssues query, not just opening a socket.

Beadboard descriptions sometimes contain literal JSON-escape sequences (the two characters backslash+n) instead of real newlines, because some bd callers (planner specialists, copy-pasted prompts, mol templates) store JSON-encoded content as the description verbatim. Any markdown renderer must run a replace(/\\n/g, '\\n')-style unescape before splitting on newlines or the body renders as a single line with visible backslash-n. Source: forge-zg3 description rendering fix.
Review run: 14d1e1 compliance audit completed with traceability evidence and build verification
xtrm-console tokens now centralized in packages/ui; app chrome imports canonical tokens and square badge radii use var(--radius-sm)/var(--radius-pill)=0

sp merge fails with conflict on .beads/issues.jsonl when the orchestrator session mutates main's bd state (create child beads, add deps, claim, close) while a specialist worktree is open. Worktree forked from old main + main advanced + both touched issues.jsonl = unresolvable merge. Skill's chain-noise-filter applies to manual cherry-pick flows, NOT to sp merge ŌĆö sp merge bails. Mitigations: (a) batch all orchestrator bd writes before dispatching the next chain, (b) commit + push main's bd state between dispatches so worktrees fork from current state, or (c) fall back to manual cherry-pick with --strategy-option=ours on .beads/. Discovered swarm-wide on forge-3ue5.1 dispatch.

Specialist reviewers in gitboard validate their own worktree, not uncommitted main-worktree diffs; for self-implemented changes, commit or provide a branch-visible artifact before dispatching reviewer, otherwise reviews fail on missing lineage/diff evidence.

Beads UI affordance gaps vs bd CLI: 'bd sync' command does NOT exist (design buttons referencing it must use bd import or be dropped); bd does support comments, search, defer/undefer, reopen, create, history, graph ŌĆö these are absent from the xtrm-console design but should be added when the UI lands. Source: forge-tm0 explorer audit job e75d94.
GitHub REST conditional GET with auth + 304 preserves primary quota; use persisted ETag per repo/endpoint and stop paginated REST on watermark or short page, not fixed page-count only.
Beadboard dossier audit log now fetches interactions lazily on expansion; stale quick filter uses 7-day updated_at threshold.

forge-f6qk.3 graph frontend: layered LR layout in pages/console/graph/layout.ts with 9 edge weights (blocks:5, supersedes:3, parent-child:0.5, discovered-from:2, validates:2, caused-by:2, tracks:1, until:1, related:0.2); pulse filter MUST be status==='running' only (not full live set); types/graph.ts at apps/gitboard/src/types/graph.ts (NOT pages/console/graph/types.ts ŌĆö placeholder pattern at fork-from-stale-main is fine but orchestrator must rewire imports + delete placeholder at integration). MainPane Graph route does NOT pass repo prop (component reads projectId from useShellStore selection).

Graph forge-2a8a.3 polish: same-column React Flow edges (siblings ŌåÆ shared epic) need a CUSTOM bezier because getBezierPath's control-point offset scales with |dx| ŌåÆ with dxŌēł0 it collapses to a straight line that slices through chips. Fix in CustomEdge.tsx: detect sourcePosition===targetPosition && |dx|<12, build cubic 'M sx,sy C sx┬▒bend,sy sx┬▒bend,ty tx,ty' with bend = clamp(|dy|*0.35, 48, 160). Edge labels use text-shadow halo (surface-primary, 8 directions, ~2px) instead of pill background ŌĆö mimics SVG paint-order:stroke fill while staying HTML in EdgeLabelRenderer. Drop labels for parent-child/related/tracks/until (dash pattern conveys type). Non-structural edges at strokeOpacity 0.45 keep structural blocks primary.

Drawer height/resize was visually broken because .ide-main > * { flex: 1 } in globals.css overrode all inline style.height on direct children of MainPane (including BottomDrawer). Fix: explicit .ide-main > .bottom-drawer { flex: 0 0 auto; } ŌĆö opts the drawer out so its pixel-height wins. CRITICAL TEST RULE for any height-driven UI primitive in this app: verify rendered height via getBoundingClientRect().height, not element.style.height. Playwright reads inline style and gives false-positive when flex layout silently overrides.
Density pass: shrink spacing by token-level padding/gap changes, not font size; keep row height via --row-height and topbar via --topbar-height.

Custom <button> elements rendered as chips/pills MUST reset background to transparent + font: inherit + border-radius; browser user-agent button bg renders as a light block in dark-themed UIs and looks broken next to <span> siblings

forge-qc0 layout invariant: ANY MainPane content that uses @tanstack/react-virtual (ActivityTimeline, PrTimeline, IssueTimeline, ReleaseTimeline) needs a SIZED parent. Required CSS chain: html/body/#root height:100%; .ide-shell height:100vh + overflow:hidden; .ide-body flex:1 + min-height:0; .ide-main flex:1 + min-height:0 + overflow:hidden; .ide-main > * flex:1 + min-height:0. Without this chain virtualizer reports container as zero-height and renders nothing. min-height:100vh on shell is NOT enough ŌĆö must be height:100vh.

Lesson from live e2e: F8 SpecialistOwnerBadge was wired into BeadCard.tsx but the IDE shell renders rows through IssueFeed.tsx (article.row class), not BeadCard. ALWAYS verify which component actually paints the row by inspecting DOM classNames in the browser before declaring binding work done. The 'bead row' isn't always BeadCard ŌĆö IssueFeed has its own article element.
review forge-m38 fail when mono appears on non-identifier UI text in globals

Beadboard scanner historically diverged from the gitboard scanner. When porting fixes between them check ALL adjacent logic, not just the line in question. Specifically gitboard scanner reads dolt_database from BOTH config.yaml AND metadata.json ŌĆö beadboard only read config.yaml until forge-afac. Symptom: shared-server projects query the wrong DB (default 'dolt' instead of e.g. 'unitAI'), graph silently shows wrong-project data with no error.

Beadboard /api/beads/projects/<id>/issues returns BeadDependency entries with title='' (the reader/exporter doesn't denormalise dep titles). Clients must resolve dep titles by id from the loaded issues list rather than trusting dep.title. Same for dep.status ŌĆö prefer issueById.get(dep.id)?.status. Source: forge-3k5 dependency tree fix.

gitnexus_context() MCP call stalls explorers ŌĆö confirmed twice on forge-k4zy (job 7d51c7 and 9f93e8) at gitnexus_context({name: 'watcher'}). The call hangs past 120s heartbeat ŌåÆ sp marks the job errored. Symptom: explorer shows 'current: gitnexus_context' in sp ps but tokens stop advancing. Workaround: in bead CONSTRAINTS, instruct explorer to use serena find_symbol / get_symbols_overview + grep instead of gitnexus_context for symbol mapping. Affects all explorers/debuggers dispatched on this repo until gitnexus MCP is fixed.
Terminal protocol validator must enforce capabilities enum values, not string[] only; otherwise unsafe capabilities drift.

Observability metrics-dao token_trajectory_json: entries are {t, source/turn_index, token_usage: {input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_creation_tokens, cache_read_tokens, total_tokens, cost_usd}}. The token fields are NESTED under .token_usage, not top-level. Easy gotcha when summing tokens.

bd dep rule: an epic cannot block a task (bd dep add <epic-id> <task-id> errors with 'epics can only block other epics, not tasks'). Use --parent on bd create instead to nest tasks under an epic, OR use bd dep relate for non-blocking links between epic and task. Tasks can block tasks. Epics can block epics. Source: forge-qct epic linking session.

Drawer resize for real mice only works with onPointerDown + setPointerCapture + element-scoped pointermove/up/cancel listeners. The previous onMouseDown + window-scoped mousemove/mouseup pattern PASSED Playwright dispatchEvent synthetic tests but FAILED real mouse drag in browsers ŌĆö because real mouse drag triggers text-selection / drag-image behaviors that swallow window-level mousemove. PointerEvents with capture explicitly route all subsequent events to the captured element. CRITICAL TESTING RULE: synthetic dispatchEvent is NOT a valid proxy for real pointer drag ŌĆö always verify drag UI with Playwright browser_drag or page.mouse.down/move/up.

xtrm-console visual contract: bg #181818, single accent #8ed2dc, radius 0 (no pills), thin rgba(255,255,255,0.07ŌĆō0.18) borders, no gradients, Inter+JetBrains Mono with mono restricted to IDs/SHAs/branch refs/diff numbers/file paths, @primer/octicons-react as the only icon family, no charts/heatmaps/sparklines. Source: design handoff gDsOk8IG1FcsM7ojcdFJNg.
For single-node dashboard HA, SQLite WAL read model + background ingestion worker + WebSocket delta notifier beats Redis until need cross-process fan-out, durable queue, or horizontal scaling.

Code-sanity specialist must be dispatched with --job <exec-job>, never standalone. Its registry entry is worktree:none, so a solo dispatch has no filesystem to read and returns 'BLOCKED ŌĆö source not retrievable' (confirmed on forge-zgny job a937b8). Correct invocation: chain off an executor's worktree via 'sp run code-sanity --bead <id> --job <executor-job-id>'. For ad-hoc sanity passes without a prior executor, run a no-op executor first to provision a worktree, then attach code-sanity via --job.

renderPrBodyText only handled links + inline code originally; added **bold** + *italic* + .pr-rich-text h3/p/ul/li/strong/em/code styling to make GH release/PR notes render with proper visual hierarchy. Regex uses negative lookbehind `(?<![*\\w])` to prevent italic from eating bold markers.

Build config gotchas in this monorepo: (1) packages/ui/src/styles/tokens.css must NOT import 'tailwindcss' ŌĆö packages/ui has no node_modules; the consumer app's globals.css does the import. (2) apps/beadboard uses vitest with globals:false, so tests/dashboard/setup.ts must explicitly 'import { expect } from vitest' and call expect.extend(matchers) ŌĆö side-effect import of @testing-library/jest-dom fails. Setup file must be registered in vitest.config.ts setupFiles. (3) apps/beadboard/tests/core/beads-reader.test.ts imports bun:sqlite which vitest can't resolve ŌĆö that test needs to migrate to 'bun test' runner or be split out of the vitest include. Source: post-design-refresh build fixup.

forge-7xu unified shell: TopBar [GitHub|Beads] switch + tabs (7 github / 5 beads) + Sidebar single-level repos with RepoIcon + MainPane per-(surface,tab) dispatch. All 5 beads panels live in BeadsRepoView.tsx (Kanban=KanbanBoard, Feed=IssueFeed, Triage=inline TriagePanel ported from beadboard App.tsx:411, Closed=inline list, Memories=inline list). GitHub README/CHANGELOG/Reports use ReadmeView/ChangelogView/ReportsView from RepoContentPanels.tsx. CSS class prefix .ide-* in globals.css. Black bg via .ide-shell.

BeadsChangeWatcher fast-path (forge-3mdw): cache lastCommitHash per project; if cheap getCommitHash() matches last seen AND we have a previous snapshot, emit beads:source_health and return WITHOUT readSnapshot. Drops poll-tick cost from a full N+1 hydration (~600 queries for ~200 issues) to a single SELECT current_commit() (~2ms). DoltClient.getIssues + getClosedIssues use hydrateIssues(rows) which does 1 issues SELECT + 3 IN-clause batched queries (dependencies/issue_related/labels) instead of per-row sub-queries. issue_related/labels remain optional (try/catch swallow); they're absent from some Dolt schemas.
review-forge-hl1-partial-missing-chevrondownicon-import

Beadboard PR auto-link: client-side substring match of beads issue.id (regex with word-boundary lookahead/behind, escape dots for epic children like 'forge-z6j.1') against OPEN PR title+body from /api/github/prs. Stored github_prs has no head_ref column, so branch-based matching would require schema+poller change ŌĆö title/body matching is enough for repos using 'Closes <id>' convention.

forge-zpw8 logger: structured emit() + LogEntry types + WS fan-out on 'system' channel + JSONL daily disk sink with 7-day retention. Singleton at apps/gitboard/src/core/logger.ts. Call sites in poller (cycle/rate_limit/etag), watcher (poll.skipped/snapshot_read/source_health.changed/batch.published/drift.detected), dolt-client (breaker.opened/query.slow/reconnect.attempt/recovered), ws/handler (client.connected/disconnected/subscribe.version_mismatch), server (request.slow/error). Endpoints: GET /api/internal/logs (ring query, level/component/event/since/limit), /api/internal/logs/files (list jsonl files).

forge-a72 tsc cleanup: 28ŌåÆ0 errors via mechanical fixture/type-cast updates. Concrete patterns: RepoStat test fixtures need issues_open/releases/last_event_at; dolt-client mysql2 overloads need 'as unknown as' casts; bun:sqlite bindings tighten via explicit row types; happy-dom (not jsdom) for hook tests; Record<string,unknown>[] casts via 'as unknown' for typed arrayŌåÆgeneric-record conversions; null-guard string params before encodeURIComponent.

Drawer/panel styling rule for gitboard UI: ANY new panel surface MUST use --surface-primary (= #000 dark / #fff light) ŌĆö NEVER define custom drawer-bg-* or panel-bg-* gray tokens. Layered grays read as '3D' and clash with the rest of the app's plain flat aesthetic. Visual dividers between surfaces should be a single 1px hairline in --border-subtle that lights up to --accent on interaction (e.g. resize handle). Hit areas for thin dividers must be Ōēź6px (use transparent padding around a 1px ::before line) ŌĆö 2px is mouse-ungrabbable in practice.

forge-5w9.4 sidebar component: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/Sidebar.tsx. Flat visible-row list approach for ARIA tree (no recursion). focusedId state synced via onFocus handler ŌĆö critical to avoid stale-state when DOM focus moves out-of-band (e.g. tests calling .focus()). CSS in globals.css under '/* forge-5w9: IDE shell sidebar */' marker. Preview route /gitboard/shell-preview in App.tsx (ShellPreview component) ŌĆö REMOVE in forge-5w9.6 once MainPane lands.

GraphŌåÆReact Flow spike (forge-2a8a.1) outcome: bundle delta = 77.35 kB gzip (75.07 JS + 2.28 CSS), tighter than published @xyflow/react 61 kB because @dagrejs/dagre adds ~16 kB. Under 80 kB target but only ~2.6 kB headroom ŌĆö .2/.3 must NOT add elkjs alongside dagre. dagre.setGraph({rankdir:'LR'}) drives layering off STRUCTURAL_EDGE_TYPES from clusters.ts; render all edges but only structural ones set positions. ClusterNode JSX (from Graph.tsx) ports cleanly to a custom node with <Handle> at left/right; .g-node CSS works unchanged. /gitboard/spike/reactflow route confirmed working with cluster forge-ojn4.9ŌåÆforge-ojn4.4.

forge-5w9.7 beads port: 5 components copied from apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/* into apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/. Import rewrites: { api } ŌåÆ { beadsApi as api }; ../../lib/api.ts ŌåÆ ../../lib/beads-api.ts; api.getInteractions ŌåÆ api.listInteractions. Backend /api/beads/projects/{id}/issues/closed returns 404 for some Dolt-backed projects ŌĆö BeadsRepoView catch-isolates each load so partial data still renders. gitboard bottom drawer logs are backed by core/logger ring + system WebSocket channel; useSystemLogs must fetch /api/internal/logs on mount because ws client.connected is emitted before the system subscription exists and old ring entries otherwise never appear.

Gitboard global theme is controlled by ShellState.theme persisted in localStorage key forge-vk47:theme and applied via .ide-shell[data-theme=light|dark]; keep dark default and preserve borderless feed/list rows when tuning light mode.

Reconciliation pattern when local + origin independently land the same feature (happens when work gets pushed via squash-PR while local branch keeps original commits): expect add/add + content conflicts on the SAME files. Resolution: 'git checkout --theirs' on the duplicate-PR files (origin's squashed version becomes canonical), then patch any downstream consumer whose imports broke because origin's API surface evolved. Concrete instance from v0.2.0 reconcile: PR#10 landed forge-1qz/igg/dgd/3mdw as one squash; local had those as separate commits. apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts, beads-change-watcher.ts, useBeadsLive.ts, types/realtime.ts all conflicted ŌĆö took --theirs for all. apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/internal-dolt-health.ts then broke because origin renamed getDoltHealthSnapshot away ŌĆö stubbed it.
forge-f6qk.4 review: missing explicit onClose callback in BeadSideDrawer despite close paths working

forge-gud9 implementation: VSCode-style bottom drawer mounted as sibling of MainPane content inside .ide-main. Uses zustand shell.ts drawer state (drawerOpen/drawerHeight 120-600/drawerTab) persisted via forge-gud9: localStorage keys. Tab bar with Logs + Specialists tabs, terminal-style rows. Logs tab uses useSystemLogs hook subscribing to useWebSocket('system', ...) ŌåÆ 2000-entry ring + level chip filter + search + autoscroll-on-tail + clear button + W/E count status. Specialists tab uses useAllSpecialistJobs polling /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight every 5s returning {in_flight, recent_history}. Backend added recentJobs(limit) DAO method + extended /jobs/in-flight to return both buckets. Theme tokens: --bottom-drawer-bg/--bottom-drawer-tabbar-bg/--bottom-drawer-border at :root with .ide-shell[data-theme=light] override. Ctrl/Cmd+J global keydown handler in MainPane. Collapsed by default to prevent first-load layout shift. @tanstack/react-virtual used in Logs panel when entries >= 500. Live e2e via Playwright confirmed: 31 in_progress + 50 history rows, tab switching, theme toggle, ctrl+j toggle all work. Reviewer PASS after rebuttal evidence on wrapper test path.

sp merge intermittently fails with 'Merge conflict while merging' even when git merge-tree shows zero conflicts. Root cause: bd auto-export races with sp merge's working-tree check, writing .beads/issues.jsonl mid-operation. .beads/issues.jsonl IS in sp merge's ignore list per --help, but the race still triggers git's local-changes-would-be-overwritten guard. Workaround: fall back to manual squash-merge per cherry-pick playbook ŌĆö git reset --hard origin/main, git checkout HEAD -- .beads/, git merge --squash <feature-branch>, then commit with conventional message. Do not retry sp merge in a loop ŌĆö it will keep failing the same way.

React Flow rebrand: as of 2026, install '@xyflow/react' (v12+), not legacy 'reactflow' (v11.x, frozen). Imports: { ReactFlow, Handle, Position, type NodeProps } from '@xyflow/react'; CSS: '@xyflow/react/dist/style.css'. v12 renamed custom-node position props to positionAbsoluteX/Y. For LR DAG auto-layout pair with '@dagrejs/dagre' (g.setGraph({rankdir:'LR'})). Bundle: ~58kB gzip JS + ~3kB CSS = ~61kB total.

Codex review findings on already-merged PRs are worth acting on quickly ŌĆö they catch real regressions even with good test coverage. Pattern: (1) my forge-qa5g depth-0 fall-through caused duplicates for flat layouts because both the fast path AND recursion loaded the same dir; fix is to track flatLoadedChildren and skip them in the for-loop. (2) my forge-afac SQL status filter (status IN [open,in_progress,blocked,deferred]) was correct for the default view but silently regressed include_closed=true; fix is to thread includeClosed through readIssues + cache key. (3) status filter forgot in_review which is a valid Beads status normalised in buildGraph; always check normalizeStatus when writing a status allowlist. Constant ACTIVE_GRAPH_STATUSES exported from graph-dao.ts is the authoritative non-closed set.
Microtask deferral alone does not fix cold-start contention; need background attach work with event-loop yields and a parallel integration gate.
Session close report for terminal recovery lives at .xtrm/reports/2026-05-20-38fedec7.md and documents PR #16 plus follow-up specialist-feed/stale-session work.

Replaces hand-rolled bd ready polling with epic-scoped, wave-grouped status. v3 bead contracts/job rules unchanged.
